gmw_00007784.name: COSBO-1
gmw_00007784.overview: COSBO-1 is a millimeter-emitting source detected with the Max-Planck Millimeter Bolometer [arXiv:0911.4297]. Its millimeter emission is likely produced by a radio- and optically undetected galaxy located at a high redshift of z > 3.5 [arXiv:0911.4297].

sources.summary: The paper treats COSBO-1 as a MAMBO source whose millimeter emission likely comes from a radio- and optically undetected z>3.5 galaxy, not the lower-redshift IRAC/optical counterpart or overdensity.

quotes.quote: For COSBO-1, a recent Submillimetre Array (SMA) detection (which will be published elsewhere) indicates that the millimeter emission is produced by a radio and optically undetected galaxy at z>3.5 as also implied by its radio-to-millimeter spectral index; Bertoldi2007 . The counterpart to this MAMBO source selected by Bertoldi2007 , a relatively bright IRAC/optical source with a photometric redshift of 1.2 , is related to the galaxy group at this redshift. However, it is very probably not responsible for the millimeter emission. The association between this source and the overdensity is unlikely, P_ d 0.05 . Hence, we discard this one as a real association between a SMG and a galaxy group at z 1.5 .

gmw_00007785.name: COSBO-3
gmw_00007785.overview: COSBO-3 is a high-redshift galaxy located in a neighborhood where galaxies cluster at redshifts between 2.2 and 2.4 [arXiv:0911.4297]. Its most likely counterpart, COSBO-3S, has a matching photometric redshift, and observations indicate its emission is produced by at least two sources [arXiv:0911.4297].

sources.summary: COSBO-3 lies in a neighborhood whose galaxies cluster near z 2.2-2.4; its likely counterpart COSBO-3S has a matching photometric redshift, while CARMA indicates multiple MAMBO-emitting sources.

quotes.quote: For COSBO-3, the redshift distribution of the galaxies in its close neighbourhood is consistent with most of them being at z 2.2-2.4 (Fig. fig:photo_z ). Two radio sources can be identified within 10 \ from the MAMBO source. The most likely radio/IR/optical counterpart (COSBO-3S) has a photometric redshift of 2.3 Bertoldi2007 . Based on the i^+ band selected catalog, we find a redshift of 2.4 for this source, consistent with the likely redshift of the galaxy group. Recent CARMA observations indicate the MAMBO emission is produced by at least two sources (Sm o lci et al., in prep.).

gmw_00007786.name: COSBO-6
gmw_00007786.overview: COSBO-6 is a high-redshift galaxy with millimeter emission [arXiv:0911.4297]. It is associated with a surrounding galaxy group at a redshift of approximately 1.2 to 1.8, while its own radio and optical counterpart has a photometric redshift estimated at 1.9 [arXiv:0911.4297].

sources.summary: COSBO-6 is associated with a surrounding galaxy group at z 1.2-1.8; the radio/optical counterpart to its millimeter emission has photometric redshift 1.9, consistent within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Most galaxies in the group around COSBO-6 lie at z 1.2-1.8 . Bertoldi2007 estimated a photometric redshift of 1.9 for the radio/optical identified counterpart to the millimeter emission. Although the redshift for the likely counterpart is slightly larger than the one implied by the redshift distribution of the surrounding high-redshift BzK galaxies, it agrees within z = 0.2 .

gmw_00007787.name: COSBO-16
gmw_00007787.overview: COSBO-16 is a millimeter-detected source surrounded by a group of galaxies at a photometric redshift of 1.4 [arXiv:0911.4297]. Although the redshift of its likely radio and optical counterpart is somewhat ambiguous, a redshift of 1.4 is slightly favored for both the source and its surrounding peak [arXiv:0911.4297].

sources.summary: COSBO-16 is surrounded by galaxies at photometric redshift 1.4, but the likely counterpart has ambiguous redshift solutions; the authors slightly favor z=1.4 for both source and surrounding peak.

quotes.quote: The galaxies surrounding COSBO-16 have a photometric redshift of 1.4 . The photometric redshift derived for the likely radio/optical counterpart from the i^+ band selected catalog is 0.5 Bertoldi2007 , however the secondary solution (second minima in the ^2 distribution) implies z 1.4 . The photometric redshift listed in the COSMOS catalog is, however, 2.55 which agrees better with that implied by the radio to millimeter spectral index Bertoldi2007 . Because of the somewhat ambiguous redshift derivation for the millimeter source, it is difficult to relate it to the redshift peak in the surrounding galaxies, although we may slightly favor to use z=1.4 as the most likely case for both.

gmw_00007788.overview: The galaxy 401126746 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:2404.05793]. Its supermassive black hole mass has been derived using Gemini Near-Infrared Spectrograph observations of the hydrogen emission line [arXiv:2404.05793].

sources.summary: For source 401126746, the paper avoids an artifact-affected CIV line and derives the black-hole mass from Gemini/GNIRS H-line spectroscopy, using it in the z>1 low-mass AGN galaxy sample.

quotes.quote: For one of these three sources, 401126746, the CIV line of the SDSS spectrum shows an artifact that prevents a reliabe use of the FWHM to derive the BH mass. For this source, the BH mass is derived from the FWHM and luminosity of the H emission line ( 2020ApJ...889...32S ) detected in Gemini/GNIRS Fast Turnaround observations (PI: Suh; see Appendix Gemini ).

We obtained a 0.8-2.5 m spectrum of 401126746 using the Gemini Near-Infrared Spectrograph (GNIRS) on the Gemini telescope with queue-observing mode, under the program GN-2021A-FT-216. GNIRS was configured using a Short Blue camera (0,15"/pixel), 32 l/mm cross-dispersed mode, and a 0.675" wide slit. Observations utilized the standard ABBA method of nodding along the slit to enable sky subtraction.

gmw_00007789.name: NGC 2559
gmw_00007789.overview: NGC 2559 is a nearby spiral galaxy classified as morphological type SB(s)bc pec [arXiv:1012.1655]. Located at a distance of 20.8 Mpc, it is an infrared-bright galaxy with a dust temperature of approximately 26 to 29 K [arXiv:1012.1655].

sources.summary: The SASSy pilot targeted NGC 2559 as a nearby spiral, detected it at 850 microns, and found its dust temperature warmer than comparison galaxy samples.

quotes.quote: Extragalactic observations The first set of observations planned was imaging of a region measuring 0.5^ 2^ , with one end centred on NGC 2559, a nearby galaxy, and the other end extending into the Galactic Plane cirrus. NGC 2559 is a spiral galaxy at a distance of 20.8 Mpc (from the systemic velocity and assuming a Hubble constant of 75 km s ^ 1 ), with morphological type SB(s)bc pec deV91 . It was chosen as a target in this study since it is the IR-brightest nearby galaxy that lies in the SASSy area, and had not been previously observed by SCUBA.

ngc2559new shows an optical image (from the Digitized Sky Survey) of NGC 2559 with matched-filtered SCUBA-2 contours overlaid. NGC 2559 shows up at a flux density of 156 26 mJy and would be detectable in data representative of SASSy (with target rms of 30 mJy).

We see that the dust temperature of NGC 2559 is around 26-- 29 K. This is warmer than for most of the galaxies studied in the SINGS sample SINGS , as well as those studied using BLAST Wiebe09 . This is consistent with requiring a relatively higher value of U than for those other galaxies.

gmw_00007790.name: PKS 1138 262
gmw_00007790.overview: PKS 1138 262 is a massive high-redshift radio galaxy located within a dense protocluster [arXiv:1809.08755, arXiv:astro-ph/0410202]. The system is a composite of active star formation and an active galactic nucleus, surrounded by a giant, structured nebula [arXiv:1809.08755, arXiv:astro-ph/0410202].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 1138 262 as the Spiderweb radio galaxy, maps its enormous nebula, and describes it as an AGN/starburst system likely to become a brightest cluster galaxy.

quotes.quote: PKS 1138 262 This paper focuses on a dense protocluster associated with a radio galaxy, PKS 1138 262 (or MRC 1138 262, _ J2000 = 11 ^ h 40 ^ m 48 ^ s , _ J2000 = 26 ^ d 29 ^ m 09 ^ s , Bolton:1979,Roettgering:1994,Roettgering:1997,Carilli:1997 ) at z=2.156 known as the Spiderweb galaxy Pentericci:1998,Miley:2006 .

The most energetic source present is the Spiderweb radio galaxy. We identified the enormous \ structure extending over 100 ph-kpc associated with this massive system. The spatial extent broadly agrees with previous findings which have reported an extended component in the \ line Gopal:2000,Kurk:2002 , X-ray Carilli:2002 , UV Pentericci:1998,Hatch:2008 , and the CO( 1-0 ) line Emonts:2016 .

The Spiderweb galaxy is also known to be a composite of active star formation and AGN Ogle:2012,Drouart:2014 . The starburst component has SFR of 1400 yr ^ 1 Seymour:2012,Rawlings:2013,Dannerbauer:2014 , supported by a rich gas reservoir of 6 10^ 10 \ Emonts:2013,Emonts:2016,Gullberg:2016 . This monster galaxy is expected to grow into a brightest cluster galaxy, as seen in the local Universe Hatch:2009 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1138 262 as a massive z=2.16 radio galaxy in a dense proto-cluster, with a very bright K-band host, giant nebula, radio-jet structure, and clumpy optical counterpart.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: one centered at the position of the radio galaxy as the optical observations ( , _ J2000 = 11^h40^m48^s , -26 ^ 29 10 , hereafter )

quotes.quote: In this paper we present deep VLT multi wavelength imaging observations of the field around the radio galaxy PKS 1138 262 aimed at detecting and studying a (potential) proto-cluster centered at this radio source. \ is a massive galaxy at z = 2.16 , located in a dense environment as indicated by optical, X-ray and radio observations. We had already found an over-density of \ emitting galaxies in this field, consistent with a proto-cluster structure associated with the radio galaxy.

The magnitude of \ is the brightest of all known radio galaxies close to z = 2 . After correction for possible non stellar components, the K band magnitude is 16.8, from which a stellar mass of 10^ 12 M _ was inferred pen97 . The radio galaxy possesses a giant ( 120 kpc) and luminous \ nebula, with a wealth of structure: a bright region associated with the radio jet and filaments extending over > 40 kpc pen97,kur00b . The optical counterpart of the radio galaxy is extremely clumpy and resolved into many components by the HST pen98 .

gmw_00007791.name: 2MASX J04180598-6336523
gmw_00007791.overview: 2MASX J04180598-6336523 is an early-type galaxy located at a luminosity distance of approximately 74.2 Mpc [arXiv:1903.08665]. The galaxy has a heliocentric redshift of z=0.0170 and is dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:1903.08665].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J04180598-6336523 as ASASSN-18tb's extended host, gives its redshift and distance, and notes that it is an early-type galaxy dominated by old stars.

quotes.quote: ASASSN-18tb is located 4 8 south and 1 9 east of the center of 2MASX J04180598--6336523, an extended source in the Two Micron All Sky Survey 2MASS; 2006Skrutskie with magnitudes m_J = 15.06 0.11 , m_H = 14.23 0.13 , and m_K = 13.88 0.17 . Prior to the discovery of ASASSN-18tb, there were no public spectroscopic observations of 2MASX J04180598-6336523. However, when obtaining a classification spectrum of the supernova, also obtained a spectrum of the host galaxy. Using cross-correlations with galaxy templates, they found that it has a heliocentric redshift of 5090 30 km s ^ 1 (z=0.0170 0.0001) . This redshift yields a luminosity distance of 74.2 Mpc assuming H_0 = 69.6 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _M = 0.286 , and _ = 0.714 2006Wright,2014Bennett .

Additionally, while all of the SNe Ia-CSM identified by were found in late-type spirals or dwarf irregulars (star-forming galaxies indicative of young stellar populations), as noted by , the host of ASASSN-18tb is an early-type galaxy dominated by old stellar populations.

gmw_00007792.name: UGC8802
gmw_00007792.overview: UGC8802 is a disk galaxy characterized by a high neutral gas content [arXiv:1206.0842]. Compared to other disk galaxies of similar stellar mass, it exhibits a flat star formation rate profile and a steep metallicity gradient [arXiv:1206.0842].

sources.summary: The paper models UGC8802 as a gas-rich disk galaxy with flat star formation, a steep metallicity gradient, and gradual inside-out growth driven by late gas infall.

quotes.quote: The disk galaxy UGC8802 has high neutral gas content and a flat profile of star formation rate compared to other disk galaxies with similar stellar mass. It also shows a steep metallicity gradient. We construct a chemical evolution model to explore its growth history by assuming its disk grows gradually from continuous gas infall, which is shaped by a free parameter -- the infall-peak time. By adopting the recently observed molecular surface density related star formation law, we show that a late infall-peak time can naturally explain the observed high neutral gas content, while an inside-out disk formation scenario can fairly reproduce the steep oxygen abundance gradient.

gmw_00007793.name: S5100822662
gmw_00007793.overview: S5100822662 is a non-target ALMA source detected in available imaging [arXiv:2006.04837]. Its close association with the ALPINE target in the same pointing suggests it is a high-redshift galaxy that may be interacting [arXiv:2006.04837].

sources.summary: The paper describes S5100822662 as a non-target ALMA source whose association with the ALPINE target favors a high-redshift line interpretation and possible interaction.

quotes.quote: A similar argument applies to S5100822662 We note that this particular source was included also in the luminosity function of Yan et al. 2020, as they used the total flux of the central target vuds cosmos 5100822662 (see also Figure fig:cutouts ), which was not deblended from the serendipitous companion (see 2020arXiv200200962B ). However, since the latter respects the criterion to be a non--target source (i.e., distance from the phase higher than 1 " ) we included it in our calculations. We note that its exclusion from our sample does not alter the result significantly.

Differently from S818760, this source is detected in the available images (see Figure fig:cutouts ) and based on the 2016ApJS..224...24L catalog it has a photometric redshift of 0.69. However, the strict association with the ALPINE target in the same pointing (Figure fig:cutouts ) favours a high-- z interpretation for this source with the ALMA emission likely due to the \ line. It is also possible that the emission in the photometric images is produced by a foreground source that is not related to the ALMA detection (see also 2018ApJ...861...43P ).

gmw_00007794.overview: The galaxy 850.0027 is a dusty star-forming galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 4.6 [arXiv:2208.01816]. It has a stellar mass of 5.3 billion solar masses and a star formation rate of 152 solar masses per year [arXiv:2208.01816].

sources.summary: The paper shows 850.0027 can mimic a z~18 NIRCam dropout, but NOEMA and submillimeter data favor a dusty star-forming galaxy at z~4.6-5.

quotes.quote: For the main target of this paper, \ (known as 850.0027 in Zavala2017a ), the on-source integration time was around 25 min. Calibration and imaging of the uv visibilities were then performed with gildas www.iram.fr/IRAMFR/GILDAS , producing continuum maps with 0 15 0 15 pixels centered at 270 GHz . For , the achieved RMS is measured to be _ =0.10 mJy beam ^ 1 and the beam-size 1 35 0 85 .

This is illustrated by studying the source , a 850 selected galaxy with robust interferometric observations at 1.1 mm by NOEMA. The results from an SED fitting using the JWST photometry alone suggest z 18 , whereas a joint SED fitting analysis including the NIRCam constraints and the long-wavelength (sub-)millimeter data implies a photometric redshift of 4.6 0.4 (with physical properties that resemble other DSFGs: M_ =(5.3 1.3) 10^ 10 ; SFR=152 66 ; L_ dust =(1.9 0.6) 10^ 12 L_ ).

gmw_00007795.name: W2326+0608
gmw_00007795.overview: W2326+0608 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located at a distance of approximately 73 Mpc [arXiv:2305.16786]. It shares a common neutral hydrogen environment with a nearby companion galaxy, SDSS J232603.86+060835.8, which is located at a projected distance of 8 kpc [arXiv:2305.16786].

sources.summary: The paper describes W2326+0608 as a BCDG with strong dust emission, high H I column density near the galaxy, and likely tidal triggering by a neighbor.

quotes.quote: W2326+0608 W2326+0608 is a BCDG at z=0.01678 ( D_ L 73 Mpc ). A nearby galaxy, SDSS J232603.86+060835.8, is also seen towards the north at an angular distance of 22.7 (see Fig. fig1a ). This angular distance corresponds to a projected distance of 8 kpc if this system is at same redshift as the BCDG. The stellar mass is estimated as (M_ / M _ ) 7.0 (Chandola et al. submitted).

W2326+0608 Low resolution (20.8 19.6 , P.A. 28.8 ^ ) image (Fig. fig1a , which is sensitive enough to detect H i gas with column density 5.9 10 ^ 19 cm ^ 2 , shows both W2326+0608 and nearby galaxy SDSS J232603.86+060835.8 share same H i environment. H i region in this image extends up to 1.7 which corresponds to projected linear size 35 kpc. Integrated H i profile for H i region in this image is extracted from the same low resolution H i cube (Fig. fig7 ).

gmw_00007796.name: SDSS J232603.86+060835.8
gmw_00007796.overview: SDSS J232603.86+060835.8 is a galaxy located at a projected distance of approximately 8 kpc from the blue compact dwarf galaxy W2326+0608 [arXiv:2305.16786]. The two galaxies share a common neutral hydrogen environment and are tidally interacting [arXiv:2305.16786].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J232603.86+060835.8 as W2326+0608's nearby galaxy, sharing a common H I environment and likely tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: W2326+0608 W2326+0608 is a BCDG at z=0.01678 ( D_ L 73 Mpc ). A nearby galaxy, SDSS J232603.86+060835.8, is also seen towards the north at an angular distance of 22.7 (see Fig. fig1a ). This angular distance corresponds to a projected distance of 8 kpc if this system is at same redshift as the BCDG.

W2326+0608 Low resolution (20.8 19.6 , P.A. 28.8 ^ ) image (Fig. fig1a , which is sensitive enough to detect H i gas with column density 5.9 10 ^ 19 cm ^ 2 , shows both W2326+0608 and nearby galaxy SDSS J232603.86+060835.8 share same H i environment. H i region in this image extends up to 1.7 which corresponds to projected linear size 35 kpc. Integrated H i profile for H i region in this image is extracted from the same low resolution H i cube (Fig. fig7 ).

W2326+0608 is located in the H i gas-rich environment of nearby galaxy SDSS J232603.86+060835.8 and they are tidally interacting. Due to this tidal interaction, there might be a collapse or infall of gas which has triggered the starburst in W2326+0608. Though we do not have enough spatial resolution to look into the details of W2326+0608 itself, the asymmetric H i profile hints at this scenario.

gmw_00007797.name: UGC 5540
gmw_00007797.overview: UGC 5540 is a gas-rich, edge-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0039 [arXiv:2305.16786]. It is positioned near the dwarf galaxy W1016+3754, and its proximity may trigger gas infall into its neighbor through gravitational perturbation [arXiv:2305.16786].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 5540 as a same-redshift, gas-rich edge-on disk near W1016+3754 whose proximity could trigger gas infall into the dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: An edge-on disk galaxy, UGC 5540, is at same redshift, z=0.0039 , and angular distance of 8 towards the south.

The H i total intensity map with synthesized beam 58.7 57.0 , P.A. 18.2 ^ overlaid on -band Digitized Sky Survey (DSS) optical image (Fig. fig1 ) shows a gas-rich galaxy UGC 5540 towards the south at 38.4 kpc in projection from BCDG W1016+37. In addition to this, we detected another blue compact starburst galaxy, LEDA 2108569 2015ApJS..217...27A , at an angular distance of 6.9' towards the south-west from UGC 5540 and redshifted by 100 km s ^ 1 . reported the presence of a H i cloud towards the east of W1016+3754 in the low resolution ( 1 ) image.

Since the projected distance ( 38.4 kpc) of W1016+3754 from UGC 5540 is close enough (so as the star-burst dwarf irregular LEDA 2108569) to cause gravitational perturbation 2008gady.book.....B, 2015ApJ...802...82F , this infall of gas in W1016+3754 might be triggered by UGC 5540. Although no direct evidence of tidal interaction like tidal filaments is visible in H i image possibly due to the sensitivity limit of our observation.

gmw_00007798.name: LEDA 2108569
gmw_00007798.overview: LEDA 2108569 is a blue compact starburst galaxy located south-west of UGC 5540 [arXiv:2305.16786]. The galaxy contains neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:2305.16786] and may exert a gravitational influence on the nearby dwarf galaxy W1016+3754 [arXiv:2305.16786].

sources.summary: The paper reports LEDA 2108569 as a blue compact starburst galaxy near UGC 5540 with detected H I and possible gravitational influence on W1016+3754.

quotes.quote: In addition to this, we detected another blue compact starburst galaxy, LEDA 2108569 2015ApJS..217...27A , at an angular distance of 6.9' towards the south-west from UGC 5540 and redshifted by 100 km s ^ 1 . reported the presence of a H i cloud towards the east of W1016+3754 in the low resolution ( 1 ) image. However, we didn't find evidence of such a cloud from deeper GMRT observations in H i map of similar resolution (see the H i moment map in Fig. fig1 and channel map in Fig. fig10 ). Global H i profiles of the BCDG W1016+37, UGC 5540 and LEDA 2108569, extracted from the H i cube of the same resolution as these images, are shown in Fig. fig2 .
UGC 5540 and LEDA 2108569 have M_ HI values 10 ^ 8.5 M _ and 10 ^ 7.6 M _ respectively.

Since the projected distance ( 38.4 kpc) of W1016+3754 from UGC 5540 is close enough (so as the star-burst dwarf irregular LEDA 2108569) to cause gravitational perturbation 2008gady.book.....B, 2015ApJ...802...82F , this infall of gas in W1016+3754 might be triggered by UGC 5540.

gmw_00007799.name: NGC 2906
gmw_00007799.overview: NGC 2906 is a late-type spiral galaxy characterized by a low bulge-to-total light ratio [arXiv:2004.08182]. The galaxy hosts numerous H II regions [arXiv:1511.01495] and exhibits a distinct two-peak distribution in its specific star formation rate [arXiv:1511.01495].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2906 as a late-type spiral example with low bulge-to-total light ratio, broad symmetric stellar angular-momentum distribution, and model failures tied to non-constant central rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 2906 is a late-type spiral galaxy with low bulge-to-total light ratio = 0.06 . Its is broad and symmetric, and peaks near = 1. NGC 2592 is an early-type galaxy with large = 0.54 ; its is strongly-skewed and peaks near = 0. In between these two extremes, the for NGC 7311 ( = 0.27 ) has characteristics of both disk and bulge. In Figure fig:pdfj_c we show derived using circular-only velocity, that is, calculated without the dispersion term. The circular for late-type NGC 2906 is symmetric, centred near = 1. For early-type NGC 2592 the circular peaks nearer = 0.5 and is positively skewed, while NGC 7311 has skewness intermediate between them.

It is clear that the halo curves do not at all describe late-type NGC 2906, intermediate NGC 7311, or even early type NGC 2592. In all cases there is less baryonic material compared with the DM halo at low ( 0 ), more at average ( 1 ), and less at high ( 3 ). The analytic solution for an exponential disk with constant rotation velocity is p(s)=4s e ^ 2s , plotted in Fig. fig:pdfj_o as orange dot-dashed lines. As expected, this model fails to describe the galaxies in our sample, even the almost-pure disks such as NGC 2906 which has =0.06 , since the rotation velocities are not constant but decrease towards the galaxy centres.

sources.summary: NGC 2906 contributed 241 HII regions, had higher SFR density than similarly star-forming NGC 6754 because of smaller HII regions, and showed the two-peak sSFR distribution behavior.

quotes.quote: After these cuts, the number of Hii regions that we are left with is: IC 1158 (292), NGC 6754 (423), ESO 362-18 (91), NGC 7742 (323), NGC 7469 (169), NGC 2906 (241).
Although NGC 6754 has a similar average SFR and sSFR than NGC 2906 and NGC 7742, we see that the two latter have significantly higher SFR due to their smaller physical size of the Hii region.
Interestingly, and with different strengths, all sSFR CDs show the same behavior of a two-peak distribution. In NGC 2906, NGC 7469 and NGC 7742 this is clearly seen.

gmw_00007800.name: Snape
gmw_00007800.overview: Snape is a face-on disk galaxy that lacks a central bar structure [arXiv:1909.01355]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus host and represents a possible non-detection for galactic outflows [arXiv:1909.01355].

sources.summary: Snape is treated as a possible outflow non-detection with calculated gas mass below 3.54 10^6 M_ and is the only face-on sample galaxy without a bar.

quotes.quote: There are also arguably 3 non-detections of outflows (Snape, Crabbe and Neville) which we discuss below.

In this instance, we can therefore assign this as an upper limit to those galaxies with detected outflows in the spectra which had calculated gas masses less than this value, including Snape and Crabbe, which we earlier identified as possible non-detections and which have calculated gas masses lower than 3.54 10^ 6 M _ .
For the face-on galaxies in the sample, all but one (Snape) host either a strong or a weak bar.

gmw_00007801.name: object A
gmw_00007801.overview: Object A is a low-mass, star-forming host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.376 [arXiv:1201.5393]. It has an estimated luminosity-weighted age of 1.28 Gyr and a subsolar oxygen abundance [arXiv:1201.5393].

sources.summary: The paper identifies object A/galaxy A as SN 2006oz's host and models its colors as a low-mass, star-forming galaxy at z=0.376 with a 1.28 Gyr luminosity-weighted age and subsolar oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: The SN position is spatially coincident with a candidate host galaxy (object A; Fig. fig:Host ), but within a radius of 7 \ there are four more galaxies, including one that appears extended (object E).
Because of its spatial proximity, we identify galaxy A as the host of SN 2006oz.

To gain a more quantitative understanding of the nature of the host galaxy, its observer-frame colors were compared to those predicted by a Monte Carlo library of model spectra, redshifted to z=0.376 , based on population synthesis models spanning a wide range of star formation histories, metallicities, and dust attenuation modeled as in 2000ApJ...539..718C . In this way, we constructed probability density functions and derived the following estimates for the host properties: a stellar mass of (M/ M_ ) = 8.7 0.23 , a luminosity-weighted age of 1.28^ +1.05 _ 0.61 Gyr, and a SFR of 0.17^ +0.18 _ 0.07 M_ yr ^ 1 . This results in a specific SFR of ( SFR /M_ ) = -9.47 0.39 yr ^ 1 , consistent with, but at the lower end of, the values reported by (although their uncertainties are high). With an absolute luminosity of M_g = -16.9 , this galaxy is probably metal-poor. By using the mass-metallicity relation of , we obtain an indicative value of 12+ ( O/H ) = 8.34 0.16 for the oxygen abundance that corresponds to 0.45 Z_ assuming the solar abundances of 2009ARA&A..47..481A .

gmw_00007802.name: SDSS J122836.32+153449.6
gmw_00007802.overview: SDSS J122836.32+153449.6 is a faint, extended galaxy that hosted the superluminous supernova SN 2008am [arXiv:1101.3581]. The galaxy has a sub-solar metallicity and an optical spectrum that closely matches an SB1 template [arXiv:1101.3581].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J122836.32+153449.6 as SN 2008am's host, giving its position, redshift, normal luminosity, SB1-like spectrum, and subsolar metallicity.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The position of the centroid of the host is = 12 ^ h 28 ^ m 36.3 ^ s and = +15 ^ d 34 ^ m 50 ^ s

quotes.quote: The host of SN 2008am is SDSS J122836.32+153449.6 that appears to be a faint, extended object in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey, with an ^ magnitude of 20 (see Figure 1). The position of the centroid of the host is = 12 ^ h 28 ^ m 36.3 ^ s and = +15 ^ d 34 ^ m 50 ^ s and its redshift is z = 0.2338 (Yuan et al. 2008a; 2.4).

On that basis, the host has an absolute magnitude M_ ^ -20 mag and thus is not a dwarf galaxy. The morphology of this galaxy is unknown, but the shape of its SDSS photometric SED and the optical spectrum showing narrow emission features of H, [ NII ] , [ OII ] and [ OIII ] agree well with an SB1 galaxy template spectrum (Figure 9; 2.4). The metallicity of the host, estimated from the flux ratios of these narrow emission features, is sub-solar ( Z 0.4 Z_ ), similar to the hosts of other SLSNe (Neill et al. 2010; Stoll et al. 2010; 2.4).

gmw_00007803.name: ID=10537
gmw_00007803.overview: ID=10537 is a BL Lacertae object whose specific blazar subclass is difficult to determine [arXiv:1512.05589]. It is detected in gamma rays by the Fermi Large Area Telescope [arXiv:1809.01173], but it lacks parsec-scale radio core emission and is classified as a lobe-dominated source [arXiv:1809.01173].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J1534 + 3715 as LAT-detected despite no VLBA radio emission, low NVSS flux density, low core power, anomalous radio/gamma luminosity placement, and LD classification.

quotes.quote: The only LAT-detected sources undetected at parsec scale are J0847 + 1133 and J1534 + 3715. However, the lack of detection of J0847 + 1133 is likely due to observational problems during VLBI observations reported in Liuzzo2013 . Nevertheless, a compact emission has been observed with the European VLBI Network (EVN) at 5 GHz mantovani15 and with VLBA at 8 GHz bourda10 for this source. On the other hand, no radio emission has been revealed at both 8 GHz and 15 GHz for J1534 + 3715 with VLBA. It is worth noticing that this source has the lowest NVSS flux densities at 1.4 GHz among the LAT-detected sources in our sample, suggesting that the radio emission is not concentrated in the core region but extended emission is present on scales which are not probed by VLBI arrays.

On the other hand, only one source with P_ core, 8 GHz < 3 10 ^ 23 W Hz ^ 1 , J1534 + 3715, has been detected by LAT. This indicates that LAT-detected sources are the most luminous radio sources of our sample on parsec scale.

All the sources that lie in that part of the luminosity-luminosity plot are not yet detected by Fermi LAT, except for J1534 + 3715. On the basis of the radio properties (i.e. source compactness, Core Dominance, CD CD is defined as ratio between the observed core radio power and the estimated unbeamed total radio power at low frequency , source morphology and radio spectrum) in Liuzzo2013 , all the sources have been classified in three groups: Doppler-dominated (DD, 23 cases), lobe-dominated (LD, 11 cases), and undetermined type (U, 8 cases) sources. In DD sources, the luminosity of the core is much larger than the luminosity of the extended component, implying relativistic jets and small viewing angles; LD sources have less prominent or undetected cores, and significant extended emission; U sources are undetected with VLBI but their overall flux density is so low that it does not provide stringent limits on the ratio between nuclear and extended emission. Among the 23 LAT-detected sources, 20 are classified as DD, and 3 as LD (J0847 + 1133, J1341 + 3959, and J1534 + 3715).

sources.summary: The paper says ID=10537 is a BL Lac whose flavor is difficult to assess because SED fits fail and optical, X-ray, and gamma-ray indicators conflict.

quotes.quote: ID=10537 The flavor of this BL Lac is difficult to assess. Both the four-point and three-point SED fits fail to give a satisfactory reproduction of the data. The high optical flux suggests an HBL classification, which is supported by a possible soft X-ray spectrum, but contradicted by the mildly soft ray spectrum.

gmw_00007804.overview: The active galactic nucleus 0945+408 is classified as a blazar [arXiv:astro-ph/0401501] that features a transverse rotation measure gradient across its core [arXiv:1709.09062]. Radio monitoring shows potential, though ill-defined, variability periods in its light curves [arXiv:astro-ph/0401501].

sources.summary: The paper finds 0945+408 has a transverse RM gradient across the core, after image alignment, with circular-beam significances of about 4 to 5 sigma.

quotes.quote: 0945+408. The RM maps for this source are shown in Fig. fig:rmmaps-BG173-1 (second row). Although the shift between the intensity images at the different frequencies due to the change in the position of the VLBI core with frequency was essentially negligible, there was a large shift between the maps at 4.6 and 5.0 GHz and the maps at the higher frequencies, due to the fact that a bright knot in the inner jet was the brightest feature at 4.6 and 5.0 GHz, while the core was the brightest feature at the higher frequencies. The polarization angle images at 4.6 and 5.0 GHz were shifted to correct for this large misalignment before the RM maps were made. The RM map made using the elliptical convolving beam (left panel) shows a transverse RM gradient, which has a significance of about 3.5 . The RM map made using an equal-area circular beam (middle panel) shows a clear region of transverse RM gradients across the core, with significances of 4-5 (right panel).

sources.summary: The paper finds 0945+408 periodic in the Mets ahovi 22 and 37 GHz data, but UMRAO observations do not confirm it; only a possible ill-defined 2690-day 14.5 GHz period remains.

quotes.quote: We have carried out a multifrequency analysis of the radio variability of blazars, exploiting the data obtained during the extensive monitoring programs carried out at the University of Michigan Radio Astronomy Observatory (UMRAO, at 4.8, 8, and 14.5 GHz) and at the Mets ahovi Radio Observatory (22 and 37 GHz). Two different techniques detect, in the Mets ahovi light curves, evidences of periodicity at both frequencies for 5 sources ( 0224+671 , 0945+408 , 1226+023 , 2200+420 , and 2251+158 ).

On the other hand, the 22 and 37 GHz periodicities of 0224+671 and 0945+408 (which were less extensively monitored at Mets ahovi and for which we get a significant false-alarm probability) are not confirmed by the UMRAO database, where some indications of ill-defined periods about a factor of two longer are retrieved.

For 0945+408 (Fig. A094+540 ) no periodicity was found at 4.8 and 8.0 GHz, while an ill-defined period of 2690 days (STIMA), about a factor of 2 larger than found at the Mets ahovi frequencies, could be present in the 14.5 GHz signal.

gmw_00007805.overview: 0212+735 is a radio-loud blazar located at a redshift greater than 2 [arXiv:0707.2925]. The active galactic nucleus features a monotonic transverse rotation measure gradient across its core and inner jet region [arXiv:1709.09062].

sources.summary: The paper treats 0212+735 as one of four z>2 radio-loud blazars detected by Swift/BAT, with soft-medium X-ray emission and hard XRT/BAT spectra.

quotes.quote: We report on \ observations of four z>2 radio-loud quasars (0212+735, 0537--286, 0836+710, and 2149--307), classified as blazars. The sources, well-known emitters at soft-medium X-rays, were detected at >5 with the BAT experiment in 15--150 keV. No flux variability was detected within the XRT and BAT exposures, with the exception of 0836+710 which shows an increase of a factor 4 of the 15--150 keV flux on a timescale of one month. The 0.3--10 keV spectra are well fitted by power law models, with rather hard continua (photon indices _ XRT 1.3-1.5 ); similarly, the 15--150 keV spectra are described by power laws with _ BAT 1.3-1.8 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0212+735 as an AGN with core/inner-jet transverse RM gradients, clear in both beam treatments and significant at about 7 to 8 sigma.

quotes.quote: 0212+735. The RM maps for this source are shown in Fig. fig:rmmaps-ZT (top row). Both the RM maps made using the elliptical (left panel) and equal-area circular (middle panel) beams show a very clear, monotonic transverse RM gradient across the core and inner jet region, with significances of 7-8 .
Monotonic transverse RM gradients with significances of 3 or more are observed across the core regions of 0212+735, 0300+470, 0945+408, 1502+106, and 1611+343.

gmw_00007806.overview: The active galactic nucleus 1611+343 is a radio-active source [arXiv:1709.09062]. Rotation measure maps of its core region reveal a clear, monotonic transverse gradient [arXiv:1709.09062].

sources.summary: The paper finds 1611+343 has a clear monotonic transverse RM gradient across its core, becoming significant at 3.3 sigma when mapped with a circular beam.

quotes.quote: 1611+343. The RM maps for this source are shown in Fig. fig:rmmaps-ZT (third row). Both the RM maps made using the elliptical (left panel) and equal-area circular (middle panel) beams show a clear, monotonic transverse RM gradient across the core region. The significance of this gradient is 2.5 in the elliptical-beam RM map, but increases to 3.3 in the equal-area circular-beam RM map.

gmw_00007807.overview: The low-latitude quasar 2005+403 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.736 [arXiv:2308.15274, arXiv:1804.03703]. It features parsec-scale superluminal kinematics with an inner radio jet that exhibits a possible core transverse rotation measure gradient [arXiv:2308.15274, arXiv:1709.09062].

sources.summary: The paper presents 2005 + 403 as a low-latitude quasar with strong Cygnus-line-of-sight scattering, parsec-scale superluminal kinematics, and rare AGN multiple imaging.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, this manual search for refractive-dominated scattered sources over a fairly large sample of AGNs yielded only a few examples, including the quasar 2005 + 403. In this paper we focus on investigating this specific object, while the results for other identified sources will be presented elsewhere. The line of sight towards the quasar 2005 + 403 passes through the Cygnus region, which is known for its prominent interstellar medium influence.

Along the line of sight towards 2005 + 403, a derived k value of 1.71 was obtained Koryukova2022 , indicating significant scattering of the source. The extragalactic radio source TXS 2005 + 403 (J2007 + 4029) is a low-spectral peaked quasar at redshift z=1.736 Boksenberg76 that corresponds to a scale-factor 8.55 pc mas ^ 1 . The analysis of parsec-scale kinematics shows superluminal motion with maximum apparent speed 9.76c Lister19 . The source Doppler-factor inferred from brightness temperature analysis is quite low, =3.1 Homan21 , which is likely the reason of not been detected at high energies Lister15 . The quasar is at low galactic latitude b=4 3 towards the Cygnus region ( l=76 8 ).

sources.summary: The paper says 2005+403 shows a possible core transverse RM gradient that becomes much clearer in the circular-beam map, with significance around 8 to 9 sigma.

quotes.quote: 2005+403. The RM maps for this source are shown in Fig. fig:rmmaps-ZT (fourth row). Bearing in mind that the direction of the inner jet is slightly south of east (as can be seen, for example, in the 15-GHz maps of Zavala Taylor (2003) and Lister Homan 2005), the RM map made with the elliptical convolving beam shows a possible transverse gradient in the vicnity of the core with a significance of about 5 . The RM map made using an equal-area circular beam shows this gradient and its direction relative to the jet much more clearly; its significance in the circular-beam RM map is 8-9 . The reduced clarify of this gradient in the elliptical-beam RM map is due to the fact that, although the elliptical beam is not extremely elongated, its orientation is close to orthogonal to the jet direction.

sources.summary: The paper says 5BZQJ 2007+4029 is a high-redshift object near the Galactic plane with only a very low-significance gamma-ray signature and no clear point-source detection.

quotes.quote: 5BZQJ 2007+4029. This object is close to the Galactic plane, at latitude b = 4.30 ^ , with relatively high redshift, i.e., z = 1.736. A likelihood analysis integrating over 7.5 years, considering the full energy band 0.1-500 GeV shows a very low-significance \ signature, where TS 5.2, = 2.61 0.26, and N _ 0 = 2.4 1.2 10 ^ 13 ph/cm ^2 /s/MeV. However, from the TS map (300 MeV-10 GeV) there is no clear evidence for a point source, therefore we do not consider this as detection.

gmw_00007808.name: HZ5
gmw_00007808.overview: HZ5, also identified as DEIMOS COSMOS 845652, is a high-redshift galaxy classified as an active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:1806.07423, arXiv:2207.07137]. It has been characterized as a weak, X-ray selected Type 1 quasar [arXiv:1806.07423] with a rest-frame 2–10 keV luminosity of 10^44.4 erg/s [arXiv:2207.07137].

sources.summary: The paper treats HZ5 as a high-redshift zombie galaxy that is actually a weak Type 1, X-ray selected quasar and classifies it as an AGN host.

quotes.quote: For instance, the zombie galaxy HZ5, though satisfying the object selection performed by Capak2015 , is actually a weak Type 1 quasar Brightman2014,Kalfountzou2014 .

Only HZ5 and HZ8W can be classified as AGN hosts at a 90 confidence level, with HZ5 already recognized as an X-ray selected quasar Capak2015 , whereas HZ1 to HZ3 and HZ10 are likely to be normal galaxies.
Of the objects listed in Table table:data , HZ10 and HZ10W fall outside the area of 0.9 deg ^2 covered by the C COSMOS program, and the weak quasar HZ5 is detected as a resolved source.

sources.summary: The paper identifies DEIMOS COSMOS 845652 as an ALPINE galaxy with X-ray AGN activity, giving its rest-frame 2-10 keV luminosity and noting it is not Type-1.

quotes.quote: One ALPINE galaxy (``DEIMOS COSMOS 845652'') was found to be an AGN detected with L_ 2-10 keV = 10^ 44.4 erg s ^ 1 , using flux measured in the 0.5–2 keV band (corresponding to rest-frame 2–10 keV band at z 5) and assuming a = 1.4, following eq. \ 4 in Marchesi2016c . Such luminosity places it in the AGN regime, with L_ 2-10 keV = 10^ 42 erg \ s ^ 1 being the typical threshold to separate AGN and SFGs Marchesi2016b . Nevertheless, none of our results would be affected by this galaxy, since all of our results are based on median stacking which eliminate the possible effect of single galaxy.

gmw_00007809.name: HZ8W
gmw_00007809.overview: HZ8W is a high-redshift galaxy classified as an active galactic nucleus host candidate [arXiv:1806.07423]. It is among the far-infrared undetected sources whose spectral properties suggest active galactic nucleus domination [arXiv:1806.07423].

sources.summary: The paper classifies HZ8W as an AGN host candidate and includes it among FIR-undetected sources whose spectral offsets suggest AGN domination.

quotes.quote: Only HZ5 and HZ8W can be classified as AGN hosts at a 90 confidence level, with HZ5 already recognized as an X-ray selected quasar Capak2015 , whereas HZ1 to HZ3 and HZ10 are likely to be normal galaxies.

We only stack sources with an upper limit in L_ FIR , i.e., HZ1, HZ2, HZ3, HZ5a, HZ7, HZ8, and HZ8W, because the objects with FIR detection could host heavily obscured AGN in which the X-ray emission is almost totally suppressed by the presence of high column densities of gas and IR-emitting dust e.g., Ricci2017b . In addition, such objects are the most distant from the traditional IRX-to- relations adopted for galaxies, further suggesting that their spectral properties can only be explained by AGN domination.

gmw_00007810.overview: The galaxy 1-217022, also known as Akira, is the prototype of the "Red Geyser" class of galaxies [arXiv:1903.08032, arXiv:2103.14928]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus that powers a large-scale bipolar gas outflow and exhibits LINER-like emission line ratios [arXiv:1903.08032, arXiv:2103.14928].

sources.summary: The paper studies Akira as the prototype Red Geyser, finding LLAGN-powered gas emission and a precessing outflow whose orientation changes by about 50 degrees from nucleus to kiloparsec scales.

quotes.quote: In order to better constrain the gas kinematics, probing scales 3 times smaller than those probed by MaNGA, we obtained IFS with Gemini GMOS of the inner 1.7 kpc 2.5 kpc of mangaid 1-217022 -- dubbed as Akira -- to complement the information from MaNGA IFU data. Akira is the prototype of the Red Geyser class, harbors a LLAGN and presents a well defined large scale bipolar outflow cheung16 . It has a redshift z =0.0245, for which 1 ^ corresponds to 500 pc at the distance of the galaxy, adopting a Hubble constant of H_0 =71 Mpc ^ 1 .

Both GMOS and MaNGA BPT diagrams for Akira (Fig. bpt ) show that the emission line ratios at most locations of the galaxy fall in the region occupied by LINERs and emission-line galaxies whose ionizing photons are produced in the atmospheres of evolved low-mass stars stasinska08,cid11,singh13,belfiore16 . Since the BPT diagram is not efficient in discriminating between these two excitation agents, the WHAN diagram has been used; it shows that most of the gas emission in Akira is photoionized by a LLAGN. Integrating the fluxes within an aperture of 2 ^ diameter centered at the nucleus, we find that in all diagrams the nucleus of Akira is classified as an AGN.

We used IFS obtained with the Gemini GMOS-IFU and SDSS-IV--MaNGA to investigate the gas kinematics of the galaxy Akira -- the prototypical ``Red Geyser''. We found that the outflow can be resolved with GMOS-IFU down to the nucleus and changes orientation by about 50^ from the nucleus of the galaxy to kiloparsec scales on a timescale of 10^7 years, being spatially correlated with the complex structure of high EW emission-line values.

sources.summary: The paper presents 1-217022 as the prototypical red geyser Akira, with extended H emission, bi-symmetric equivalent width, LINER-like ratios, and radio-AGN evidence for outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: The first galaxy (ID: 1-217022) is the prototypical red geyser cheung16 which shows all the characteristics mentioned above and was among the first ones discovered in MaNGA. Its relevant characteristics are highlighted in Fig. fig:prototype . The top leftmost panel in the figure shows the H flux distribution, which is extended in nature and shows a high value compared to other passive quenched galaxies, surpassing 5 10^ 16 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 . The galaxy has a clear bi-symmetric pattern in the H EW map with an average EW 4.5 (bottom left panel). The top and bottom middle panels show the stellar and gas velocity maps respectively.

The spatially resolved BPT diagrams (rightmost panels) show LINER-like line ratios through out the galaxy. This galaxy has a central radio source detected in FIRST and Jansky Very Large Array (JVLA) 1.4 GHz continuum emission cheung16 , which indicates a low-luminosity radio AGN with low Eddington ratio ( 10^ 4 ).

gmw_00007811.overview: The galaxy 1-145922 is classified as a red geyser that exhibits a bi-symmetric emission pattern [arXiv:2103.14928]. It displays LINER-like line ratios throughout most of its structure, while AGN or Seyfert-like line ratios are concentrated in its center [arXiv:2103.14928].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1-145922 as a red geyser with bi-symmetric emission, LINER/AGN-like line ratios, unclear stellar rotation, no FIRST detection, and asymmetric slit spectra.

quotes.quote: The second red geyser target (ID: 1-145922) also shows a clear bi-symmetric EW pattern (Fig. fig:prototype2 ), but with a much lower H EW value 1 - 2.5 (bottom left panel). LINER-like line ratios are observed through most of the galaxy while AGN/Seyfert line ratios appear in the center (right panels), with log( [ NII ] /H ) > 0.4. Unlike the first target, this galaxy doesn't show systematic rotation in the stellar kinematics (upper middle panel) and hence the exact misalignment angle between the gas and stellar velocity field is unclear. However, the estimated average second velocity moment ( V_ rms V^2 + ^2 ) of the ionized gas and stars are similar to the first target. This galaxy is not radio detected in the FIRST survey and has not been followed up with JVLA.

The second target (MaNGAID: 1-145922) includes only one slit observation along its bi-symmetric feature. The slits that align with the bi-conical axis for both galaxies show strong asymmetry in the emission lines.

gmw_00007812.name: COS-2987030247
gmw_00007812.overview: COS-2987030247 is a star-forming Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of z = 6.8076 [arXiv:2206.13770, arXiv:1706.04614]. The system features a main central source with similar ultraviolet and [C II] spatial distributions, alongside candidate companion galaxies located approximately 10 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:2206.13770].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes COS-2987030247 as a z=6.8076 star-forming galaxy whose ALMA data show matched UV/[CII] structure, ordered rotation-like kinematics, and possible accreting companions.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyzed the spatial distribution and kinematics of the cold ionized gas of the normal star-forming galaxy COS-2987030247 at z=6.8076 , based on new high-resolution observations of the 158 m line emission obtained with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA). These observations allowed us to compare the spatial distribution and extension of the and rest-frame UV emission, model the line data-cube using , and measure the luminosity and star formation rate (SFR) surface densities in the galaxy subregions. The system is found to be composed of a main central source, a fainter north extension, and candidate companions located 10-kpc away. We find similar rest-frame UV and spatial distributions, suggesting that the emission emerges from the star-forming regions. The agreement between the UV and surface brightness radial profiles rules out diffuse, extended emission (often called a halo) in the main galaxy component.

In this paper, we report sensitive ALMA high-resolution line observations of the typical star-forming galaxy COS-2987030247 (hereafter as COS2987) at z=6.8076 . This system was a z 7 Lyman-break galaxy candidate with a high [ OIII ] +H equivalent-width (EW) obtained from optical to infrared spectral energy distribution (SED) modelling 2015smit, 2017laporte , and later confirmed to be a gas-rich star-forming galaxy based on the detection of its bright line with ALMA 2018smit . Dynamical analysis of the line velocity field in low-resolution observations ( 1.1'' x 0.7'' , translated to 5.8×3.7 kpc at z = 6.8) suggested that disk rotation could be in place 2018smit .

sources.summary: The paper identifies COS-2987030247 as a z~6.81 Lyman-break galaxy with a significant [C II] line, luminous UV star formation, extended [C II] emission, and a measured velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: Particularly important, this opens up opportunities for high angular-resolution [ C 2 ] dynamics in galaxies less than one billion years after the Big Bang. h h We have obtained spectroscopy with the Atacama Large Millimetre Array (ALMA) at 241--245 GHz of two Lyman-break galaxies (LBGs) COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247 at an estimated photometric redshift just below 7, roughly 800 million years after the Big Bang. These two sources are luminous in the rest-frame ultraviolet (UV; L_ UV 2 L^ ), but are still representative of `normal' star-forming galaxies at z 7 with a UV star-formation rate (SFR) of 19-23 M_ yr^ 1 .

We detect a line in each of the targets at 241.97 0.01 GHz and 243.42 0.01 GHz , for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247 respectively, at >5 significance in both the one-dimensional spectra and the spectral line-averaged maps (Fig. fig:COS3 ). We derive spectroscopic redshifts of z_ CII =6.8540 0.0003 and z_ CII =6.8076 0.0002 , in excellent agreement with the photometric redshift estimates of 6.76 0.07 and 6.66 0.14 for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247, and line-widths of 232 30 and 124 18 km s^ 1 respectively.

We determine the [ C 2 ] half-light radii (deconvolved from the beamsize) that extend 2.6 0.8 and 3.1 1.0 kpc for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247, nearly twice the size of the UV in the brightest LBGs at this redshift . We use the spatial extent of the [ C 2 ] detection to investigate the velocity structure of these sources, which reveals a projected velocity difference over the galaxy of 111 28 and 54 20 km s^ 1 for COS-3018555981 and COS-2987030247 respectively (Fig. fig:kin ), similar to the velocity gradients observed in two galaxies recently studied at z 5-6 .

gmw_00007813.overview: 1548+056 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:1001.0731]. Although previously categorized as a core-only source, more sensitive radio observations have revealed faint, halo-like emission surrounding it [arXiv:1001.0731].

sources.summary: The MOJAVE paper highlights 1548+056 as a formerly core-only quasar where deeper radio data revealed faint halo-like emission, implying similar sources may need more sensitive observations.

quotes.quote: We expect more sensitive radio observations, however, to detect faint emission in these sources, as we have detected in the erstwhile ``core-only'' source, 1548+056.

While some sources, like 1749+096, failed to reveal any extended emission in a number of different datasets see also Rector01 , we did detect faint halo-like emission around 1548+056, which was listed as a core-only source by Murphy93 .
We expect more sensitive radio observations to detect faint emission in these sources, as we have detected in the case of 1548+056, previously classified as a core-only quasar.

gmw_00007814.name: G17
gmw_00007814.overview: G17 is a Type-1 active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits broad components in its optical and near-infrared spectra [arXiv:1212.3008]. It is exceptionally bright at 24 microns, with diagram positions indicating a strong contribution from star-heated dust [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G17 is described as a Type-1 galaxy with broad components, exceptional 24 micron brightness, and diagram positions implying strong star-heated dust.

quotes.quote: For seven of the galaxies, namely G17, G55, G60, G63, G78, G90, and G107, an additional broad Gaussian component was needed to reproduce the observed line profiles of the permitted lines.
The three galaxies fitted with Type-1 templates (G17, G63, and G78; see Figure seds ) show broad components in their optical and/or NIR spectra.

The position of the galaxies G17 and G90 in Figure alonso is remarkable. They are the brightest 24 sources in the whole sample studied in Barmby06 13.72 0.01 and 14.51 0.01 mag at 24 for G17 and G90 respectively. and despite their strong H and H broad components, they lie well below the AGN area of the diagram, indicating a strong contribution from star-heated dust at 24 .

gmw_00007815.name: G36
gmw_00007815.overview: G36 is an early-type galaxy with a morphology resembling an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1212.3008]. It features a strong dust lane crossing its nucleus, and high dust attenuation may affect its hydrogen-alpha emission [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G36 is described as an NG-classified early-type galaxy with an elliptical morphology, a strong dust lane, large J-band photometry-spectroscopy differences, and possibly attenuated H emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, the morphology of G36 resembles that of an elliptical galaxy with a strong dust lane crossing the nucleus.

The largest differences between the J-band photometry and spectroscopy are found for the galaxies G36, G43, and G47. The three of them are NG according to our SED fits, which are in agreement with their early-type morphologies, at least for G36 and G43 (those with ACS images available; see Figure acs ).

G27 and G36). In the case of G36, high attenuation could be affecting its H emission, whereas in the case of G27 (A _V 0.23 mag) its H flux could be compromised by the line position, close to the edge of the detector (see Figure groth5 ).

gmw_00007816.name: G43
gmw_00007816.overview: G43 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1212.3008] that exhibits low X-ray luminosity [arXiv:1212.3008]. It displays Balmer absorption lines, including H-beta and H-gamma in absorption, which suggests that any active galactic nucleus is either deeply buried or entirely absent [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G43 is described as an NG early-type galaxy with low X-ray luminosity and Balmer absorption, implying a deeply buried or possibly absent AGN.

quotes.quote: The largest differences between the J-band photometry and spectroscopy are found for the galaxies G36, G43, and G47. The three of them are NG according to our SED fits, which are in agreement with their early-type morphologies, at least for G36 and G43 (those with ACS images available; see Figure acs ).

Only 8/28 sources have L _X<L_X^ AGN , namely G43, G47, G55, G59, G62, G76, G105 and G107. Thus, for these galaxies, either their X-ray and MIR emission are due to intense star formation, or to a heavily obscured AGN.

For G43 and G76 we detect H in absorption (as well as H in the case of G43), which is characteristic of early-type galaxies. This agrees with our SED classification as NG (see Table comparison_broad ) and shows how deeply buried --or possibly absent-- these AGN are.

gmw_00007817.name: G47
gmw_00007817.overview: G47 is classified as a normal galaxy based on spectral energy distribution fitting [arXiv:1212.3008]. It exhibits a very faint or practically featureless spectrum, and its low X-ray luminosity suggests either intense star formation or a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G47 is treated as an NG object with large J-band discrepancies, low X-ray luminosity, and very faint or featureless spectra, allowing star formation or heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: The largest differences between the J-band photometry and spectroscopy are found for the galaxies G36, G43, and G47. The three of them are NG according to our SED fits, which are in agreement with their early-type morphologies, at least for G36 and G43 (those with ACS images available; see Figure acs ).

Only 8/28 sources have L _X<L_X^ AGN , namely G43, G47, G55, G59, G62, G76, G105 and G107. Thus, for these galaxies, either their X-ray and MIR emission are due to intense star formation, or to a heavily obscured AGN.

Of the other galaxies classified in this group, some of them show narrow permitted lines (G25, G36, G59, and G74) and the rest have either very faint emission lines or practically featureless spectra (G47, G73, and G99).

gmw_00007818.name: G55
gmw_00007818.overview: The galaxy G55 exhibits a broad Gaussian component in its permitted emission line profiles [arXiv:1212.3008]. This broad emission suggests the galaxy is either a normal galaxy experiencing intense star formation or a heavily obscured Type-1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G55 needed a broad-line component but was not SED-classified as Type-1, leaving it ambiguous between intense star formation and a heavily obscured Type-1 AGN.

quotes.quote: For seven of the galaxies, namely G17, G55, G60, G63, G78, G90, and G107, an additional broad Gaussian component was needed to reproduce the observed line profiles of the permitted lines.

On the other hand, there are another three galaxies with broad lines detected in their spectra (G55, G60, and G107) which were not classified as Type-1 AGN from their SED fits. Note, however, that those broad components have FWHMs 1500 km s ^ 1 , and the luminosities of their broad H components are considerably lower than those of the galaxies G63, G78, and G90, as we show in Table comparison_broad . In fact, broad H emission is also detected in starburst galaxies (up to 2200 km s ^ 1 in the case of stellar winds or supernovae remnants; see e.g. Boyle95,Homeier99,Westmoquette11 ). Thus, G55 and G107 could be either normal galaxies with intense star formation, or heavily obscured Type-1 AGN.

Summarising, in view of all the discussed diagnostic diagrams, X-ray luminosities and SED classification, we cannot confirm the presence of nuclear activity in 4/28 sources (14 of the sample): G47, G55, G62 and G107.

gmw_00007819.name: G59
gmw_00007819.overview: The galaxy G59 is classified as a spiral galaxy based on its spectral energy distribution template [arXiv:1212.3008]. Although its classification remains somewhat unclear, diagnostic diagrams and the presence of an active nucleus suggest it is likely a heavily obscured Type-2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G59 has a spiral-template SED and low X-ray luminosity but falls in the AGN region of the BPT diagram, making it likely a heavily obscured Type-2 AGN.

quotes.quote: The position of the sources G53 and G59 in the BPT diagram is noticeable. We classified G53 as a Type-2 , and its L _X>L_X^ AGN , but it is well below the H II boundary in the more restrictive Kewley01 definition. This source is likely a weak AGN with intense star formation. On the other hand, G59, whose SED was fitted with a spiral template in Ramos09 and has L _X<L_X^ AGN , appears in the AGN region of the diagram, very close to the quasar G63. Thus, the galaxy G59 is likely a heavily obscured Type-2 AGN.

It is also worth mentioning the position of the galaxies G53 and G59 in Figure alonso . Whereas G59 lies in the AGN region of the BPT diagram shown in Figure bpt , in Figure alonso appears considerably shifted from the local AGN region. Considering its low hydrogen column density (N _H<0.58 10^ 22 cm^ 2 ; Georgakakis06 ), the classification of this galaxy is unclear, althougth the BPT diagram confirms the presence of an active nucleus.

gmw_00007820.name: G74
gmw_00007820.overview: G74 is a spiral galaxy that is fitted with a spiral template [arXiv:1212.3008]. It is classified as an NG galaxy, displaying narrow permitted lines and a composite diagnostic position [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G74 is described as a spiral-template, NG-classified galaxy with narrow permitted lines and a composite diagnostic position that confirms its SED classification.

quotes.quote: Examples of spiral galaxies are G74 and G107, which were fitted with spiral templates in Ramos09 .

Of the other galaxies classified in this group, some of them show narrow permitted lines (G25, G36, G59, and G74) and the rest have either very faint emission lines or practically featureless spectra (G47, G73, and G99).
Similar positions, also in the composite region, are occupied by the two SB-dom G26 and G62 and the NG s G25 and G74, confirming their SED classifications.

gmw_00007821.name: G78
gmw_00007821.overview: G78 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1212.3008]. It is classified as a Type-1 galaxy that is securely dominated by its active galactic nucleus, exhibiting broad optical or near-infrared spectral components [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G78 is treated as a Type-1 galaxy with broad optical or NIR components and included among the paper's securely AGN-dominated sources.

quotes.quote: For seven of the galaxies, namely G17, G55, G60, G63, G78, G90, and G107, an additional broad Gaussian component was needed to reproduce the observed line profiles of the permitted lines.

The three galaxies fitted with Type-1 templates (G17, G63, and G78; see Figure seds ) show broad components in their optical and/or NIR spectra. In fact, these are the only three galaxies with broad lines of FWHM > 3000 km s ^ 1 .

Thus, we can derive an empirical relationship between L _ H and L _X using the four galaxies that we can definitely classify as AGN-dominated from their spectra, SED fits, and diagnostic diagrams presented in this work: G60, G63, G78, and G93.

gmw_00007822.name: G93
gmw_00007822.overview: G93 is a Type-2 narrow-line galaxy [arXiv:1212.3008]. Its spectral emission line ratios place it in the active galactic nucleus region, specifically within the Seyfert/LINER transition zone [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G93 is a Type-2 narrow-line galaxy lying in the AGN region near the Seyfert/LINER transition and used among the pure AGN calibrators.

quotes.quote: Finally, the galaxies fitted with Type-2 templates show narrow emission lines (G53, G56, G57, G93, G105, G106, and G110), as well as the SB-cont G27, whose SED was fitted with a Seyfert 2/starburst template in Ramos09 .
The Type-2 s G60 and G93 also lie in the AGN region of the diagram, but with ratios more typical of the Seyfert/LINER transition zone.

Thus, we can derive an empirical relationship between L _ H and L _X using the four galaxies that we can definitely classify as AGN-dominated from their spectra, SED fits, and diagnostic diagrams presented in this work: G60, G63, G78, and G93.

gmw_00007823.name: O3ELG1
gmw_00007823.overview: O3ELG1 is a faint dwarf galaxy with a compact morphology [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is characterized by a low stellar mass, low metallicity, and a high star formation rate for its mass [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG1 is presented as a typical HETDEX fall-field object with low metallicity and mass, high SFR for its mass, faintness, and compact morphology.

quotes.quote: O3ELG1 This object lies in the HETDEX fall field and represents a typical object for our sample. It has fairly low metallicity and mass, agreeing with the ber12 trend for typical low-mass galaxies. Like most objects in our sample it has a high SFR for its mass, similar to the blueberry galaxies. O3ELG1 is quite faint and has a compact morphology.

gmw_00007824.name: O3ELG2
gmw_00007824.overview: O3ELG2 is a low-mass dwarf galaxy with a blue, compact morphology [arXiv:2105.02892]. Although it has a typical metallicity for field galaxies of its mass, it exhibits a high star formation rate and a rich emission-line spectrum [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG2 is low mass but otherwise field-galaxy-like in metallicity and mass, with blueberry-like SFR, blue compact morphology, rich emission lines, and high EW.

quotes.quote: O3ELG2 O3ELG2 is on the lower mass end for objects in the sample; however, it appears to be fairly typical of field galaxies in terms of its metallicity and mass. This situation is not the case for its SFR, as it falls in a similar regime as the blueberry galaxies. It appears to have an incredibly blue and compact morphology. This object, despite being fairly faint, has a rich emission-line spectrum that includes the 4363 line. This object also has the second highest EW in our sample overlapping with the lower end of the EW distribution of blueberry galaxies.

gmw_00007825.name: O3ELG3
gmw_00007825.overview: O3ELG3 is a dwarf galaxy with a blue, compact morphology [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is characterized by a very low stellar mass, low metallicity, and a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG3 is one of the faintest, lowest-metallicity, lowest-mass objects, with high specific star formation and blue compact morphology.

quotes.quote: O3ELG3 O3ELG3 is one of our faintest objects and has the second highest O3O2 ratio from its VIRUS spectrum. This object is our second lowest metallicity object and also one of the lowest mass objects. Like most of our objects, ours falls in the same regime as the typical low-mass galaxies in terms of its mass and metallicity. However, it has a high specific star formation rate similar to blueberry galaxies. It appears very blue and has a compact morphology.

gmw_00007826.name: O3ELG4a
gmw_00007826.overview: O3ELG4a is a galaxy that forms part of a two-galaxy structure at the same redshift [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is the highest-mass galaxy in its sample, exhibiting low metallicity for its mass and a star formation rate slightly higher than typical SDSS field galaxies [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG4a is part of a two-galaxy same-redshift structure, centered on the HETDEX detection, and is the highest-mass sample galaxy with low metallicity for its mass.

quotes.quote: O3ELG4a+b This object is one of the most interesting objects photometrically. The imaging shows two long, extended structures with a redder, compact object in between. The LRS2 follow-up confirmed that the two extended structures are galaxies at the same redshift, while the red compact object is likely a foreground star. The HETDEX detection found from our candidate selection was centered on O3ELG4a, however, since LRS2 follow up revealed O3ELG4b to be at the same redshift we determine properties of both out of interest.

O3ELG4a is the highest mass galaxy included in our sample. It has low metallicity for its mass compared to typical field galaxies where its SFR is slightly higher than typical SDSS field galaxies however much more typical compared to our sample.

gmw_00007827.name: O3ELG4b
gmw_00007827.overview: O3ELG4b is a dwarf companion galaxy to O3ELG4a, and the two systems share the same redshift [arXiv:2105.02892]. Compared to its companion, O3ELG4b is a more massive, typical field galaxy in terms of its mass-metallicity and mass-star formation rate relations [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG4b is a same-redshift companion to O3ELG4a, excluded from the final sample but measured as higher mass and more typical in mass-metallicity and mass-SFR.

quotes.quote: O3ELG4a+b This object is one of the most interesting objects photometrically. The imaging shows two long, extended structures with a redder, compact object in between. The LRS2 follow-up confirmed that the two extended structures are galaxies at the same redshift, while the red compact object is likely a foreground star. The HETDEX detection found from our candidate selection was centered on O3ELG4a, however, since LRS2 follow up revealed O3ELG4b to be at the same redshift we determine properties of both out of interest.

O3ELG4b is not included in our final sample count since it did not meet our selection criteria and its properties reveal it has higher mass and is a fairly typical field galaxy in terms of its mass-metallicty and mass-SFR. It is interesting how much these two galaxies differ in properties, since it is likely they live in the same dark matter halo.

gmw_00007828.name: O3ELG5
gmw_00007828.overview: O3ELG5 is a high-mass, high-metallicity emission line galaxy that is consistent with the mass-metallicity relation [arXiv:2105.02892]. It exhibits a slightly higher specific star formation rate than a typical field galaxy at its redshift [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG5 had multiple HETDEX detections and high initial O3O2 ratios, but optimal extraction gave lower ratios; it remains high mass/metallicity with elevated specific SFR.

quotes.quote: O3ELG5 This object was found with several independent detections in the HETDEX data; among these detections, it had one of the highest O3O2 ratios in sample. However, the line ratios were not as high when the spectrum of the object was optimally extracted. This is one of the highest metallicity and mass objects in our sample, but it still consistent with the mass-metallicity relation. However, it does have slightly higher specific star formation rate than a typical field galaxy at this redshift, falling at the lower end of the regime occupied by blueberry galaxies.

gmw_00007829.name: O3ELG6
gmw_00007829.overview: O3ELG6 is a very low-mass, faint dwarf galaxy with a compact morphology [arXiv:2105.02892]. Although it has one of the lowest star formation rates in its sample, this rate is much higher than that of typical field galaxies of similar mass [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG6 is a very low-mass, faint compact object found in multiple HETDEX detections, with moderate combined O3O2, typical metallicity, and high SFR for its mass.

quotes.quote: O3ELG6 O3ELG6 is one of the lowest mass objects in our sample, with photometry revealing a faint compact morphology. This is another object found with multiple detections in HETDEX, some with very high O3O2 ratios, but others with more moderate ratios, and optimal extraction yields a more moderate ratio in combination. This object has a typical metallicity for our sample, and its low mass is typical in comparison to the and13 and ber12 star forming samples. O3ELG6 has one of the lowest SFR for the sample, but for its low mass it is much higher than typical field galaxies.

gmw_00007830.name: O3ELG7
gmw_00007830.overview: O3ELG7 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0995 [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is characterized by a very low mass, low metallicity, and an extremely high specific star formation rate [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG7 is the sample's most blueberry-like object, with the highest O3O2 ratio and EW, very low metallicity and mass, high redshift, extreme sSFR, and blue compact morphology.

quotes.quote: O3ELG7 O3ELG7 is by far our highest O3O2 ratio and 5007 EW object. It is also the lowest metallicity source without a 4363 detection. O3ELG7 has one of the lowest masses and is also our highest redshift object at z=0.0995 . This object has a mass and metallicity consistent with the ber12 low-mass field galaxies. For its mass, this object is one of the most extreme in terms of its specific star formation rate.
It clearly falls in a similar regime occupied by blueberry galaxies in terms of these two properties. It appears blue and compact in its morphology.

gmw_00007831.name: O3ELG8
gmw_00007831.overview: O3ELG8 is a galaxy with a fairly compact morphology that lies near the high-mass and high-metallicity end of its population [arXiv:2105.02892]. Although it is not as blue as other similar objects, it exhibits a slightly elevated star formation rate for its mass [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG8 lies near the high-mass, high-metallicity end of the sample, with low SFR but still elevated SFR for its mass and compact morphology.

quotes.quote: O3ELG8 This object is near the high mass and metallicity end for our sample and has the second lowest SFR . In terms of its mass-metallicity and mass-SFR, O3ELG8 is one of the more typical galaxies in our sample; it is, however, still slightly high SFR for its mass. It is not as blue as other objects, but it still has a fairly compact morphology.

gmw_00007832.name: O3ELG9
gmw_00007832.overview: O3ELG9 is a dwarf emission-line galaxy characterized by a high star formation rate [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is a relatively massive but low-metallicity system with an extended structure and a spectrum rich in emission lines [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG9 is the brightest and most extended sample object, with high mass, low metallicity, the highest SFR, rich emission lines, 4363 detection, and high EW.

quotes.quote: O3ELG9 This object is by far the brightest object and is the only object targeted for SDSS spectroscopy in our sample. Despite being quite extended in the images, it is less than 1 from the mean redshift of the sample, suggesting that it is likely more extended than most of our other objects. It is the third lowest metallicity object, but has one of the highest masses in the sample.

O3ELG9 has the highest SFR for our sample. The spectrum of O3ELG9 is rich with emission lines, including a significant 4363 detection. This object also has one of the highest EWs in our sample, overlapping the low end of the EW distribution for the blueberry galaxies.

gmw_00007833.name: O3ELG10
gmw_00007833.overview: O3ELG10 is a very faint dwarf emission-line galaxy with a low star formation rate [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is likely the lowest-mass object in its sample, exhibiting a typical metallicity for its mass [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG10 is very faint and low-SFR, missed by the strongest-candidate cut but confirmed spectroscopically; its SED is unreliable, likely making it the lowest-mass object.

quotes.quote: O3ELG10 Despite being the second lowest redshift object in the sample, O3ELG10 is the faintest object with g=21.9 and is almost undetected in the available imaging. This object has such a low S/N ratio VIRUS spectrum that it did not make the initial sample of strong candidates. However, it was among a group of promising fainter candidates and we were able to confirm its redshift with LRS2 spectra. This is one of two objects that does not have a GALEX detection, possibly due to its very low SFR (lowest in the sample).

Unfortunately due to the faintness and limited photometric data available, we were not able to obtain reliable SED fits. However, since this object has the second lowest redshift and is also the faintest in photometry in our sample, we expect this is the lowest mass object. O3ELG10 has a metallicity typical for our sample and typical, if not high, for its mass in comparison with other field samples. This object has a SFR that falls between that of typical low mass galaxies and the more extreme blueberry galaxies.

gmw_00007834.name: O3ELG11
gmw_00007834.overview: O3ELG11 is an extended dwarf emission-line galaxy that was identified with multiple HETDEX detections [arXiv:2105.02892]. Although its mass and metallicity are typical of field galaxies, it exhibits a high star formation rate for its mass [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG11 is extended, multiply detected by HETDEX, has a 4363 line enabling direct-method metallicity, and combines field-like mass-metallicity with high SFR for its mass.

quotes.quote: O3ELG11 O3ELG11 is one of the most extended objects in our sample and was found with multiple HETDEX detections. Its LRS2-B spectrum includes a significant 4363 detection, allowing us to infer metallicity from the direct T_e method. This object has properties close to typical field galaxies in terms of its mass and metallicity. O3ELG11 does have high SFR for its mass, however, and falls in a similar SFR regime as the blueberry galaxies.

gmw_00007835.name: O3ELG12a
gmw_00007835.overview: O3ELG12a is a compact emission line galaxy with a low stellar mass and a high star formation rate [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is spatially close to a smaller, less massive companion galaxy, suggesting the two systems are currently interacting [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG12a is one of two spatially separate same-redshift sources from one HETDEX detection, compact and possibly interacting, with typical low-mass metallicity but high SFR.

quotes.quote: O3ELG12a+b O3ELG12a and O3ELG12b were found with a single HETDEX detection. LRS2 observations confirmed the existence of two spatially separate sources at the same redshift. O3ELG12b has much higher O3O2 ratio than O3ELG12a, and hence a lower log(O/H)+12 by 0.2 dex and has lower stellar mass by 0.9 dex. O3ELG12b also appears physically much smaller in imaging; however, both have a compact morphology. Both have mass-metallicities consistent with the typical low-mass ber12 sample but high SFRs for their mass.

This object is an interesting case, because the two components are sufficiently close to imply an interaction, but exhibit low but different metallicities, with O3ELG12b much less massive. Interestingly O3ELG12b has a much higher EW consistent with the less extreme end of the blueberry sample where O3ELGa has an EW typical of our sample and generally lower than the blueberries.
