The disparity in mass and metallicities between the galaxies is shared with the O3ELG4a and b galaxy pair.

gmw_00007836.name: O3ELG12b
gmw_00007836.overview: O3ELG12b is a compact, low-mass emission line galaxy that is the smaller component of the O3ELG12 pair [arXiv:2105.02892]. It exhibits a high star formation rate for its mass and is likely undergoing an interaction with its companion, O3ELG12a [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG12b is the smaller, lower-mass component of the O3ELG12 pair, with higher O3O2, lower metallicity, higher EW, and a likely interaction with O3ELG12a.

quotes.quote: O3ELG12a+b O3ELG12a and O3ELG12b were found with a single HETDEX detection. LRS2 observations confirmed the existence of two spatially separate sources at the same redshift. O3ELG12b has much higher O3O2 ratio than O3ELG12a, and hence a lower log(O/H)+12 by 0.2 dex and has lower stellar mass by 0.9 dex. O3ELG12b also appears physically much smaller in imaging; however, both have a compact morphology. Both have mass-metallicities consistent with the typical low-mass ber12 sample but high SFRs for their mass.

This object is an interesting case, because the two components are sufficiently close to imply an interaction, but exhibit low but different metallicities, with O3ELG12b much less massive. Interestingly O3ELG12b has a much higher EW consistent with the less extreme end of the blueberry sample where O3ELGa has an EW typical of our sample and generally lower than the blueberries. The disparity in mass and metallicities between the galaxies is shared with the O3ELG4a and b galaxy pair.

gmw_00007837.name: O3ELG13
gmw_00007837.overview: O3ELG13 is a galaxy characterized by a blue, compact morphology [arXiv:2105.02892]. It exhibits a high star formation rate for its mass, falling into the typical regime of low-mass dwarf galaxies in terms of both mass and metallicity [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG13 was a strong candidate with moderately high O3O2, high mass for the sample, dwarf-like mass-metallicity behavior, high SFR for its mass, and blue compact morphology.

quotes.quote: O3ELG13 O3ELG13 was a strong candidate, with a moderately high O3O2 ratio in its HETDEX spectrum. This object is one of the highest mass objects in the sample and falls in the regime typical of low-mass dwarfs in terms of its mass and metallicity. It does, however, have a fairly high SFR for its mass. Like most of the objects in the sample it has a blue, compact morphology.

gmw_00007838.name: O3ELG14
gmw_00007838.overview: O3ELG14 is a dwarf emission-line galaxy with a redshift of z=0.0904 [arXiv:2105.02892]. While it is typical in mass and metallicity, it exhibits a high specific star formation rate and a moderately high O3O2 ratio [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG14 has moderately high O3O2 and the second-highest redshift in the sample, with typical mass-metallicity values but among the highest SFRs and high specific SFR.

quotes.quote: O3ELG14 O3ELG14 has a moderately high O3O2 ratio in its HETDEX spectrum, and its redshift, z=0.0904 , is the second highest in our sample. It is fairly typical in terms of its mass and metallicity. It does, however, have one of the highest SFR and has a high specific SFR.

gmw_00007839.name: O3ELG15
gmw_00007839.overview: O3ELG15 is an extended emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0267 [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is highly star-forming for its mass and has a low metallicity determined via the direct method [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG15 is extended, lowest-redshift, emission-line rich, directly measured in metallicity via 4363, and highly star-forming for its mass despite being SFR-typical for the sample.

quotes.quote: O3ELG15 O3ELG15 is an extended source in imaging data. It is also the lowest redshift object in the sample at z=0.0267 and has a spectrum rich in emission lines. Since its 4363 line is well-detected, the direct T_e method was employed to infer its metallicity. Its log(O/H)+12=7.45 makes it the lowest metallicity object with an 4363 detection.

This object has typical metallicity for its mass consistent with the ber12 sample. It is still highly star forming for its mass, however, it is one of the more typical objects in terms of its SFR for our sample falling in the very low end of specific star formation rates of blueberries.

gmw_00007840.name: O3ELG16
gmw_00007840.overview: O3ELG16 is a faint, low-mass dwarf galaxy that exhibits one of the highest O3O2 ratios in its sample [arXiv:2105.02892]. It is highly star-forming for its mass compared to typical field galaxies, though it lacks a GALEX detection [arXiv:2105.02892].

sources.summary: O3ELG16 has one of the highest O3O2 ratios and lowest masses, is faint and near a bright star, lacks GALEX detection, and is highly star-forming for its mass.

quotes.quote: O3ELG16 O3ELG16 has one of the highest O3O2 ratios and is one of the lowest mass objects in the sample. This object is also one of our faintest objects and is challenging to observe due to its proximity to a bright star on-sky. This is one of two objects that does not have a GALEX detection either due to it being a faint UV source or its proximity to the bright star. It has metallicity fairly typical for its low mass; however, it is very highly star forming for its mass compared to typical field galaxies.

gmw_00007841.name: Q0056-363
gmw_00007841.overview: Q0056-363 is a radio-quiet X-ray quasar located at a redshift of z=0.162 [arXiv:astro-ph/0306550]. Its spectrum exhibits a large, featureless soft X-ray excess and a strong, broad iron K-alpha emission line originating from near-neutral iron [arXiv:astro-ph/0306550].

sources.summary: The paper presents Q0056-363 as a radio-quiet, powerful X-ray quasar with a luminous soft excess and an unusually broad, intense near-neutral Fe K line.

quotes.quote: We present an XMM-Newton observation of the radio-quiet quasar Q0056-363 (z=0.162). This is the first time that this quasar is observed in the hard X-ray range (above 2 keV). We find that Q0056-363 is a powerful X-ray quasar, with a 0.3--12 keV unabsorbed luminosity of about 1.2 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 with the largest part ( 67 ) emitted below 2 keV.

The spectrum reveals a large featureless soft X-ray excess below 2 keV and a strong broad Fe K _ line at 6.4 keV (in the quasar frame). The Fe K _ line is due to low to moderate ionization states of iron (i.e., < Fe xvii ), with an equivalent width of about 250 eV and a velocity width of about 25,000 km s ^ 1 . Q0056-363 is presently the most luminous AGN known to exhibit such a broad and intense Fe K _ line profile from near neutral iron.

gmw_00007842.name: host galaxy of GRB 190114C
gmw_00007842.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 190114C is a massive, star-forming spiral galaxy [arXiv:1911.07876, arXiv:2006.07251]. It is part of a close pair of interacting galaxies whose tidal interactions likely triggered its enhanced star formation [arXiv:1911.07876].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 190114C host as a massive, star-forming member of an interacting pair, with high molecular gas and a nuclear GRB site.

quotes.quote: The host system is composed of a close pair of interacting galaxies ( v = 50 km s ^ 1 ), both of which are well-detected by ALMA in CO(3-2). The GRB occurred within the nuclear region ( 170 pc from the centre) of the less massive but more star-forming galaxy of the pair. The host is more massive (log(M/M _ ) = 9.3) than average GRB hosts at that redshift and the location of the GRB is rather unique. The enhanced star-formation rate was probably triggered by tidal interactions between the two galaxies.

The host galaxy of GRB 190114C has a stellar mass of log(M/M _ )=9.27_ 0.28 ^ +0.25 , larger than typical GRB hosts at this redshift and a metallicity of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.3 or 43 solar, lower than typical field galaxies with similar mass. The host galaxy is star-forming, with a SFR that has been measured using different methods, with values in the range 3 - 25 M _ yr ^ 1 . The presence of He i emission lines in the spectrum indicates the existence of a young stellar population, in spite of the larger (although unconstrained) age derived from the SED fit.

sources.summary: HST observations identify the GRB 190114C host as a spiral or tidally disrupted, strongly star-forming galaxy, with the burst located within 250 pc of its nucleus.

quotes.quote: HST The Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) imaged the afterglow and host galaxy of GRB 190114C on 11 February and 12 March 2019. HST observations clearly reveal that the host galaxy is spiral (Extended Data Fig. fig:3colHST ). A direct subtraction of the epochs of F850LP observations yields a faint residual close to the nucleus of the host (Extended Data Fig. fig:diffHST ). From the position of the residual we estimate that the burst originated within 250 pc of the host galaxy nucleus.

A portion of the second spectrum is shown in Extended Data Fig. fig:VLTspectrum , illustrating the strong emission lines characteristic of a strongly star-forming galaxy, whose light is largely dominating over the afterglow at this epoch.

gmw_00007843.name: GAMA593680
gmw_00007843.overview: GAMA593680 is a kinematically complex galaxy featuring a central bar, clumpy spiral structures, and concentric gas rings [arXiv:2204.02925, arXiv:2011.03566]. It hosts an old stellar population and exhibits a starburst-driven galactic wind that expels shocked gas in a bipolar outflow [arXiv:1707.03879].

sources.summary: The paper treats GAMA593680 as the most kinematically complex object, with multiple molecular gas components, central velocity flaring, minor-axis twisting, and BIC-supported radial bar-flow.

quotes.quote: GAMA593680 appears to be the most complex in terms of kinematic structure and is modelled by a superposition of two Gaussian profiles; an inner Gaussian disc-like profile (we say disc-type here as the mean position of the ring is located at the kinematic centre) and outer Gaussian ring-type profile. The inner region of the PVD also follows a linear rotation pattern, emulating solid-body rotation, instead of the more sharply rising central rotation predicted by our MGE model. As previously noted, this is what we would expect to observe when viewing gas rings in projection. Again, we interpret the multiple ring structure of this object as indicative of an axi-asymmetric forcing, most likely by a bar. In this instance, we may have inner Lindblad resonances/X2 orbits occupied in addition to the co-rotation radius or outer resonances, as the gas profile appears to extend beyond the turnover radius.

The bulk of the profile is in reasonable agreement with the MGE-derived rotation (there is some indication that the turnover radius of the model is larger than that of the MGE-derived rotation curve). However, the gas clearly has a central flare in the velocity direction. This central flare is not captured by either the MGE-rotation or by that of the model, which is evident in the residual. We can not, therefore, attribute this flare to the inner Gaussian ring in this profile assuming the dynamics are purely driven by circular rotation. Furthermore, in the residual panel the receding side of the PVD is clearly more gas rich than the approaching side, implying a flux asymmetry in this object.

sources.summary: The paper describes GAMA 593680 as a wind-dominated galaxy with extraplanar radio emission, bipolar outflow evidence, old stellar populations, and shocked gas expelled by a starburst wind.

quotes.quote: GAMA 593680 shows extended radio emission reaching to heights of 5 kpc above the disk. Furthermore, the / line ratio map shows evidence of a bipolar outflow, likely a result of a supernova-driven wind. Like the other wind-dominated galaxies, GAMA 593680 lies above the median D _n (4000)-H _A relation of SDSS star forming galaxies (by 0.47 ).

GAMA 593680 has the largest D _n (4000) value, implying it has the oldest stellar populations. The strong increase of emission line ratio with disk height ( /H increases by 0.2 dex parsec ^ 1 ) argues against the line ratios being caused by AGN activity and supports the idea that shocked gas is being expelled from the galaxy in a starburst-driven galactic wind, likely aided by cosmic ray pressure. The radio emission is strongly centred on the nucleus of GAMA 593680.

sources.summary: The paper describes GAMA593680 as an outflow-type galaxy whose CO kinematics suggest concentric rings, clumpy spiral/bar structure, and enhanced molecular-gas-density spaxels relative to controls.

quotes.quote: From a simple visual analysis, we identify three different gas structures from the PVDs and intensity profiles: GAMA593680 (left panel) possesses no traits of the classic Sersic/exponential surface brightness profile and, by eye, would appear to be suggestive of two concentric rings, reminiscent of those caused by bar resonances. The apparent asymmetry may be due to a clumpy spiral structure within the galaxy and displays classic signatures of a central bar; GAMA106389 (centre panel) is more prosaic, having a structure and surface brightness profile suggestive of a Sersic/exponential disk at the centre of the galaxy (there is suggestion of a ``hole'' towards the centre of the distribution); GAMA417678 (right panel) appears far more extended on one side of its kinematic centre than the other, which may be indicative of a disturbed system.

Low- M_* object GAMA228432 and high- M_* objects GAMA106389, GAMA417678 and GAMA593680 possess an enhancement of _ CO spaxels with respect to the ALMA control sample (where both GAMA228432 and GAMA417678 were also noted in Figure fig: sfrdhists to have elevated _ SFR spaxels). Only GAMA593680, however, appears to possess significantly enhanced _ CO relative to the SAMI control sample (transformed via Equation eq6 ).

gmw_00007844.name: GAMA106389
gmw_00007844.overview: GAMA106389 is a galaxy featuring a central Sersic-like molecular gas disk [arXiv:2011.03566] that is best modeled as a Gaussian ring [arXiv:2204.02925]. This ring structure exhibits a flux asymmetry [arXiv:2204.02925] and may be driven by resonances associated with a central bar [arXiv:2204.02925].

sources.summary: The paper models GAMA106389 with a molecular gas ring, solid-body-like projected rotation, possible bar-driven resonance, and flux asymmetry without clear radial motion.

quotes.quote: GAMA106389: the molecular gas distribution is best modelled by a Gaussian ring, as opposed to an exponential disc (see Section subsec: Kin ). The central emission in the PVD also appears to follow the solid-body rotation pattern (i.e. where velocity increases linearly with radius), rather than the steeper central stellar rotation predicted by the MGE model. This is what we would expect to observe for a gas ring viewed in projection. The most likely interpretation of this object's kinematic profile is the existence of a resonance system, which may be driven by the forcing frequency associated with a bar.

The pattern frequency of a bar causes gas to accumulate on stable, closed orbital paths where the gas rotates at a rate that is commensurate with the rate at which it encounters the forcing. In this case, it is probable that either the inner Lindblad resonance or X2 orbits are populated by gas (those at and within co-rotation, which usually coincides with the turnover radius). The object appears to have a flux asymmetry in the approaching velocity side of its PVD, suggesting the gas is not evenly distributed around this ring. This asymmetry is also present in the residual plot for the object.

sources.summary: The paper uses GAMA106389 as a PVD example, describing a central Sersic-like CO disk with a possible central hole and enhanced molecular-gas-density spaxels.

quotes.quote: We give examples of the output of this this procedure in Figure fig: pvds , where we show PVDs of GAMA593680, GAMA106389 and GAMA417678 (using slit widths of 1.6 , 2.4 and 2.0 respectively).

From a simple visual analysis, we identify three different gas structures from the PVDs and intensity profiles: GAMA593680 (left panel) possesses no traits of the classic Sersic/exponential surface brightness profile and, by eye, would appear to be suggestive of two concentric rings, reminiscent of those caused by bar resonances. The apparent asymmetry may be due to a clumpy spiral structure within the galaxy and displays classic signatures of a central bar; GAMA106389 (centre panel) is more prosaic, having a structure and surface brightness profile suggestive of a Sersic/exponential disk at the centre of the galaxy (there is suggestion of a ``hole'' towards the centre of the distribution); GAMA417678 (right panel) appears far more extended on one side of its kinematic centre than the other, which may be indicative of a disturbed system.

Low- M_* object GAMA228432 and high- M_* objects GAMA106389, GAMA417678 and GAMA593680 possess an enhancement of _ CO spaxels with respect to the ALMA control sample (where both GAMA228432 and GAMA417678 were also noted in Figure fig: sfrdhists to have elevated _ SFR spaxels).

gmw_00007845.name: GAMA417678
gmw_00007845.overview: GAMA417678 is a galaxy in a close pair with GAMA 417676 [arXiv:1707.03879] that displays an asymmetric, extended gas structure indicative of a disturbed system [arXiv:2011.03566]. It features a high star formation rate [arXiv:1707.03879, arXiv:2011.03566] and a 7.5 kpc radio tail that may represent a galactic wind, tidal feature, or weak jet [arXiv:1707.03879].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GAMA 417678 as the sample extreme in star formation and kinematics, likely interacting with GAMA 417676 and showing a radio tail possibly from a wind, tide, or jet.

quotes.quote: GAMA 417678 is the most extreme galaxy in the sample, having the highest optical SFR and _ SFR as well as the highest _ 50 and kinematic parameters. GAMA 417678 is likely to have experienced a recent burst of star formation because it lies well above the median SDSS relation in the D _n (4000)-H _A plane. GAMA 417678 is in a close pair (with GAMA 417676, M _* = 10^ 9.98 M _ ). Given the close proximity to another massive galaxy, a recent burst of star formation resulting from an interaction is likely for GAMA 417678.

One of the most noticeable features of the radio emission from GAMA 417678 is a strong ( >3 rms) tail extending 7.5 kpc to the south-east. This tail is not in the direction of any neighbouring galaxies, so it is possible that the tail may be a result of a starburst-driven galactic wind which has blown a hole in the disk, allowing CRs to be advected into the halo. However, we cannot rule out the possibility that the tail is a tidal feature. The tail could also be a weak radio jet.

sources.summary: The paper models GAMA417678 with a Gaussian-ring molecular profile, likely resonance structure, receding-velocity gas enhancement, central velocity flaring, and no clear ongoing radial motion.

quotes.quote: GAMA417678 is modelled by a Gaussian ring surface brightness profile with a rotation curve that is in close agreement with the MGE-derived rotation curve. This ring-type profile may again be indicative of a resonance system, with gas occupying the inner Lindblad resonance, within the co-rotation radius. Again, with reference to Figure fig: majpvds , we observe a gas enhancement at receding velocities. We also note a slight flaring of the velocities towards the kinematic centre of the profile, which can be more clearly observed in the residual plot, where there are residual flux enhancements corresponding to this central velocity flare.
GAMA417678 : we can infer from the residual a gas enhancement at negative offsets and positive velocities, but no ``bending/twistiing'' indicative of radial motion.

sources.summary: The paper presents GAMA417678 as a PVD example with asymmetric extended CO structure, possible disturbance, and elevated dense-spaxel star-formation-rate behavior.

quotes.quote: We give examples of the output of this this procedure in Figure fig: pvds , where we show PVDs of GAMA593680, GAMA106389 and GAMA417678 (using slit widths of 1.6 , 2.4 and 2.0 respectively).

From a simple visual analysis, we identify three different gas structures from the PVDs and intensity profiles: GAMA593680 (left panel) possesses no traits of the classic Sersic/exponential surface brightness profile and, by eye, would appear to be suggestive of two concentric rings, reminiscent of those caused by bar resonances. The apparent asymmetry may be due to a clumpy spiral structure within the galaxy and displays classic signatures of a central bar; GAMA106389 (centre panel) is more prosaic, having a structure and surface brightness profile suggestive of a Sersic/exponential disk at the centre of the galaxy (there is suggestion of a ``hole'' towards the centre of the distribution); GAMA417678 (right panel) appears far more extended on one side of its kinematic centre than the other, which may be indicative of a disturbed system.
In the instances of GAMA417678 and GAMA228132 (a high- M_* and a low- M_* object respectively), there does appear to be a significant elevation in the dense spaxel tail of their _ SFR distribution.

gmw_00007846.name: ESO 374 032
gmw_00007846.overview: ESO 374 032 is a post-merger galaxy hosting an OH megamaser and an increasingly intense nuclear starburst [arXiv:1204.5520, arXiv:1509.08468]. The system exhibits ongoing star formation in its southwestern tidal arm, extended off-nuclear shock emission [arXiv:1204.5520, arXiv:1509.08468], and is identified as a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2402.05992].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 374 032 as an SED-identified AGN candidate undetected by NuSTAR, with obscured-AGN WISE/PAH indicators, estimated N_H=25.6, and possible heavy obscuration or starburst emission.

quotes.quote: SED-identified three AGN candidates Yamada2023 identified, on the basis of SED decomposition, three AGN candidates (ESO 350 38, ESO 374 032, and NGC 4418), which had been observed in X-rays, specifically with NuSTAR in the 8--24 keV band, but not been detected presumably due to insufficient exposures ( 50 ks) (see Section sub2-1-GOALS ). Their WISE color criteria Stern2012 and equivalent widths of the 6.2 m polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon features suggest that they are obscured AGNs e.g., Yamada2021 . Using Equation 7 in Asmus2015 in combination with their 12- m AGN fluxes and observed 2--10 keV fluxes, we estimate their N_ H to be 25.3 1.8 (ESO 350 38), 25.6 1.9 (ESO 374 032), and 26.2 1.9 (NGC 4418).

The 3 upper limits of intrinsic 2--10 keV luminosities are then estimated to be L_ 2-10 ^ (lim) = 42.42 , 42.69, and 41.49, respectively, from the 3 upper limit of their 8--24 keV luminosities under the assumption of N_ H = 25 Yamada2021 . Comparison between their X-ray and bolometric AGN luminosities yields 2--10 keV bolometric correction factors of 20--160. The values are roughly consistent with the typical one among local AGNs with a wide range of _ Edd = - 3--0 Vasudevan2007 . Thus, these three sources are expected to be heavily obscured AGNs with N_ H 25 , or pure starbursts.

sources.summary: IRAS F10038-3338 is described as a post-merger with tidal-arm star formation, flattened gradients, an OH megamaser, soft X-ray emission, nuclear starburst activity, and off-nuclear shocks.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10038-3338 (ESO 374-IG032) This post-merger exhibits significant ongoing star formation in its southwestern tidal arm unlike the two other coalesced systems in our sample. The total line emission in this region is much weaker than in the nucleus, inducing a large uncertainty in the extinction map and [ OII ] lines, creating the discrepant values seen in the metallicity gradient plots for this galaxy. Either gradient, however, is consistent with the overall flattening trend seen in all of our systems. This system hosts an OH megamaser and has soft x-ray emission, all consistent with the advanced stage of merging and increasingly intense nuclear starburst Henkel90,Staveley-Smith92,Darling02,Iwasawa09 . Our spectra also show evidence for extended off-nuclear shock emission dominating in areas where there is little to no evidence of ongoing star formation, consistent with the IFU observations of Monreal10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes this post-merger as having star formation in a tidal arm, an OH megamaser, soft X-rays, and extended off-nuclear shock emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS F10038-3338 (ESO 374-IG032) This post-merger exhibits significant ongoing star formation in its southwestern tidal arm unlike the two other coalesced systems in our sample. The total line emission in this region is much weaker than in the nucleus, inducing a large uncertainty in the extinction map and lines, creating the discrepant values seen in the metallicity gradients for this galaxy Rich12 . This system hosts an OH megamaser and has soft x-ray emission, all consistent with the advanced stage of merging and increasingly intense nuclear starburst Henkel90,Staveley-Smith92,Darling02,Iwasawa09 . Our spectra also show evidence for extended off-nuclear shock emission dominating in areas where there is little to no evidence of ongoing star formation, consistent with the IFU observations of Monreal10 .

gmw_00007847.name: zdrop-A032
gmw_00007847.overview: zdrop-A032 is one of the brightest z_850 dropout galaxy candidates [arXiv:0910.0077]. It has an estimated photometric redshift of 6.8 and a stellar mass of a few 10^9 solar masses [arXiv:0910.0077].

sources.summary: The paper identifies zdrop-A032 as one of the brightest z_850 dropout galaxy candidates and reports deblended IRAC-based stellar masses of a few 10^9 solar masses at z_ph 6.8.

quotes.quote: Except two previously studied sources, no other objects in our three dropout samples show convincing case of detection in the deep GOODS IRAC images. These two sources are the two brightest z_ 850 dropouts, zdrop-A032 and A025. In the IRAC images, their locations are unfortunately very close to other unrelated objects nearby, and the extraction of their fluxes in IRAC passbands is non-trivial.

Nevertheless, their stellar masses have been derived by Labb e et al. (2006), using the deblended IRAC photometry as the major constrain on their SEDs in the rest-frame optical. Depending on the models used, they derived stellar masses of a few 10^9 M_ at z_ ph 6.8 and 7.3 for zdrop-A032 (their ID 1147) and A025 (their ID 963), respectively. Interestingly, these values are not inconsistent with the best-fit values that we have obtained (see Table 4) using only the rest-frame UV flux measurements.

gmw_00007848.name: zdrop-A025
gmw_00007848.overview: The high-redshift galaxy candidate zdrop-A025 is classified as a z_850 dropout [arXiv:0910.0077]. Located at a photometric redshift of approximately 7.3, it has an estimated stellar mass of a few billion solar masses [arXiv:0910.0077].

sources.summary: The paper treats zdrop-A025 as one of the two brightest z_850 dropout candidates, noting difficult IRAC deblending and stellar masses of a few 10^9 solar masses at z_ph 7.3.

quotes.quote: Except two previously studied sources, no other objects in our three dropout samples show convincing case of detection in the deep GOODS IRAC images. These two sources are the two brightest z_ 850 dropouts, zdrop-A032 and A025. In the IRAC images, their locations are unfortunately very close to other unrelated objects nearby, and the extraction of their fluxes in IRAC passbands is non-trivial.

Nevertheless, their stellar masses have been derived by Labb e et al. (2006), using the deblended IRAC photometry as the major constrain on their SEDs in the rest-frame optical. Depending on the models used, they derived stellar masses of a few 10^9 M_ at z_ ph 6.8 and 7.3 for zdrop-A032 (their ID 1147) and A025 (their ID 963), respectively. Interestingly, these values are not inconsistent with the best-fit values that we have obtained (see Table 4) using only the rest-frame UV flux measurements.

gmw_00007849.name: UGC 2162
gmw_00007849.overview: UGC 2162 is a nearby ultra-diffuse, low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:1907.10240, arXiv:1810.08453] located approximately 300 kpc from the center of the M 77 group [arXiv:1907.10240]. It features an extended neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1907.10240], a dwarf-like dark matter halo [arXiv:1907.10240], and a relatively high gas-phase metallicity [arXiv:1810.08453].

sources.summary: GMRT observations show UGC 2162 is a nearby UDG with an extended HI disk, moderate rotation, dwarf-like dark halo properties, and constraints against several formation scenarios.

quotes.quote: Our GMRT \ observations of the ultra diffuse galaxy (UDG) UGC 2162, black projected 300 kpc from the centre of the M 77 group, reveal it to a have an extended black \ disk (R _ HI/R_ 25 3.3) with a moderate rotational velocity (V _ rot 31 ). This V _ rot is in line with that of dwarf galaxies with similar \ mass. We estimate an M _ dyn of 1.14 10 ^ 9 \ within the galaxy's R _ HI 5.2 kpc. Additionally, our estimates black of M _ 200 for the galaxy from NFW models black are in the range of 5.0 to 8.8 10 ^ 10 . black Comparing UGC 2162 to samples of UDGs with \ detections show it to have amongst the smallest R _e with its M / \ being distinctly higher and g -- i colour slightly bluer than typical values in those samples.

We also compared \ and dark matter (DM) halo properties of UGC 2162 with dwarf galaxies in the LITTLE THINGS sample and find its black DM halo mass and profile are within the range expected for a dwarf galaxy . While we were unable to to determine the origin of the galaxy's present day optical form from our study, its normal \ rotation velocity in relation to its \ mass, \ morphology, environment and dwarf black mass DM halo ruled out some of the proposed ultra diffuse galaxy formation scenarios for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 2162 as a nearby ultra-diffuse, low-surface-brightness galaxy with high gas-phase metallicity, host-like stellar mass and star-formation density, and outlier status on the mass-metallicity relation.

quotes.quote: The exception may be UGC 2162, a nearby ultra-diffuse galaxy (UDG) which combines low surface brightness and relatively high metallicity. We confirm the high metallicity of UGC 2162 ( 12+ ( O/H )=8.52^ +0.27 _ 0.24 ) using spectra taken with the 10-m GTC telescope. UGC 2162 has the stellar mass, metallicity, and star-formation rate (SFR) surface density expected for a host galaxy in between outbursts. This fact suggests a physical connection between some UDGs and metal-poor galaxies, which may be the same type of object in a different phase of the SF cycle. UGC 2162 is a high-metallicity outlier of the mass-metallicity relation, a property shared by the few UDGs with known gas-phase metallicity.

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 2162 as a comparison UDG with resolved GMRT kinematics, noting mass and rotation-curve caveats, less extreme structure, and inclination tension with the BTFR.

quotes.quote: UGC 2162 is an UDG which has been studied using ^ 3D Barolo in resolved GMRT observations sengupta . Two caveats exist regarding this galaxy. First, sengupta mention that it has large uncertainties in its baryonic mass (probably because of the uncertainty associated with its distance; the galaxy is much closer than those in our sample), and second, its rotation curve does not show signs of flattening (the emission is less extended than in our data), although it is presumably close to reaching the flat part (see figure 2 in kyle2015 ).

Note also that UGC 2162 is less `extreme' than our UDGs: it is less massive, smaller and slightly brighter. Assuming that the amplitude of the rotation curve does not increase significantly beyond the outermost measured radius, the galaxy would need an inclination of 39 ^ to be on the SPARC BTFR, far from the measured inclination of 55 ^ in sengupta .

gmw_00007850.name: DESI-070.4050-50.4559
gmw_00007850.overview: DESI-070.4050-50.4559 is a low-confidence gravitational lens candidate [arXiv:2206.02764]. The system features a faint possible arclet with no visible counterimage, as well as two nearby objects of similar brightness that are too faint to confidently identify as lensed images [arXiv:2206.02764].

sources.summary: The paper describes DESI-070.4050-50.4559 as a low-confidence lens candidate with a faint possible arclet, no visible counterimage, and two similarly bright nearby objects too uncertain to identify as lensed images.

quotes.quote: DESI-070.4050-50.4559 (first row, third column) has a faint possible arclet with no visible counter image. There are two other objects of similar brightness but somewhat different color in the cutout (southeast and northwest of the putative lens). They are too faint for us to have high confidence that they are lensed images and thus we assign a grade of C.

gmw_00007851.name: DESI-153.6301+24.4854
gmw_00007851.overview: DESI-153.6301+24.4854 is a grade-C gravitational lens candidate that displays an apparent arclet [arXiv:2206.02764]. This feature has a surface brightness and color consistent with a lensed arc, but it lacks a clear counterimage and may be shaped by source structure [arXiv:2206.02764].

sources.summary: The paper describes DESI-153.6301+24.4854 as a grade-C lens candidate with an apparent arclet whose color fits lensing, but without a counterimage and possibly shaped by source structure.

quotes.quote: DESI-153.6301+24.4854 (second row, third column) also shows what appears to be an arclet. While the surface brightness and color are consistent with the expectation of a lensed arc, there is no apparent counter image and the shape of the arc does not conform to the typical morphology (there appears to be a ``kink" in the putative arc, possibly indicating two galaxies in close proximity on the sky). It is also possible that the shape of the arc may be due to source structure. Given the limitations of this ground-based image and the absence of a clearly identifiable counter-image, we assign a grade of C.

gmw_00007852.name: DESI-066.4365-57.6926
gmw_00007852.overview: DESI-066.4365-57.6926 is a galaxy featuring a bright, ring-like structure [arXiv:2206.02764]. Although it resembles a perfect Einstein ring, its high surface brightness and structural characteristics suggest it is instead a ring galaxy [arXiv:2206.02764].

sources.summary: The paper argues DESI-066.4365-57.6926 is unlikely to be a strong lens: a perfect Einstein-ring alignment would be rare, the putative ring is bright, and its structure resembles a ring galaxy.

quotes.quote: DESI-066.4365-57.6926 (first row, fourth column) is a type of image that we have seen in our searches a number of times. If it is lensing, this would be a perfect Einstein ring. Since such perfect alignment is very rare even among strong lensing systems, we are generally sceptical. Lensed arcs typically have low surface brightness, whereas this putative ring appears quite bright. The structure seen in the putative ring is, in our experience, consistent with a ring galaxy e.g, timmis2017a .

gmw_00007853.name: DESI-035.4194-46.8752
gmw_00007853.overview: DESI-035.4194-46.8752 is classified as a spiral galaxy rather than a gravitational lensing system [arXiv:2206.02764]. The galaxy features a reddish core and blue spiral arms, and its photometric redshift is consistent with this non-lensing interpretation [arXiv:2206.02764].

sources.summary: The paper considers DESI-035.4194-46.8752 more likely a spiral galaxy than a lensing system, citing its reddish core, blue arms, and photometric redshifts consistent with no lensing.

quotes.quote: For DESI-035.4194-46.8752 (second row, fourth column), we consider the putative arc to be more likely a spiral galaxy (perhaps with a high inclination angle), with what appears to be a reddish core and blue spiral arms. In this and other select ambiguous cases, photometric redshfits zhou2020a can be helpful. The photometric redshift of the putative arc at 0.268 0.047 relative to that of the putative lens (the orange galaxy at the center of the image) at 0.248 0.038 is consistent with this not being a lensing system. While photometic redshifts of elliptical galaxies are often reliable, this is not always the case with blue lensed sources. The photometric redshift of the putative arc in DESI-035.4194-46.8752 is centered on the orange core and is likely more reliable. Hence we assign a grade of D.

gmw_00007854.name: NGC 7805/6 (Arp 112) system
gmw_00007854.overview: The NGC 7805/6 (Arp 112) system is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of the galaxies NGC 7805 and NGC 7806 [arXiv:2011.06368]. These two galaxies display physical signatures of a recent tidal interaction, but they show no gas or optical tidal trail connecting them to the nearby candidate KUG 2359+311 [arXiv:2011.06368].

sources.summary: The paper studies the interacting NGC 7805/6 (Arp 112) pair, finding recent tidal interaction and no gas or optical tidal trail connecting KUG 2359+311 to the system.

quotes.quote: We present results from our Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) \ , Himalayan Chandra Telescope (HCT) H , 1m Sampurnanand Telescope (ST) and 1.3m Devasthal Fast Optical Telescope (DFOT) deep optical observations of NGC 7805/6 (Arp 112) system to test KUG 2359+311's tidal dwarf galaxy (TDG) candidacy and explore the properties of the interacting system.
Our GMRT \ map shows no \ detection associated with KUG 2359+311, nor any \ tail or bridge-like structure connecting KUG 2359+311 to the NGC 7805/6 system.
Arp 112 consisting of the galaxies NGC 7805 and NGC 7806 (radial velocities 4811 \ and 4768 \ respectively) display signatures of a recent tidal interaction.

gmw_00007855.name: NGC 7805
gmw_00007855.overview: NGC 7805 is a lenticular S0-type galaxy that forms the interacting pair Arp 112 alongside the Sbc spiral galaxy NGC 7806 [arXiv:2011.06368]. The pair displays signatures of a recent tidal interaction, though no gas or H-alpha emission is detected from NGC 7805 itself [arXiv:2011.06368].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7805 as the S0 member of Arp 112, finds no detected gas or H-alpha emission from it, and links it to interaction-driven activity.

quotes.quote: Arp 112 consisting of the galaxies NGC 7805 and NGC 7806 (radial velocities 4811 \ and 4768 \ respectively) display signatures of a recent tidal interaction.

Since NGC 7805 is an S0 type galaxy and NGC 7806 is an Sbc spiral, and all the detected \ is associated to NGC 7806, it can black reasonably be assumed that NGC 7806 contained most of the pair's \ before they started interacting. The velocity of the detected \ (4751 ) is closer to the optical radial velocity of NGC 7806 (4768 ) compared to NGC 7805 (4811 ), black confirming the bulk of the detected \ is most likely to have originated in NGC 7806.
No H detection is seen from NGC 7805, an S0 galaxy.

gmw_00007856.name: NGC 7806
gmw_00007856.overview: NGC 7806 is an Sbc spiral galaxy that is interacting with NGC 7805 [arXiv:2011.06368]. This interaction has likely removed a portion of its gas content, leaving a faint northern tidal tail and UV-bright star-forming clumps in its northern disk [arXiv:2011.06368].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7806 as the Sbc spiral in Arp 112, with displaced gas, a northern tidal tail, star-forming clumps, and likely gas loss from interaction.

quotes.quote: The entire black \ emission detected in the system is associated with NGC 7806. The deep optical image of NGC 7806 black in Figure fig2 shows a faint tidal tail which extends to the north beyond the optical disk. Additionally, the northern disk of NGC 7806 hosts UV bright clumps identified in GALEX images smith10 . The \ column density maxima are projected on this UV bright section of the NGC 7806 northern disk (Figure 1) and offset by 25 ^ (8 kpc) from the optical centre .

However, the measured total \ mass for NGC 7806 is only 1.4 10^ 9 M _ giving an \ deficiency of 0.41. black This \ deficiency value suggests, that the NGC 7805/06 interaction has removed a significant quantity of the NGC 7806 \ content.

gmw_00007857.name: KUG 2359+311
gmw_00007857.overview: KUG 2359+311 is a dwarf galaxy with ongoing star formation and an old stellar population [arXiv:2011.06368]. It is likely a regular gas-rich dwarf undergoing morphological transformation after losing its gas to an interaction with the Arp 112 system [arXiv:2011.06368].

sources.summary: The paper tests KUG 2359+311 as an Arp 112 tidal dwarf candidate but favors a gas-rich dwarf transformed by interaction after losing its gas.

quotes.quote: Our GMRT \ map shows no \ detection associated with KUG 2359+311, nor any \ tail or bridge-like structure connecting KUG 2359+311 to the NGC 7805/6 system.

Our HCT H _ image on the other hand, shows strong detections in KUG 2359+311, with net SFR 0.035 0.009 M _ yr ^ 1 . The H data constrains the redshift of KUG 2359+311 to 0.00 z 0.043 , compared to the redshift of NGC 7806 of 0.015 .

Lack of \ , presence of an old stellar population, ongoing star formation, reasonably high SFR compared to normal dwarf galaxies suggest that KUG 2359+311 may not be an Arp 112 TDG. It is most likely a case of a regular gas-rich dwarf galaxy undergoing a morphological transformation after having lost its entire gas content to an interaction with the Arp 112 system.

gmw_00007858.name: LBDS 53W091
gmw_00007858.overview: LBDS 53W091 is a high-redshift, weak radio early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.55 [arXiv:astro-ph/0402266, arXiv:1011.6122]. Its morphology suggests a two-component system consisting of a compact spheroid and an extended exponential disk, with stellar age estimates ranging from 1.5 to 3.5 Gyr [arXiv:astro-ph/9911067, arXiv:astro-ph/0402266].

sources.summary: The paper presents 53W091 as an old z=1.55 elliptical whose 3.5 Gyr stellar age and high mass constrain galaxy formation and cold-dark-matter models.

quotes.quote: 1996) we have reported the discovery of the oldest known galaxy at z = 1.55 (53W091). Once the spectrum of the galaxy has been obtained it is necessary to build synthetic stellar populations models in order to compute the age of the stellar system.

In Fig. raul:raulfig6 we have plotted the spectrum of 53W091 and three different synthetic stellar population models. The effect with age on the spectral features is quite well known and the best fit is obtained for an age of 3.5 Gyr. 53W091 is a ``clean'' elliptical (i.e. no contamination from any AGN contribution (Dunlop Peacock 1993) and no new star formation episodes), so the only evolution is due to aging stars.

For the case of 53W091 the mass is about 10^ 12 M _ (see Kashlinsky Jimenez 1997). We assume in what follows that the data on 53W091 imply that the galaxy at z =1.55 has mass in excess of 10^ 12 M_ and its stellar population has an age of 3.5 Gyr. The left box in Fig. raul:raulfig4 shows the redshift z_ gal at which the galaxy 53W091 must have formed its first stars for + =1 Universe.

sources.summary: The paper re-estimates LBDS 53W091 as a high-redshift red galaxy aged about 1.5-2.0 Gyr, easing earlier cosmological constraints from a 3.5 Gyr age.

quotes.quote: The recent spectral analysis of LBDS 53W091 by Spinrad and his collaborators has suggested that this red galaxy at z = 1.552 is at least 3.5 Gyr old. This imposes an important constraint on cosmology, suggesting that this galaxy formed at z 6.5 , assuming recent estimates of cosmological parameters. While their analysis was heavily focused on the use of some UV spectral breaks as age indicators, we have performed ^ 2 tests to the continuum of this galaxy using its UV spectrum and photometric data ( R , J , H , K : 2000 -- 9000 in rest-frame).

If the mean metallicity of the stars in this galaxy is assumed to be twice solar, the models including these two effects match the UV spectrum and photometric data of LBDS 53W091 near the age of 1.5 -- 2.0 Gyr.

Our UV-based estimate is approximately 2.0 0.2 Gyr and apparently inconsistent with that of Spinrad et al., 3.5 Gyr, but consistent with the age estimates we obtained using the new Jimenez models. The photometric data of LBDS 53W091 indicate 1.5 0.2 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper uses LBDS 53W091 as a 3.5 Gyr old galaxy at z=1.55 in its OHRO age test and reports that NADE accommodates the lower-redshift old galaxies.

quotes.quote: There have been some OHROs discovered, including, the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 and the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 . In particular, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 is an important one, which has been used as a ``cosmic clock'' to constrain cosmological models. Its age is estimated to be 2.0-3.0 Gyr .

We will use three OHROs: the old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 , the old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at z=1.43 and the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 . The ages of the OHROs at z=1.55 and z=1.43 are 3.5 Gyr and 4.0 Gyr, respectively. For the age of the OHRO at z=3.91 , following , we use the lower bound estimate of t_ obj (3.91)=2.0 Gyr. We calculate the age of the universe at different redshifts using the best fit results of the NADE model, n=2.886 and h=0.685 , and then we obtain the values of : (1.55)=1.215 , (1.43)=1.136 , and (3.91)=0.833 , also shown in Table table3 . So, the NADE model can easily accommodate the OHROs at z=1.55 and 1.43, but cannot accommodate the old quasar at z=3.91 .

sources.summary: The paper presents LBDS 53W091 as a high-redshift red galaxy whose inferred age was debated because old-age estimates challenged galaxy formation in an Einstein-De Sitter universe.

quotes.quote: The first high z red galaxies detected were two faint radio sources from the Lieden-Berkeley Deep Survey (LBDS): LBDS 53W091 ( z= 1.55) and LBDS 53W069 ( z= 1.43) dunlop96,spinrad97,dunlop99 . The analysis of these systems was soon a subject of much debate. For instance, spinrad97 determined an age of 3.5 Gyr for LBDS 53W091, which posed complications to explain galaxy formation under an Einstein-De Sitter universe. This age was soon contested by a series of authors bruzual97,heap98,yi00 that derived much younger ages ( < 2 Gyr), which allowed for more comfortable estimates for the formation redshift ( z_ F ) of the galaxies. Subsequent analyses revived the polemic by confirming the first determinations, i.e. ascribing ages in excess of 3 Gyr nolan03,ferreras04 .

sources.summary: The paper treats LBDS 53W091 as a high-redshift weak radio early-type galaxy whose stellar-age estimates remain degenerate, with evidence for young-star contamination in a two-component morphology.

quotes.quote: Age derivation techniques for unresolved stellar populations at high redshifts are explored using the NUV spectrum of LBDS 53W091 ( z=1.55 ) and LBDS 53W069 ( z=1.43 ). The photometry and morphology of these galaxies -- which are weak radio sources -- suggest they are early-type systems, a feature that makes them ideal test beds for the analysis of their ages and metallicities with the use of population synthesis models.

Furthermore, the morphological analysis of LBDS 53W091 performed on HST images using WFPC2 and NICMOS in the F814W, F110W and F160W passbands is suggestive of a two component system, comprising a compact de Vaucouleurs spheroid ( r_e=2.7 0.7  kpc) plus an extended exponential disk ( h=4.2 1.7  kpc), which only appears in the bluer passband (Waddington et al. 2002). This is suggestive of the presence of young stellar populations. The contamination in the rest-frame NUV from young stars is estimated to be up to 20 (Waddington et al. 2002).

sources.summary: The paper uses LBDS 53W091 as an old high-redshift galaxy age constraint, noting its 3.5 Gyr age at z=1.55 and finding the agegraphic dark-energy model can accommodate it.

quotes.quote: We investigate the age constraint on the agegraphic dark energy model by using two old galaxies (LBDS 53W091 and LBDS 53W069 ) and the old high redshift quasar APM 08279+5255 . We find that the agegraphic dark energy model can easily accommodate LBDS 53W091 and LBDS 53W069 .
LBDS 53W091 was 3.5 Gyr old at z=1.55 .
The constraints given by LBDS 53W091 ( z=1.55 ) and LBDS 53W069 ( z=1.43 ) can be easily satisfied in the agegraphic dark energy model, as many other dark energy models.

sources.summary: The paper treats LBDS 53W091 as a 3.5 Gyr old high-redshift galaxy at z = 1.55 used with other OHROs to test LTB void cosmologies.

quotes.quote: There are some old high redshift objects (OHROs) considered extensively in the literature, for instance, the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55   , the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43   . In addition, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91   is also used extensively.

In the literature, these three OHROs have been extensively used to test various dark energy models (see e.g. ) and modified gravity models (see e.g. ). In the present work, we will use them to test various LTB void models.

Here, we try to test various LTB void models with three OHROs mentioned in Sec.  sec1 , namely the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55   , the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43   , and the 2.0 Gyr old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91   .

sources.summary: The paper uses LBDS 53W091 as a 3.5 Gyr galaxy at z=1.55 and says prior holographic-dark-energy tests could accommodate this old high-redshift object.

quotes.quote: Now, there are some OHROs discovered, for example, the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 and the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 .

In the work of Wei and Zhang , the original holographic dark energy model (neither spatial curvature nor interaction is involved) has been examined by using the three OHROs, the old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 , the old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 , and the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 . It is found in Ref. that the former two OHROs, the old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 and the old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 , can be easily accommodated, but the last one, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 , cannot be accommodated in the model.

sources.summary: The paper treats 53W091 as a 3.5-Gyr-old radio galaxy at z=1.55 and uses coupled dark-energy models to estimate formation redshifts for this aging problem.

quotes.quote: Not to mention the issues that the 14-Gyr age of the universe obtained by Pont et al. , which is in tension with 13.797 0.023 Gyr , the 3.5-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W091 at z = 1.55 and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 .

Model 1 With the best-fitting parameters in this model, we have: EoR ( 6 z 15 ), for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), from around 379.9 Myr (402.6 Myr) to 1.825 Gyr (1.718 Gyr), which lasts 1.445 Gyr (1.315 Gyr); The seed redshift of J0100+2802, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), z_ seed 9.74 (11.03); If the first star formed at z 20 , then the age of the universe at that time would be, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 272.8 Myr (234.1 Myr); Old globular cluster M92 (NGC 6341) would appear at z 7.53 \ (9.27) with age of 14.0 Gyr for OHD ( H(z) + BAO); For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 3.5-Gyr-old ratio galaxy 53W091 ( z 1.55 ) and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 ( z 1.43 ) are formed at z 4.98 \ (5.40) and z 5.41 \ (5.99) , respectively; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), QSO APM 08279+5255 with age around 2.1 Gyr would have formed at z 9.37 \ (12.32) .

Model 2 With the best-fitting parameters in this Model, we have: EoR ( 6 z 15 ), for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), from around 478.7 Myr (534.1 Myr) to 1.371 Gyr (1.462 Gyr), which lasts 892.4 Myr (927.8 Myr); The seed redshift of J0100+2802, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), z_ seed 17.75 \ (16.46) ; If the first star formed at z 20 , then the age of the universe at that time would be, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 337.1 Myr (381.4 Myr); Old globular cluster M92 (NGC 6341) would appear at z 18.19 \ (18.98) with age of 14.0 Gyr for OHD ( H(z) + BAO) ; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 3.5-Gyr-old ratio galaxy 53W091 ( z 1.55 ) and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 ( z 1.43 ) are formed at z 5.95 \ (6.20) and z 6.85 \ (7.22) , respectively; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), QSO APM 08279+5255 with age around 2.1 Gyr would have formed at z 92.16 \ (44.83) .

gmw_00007859.name: GAMA 551202
gmw_00007859.overview: GAMA 551202 is a galaxy that shows evidence of a star formation burst within the past 1 Gyr [arXiv:1707.03879]. It features resolved extraplanar radio emission extending up to 4 kpc perpendicular to its disk, as well as increased velocity dispersion and elevated emission line ratios [arXiv:1707.03879].

sources.summary: The paper finds GAMA 551202 has resolved extraplanar radio emission, elevated line ratios, recent star-formation indicators, and possible low-velocity cosmic-ray-driven wind signatures.

quotes.quote: Although only the central regions ( 2.5 kpc) of GAMA 551202 are probed with SAMI, evidence for increased velocity dispersion and emission line ratios above and below the disk is seen, particularly north of the disk away from the dust lane. GAMA 551202 is the least massive galaxy in the sample, but its D _n (4000) and H _A values (H _A 0.47 above the median relation) indicate that a burst of star formation could have occurred in the past 1 Gyr. GAMA 551202 is the nearest galaxy in our sample and its large angular size allows us to resolve the extraplanar radio emission. This galaxy has extended radio emission that is offset from the optical brightness profile (position angles differ by 30 degrees). Fingers of radio emission can be seen perpendicular to the disk of GAMA 551202 out to 4 kpc.

gmw_00007860.name: GAMA 600030
gmw_00007860.overview: GAMA 600030 is a galaxy characterized by high star formation rates [arXiv:1707.03879] and clumps of hydrogen emission located north and south of its nucleus [arXiv:1707.03879]. It exhibits a large star formation rate surface density that may drive a thermal, starburst-driven wind [arXiv:1707.03879].

sources.summary: The paper notes GAMA 600030 has high star-formation measures, weak unresolved radio emission, possible thermal outflow, and plausible starburst-driven wind signatures.

quotes.quote: Clumps of H emission are seen to the north and south of the nucleus of GAMA 600030, which are not present in the optical continuum nor radio continuum emission. GAMA 600030 shares some properties with the galaxies that have extended radio emission - it has high sSFR and high _ SFR . On the other hand, its radio emission is only 40 of that expected from the H SFR and the emission was not spatially resolved in the FIRST images.

The large star formation rate surface density (0.044 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ) could drive a thermal outflow, raising the velocity dispersion (from 50 km s ^ 1 to 150 km s ^ 1 ) while producing shocks as the outflowing gas collides with its surroundings thereby raising the emission line ratios. GAMA 600030 has the second highest SFR and has a map very similar to that of GAMA 417678. GAMA 600030 could be wind dominated. GAMA 600030 lies above (0.58 ) the median SDSS D _n (4000) - H _A relation. It is therefore plausible that this galaxy is experiencing a starburst-driven (radiation pressure and/or cosmic ray-driven) wind.

gmw_00007861.name: GAMA 376293
gmw_00007861.overview: GAMA 376293 is an isolated galaxy featuring a star-forming disk knot on its south-west side [arXiv:1707.03879]. It lies on the borderline of being classified as wind-dominated, showing enhanced emission line ratios and high velocity dispersion above and below its disk [arXiv:1707.03879].

sources.summary: The paper presents GAMA 376293 as an isolated borderline wind case, with a star-forming disk knot, possible extraplanar radio clump, and kinematic/line-ratio evidence for a wind.

quotes.quote: GAMA 376293 is an isolated galaxy with a knot of star formation on the south-west side of its disk seen in both the H and optical continuum images. This region also corresponds to a region where there is a possible extraplanar clump of radio emission at 2 the background rms. GAMA 376293 lies on the boundary of being classified as wind-dominated or not wind-dominated. Based on the gas kinematic classification of , GAMA 376293 has the high ratio of velocity dispersion-to-rotational velocity expected for a wind-dominated galaxy but its velocity field is too symmetrical.

Given that galaxies with CR-driven winds have not been well studied observationally, it is possible that they result in more symmetrical velocity fields than thermal winds. The velocity dispersion of the emission line gas of GAMA 376293 is largest in the nucleus of the disk and above and below the disk, where the emission line ratios are enhanced. This spatial correlation is in support of a galactic wind in GAMA 376293.

gmw_00007862.name: GAMA 227607
gmw_00007862.overview: GAMA 227607 is a massive galaxy featuring a distinct bulge and a dust lane [arXiv:1707.03879]. It resides in a group with a star-forming disk galaxy, and its disk-concentrated radio emission is likely associated with star formation activity [arXiv:1707.03879].

sources.summary: The paper describes GAMA 227607 as the sample’s most massive galaxy, with bulge and dust lane, no extended radio continuum, possible shocks, and a weak wind or interaction signature.

quotes.quote: GAMA 227607 is the most massive galaxy in our sample with a clear bulge and dust lane. GAMA 227607 is the most difficult candidate in which to find a wind because of the low S/N spectra obtained with SAMI away from the disk. No evidence is seen for extended radio continuum emission (down to 0.3 mJy), expected if CRs were aiding the driving of gas into the halo. The galaxy does show a bi-conical /H emission line ratio map and composite spectra which could reflect a contribution from shocks.

GAMA 227607 is in a group with another star-forming disk galaxy (GAMA 227619) with stellar mass 10 ^ 10.59 M _ , located south-east of GAMA 227607. An interaction with GAMA 227619 could explain the region of high velocity dispersion gas ( =140 km s ^ 1 ) to the west of the disk in GAMA 227607. The radio emission of GAMA 227607 is concentrated along the galaxy disk and is likely to be associated with star formation activity. It is possible that GAMA 227607 hosts a weak galactic wind, but it is perhaps more likely that the enhanced emission line ratios seen are a result of diffuse ionized gas.

gmw_00007863.name: J0107+1408
gmw_00007863.overview: J0107+1408 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0801.3663]. Its EPIC-pn X-ray spectrum is well fitted by a simple power-law model with a steep photon index, which suggests the possible presence of soft X-ray excess emission [arXiv:0801.3663].

sources.summary: The paper finds J0107+1408's EPIC-pn spectrum is acceptably fitted by a simple power law with a steep photon index, while an added MCD component is not statistically required.

quotes.quote: The simple power-law model provided statistically acceptable fits to the pn data of J0107+1408, J0829+5006 and J1434+0338 with photon indices = 2.4 0.1 , 2.65 0.08 , 2.05 0.07 , respectively. The steep photon indices suggest possible presence of the soft X-ray excess emission from these AGNs. However, addition of an MCD component did not improve any of the fits statistically at 95 level.

gmw_00007864.name: J0829+5006
gmw_00007864.overview: The object J0829+5006 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:0801.3663]. It contains an active galactic nucleus with an intermediate-mass black hole and has been the subject of multiple astronomical observations [arXiv:0801.3663].

sources.summary: The paper abbreviates SDSS J082912.67+500652.3 as J0829+5006, notes it was observed multiple times, and says several observations were discarded because of flaring particle background.

quotes.quote: In the following, we abbreviate the names of the AGNs with IMBHs discovered with the SDSS e.g., we mention SDSS J082912.67+500652.3 as J0829+5006.
NGC 4395, J0829+5006 and J1357+6525 were observed multiple times.

Examination of the background rate above 10 showed that the first observation of NGC 4395 on 2002 June and the first, second and fourth observations of J0829+5006 were completely swamped out by the flaring particle background and therefore these observations were discarded from further study.

gmw_00007865.name: J1357+6525
gmw_00007865.overview: J1357+6525 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits strong and rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:0801.3663]. Spectral observations of the source reveal weak soft excess emission, as well as a narrow iron K line detected during its first observation [arXiv:0801.3663].

sources.summary: The paper gives J1357+6525 two lightcurves and two spectral observations, finding weak soft excess emission in both and a narrow iron K line in the first observation.

quotes.quote: There are two lightcurves for each of NGC 4395 and J1357+6525 extracted from different observations. All AGNs exhibit strong and rapid X-ray variability. Individual AGNs show different amplitudes of variability.

J1357+6525 has two observations. Both data show evidence for weak soft excess emission. The spectral shapes are similar withing errors for both the observations except for a narrow iron K line at 6.65 which has been detected in the first observation at a level of 99.3 based on the F-test.

gmw_00007866.name: J1434+0338
gmw_00007866.overview: J1434+0338 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.0286 [arXiv:0802.0821]. The galaxy is mildly X-ray weak [arXiv:0801.3663] and exhibits optical continuum variability [arXiv:0802.0821].

sources.summary: The paper describes monitoring SDSS J143450.62+033842.5 as a low-mass candidate AGN, producing optical continuum light curves for black-hole mass work with multi-observatory support.

quotes.quote: One such study, carried out with the C18, is the monitoring of the low-mass candidate AGN SDSS J143450.62+033842.5 (Greene Ho 2004; z=0.0286 , m_r=15 ). During a one-week period in April 2007 this AGN was monitored with a typical exposure time of 240 sec. The images were reduced in the same way as described in Section NEOs . The typical uncertainty of the measurements is about 0.1 magnitude which is sufficient to detect the variability of this AGN.

Figure AGN-LC shows the light curve, derived using differential photometry and the ``daostat'' program described in Netzer et al. (1996, section 2.3). Combining this light curve with additional data from the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory in Ukraine, the MAGNUM-2m telescope in Hawaii, and the 2m Faulkes Telescope in Australia, produced a fully-sampled continuum light curve for SDSS J143450.62+033842.5 covering several days. Together with an emission-line light curve obtained at the Magellan telescope, the central mass of the black hole in the center of this AGN will be determined.

sources.summary: The paper fits J1434+0338 with an acceptable steep power law and finds no statistically required MCD component; it also identifies the source as mildly X-ray weak.

quotes.quote: The simple power-law model provided statistically acceptable fits to the pn data of J0107+1408, J0829+5006 and J1434+0338 with photon indices = 2.4 0.1 , 2.65 0.08 , 2.05 0.07 , respectively. The steep photon indices suggest possible presence of the soft X-ray excess emission from these AGNs. However, addition of an MCD component did not improve any of the fits statistically at 95 level.

While six of our sources appear to be consistent with the Strateva et al. relation, SDSS J1434+0338 and POX 52 are clearly X-ray week. While broad absorption line quasars are often undetected in X-rays, and thus observed as X-ray faint, their intrinsic X-ray fluxes, corrected for absorption are usually similar to non-BALQSOs; 1996ApJ...462..637G ), X-ray weakness of the two AGNs with IMBHs is not due to intrinsic absorption as the _ ox values were derived after correctiong for the Galactic as well any intrinsic neutral/warm absorption. It is worth noting that POX 52 and J1434+0338 are only mildly X-ray weak.

gmw_00007867.name: Galaxy 1
gmw_00007867.overview: Galaxy 1 is a green-valley galaxy with a disk that lies almost on the resolved star-forming main sequence [arXiv:1710.08610]. It shows signs of a past interaction and post-merger state, which may have triggered both central star formation and active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2012.04772].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy 1 as the MaNGA green-valley target closest to the main sequence, with its disk almost on the resolved sequence and bulge only slightly below it.

quotes.quote: The three green valley targets (MaNGA 1-596678, 1-114956, and 1-596598) for the ALMA follow-up, highlighted by the color-coded stars, were drawn from the first 118 galaxies observed by MaNGA at the time when the ALMA proposal was prepared.

We number them 1 to 3 (hereafter Galaxy 1, Galaxy 2, and Galaxy 3) according to their sSFR(see Table 1). Although Galaxy 1 lies close to the lower edge of the star-forming main sequence on the global sSFR -- plane, all three galaxies are referred to as `green valley galaxies' loosely in this work.
For Galaxy 1, the disk almost lies on the resolved main sequence while the bulge is only slightly below the line.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 7977-12705 as a galaxy with central high-H EW composite spaxels and a retired-spaxel ring, noting past-interaction and post-merger signs that may connect to central star formation and AGN activity.

quotes.quote: The remaining two galaxies (7977-12705 and 7977-9101) have high H EWs in their central regions, with the retired spaxels arranged in a ring around. However, for both galaxies these central high H EW spaxels are not classified as star-forming as they are above the K03 demarcation, and would be traditionally be classified as `composite' as they are below the Kewley et al. (2001) boundary. One of these galaxies (7977-12705) shows signs of a past interaction, which may have triggered both central star formation and AGN activity (e.g. Ellison et al. 2013).
However (perhaps with the exception of 7977-12705, which is a post-merger), the retired rMGMS is clearly not a simple extension to higher \ of the star-forming rMGMS.

gmw_00007868.name: Galaxy 2
gmw_00007868.overview: Galaxy 2 is a green-valley galaxy [arXiv:1710.08610] that hosts older central spectra with LINER or AGN-like emission lines [arXiv:2112.03425]. Its bulge has a significantly lower gas fraction than its disk [arXiv:1710.08610], while its outer regions are star-forming and show signs of recent merger or accretion events [arXiv:2112.03425].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy 2 as a MaNGA green-valley target whose bulge departs strongly from the resolved main sequence and has significantly lower gas fraction than its disk.

quotes.quote: The three green valley targets (MaNGA 1-596678, 1-114956, and 1-596598) for the ALMA follow-up, highlighted by the color-coded stars, were drawn from the first 118 galaxies observed by MaNGA at the time when the ALMA proposal was prepared.

For Galaxy 1, the disk almost lies on the resolved main sequence while the bulge is only slightly below the line. On the other hand, the bulge of Galaxy 2 shows significant departure from the resolved main sequence.

On the other hand, for Galaxies 2 and 3, 2 is quite uniform across the bulges, despite the fact that the 2 correlates with in disks. The lower panel shows the median gas fraction as a function of in bulges (circles) and disks (stars). The gas fraction in the bulges varies significantly among the 3 galaxies by 1.6 dex, being lower toward the Galaxy 3. On the other hand, the gas fractions in disks are comparable in the three cases, although slightly lower in Galaxy 3. Except for Galaxy 1, in the bulges is significantly lower than in the disks for the other two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses MaNGA ID: 1-114956 to demonstrate DESOM fingerprints, finding older central LINER/AGN-like spectra, mixed intermediate-radius spectra, star-forming outer regions, and signs of episodic star formation or recent merger/accretion.

quotes.quote: As an example, we input the baxels from a selected galaxy (MaNGA ID: 1-114956) to the model and show their distribution onto the SOM in Figure fig:fig-som1-galaxy . Numbers above each prototype indicate the number of baxels that are best represented by that node, and the prototypes are colour-coded by their galactocentric radius in units of the half-light radius. This galaxy appears to have several significant groupings of baxels. The ones near node (11, 13) are in the centre of the galaxy with older spectra and LINER/AGN-like emission lines. The ones near (6,12) are at intermediate-to-large radii are a group with both older and younger spectra.

The group of baxels near (9, 2) are at mostly large radii and more strongly star-forming. Since these groupings of baxels are widely separated, this suggests an episodic star-formation history for this galaxy, or recent merger or accretion events.

gmw_00007869.name: Galaxy 3
gmw_00007869.overview: Galaxy 3 is a green-valley galaxy with central emission dominated by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1710.08610, arXiv:2401.05976]. Its bulge and disk regions lie systematically below the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:1710.08610], exhibiting a low bulge gas fraction and very few star-forming spaxels [arXiv:1710.08610, arXiv:2401.05976].

sources.summary: The paper presents Galaxy 3 as the green-valley target farthest below the main sequence, with AGN-dominated central emission, low bulge gas fraction, and possible AGN feedback reducing cold gas.

quotes.quote: We note that the central emission of Galaxy 3 is dominated by a broad emission lines associated with an AGN, and therefore the from is an upper limit in the central part of this galaxy. In spite of this, it is clear that both the bulge and disk regions of Galaxy 3 are systematically below the resolved main-sequence relation. There is also a trend that the disk sSFR declines from Galaxy 1 to Galaxy 3, following a similar behavior of the global sSFR.

For Galaxies 2 and 3, which are clearly below the star-forming main sequence, the gas fraction in the bulges is lower compared to that in the disks. The gas fraction in the bulges drops by 1.6 dex from Galaxy 1 to Galaxy 3.

As noted earlier, one of our three green valley galaxies, Galaxy 3, shows broad-line features and hence potentially hosts an AGN. It is consistent with the AGN feedback framework that the presence of AGN diminishes the available cold gas in the bulge and even in the disks.

sources.summary: The paper highlights 8081-12703 as a green-valley galaxy with unusually low HCN/HCO+ ratios, few star-forming spaxels, and central diagnostics consistent with composite or AGN activity.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, galaxy 8081-12703, one of the GV galaxies, has the spaxel distributions centered at HCN/ ratio 1, systematically lower than the values of other galaxies. About half the spaxels have the HCN/ ratio less than 1, indicating that this galaxy might be affected by either AGN or supernova explosions. The BPT diagnostics using the / vs. / ( / vs. / ) ratios reveal that the central spaxels are consistent with composite (star-forming + LINER regions) and may indicate the presence of central AGN. Nevertheless, this galaxy only gas very few star-forming spaxels (Figure fig:sfr-M ) that contribute to the dense gas SK relation of star-forming spaxels (Figure fig:sfr-M_all ).

gmw_00007870.name: NGC 6181
gmw_00007870.overview: NGC 6181 is classified as a Milky Way analog galaxy [arXiv:1705.06743]. It possesses a satellite luminosity function that is as steep as that of the Andromeda Galaxy, M31 [arXiv:1705.06743].

sources.summary: The SAGA paper treats NGC 6181 as its first Milky Way analog, reporting highly complete spectroscopy within its virial radius and a satellite luminosity function as steep as M31's.

quotes.quote: Complete Spectroscopy for NGC 6181 For the first Milky Way analog in the SAGA Survey, NGC 6181, we aimed to measure spectroscopic redshifts for galaxies in our survey region without imposing a color criterion. We obtained spectroscopy for the majority of objects classified by SDSS as galaxies (1580 out of 1850, 85 ) within the virial radius down to r_o < 20.75 . A more detailed discussion of data for this individual system will be in a forthcoming paper by Weiner et al. (in preparation). We plot spectroscopic completeness for the NGC 6181 sample as a function of band magnitude (black line, top panel fig_complete ), achieving greater than 80 completeness in any given magnitude bin.
In particular, NGC 6181 has a satellite luminosity function as steep as that of M31, which is about twice as massive as the Milky Way.

gmw_00007871.name: z=2.168 quasar
gmw_00007871.overview: The z=2.168 quasar is a high-redshift active galaxy [arXiv:1105.3524]. Narrowband observations of its surrounding volume have yielded a null detection of nearby emitters, suggesting that the quasar's intense ultraviolet radiation may suppress star formation in neighboring galaxies [arXiv:1105.3524].

sources.summary: The paper models a null narrowband detection around the z=2.168 quasar, arguing its ionizing environment raises detectable host-halo masses and may suppress nearby star formation.

quotes.quote: Narrowband observations have detected no emission within a 70 ^ 3 volume centered on the z=2.168 quasar . This is in contrast to surveys of emitters in the field at similar redshifts and flux limits, which indicate that tens of sources should be visible within the same volume. The observed difference indicates that the quasar environment has a significant influence on the observed density of emitters.

To quantify this effect we have constructed a semi-analytic model to simulate the effect of a luminous quasar on nearby emitters. We find the null detection around implies that the minimum isothermal temperature of emitter host halos is greater than 3.4 10^ 6 ( 68 level), corresponding to a virial mass of 1.2 10^ 12 . This indicates that the intense UV emission of the quasar may be suppressing the star formation in nearby galaxies.

gmw_00007872.name: QSO SDSS J0211-0009
gmw_00007872.overview: QSO SDSS J0211-0009 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=4.9 [arXiv:1105.3524]. It is surrounded by a filamentary structure of Lyman-break galaxies and a population of Lyman-alpha emitters that are suppressed in the quasar's immediate vicinity [arXiv:1105.3524].

sources.summary: The paper compares QSO SDSS J0211-0009 with its model, noting a wide-field survey found 221 emitters, filamentary Lyman-break structure, and suppressed emitters near the quasar.

quotes.quote: Kashikawa:2007p5789 conducted a deep wide field narrowband survey for Lyman break galaxies and s around QSO SDSS J0211-0009 at z=4.9 . They surveyed 830 ^ 3 in the vicinity of the quasar and detected 221 s. Our simulation predicted 260 detectable s for a similar volume, quasar, and redshift. Kashikawa:2007p5789 found that while the observed Lyman break galaxies formed a distributed filamentary structure which included the quasar, the s were preferentially clustered around the quasar while avoiding a vicinity of 4.5 from the quasar. This region was calculated to have a UV radiation field roughly 100 times that of the background, and this was posited to be suppressing the formation of s.

gmw_00007873.name: 3C 19
gmw_00007873.overview: 3C 19 is a radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0005196]. It is undetected in all ISOPHOT bands and is roughly a factor of two fainter than its paired quasar, 3C 334 [arXiv:astro-ph/0005196].

sources.summary: The paper describes radio galaxy 3C 19 as undetected in the ISOPHOT bands and clearly fainter than paired quasar 3C 334 by roughly a factor of two.

quotes.quote: In the pair 3C 334 and 3C 19, the radio galaxy is not detected in any band, whereas the quasar has three solid detections (lowest S/N is 6). Since the upper limits take the difference of the backgrounds into account, the radio galaxy is definitely fainter than its paired quasar.
Still, this is not enough to explain the difference between 3C 19 and 3C 334, given that their flux densities appear to differ by about a factor of two.

gmw_00007874.name: 3C 277.1
gmw_00007874.overview: 3C 277.1 is a compact steep-spectrum quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0005196, arXiv:astro-ph/0504330] classified as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy [arXiv:1504.02772]. It is hosted by a spiral galaxy with large tidal arms [arXiv:1504.02772] and contains very dusty gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0504330].

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 277.1 as a compact steep-spectrum quasar with comparable far-infrared output to 3C 67 and radio measurements complicated by lobe/core blending.

quotes.quote: In the 6 cm image of the compact 3C 277.1 the core is convolved with the lobe on the western side. Deconvolution with MAXFIT produces a peak flux of around 40 mJy, but since the lobe is not well approximated by a gaussian this value is to be taken as an upper limit. Instead we use the 6 cm literature value (Akujor et al. 1991) of 28 mJy.

In contrast, the pair consisting of quasar 3C 277.1 and radio galaxy 3C 67 has entirely comparable far-infrared output. Remarkably, this is the pair of Compact Steep Spectrum sources (CSS), objects believed to be young FRIIs where the radio emission is still confined within the host galaxy. Both objects are well detected, with S/N values around 5.

sources.summary: J1252+5634 is described as a Seyfert 1.5 in a spiral with large tidal arms, compact steep-spectrum radio structure, aligned emission-line gas, and shell-like gas morphology.

quotes.quote: J1252+5634 This is an Seyfert 1.5 galaxy hosted in a spiral galaxy with large tidal arms. It is a compact steep-spectrum object with a triple structure that shows emission-line gas aligned with the radio source . The gas structure forms a double shell-like morphology, with one lobe brighter and better defined than the other .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 277.1 as a compact steep spectrum quasar, finding precursor-gas dominated emission-line modeling and high nuclear values consistent with dusty gas.

quotes.quote: We are carrying out a study of the kinematics and ionization of the aligned emission line nebulae in three CSS radio sources: 3C 67 (galaxy, z=0.310 , linear size D=10.1 Kpc), 3C 277.1 (quasar, z=0.321 , D=6.9 Kpc), 3C 303.1 (galaxy, z=0.267 , D=6.2 Kpc) We adopt a Hubble constant of H_o = 75 km s \ Mpc \ and a deceleration parameter of q_o = 0.0 .
In 3C 277.1 , the data scatter around the model for 100 contribution to the luminosity from precursor gas.
In 3C 277.1 we also found high values for the nucleus (10.4 0.2) consistent with the measurements of CSS quasars by , who argued for very dusty gas in these objects.

gmw_00007875.name: 3C 67
gmw_00007875.overview: 3C 67 is a compact steep-spectrum radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.31 [arXiv:1301.1677, arXiv:astro-ph/0504330]. It features an aligned emission-line nebula [arXiv:astro-ph/0504330] and radio lobes that extend over kiloparsec scales [arXiv:1301.1677].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C67 as a compact steep-spectrum radio-galaxy/type 2 quasar whose lobes and optical line emission extend over kiloparsec scales.

quotes.quote: The third radio-loud target is 3C67 at z =0.311, a compact steep-spectrum source with a total extent of 3 \ and with lobes oriented at 173 ^ East of North in the 5.0 GHz radio band erac04 .
