The optical spectrum of this object is consistent with type 2 quasar classification spin06 , and its \ line luminosity is slightly lower than the majority of the other objects (see Table tab1 ). The HST /STIS long-slit spectroscopy finds the \ line emission to extend out to 2.7 kpc away from the nucleus odea02 . Not enough information is available for a direct comparison between the sensitivity of these observations and ours. We find general agreement between the orientation of the extended emission, while our detection of the faint \ emission extends further out, presumably because of better sensitivity.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 67 as a compact steep-spectrum radio galaxy, well detected in the far infrared and likely a broad-line radio galaxy rather than a standard narrow-line match.

quotes.quote: In contrast, the pair consisting of quasar 3C 277.1 and radio galaxy 3C 67 has entirely comparable far-infrared output. Remarkably, this is the pair of Compact Steep Spectrum sources (CSS), objects believed to be young FRIIs where the radio emission is still confined within the host galaxy. Both objects are well detected, with S/N values around 5.
It should be noted however, that 3C 67 shows a broad component in H , and hence should be classified as a broad-line radio galaxy.
3C 67 has been reported to have broad H (Eracleous Halpern 1994), but is otherwise in its spectral characteristics clearly different from strong quasars such as 3C 277.1.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 67 as a compact steep spectrum galaxy, measuring its emission-line nebula kinematics and finding modeled shock contributions plus possible nuclear photoionization.

quotes.quote: We are carrying out a study of the kinematics and ionization of the aligned emission line nebulae in three CSS radio sources: 3C 67 (galaxy, z=0.310 , linear size D=10.1 Kpc), 3C 277.1 (quasar, z=0.321 , D=6.9 Kpc), 3C 303.1 (galaxy, z=0.267 , D=6.2 Kpc) We adopt a Hubble constant of H_o = 75 km s \ Mpc \ and a deceleration parameter of q_o = 0.0 .
In 3C 67 , the data lie between the MAPPINGS models for a contribution to the observed luminosity from shocked gas ranging from 0 to 50 .
We find that the nucleus apparently produces enough ionizing photons to power the emission line luminosity in 3C 277.1 and possibly 3C 67, but not 3C 303.1 .

gmw_00007876.name: REBELS-19
gmw_00007876.overview: REBELS-19 is a low-mass galaxy located at a high redshift of z ~ 7 [arXiv:2202.11118]. It is an outlier for its stellar mass, exhibiting a high dust mass and a dust-to-stellar mass ratio up to 18 times higher than predicted by the fiducial model [arXiv:2202.11118].

sources.summary: The paper identifies REBELS-19 as a low-mass z ~ 7 outlier with dust-to-stellar mass ratios far above the fiducial model, challenging grain-growth explanations.

quotes.quote: However, 2 of the lowest-mass galaxies (REBELS-19 and 39), with 8.7 < log (M_*/ ) < 9 , are clear outliers. These show dust masses and dust-to-stellar mass ratios ( 0.94-1.1 ) that are up to 18 higher for a given stellar mass as compared to the fiducial model (see also Table table2 ); REBELS-14 is the third of the lowest-mass galaxies (reference stellar mass of 10^ 8.73 ) that is only consistent with the fiducial model if its stellar mass lies at the observationally-inferred upper limit of M_* 10^ 9.7 . Matching REBELS-19 and 39 within error bars requires scenarios that lie between the ``no dust ejection" and ``maximal dust mass" models; the lowest-mass galaxy (REBELS-39) has a dust mass that lies between the ``production only" and ``maximal dust mass" models, even if its stellar mass is assumed to be at the upper limit predicted by observations; the above results remain qualitatively unchanged even if the higher stellar masses inferred by Topping et al. 2022 (in prep.) are used. Indeed, even ignoring any dust loss in astration, destruction and ejection, explaining the dust masses of these systems would require each SNII to produce 1.25-1.5 of dust (after SN shock processing; assuming a Salpeter 0.-100 IMF) which is higher than any observational estimate so far; using the stellar masses from Topping et al. 2022 (in prep.) would require each SNII to produce lower dust masses of 0.57-0.74 , bringing them into agreement with observed SN dust yields (ranging between 0.03-1.1 ).

gmw_00007877.name: REBELS-39
gmw_00007877.overview: REBELS-39 is an extremely ultraviolet-luminous galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.85 [arXiv:2005.02402, arXiv:2202.01219]. It exhibits strong nebular-line emission from a recent burst of star formation [arXiv:2311.16071], a broad Lyman-alpha profile [arXiv:2202.01219], and a high dust-to-stellar mass ratio [arXiv:2202.11118].

sources.summary: COS-862541 is a bright z = 6.850 galaxy with extreme IRAC colour, very large inferred [OIII]+H emission, detected line emission, and exceptionally high ionizing-photon production.

quotes.quote: Our first set of observations included only one candidate, COS-862541. We prioritized COS-862541 due to its bright UV luminosity ( J = 24.45) and very blue [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] colour of 1.33 ^ +0.26 _ 0.32 , suggesting an extreme rest-frame [ OIII ] +H EW 2250 (see Table tab:galaxy_properties ). We show postage stamp images of COS-862541 in the top panel of Fig. fig:BEAGLE_fit_COS-862541 where it is clearly seen to be a strong nb921 dropout possessing a very strong [ 3.6 ] excess. We list the full optical through mid-infrared photometry for COS-862541 in Table tab:COS-862541_photometry . We observed COS-862541 using the MMT/Binospec spectograph Fabricant2019 on the night of 13 December 2018 for 2.0 hours (eight 900 second exposures).

The MMT/Binospec observations revealed a highly significant asymmetric feature at 9543 which we identify as (see Fig. fig:COS-862541_Binospec_Spectrum ). We measure a line flux of 20.0 1.9 10^ 18 erg/s/cm ^2 and calculate the redshift to be = 6.850 using the location of peak line flux.

Because [ OIII ] EW has been shown to strongly correlate with Lyman continuum production efficiency (at least at z 3; Chevallard2018_z0 ; Tang2019 ; Emami2020 ; Nakajima2020 ), this suggests that COS-862541 is one of the largest producers of ionizing photons in the reionization era.

sources.summary: REBELS-39 is an extremely UV-luminous z=6.845 COSMOS galaxy with very large inferred EW, confident Ly detection, low escape fraction, and an extremely broad profile.

quotes.quote: REBELS-39 REBELS-39 is an extremely UV-luminous ( = -22.7 ) galaxy in the COSMOS field at = 6.845 ( Bouwens2021_REBELS ; Schouws et al. in prep) and was identified as COS-862541 in Endsley2021_OIII,Endsley2021_LyA . This source has an inferred stellar mass of = 8.7 and a very blue IRAC color ( [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] = 1.32^ +0.27 _ 0.34 ). The IRAC color suggests an extremely large EW of 3250 given that this galaxy lies at a redshift where [ OIII ] 5007 is only slightly transmitting through the [ 3.6 ] filter. The Binospec data of REBELS-39 reveal a confident (15.3 ) detection with a peak wavelength of 9542.3 corresponding to = 6.849 (Fig. fig:LyA_Detections ). We measure a total Ly flux of (15.6 1.3) 10 ^ 18 erg/s/cm ^2 , indicating an EW of 10.0 1.7 using the VIRCam Y band photometry for the continuum flux density (0.61 0.12 Jy).
Similar to REBELS-14, the profile of REBELS-39 is extremely broad with FWHM=640 ^ +60 _ 40 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes REBELS-39 as the lowest-mass REBELS outlier, with dust mass between production-only and maximal models even under high stellar-mass assumptions.

quotes.quote: However, 2 of the lowest-mass galaxies (REBELS-19 and 39), with 8.7 < log (M_*/ ) < 9 , are clear outliers. These show dust masses and dust-to-stellar mass ratios ( 0.94-1.1 ) that are up to 18 higher for a given stellar mass as compared to the fiducial model (see also Table table2 ); REBELS-14 is the third of the lowest-mass galaxies (reference stellar mass of 10^ 8.73 ) that is only consistent with the fiducial model if its stellar mass lies at the observationally-inferred upper limit of M_* 10^ 9.7 . Matching REBELS-19 and 39 within error bars requires scenarios that lie between the ``no dust ejection" and ``maximal dust mass" models; the lowest-mass galaxy (REBELS-39) has a dust mass that lies between the ``production only" and ``maximal dust mass" models, even if its stellar mass is assumed to be at the upper limit predicted by observations; the above results remain qualitatively unchanged even if the higher stellar masses inferred by Topping et al. 2022 (in prep.) are used. Indeed, even ignoring any dust loss in astration, destruction and ejection, explaining the dust masses of these systems would require each SNII to produce 1.25-1.5 of dust (after SN shock processing; assuming a Salpeter 0.-100 IMF) which is higher than any observational estimate so far; using the stellar masses from Topping et al. 2022 (in prep.) would require each SNII to produce lower dust masses of 0.57-0.74 , bringing them into agreement with observed SN dust yields (ranging between 0.03-1.1 ).

sources.summary: The paper argues REBELS-39 may have underestimated stellar mass: HST shows strong nebular-line emission from a recent burst, while [C II] kinematics imply a very large dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: In fact, Hubble Space Telescope (HST) counterparts of REBELS-14 and REBELS-39 (which are part of the low-mass subsample) show very strong emission by rest-frame optical nebular lines ( Bowler17 ), which can be explained only by the presence of a very recent, large burst of star formation. In turn, this can limit the ability of non-parametric SFHs to overcome the so-called outshining problem, i.e. the underestimation of the contribution of older stellar populations due to overwhelming light form recent bursts (e.g. Leja19 ).

The hypothesis of a stellar mass underestimation is also supported by the very large dynamical mass estimated from the [ C ii ] line width and size for these estimates, the rotation-dominated regime as described in DeCarli18 is adopted. (see Schouws et al., in prep; Topping22 ) of REBELS-39 ( >10^ 11 M_ ). The derived stellar mass for this galaxy contributes only to 1 of the total dynamical mass within the [ C ii ] emitting region, therefore leaving room for the presence of older stellar populations, significantly enhancing the total stellar mass.

gmw_00007878.name: PKS 0743 67
gmw_00007878.overview: PKS 0743 67 is a quasar powered by a central engine that drives galaxy jets with high kinetic luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/0510044]. The quasar features an ultra-luminous accretion disk with near-Eddington accretion, indicating that high accretion luminosity does not suppress its jet power [arXiv:astro-ph/0510044].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0743 67 as a quasar with enormous jet kinetic luminosity, an ultra-luminous accretion disk, near-Eddington accretion, and evidence that high accretion luminosity does not suppress jet power.

quotes.quote: In this letter, deep radio observations of the quasar PKS 0743 67 are presented that reveal a central engine capable of driving jets with enormous kinetic luminosity, Q>4.1 10^ 46 ergs/s . This result is significant because archival optical spectral data indicates that the accretion disk has a thermal luminosity, L_ bol >2 10^ 47 ergs/s . Furthermore, estimates of the central black hole mass from line widths indicate that L_ bol /L_ Edd 1 . This suggests that neither a large L_ bol nor L_ bol /L_ Edd suppresses jet power in quasars, despite claims that they do in the recent literature.

gmw_00007879.name: FORJ0332-3557
gmw_00007879.overview: FORJ0332-3557 is a gravitationally lensed Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of z=3.77 [arXiv:0803.0679]. It hosts a very young starburst containing Wolf-Rayet stars, showing sub-solar gas abundances, dust extinction, and strong outflows [arXiv:0803.0679].

sources.summary: The paper describes FORJ0332-3557 as a lensed z=3.77 Lyman-break galaxy with young stars, strong outflows, Wolf-Rayet emission, sub-solar ionized-gas abundances, and substantial dust-corrected star formation.

quotes.quote: We recently reported the discovery of FORJ0332-3557, a lensed Lyman-break galaxy at z=3.77 in a remarkable example of strong galaxy-galaxy gravitational lensing. We present here a medium-resolution rest-frame UV spectrum of the source, which appears to be similar to the well-known Lyman-break galaxy MS1512-cB58 at z=2.73 . The spectral energy distribution is consistent with a stellar population of less than 30 Ma, with an extinction of A_V=0.5 mag and an extinction-corrected star formation rate SFR _ UV of 200--300 70m1 a^ 1 .

The Lyman- line exhibits a damped profile in absorption produced by a column density of about N_ HI = (2.5 1.0) 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , superimposed on an emission line shifted both spatially (0 5 with respect to the UV continuum source) and in velocity space ( +830 with respect to the low-ionisation absorption lines from its interstellar medium), a clear signature of outflows with an expansion velocity of about 270 . A strong emission line from 164.04 nm indicates the presence of Wolf-Rayet stars and reinforces the interpretation of a very young starburst. The metallic lines indicate sub-solar abundances of elements Si, Al, and C in the ionised gas phase.

gmw_00007880.name: Mrk 409
gmw_00007880.overview: Mrk 409 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0911.2344] that hosts a Seyfert 2-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.08111] and exhibits regular ionized-gas rotation [arXiv:0911.2344]. The galaxy features a central starburst region surrounded by an outer star-forming ring [arXiv:0911.2344, arXiv:1802.08111], which is likely triggered by an expanding, starburst-driven superbubble [arXiv:0911.2344, arXiv:1802.08111].

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 409's central starburst, ionized-gas ring, young stellar component, probable low-luminosity AGN, flat oxygen abundance pattern, and regular ionized-gas rotation.

quotes.quote: We present an integral field spectroscopic study of the central 2 2 kpc ^2 of the blue compact dwarf galaxy Mrk 409, observed with the Potsdam MultiAperture Spectrophotometer . This study focuses on the morphology, two-dimensional chemical abundance pattern, excitation properties and kinematics of the ionized interstellar medium in the starburst component. We also investigate the nature of the extended ring of ionized gas emission surrounding the bright nuclear starburst region of Mrk 409. PMAS spectra of selected regions along the ring, interpreted with evolutionary and population synthesis models, indicate that their ionized emission is mainly due to a young stellar population with a total mass of 1.5 10^6 , which started forming almost coevally 10 Myr ago. This stellar component is likely confined to the collisional interface of a spherically expanding, starburst-driven super-bubble with denser, swept-up ambient gas, 600 pc away from the central starburst nucleus.

The spectroscopic properties of the latter imply a large extinction ( >0.9 ), and the presence of an additional non-thermal ionization source, most likely a low-luminosity Active Galactic Nucleus. Mrk 409 shows a relatively large oxygen abundance ( 12+ ( O/H ) 8.4 ) and no chemical abundance gradients out to R 600 pc. The ionized gas kinematics displays an overall regular rotation on a northwest-southwest axis, with a maximum velocity of 60 ; the total mass inside the star-forming ring is about 1.4 10^9 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes J0949+3213/Mrk 409 as a UV-excess Markarian galaxy formerly classified as starburst, but whose SDSS spectrum strongly indicates Seyfert nuclear activity.

quotes.quote: J0949+3213 : This object, also known as NGC 3011 and (more noteworthy) Mrk 409, is included in the Markarian survey of ultraviolet-excess galaxies. Interestingly, it has been classified repeatedly as a starburst galaxy in the literature (e.g., Balzano 1983; Salzer et al. \ 1995). Indeed, the object has a prominent blue star-forming ring located just outside the nucleus (Gil de Paz et al. \ 2003; see our Fig. 4). However, the SDSS spectrum leaves no question about the nature of the nuclear activity of this galaxy. In addition to narrow emission-line flux ratios that place it well within the Seyfert regions on the diagnostic diagrams in Figure 5 (particularly on the [ O 1 ] /H plot), the object has strong He 2 4686 emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 409 as an AGN-bearing source with central star formation, an outer star-forming ring likely driven by a superbubble, HeII emission, and Seyfert 2-type activity.

quotes.quote: Mrk 409 (Fig. Fig:lgoh12_lgsfr_agn , second row) shows a central star-forming component and an outer ring of star formation, likely triggered by an expanding starburst-driven superbubble Cairos2009-mrk409 . Although there is no radio nor X-ray confirmation, the central source is associated with HeII 4686 emission, and with a Seyfert 2-type AGN 2014AJ....148..136M . The BPT diagram of this galaxy provides evidence of an AGN exciting the emission-line spectrum.

gmw_00007881.name: the associated galaxy
gmw_00007881.overview: The galaxy associated with the damped Ly-̑ absorber B1228-113 at z ≈ 2.19 is a dusty, high-redshift system [arXiv:1803.05914]. It exhibits a large molecular gas mass [arXiv:2206.13518, arXiv:1803.05914], near-thermal CO excitation [arXiv:2206.13518], and a double-horned CO line profile that suggests rotation [arXiv:2009.04001].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(1-0) and rest-frame NUV emission from DLA B1228-113, derives a high molecular gas mass, and finds near-thermal CO excitation with significant dust extinction.

quotes.quote: We detect CO(1--0) emission from DLA B1228-113 at z 2.1933 and DLA J0918+1636 at z 2.5848 ; these are the first detections of CO(1--0) emission in high- z H i selected galaxies. We obtain high molecular gas masses, M_ mol 10^ 11 ( _ CO /4.36) \ M_ , for the two objects with CO(1--0) detections, which are a factor of 1.5-2 lower than earlier estimates. We determine the excitation of the mid J CO rotational levels relative to the J=1 level, _ J1 , in H i selected galaxies for the first time, obtaining _ 31 =1.00 0.20 and _ 41 =1.03 0.23 for DLA J0918+1636, and _ 31 =0.86 0.21 for DLA B1228-113.

The CO(1--0) line luminosities yield molecular gas masses of (10.0 2.4) ( /4.36) 10^ 10 \ (DLA B1228-113), (11.5 2.2) ( /4.36) 10^ 10 \ (DLA J0918+1636) and < 5.0 ( /4.36) 10^ 10 \ ( 3 limit; DLA B0551-366), where we have assumed = 4.36 \ Bolatto13,Tacconi20 . Both DLA J0918+1636 and DLA B1228-113 are detected in HST rest-frame NUV imaging. The HST images of DLA J0918+1636 are presented and described in Fynbo18 , and yield a half-light radius of 0 30 (Fynbo, private communication). In the case of DLA B1228-113, the rest-frame NUV emission is detected in our HST WFC3 F105W image (see Fig. fig:images A ), at RA=12h30m55.44s, Dec.=-11 ^ 39 05.87 , at an offset of 0 9 ( 7.7 kpc) from the CO(3--2) emission.
We find thermal excitation of the J=3, 4 rotational levels in the z = 2.5848 galaxy DLA J0918+1636, and near-thermal excitation of the J=3 level in the z = 2.1933 galaxy DLA B1228-113.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the associated galaxy as a dusty, high-redshift DLA counterpart with CO and H emission, large molecular mass, high star formation, and a 30 kpc impact parameter.

quotes.quote: Using the Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-millimeter Array, we have detected CO(3 2) line and far-infrared continuum emission from a galaxy associated with a high-metallicity ( [ M/H ] = 0.27 ) damped Ly- absorber (DLA) at z_ DLA =2.19289 . The galaxy is located 3.5'' away from the quasar sightline, corresponding to a large impact parameter of 30 kpc at the DLA redshift. We use archival Very Large Telescope-SINFONI data to detect H emission from the associated galaxy, and find that the object is dusty, with a dust-corrected star formation rate of 110^ +60 _ 30 M _ yr ^ 1 . The galaxy's molecular mass is large, M _ mol = (1.4 0.2) 10^ 11 ( _ CO /4.3) (0.57/r_ 31 ) M _ , supporting the hypothesis that high-metallicity DLAs arise predominantly near massive galaxies. The excellent agreement in redshift between the CO(3 2) line emission and low-ion metal absorption ( 40 ) disfavors scenarios whereby the gas probed by the DLA shows bulk motion around the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports a CO-emitting DLA galaxy in the B1228-113 field, with a double-horned CO profile suggestive of rotation and weak H emission found after the CO position was known.

quotes.quote: Line emission was clearly detected (at > 5 significance) from galaxies in five DLA fields, B0551-366 at z = 1.9622 , J0016-0012 at z=1.9730 , J2225+0527 at z = 2.1310 , B1228-113 at z = 2.1929 , and J0918+1636 at z = 2.5832 , at velocities in good agreement with those of the low-ionization metal absorption lines.

Finally, the large FWHMs of the detected CO lines ( 325-600 ) are suggestive of rotation; for the z = 2.1929 DLA galaxy towards B1228-113, this is supported by the ``double-horned'' nature of the CO line profile (see Fig. fig:fig2 D ).

Conversely, the three galaxies that showed CO emission (B1228-113, J2225+0527, and J0918+1636) were not identified in the optical or near-IR spectroscopy: the galaxy in the field of B1228-113 was found to show weak H emission after its CO position was known neeleman18 , while the galaxy in the field of J2225+0527 was earlier tentatively detected in a near-IR image krogager16 , but without a confirming redshift.

gmw_00007882.overview: The BL Lacertae object 1147+245 exhibits a classical triple radio structure at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:0711.1158] and displays relatively low optical variability [arXiv:0711.1158]. Its radio jet extends westward on milliarcsecond scales [arXiv:astro-ph/0111279, arXiv:0711.1158] before bending sharply southward and southeastward on larger scales, forming a structure compatible with a helical jet [arXiv:astro-ph/0111279].

sources.summary: The paper traces 1147+245 from a westward VLBI jet to a MERLIN component and VLA-scale bends, favoring projected curvature and noting a structure compatible with a helical jet.

quotes.quote: 1147+245 : this source has a PA=80 , and the jet at the VLBI scale (Fig. 1147e ) is extended about 45 mas towards the West. In the MERLIN image (Fig. 1147m ) a secondary component is present at about 150 mas from the core in the SW direction, at an angle of about 50 from the milliarcsecond-scale jet and 30 from the arcsecond-scale jet. There is no trace of the bending region between the pc-scale jet and the component on the MERLIN scale. The change of direction could be either due to a shock in the interstellar medium or to the passage through a region where the magnetic field changes direction abruptly (Koide, 1997), or, finally, to a curvature of the jet enhanced by projection effects. Even if we cannot exclude the first two hypotheses, we favour the last one, because of the absence, between the pc scale jet and the secondary component in the EVN+MERLIN image (Fig. ) of a bright region near to the hypothetical shock in which the jet should dissipate energy and perhaps lose collimation.

The 8.4-GHz VLA image shows that the jet continues to the South then bends sharply toward the SE direction. In the 1.36-GHz VLA image we finally see a double structure with wide jets or maybe lobes where the southern emitting region appears to bend slightly towards the SE direction. The structure is compatible with a helical jet and the abrupt change of direction in the VLBI and VLA 8.4 GHz images would be due to the projection of the helicoid on the plane of the sky.

sources.summary: The paper emphasizes 1147+245's unusual triple radio structure and low optical variability, then reports a westward VLBI jet with polarization throughout much of the jet.

quotes.quote: 1147+245 This source has two remarkable properties that are quite unusual for BL Lac objects: the radio structure at 1.4 GHz consists of a classical triple source AntonucciUlv85 , and the optical variability is rather small, with a total amplitude of only 0.77 mag Pica88 . The kpc-scale radio emission extends roughly in the north--south direction. The previous 5 GHz VLBP image of GabuzdaPushkarevCawthorne99 shows the VLBI jet extending to the west, misaligned with the large-scale structure. Only weak polarization was detected from the jet, corresponding to a transverse B field. In our new VLBI image (Fig.  fig:1133 ), polarization is detected throughout the jet, with the vectors in the inner jet at and beyond 15 mas from the core being aligned with the jet, indicating transverse B fields.

gmw_00007883.name: J1728+0427
gmw_00007883.overview: J1728+0427, also identified as PKS 1725+044, is characterized by a host galaxy [arXiv:2012.00008]. Gaia data reveals a nearby source located 1.6 arcseconds from the core that could potentially affect polarization measurements [arXiv:2012.00008].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1728+0427 with PKS 1725+044, notes its prominent host and a nearby Gaia source, and measures the nucleus with a fixed aperture because polarization could be affected.

quotes.quote: J1728+0427: PKS 1725+044 has a prominent host galaxy. There is also a nearby source located 1.6 arcsec from the core according to Gaia data. It could potentially affect the polarization. We measured the nucleus with a fixed 4 arcsec aperture.

gmw_00007884.name: J1743+1935
gmw_00007884.overview: J1743+1935 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2012.00008] classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2210.09797, arXiv:2311.12695] that features a host galaxy [arXiv:2012.00008] and an eastward-aligned radio jet [arXiv:0711.1158]. The source exhibits X-ray flux variations over long observation periods [arXiv:2311.12695], though it shows very little variability in optical and radio bands [arXiv:1606.06431].

sources.summary: The paper reproduces 1ES 1741+196 with a one-zone pp model, finding leptonic dominance and only a highest-energy TeV hadronic contribution suppressed below LHAASO sensitivity by EBL absorption.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1741+196 1ES 1741+196 is discovered as a VHE emitter by MAGIC. The observation results of MAGIC and other wavebands are provided by Ref.  2017MNRAS.468.1534A . No significant variabilities are found in the multi-wavelength light curves.

In Figure  1741 , we reproduce its SED with the one-zone pp model. Similar to the fitting result of RGB J0152+017, the multi-wavelength emission is dominated by the leptonic processes, and hadronic emission only has a contribution on the highest energy TeV data point, but does not exceed the one-year sensitivity of LHAASO because of the severe EBL absorption.

sources.summary: The paper studies 1ES 1741+196 as an extreme-synchrotron blazar candidate, using multiple AstroSat spectral states and reporting hints of extreme synchrotron nature plus significant long-term X-ray flux variations.

quotes.quote: This paper reports on recent observations carried out using \ and \ of five sources: 1ES 0120+340 (redshift z=0.272 ), RGB J0710+591 ( z=0.125 ), 1ES 1101-232 ( z=0.186 ), 1ES 1741+196 ( z=0.084 ), and 1ES 2322-409 ( z=0.1736 ), each displaying a unique range of spectral characteristics. Among these, RGB J0710+591 and 1ES 1101-232 are well-known for being extreme TeV sources with hard intrinsic TeV spectra. Although TeV data is unavailable for 1ES 0120+340, it presents itself as a potential \ candidate with hard X-ray and GeV spectra. Additionally, 1ES 1741+196 demonstrates hints of extreme Syn nature in its XRT spectrum, while 1ES 2322-409 appears as a standard HBL.

We analysed simultaneous SXT and LAXPC spectra from one single pointing observation each for RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 2322-409, 2 pointings for 1ES 0120+340, and 3 pointings for 1ES 1741+196 (as shown in Table table1 ). A1, A2 and A3 denote different X-ray spectral states for the sources 1ES 0120+340 and 1ES 1741+196, respectively, used for spectral analysis.
For 1ES 1741+196, we observe significant X-ray flux variations over a long observation period from 2010/07 -- 2020/10.

sources.summary: The paper says 1741+196 has aligned VLA, VLBA, and VLBI eastward jet structure, with polarization detected in the core and two jet components.

quotes.quote: 1741+196 VLA snapshot observations of this source see Perlman96 show a jet in PA 90 . The 5 GHz VLBA image of Rector03 shows a collimated straight jet extending to the east in PA =86 , well aligned with the VLA jet. Our VLBI map (Fig.  fig:1743 ) clearly shows a well-defined straight jet extending in PA 90 . Polarization was detected in the core and two jet components, but the vectors bear no obvious relationship to the direction of the jet structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 1741+196 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1741+196 : The source is close to the median flux density of the sample in both optical and radio band, but shows very little variability. In the optical the modulation index is the smallest of the sample and in radio among the smallest of the sample together with Mrk 501 and HB89 0317+185. Visually, there seems to be an increasing trend in the end of the radio light curve, and almost a 3 peak in the DCF with a delay of 250 days (see Fig. Fig:lc27 ). polynomial subtraction does not decrease the rms of the optical light curve.

sources.summary: The paper notes J1743+1935 is an AGN with a prominent host and nearby Gaia source, so possible polarization contamination was handled with fixed-aperture nuclear photometry.

quotes.quote: J1743+1935: This AGN has a prominent host galaxy. There is also a nearby source located 3.4 arcsec from the core according to Gaia data. It could potentially affect the polarization. We measured the nucleus with a fixed 5 arcsec aperture.

gmw_00007885.name: J2033+2146
gmw_00007885.overview: J2033+2146 is an active galactic nucleus that possesses a host galaxy [arXiv:2012.00008]. A nearby foreground star is located 2.3 arcseconds from its core according to Gaia data [arXiv:2012.00008].

sources.summary: The paper says J2033+2146 is an AGN with a prominent host and nearby foreground star, both relevant to polarization contamination handled with fixed-aperture nuclear photometry.

quotes.quote: J2033+2146: This AGN has a prominent host galaxy. There is also a nearby foreground star located 2.3 arcsec from the core according to Gaia data. It could potentially affect the polarization. We measured the nucleus with a fixed 4 arcsec aperture.

gmw_00007886.name: IC 0225
gmw_00007886.overview: IC 0225 is an isolated dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1105.4556, arXiv:1605.03991] that features a rare blue core [arXiv:1605.03991]. The galaxy exhibits low-excitation spectra [arXiv:1105.4556], Wolf-Rayet emission [arXiv:1605.03991], and a high nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio [arXiv:1105.4556].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J022628.28+010937.7 as an isolated low-redshift dwarf selected for an apparent 0.37 dex metallicity excess, then argues the new spectra favor ordinary oxygen abundance with high N/O and low excitation.

quotes.quote: For SDSS J022628.28+010937.7, SDSS J024121.80+000329.2, SDSS J082639.19+253553.5, and SDSS J082633.77+252959.2 respectively (hereafter abbreviated as SDSS- plus the first six digits of the Right Ascension), these oxygen abundances lie 0.37, 0.40, 0.55, and 0.55 dex above (and well outside the 0.1 dex scatter of) the luminosity-metallicity relationship histogram medians presented by P08. Like all of the objects in the P08 parent sample, these four galaxies are fairly isolated Note that J082639.19+253553.5 and J082633.77+252959.2 are members of the same group (Peeples, private communication, 2010). , low-redshift ( 0.0051<z<0.0227 ) dwarfs ( 17< M _ B <-15 ), with no obvious companions and somewhat depressed star formation rates (P08).

Our new observations extend the wavelength coverage to include the [ O 2 ] 3726,3729 doublet, which adds leverage in oxygen abundance estimates and allows measurements of N/O ratios. All four spectra are low excitation, with relatively high N/O ratios ( N/O 0.10 ), each of which tend to bias estimates based on strong emission lines toward high oxygen abundances. These spectra all fall in a regime where the ``standard'' strong line methods for metallicity determinations are not well calibrated either empirically or by photoionization modeling. By comparing our spectra directly to photoionization models, we estimate oxygen abundances in the range of 7.9 12 + (O/H) 8.4 , consistent with the scatter of the mass-metallicity relation.

sources.summary: The paper reports WR emission in dwarf elliptical IC 0225, notes its rare blue core, and uses R1 as a low-significance model-dependent WR case.

quotes.quote: It is surprising to find WR emission in an isolated Sa galaxy (NGC 1056), with low disc star formation activity than later type galaxies Giuricin94,Plauchu-Frayn12 , and in a dwarf elliptical (IC 0225; also detected in B08a, WR346). In fact, this dwarf galaxy is a peculiar object showing a blue core, something which is quite rare in this kind of objects Gu06,Miller08 .
This happens, for instance, in R1 in IC 0225 .

gmw_00007887.name: UGC 6320
gmw_00007887.overview: UGC 6320 is a galaxy containing Wolf-Rayet star-forming regions [arXiv:1605.03991]. In one of these regions, designated R2, the ionizing flux emerging from the Wolf-Rayet population accounts for 30 percent of the total Lyman-continuum budget [arXiv:1605.03991].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 6320 R1 as a noisy WR-region case needing renormalized O-star estimates, while R2 still has 30% WR Lyman-continuum contribution.

quotes.quote: The median values in each simulation of N _ O and N _ WR / N _ O were the same to those obtained applying Eq. eq:no directly to the derived average values of its elements, except for R1 in UGC 6320 . Taking advantage of the Montecarlo simulation, we realized that in this case a non-negligible percentage of estimates for N _ O were negative, which indicates that we were close to the noise level to obtain a physical result. Using the Bayessian approach we restricted the distribution considering only positive N _ O and then renormalized this new distribution with only physical values. We thereby obtained a more representative value for N _ O by taking the median of the renormalized distribution, and which is reported in the table.

Even in those cases where the number of WR stars is almost negligible compared to that of O stars (around 4 , in NGC 5954 and in R2 in UGC 6320 ), the ionising flux emerging from WR populations accounts for 30 of the total Lyman Continuum budget.

gmw_00007888.name: NGC 5665
gmw_00007888.overview: NGC 5665 is a star-forming galaxy that hosts Wolf-Rayet regions [arXiv:1605.03991]. These Wolf-Rayet regions are distributed in its circumnuclear areas, located within external clumps, or situated inside diffuse emission regions [arXiv:1605.03991].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5665 as a WR host with external clumps or diffuse-emission regions, including R4 and unexpectedly strong EW(4650) in R6.

quotes.quote: Not all the WR regions are located in the centre of the galaxies, but rather they are distributed in the circumnuclear regions or found in external clumps (e.g. NGC 5665 ).
In some cases, the WR regions are not apparently associated with an clump or a like region, but rather located within the diffuse emission areas (e.g., R4 in NGC 5665 ).
We even find some unexpected cases (e.g., R1 in NGC 3994, R6 in NGC 5665, R1 and R2 in UGC 00312) where EW( 4650) > 2 EW( 4686) , and where the percentage is higher than 40 .

gmw_00007889.name: NGC 3773
gmw_00007889.overview: NGC 3773 is a nearby dwarf galaxy located approximately 18 Mpc away [arXiv:2402.02107]. It features a blue nuclear region with active star formation surrounded by a redder stellar ring [arXiv:2402.02107], and it hosts a high-significance Wolf-Rayet region [arXiv:1605.03991].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3773 as a nearby dwarf in the UVIT sample with blue nuclear emission, a redder ring, central star formation, and possible feedback.

quotes.quote: NGC 3773 is the only galaxy that shows a bluer color at the center and has a redder ring around it (Figure fig3773 : lower left and middle). The variation of color in our sample galaxies, from inward to outwards implies the following (i) the central disk has more old stars than young stars, (ii) these galaxies are in a merger phase where the star formation is happening predominantly in the outskirts, (iii) the dust attenuation varies throughout the galaxy.

Only in NGC 3773 which is 18 Mpc away, we found a redder stellar ring around a blue nuclear emission. In NGC 3773, the radial profile shows a value close to zero (redder) at the center and then it goes down to 0.2 (bluer) followed by rising to 0.6 (redder). Although we can not see any variation in the blue nuclei itself (and it is hard to explain 0 to 0.2 ) and there is a small difference in color to draw any conclusions (unlike in NGC 7252), the blue nuclei and red ring may indicate that the center has more recent SF considering the calculated extinction is very small in this galaxy. As previously mentioned, linden2020 state that their sample including NGC 3773 is producing stars in the central 500 pc regions.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3773 R1 as a high-significance WR region, with continuum-improved blue-bump measurements and an attempted but uncertain 5696 emission fit.

quotes.quote: For instance, for the spectrum of R1 in NGC 3773 , is higher than _ orig by a factor of 2, but this increase practically comes from the improvement of the continuum emission.

We tried to include a fit to the 5696 emission line, which is mainly originated in carbon WR subtypes, but given the noisy residual spectrum, even in the case of NGC 3773 (see Fig. fig:rb_fits ), no successful fit could be achieved with an uncertainty of less than 50 .
Note that this does not affect the WR regions with the highest detection significance since their aperture is similar to that of the associated clump (e.g., in NGC 3353 , NGC 3773 , NGC 3991 ).

gmw_00007890.name: host of GRB 070306
gmw_00007890.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 070306 is a massive, mildly red galaxy at a redshift of z=1.496 [arXiv:1108.0674, arXiv:1407.4456]. It is a resolved, heavily obscured star-forming galaxy that shows some asymmetry and has two nearby fainter companions [arXiv:1407.4456].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB070306 host as faint in FIR, marginally detected by ALMA, and best fit without AGN/radio components at moderate SFR and IR luminosity.

quotes.quote: The GRB070306 host galaxy is faint in the rest-frame FIR and marginally detected with ALMA. We performed SED fittings for the GRB070306 host galaxy. The physical properties are shown in Table tab10 including parameters from different model settings. In all cases, the Chabrier2003 IMF performs better than the Salpeter1955 IMF. The reduced ^2 values are almost the same for all configurations, but the smallest reduced ^ 2 value was attained in the model without AGN and radio component, which is 2.324.

Physical parameters of the best-fit template are SFR_ SED =38 2 M_ yr ^ 1 , M_ * =(3.05 0.44) 10^ 10 M_ , and L_ IR =(2.59 0.26) 10^ 11 L_ . The stellar masses, SFRs and IR luminosities derived from all the four conditions are consistent within errors. The derived IR luminosities for the four settings correspond to SFR _ Kennicutt =44 to 46 M_ yr ^ 1 assuming the Kennicutt's law. The best-fit template indicates SFR _ Kennicutt =45 M_ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The host of GRB 070306 is described as a bright, mildly red z=1.496 galaxy with a 4000 break, high stellar mass, and moderate star formation.

quotes.quote: Host properties The host of GRB 070306 The galaxy hosting the strongly extinguished GRB 070306 at z=1.496 was previously discussed in 2008ApJ...681..453J . In addition to the public VLT imaging data (FORS R , ISAAC J_sHK_s ), the host of GRB 070306 was observed with GROND ( simultaneously), and its SED (Fig. hostfigs ) is further complemented by published u and I band data 2008ApJ...681..453J . The host is bright ( ^ = 23.1 mag ), mildly red We compare GRB host colors against the median (R-K)_ AB = 0.8 mag color from the SGL09 sample with (R-K)_ AB 1.5 mag and shows evidence of a 4000 break.

The data are well fit with a non-extinguished ( A'_V < 0.6 ) For galaxies, we use A'_V , or E'_ B-V to indicate measured attenuation and effective reddening, since these quantities depend for example on the topology of the ISM and galaxy geometry 2004ApJ...617.1022P , and on galaxy scales are different from the corresponding values of a given extinction law. host template, and yield an absolute AB magnitude of M_B=-22.4 0.1 mag , which, at z 1.5 corresponds to 1.7 L^* . The stellar mass of (M_ )= 10.4 0.2 puts the galaxy among the most massive hosts compared to the sample of SGL09. The star-formation rate estimate from the rest-frame UV flux is 13_ 4 ^ +11 /yr, which gives a specific star formation rate (SSFR = SFR/ M_ ) of 0.5 Gyr ^ 1 , or growth timescale (i.e., 1/SSFR) of 2 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 070306 host as a massive, resolved, heavily obscured star-forming galaxy with radio/Herschel SFR far above optical estimates.

quotes.quote: GRB 070306 was a heavily obscured gamma-ray burst Jaunsen+2008 ; its afterglow was detected only in K and H bands and its color between these filters was very red (consistent with A_V 5.5 mag of extinction.) The optical/IR host-galaxy observations of this target are all the same as previously presented in Perley+2013a (which in turn incorporated data from the earlier studies of Jaunsen+2008 and Kruehler+2011 ) and despite somewhat different modeling assumptions we derive nearly identical parameters, including a very modest UV/optical star-formation rate of 15 M_ yr ^ 1 . The radio (and Herschel-) inferred dust-unbiased star-formation rate for this source is unambiguously much larger: approximately 140 M_ yr ^ 1 , indicating the presence of substantial heavily-obscured star-formation. The large column towards the GRB is consistent with an interpretation in which the burst itself actually occurred in the dominant, heavily obscured part of the host.

The best-fit stellar mass of this galaxy is the largest of the four radio-detected hosts in our sample ( 5 10^ 10 M_ ). HST WFC3-IR imaging is available for this source Perley+2013a ; while the galaxy is resolved there are no obvious signs of an ongoing major merger at the resolution of the IR camera, though the galaxy does show some asymmetry (an extension to the northwest but not the southeast) and two much fainter companions (possibly, extended spiral arms) are present nearby.

gmw_00007891.name: host of GRB 080605
gmw_00007891.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 080605 is a morphologically complex, high-redshift galaxy located at z = 1.64 [arXiv:1203.1919, arXiv:1108.0674]. It is a bright, blue galaxy undergoing vigorous star formation [arXiv:1203.1919, arXiv:1108.0674] with a young stellar population and a metal-enriched interstellar medium [arXiv:1203.1919, arXiv:1108.0674].

sources.summary: The paper presents spectroscopy and HST imaging of the z=1.64 GRB 080605 host, finding a morphologically complex, vigorously star-forming, metal-enriched galaxy.

quotes.quote: We observed the galaxy hosting GRB 080605 at z=1.64 using optical/NIR spectroscopy and high-resolution HST/WFC3 imaging in the rest-frame wavelength range between 1150 and 8700 . These data allow us to study a z > 1 GRB host in unprecedented detail and investigate the relation between GRB hosts and field galaxies. We avail of VLT/X-shooter optical/NIR spectroscopy to measure the metallicity, electron density, star-formation rate (SFR), and reddening of the host of GRB 080605. Specifically, we use different strong-line diagnostics to robustly measure the gas-phase metallicity within the interstellar medium (ISM) for the first time based on at this redshift.

The host of the energetic ( E_ , iso 2 10^ 53 erg) GRB 080605 at z 1.64 is a morphologically complex , vigorously star-forming galaxy with an H derived SFR of 31^ +12 _ 6 M_ yr ^ 1 . Its ISM is significantly enriched with metals.

sources.summary: The host of GRB 080605 is a bright, blue z=1.640 galaxy whose SED indicates a young, unreddened stellar population and vigorous star formation.

quotes.quote: The host of GRB 080605 The host of GRB 080605 at z=1.640 was discovered in late GROND follow-up observations of the burst field 22 days after the GRB trigger.

The host is bright ( ^ 22.8 mag ), and blue with a flat g^ z^ color, and (R-K)_ AB 0.5 mag as estimated from the best fit galaxy model (Fig. hostfigs ). The SED fit further yields M_B=-22.6 0.2 mag , which is 2.1 L^* at z 1.5 , and (M_ ) = 9.6^ +0.3 _ 0.2 . The dominant stellar population of the host is young ( = 0.06^ +0.18 _ 0.03 Gyr), and there is no evidence for reddening at the 2 level ( E'_ B-V 0.22 mag ). The host galaxy is vigorously star-forming with a SFR of 40_ 20 ^ +40 /yr and a SSFR of 10 Gyr ^ 1 .

gmw_00007892.name: host of GRB 081109
gmw_00007892.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 081109 is a bright, moderately red galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.9787 [arXiv:1108.0674]. Spectral energy distribution modeling indicates that it contains a young, reddened, and actively star-forming stellar population [arXiv:1108.0674].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 081109 is a bright, moderately red galaxy whose spectrum gives z=0.9787 and whose SED implies a young, reddened, star-forming stellar population.

quotes.quote: The host of GRB 081109 GRB 081109 is the only burst in the sample where no spectroscopic redshift was available in the literature. However, the host is bright ( ^ 22.7 mag ), moderately red ( (R-K)_ AB = 1.6 mag ) and our host spectrum (see Fig. 081109spec ) reveals a single emission line above a well-detected continuum. Within the wavelength coverage of the spectrum ( 370-950 nm), this emission line is interpreted as [ OII ] [ 3727 ] at z=0.9787 0.0005 .

The host SED is shown in Fig. hostfigs and well fit with a young ( = 0.18^ +0.19 _ 0.07 Gyr) and reddened ( A'_ V = 1.0 0.2 mag ) stellar population. The absolute magnitude of M_B=-21.27 0.09 mag corresponds to 0.9 L^ at z 1 . The stellar mass obtained from the SPS fit is (M_ ) = 9.80^ +0.09 _ 0.09 , with a SFR of 33_ 13 ^ +19 /yr, which together yields a SSFR of 5 Gyr ^ 1 .

gmw_00007893.name: host of GRB 090926B
gmw_00007893.overview: The host of GRB 090926B is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.3 [arXiv:1108.0674]. It exhibits a mildly red color, dust extinction, and one of the highest optical star formation rates ever measured [arXiv:1108.0674].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 090926B is detected through [OII] emission and modeled as a mildly red, extinguished z~1.3 galaxy with high optical star formation.

quotes.quote: The host of GRB 090926B Promptly after the trigger, 2009GCN..9947....1F reported the detection of the host galaxy of GRB 090926B based on a prominent [ OII ] emission line in a VLT/FORS spectrum. The host is also imaged in later GROND and NTT observations with a brightness of ^ 23.0 mag , and a mildly red color ( (R-K)_ AB 1.5 mag ) as shown in Fig. hostfigs . The host SED is well fit with an extinguished host model ( A'_V = 1.4^ +0.3 _ 0.2 mag ) with M_B=-21.5 0.2 ( 0.9 L^ at z 1.3 ) and (M_ ) = 10.1^ +0.6 _ 0.5 . The SFR based on the extinction corrected UV flux is 80_ 50 ^ +110 /yr which is among the highest ever measured from optical data, but not well constrained given the uncertainty on the dust extinction properties. The constraints on the age of the stellar population and SSFR are weak, with values of =0.14^ +0.60 _ 0.09 Gyr and the SSFR of 7 Gyr ^ 1 , respectively.

gmw_00007894.name: A370p z1
gmw_00007894.overview: A370p z1 is a young, star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=6.803 [arXiv:2010.06241]. It exhibits a double-peaked Lyman-alpha profile, indicating that the galaxy was capable of self-ionizing its own local bubble [arXiv:2010.06241].

sources.summary: The paper reports A370p z1 as a z=6.803 double-peaked Lyman-alpha galaxy with high ionising escape fraction, young star-forming SED, and enough ionising output to create its own bubble.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a double-peaked Lyman-alpha profile in a galaxy at z=6.803 , A370p z1, in the parallel Frontier Field of Abell 370. The velocity separation between the blue and red peaks of the Lyman- profile ( v=101_ 19 ^ +38 ( 48) ) suggests an extremely high escape fraction of ionising photons > 59(51) (2 ) . The spectral energy distribution indicates a young ( 50 Myr), star-forming ( 12 6 M _ yr ^ 1 ) galaxy with an IRAC excess implying strong [ OIII ] +H emission. On the basis of the high escape fraction measured, we demonstrate that A370p z1 was solely capable of creating an ionised bubble sufficiently large to account for the blue component of its Lyman-alpha profile.

The detection of the blue peak in the Lyman- indicates A370p z1 sits in a large ionised bubble, otherwise the damping wing of even a partially neutral IGM would have absorbed it. Given its high escape fraction, we now consider whether A370p z1 could have self-ionised its local bubble. The blue wing extends to = 9479.2 , 215 from the line centre, corresponding to a physical distance r_ HII >0.26 0.04 pMpc.

gmw_00007895.name: Mrk 1393
gmw_00007895.overview: Mrk 1393 is a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2104.11263] that exhibits variable absorption [arXiv:2104.11263, arXiv:2211.04478] and is mildly absorbed in X-rays [arXiv:1005.0373]. It is a changing-look active galaxy that turned off between 1984 and 1993, but turned on again by 2022 with a brighter continuum and stronger broad emission lines [arXiv:2211.04478].

sources.summary: The paper reports Tol 1506.3 00 as mildly absorbed in X-rays, with no detected iron line and measured soft/hard fluxes plus intrinsic luminosity.

quotes.quote: Tol 1506.3 00 was observed for 3.7 ks with \ (top-right panel in Fig. A.1). Its spectrum is characterized by a thermal ( kT 0.2 keV) plus a power-law component at soft energies, while the emission above 2 keV is well parameterized by a power-law with 1.6 and mild absorption ( N_H 7.1 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ). No iron line is present, the upper limit on its EW , in the case of neutral iron, is 40 eV (90 confidence level). The 0.5--2 keV and 2--10 keV fluxes are 2.35 10^ 12 \ and 7.71 10^ 12 , respectively. The intrinsic, rest-frame 2--10 keV luminosity of Tol 1506.3 00 is 5.5 10^ 43 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies Mrk 1393 as a Sy1 source with variable absorption, intermediate covering factor, small half-opening angle, and a clumpy gas-dust distribution in a geometrically thin disk.

quotes.quote: Panel 1 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy1 (e.g. Mrk 1393): Sy1 source with absorption variability, intermediate mid-IR covering factor, and small half-opening angle. A clumpy distribution is preferred for both gas and dust. Interestingly, this object shows a large number of clouds at the equator and low optical depth. This object can be explained with a geometrically-thin disk of (high number of) clumps conformed by dust and gas, where the inner (dust-free) region is filled with these clumps. Note that the low inclination angle toward the structure (measured from the equator) might intercept some of the clouds close to the central engine producing the absorption variations.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 1393 as a changing-look AGN that turned off after 1984 and then turned on by 2022, with stronger broad lines and ZTF brightening.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1393 Mrk 1393 (BAT 757) was observed to show galaxy-dominated continuum in 1993, 2001, 2005 2009AJ....137.4002W , with no broad \ present in the 2001 March spectrum from SDSS, leading to it being classified as a type 1.9 in BASS DR1. However, a 1984 spectrum presented by 1988MNRAS.230..639M shows broad Balmer emission, suggesting the source turned off between 1984 and 1993. X-ray observations of Mrk 1393 were presented by 2009AJ....137.4002W , who discussed a scenario in which variable obscuration in the optical and X-rays could be due to dusty material disrupted from the circumnuclear torus. 2009AJ....137.4002W also detected weak broad \ in their 2005 September spectrum, with a line-of-sight extinction of 0.6 mag estimated from the Balmer decrement. A new, previously unpublished spectrum taken in 2022 May with LCO/FLOYDS shows a much brighter and bluer continuum, along with much stronger broad \ and \ emission lines consistent with an unobscured line-of-sight to the BLR.

gmw_00007896.name: Source G
gmw_00007896.overview: Source G is a galaxy cataloged as 2MASX J13590381-4751311 with a redshift of z=0.074 [arXiv:2208.04750]. Located near the center of the south subcluster of CIZA1359, it features a resolved radio structure likely to be an active galactic nucleus radio lobe [arXiv:2208.04750].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source G as a resolved radio feature near CIZA1359, likely an AGN radio lobe, cataloged as a galaxy with redshift consistent with the cluster.

quotes.quote: Source G is located near the center of the south subcluster and has an apparent size of about 25'' 22'' (=38 kpc x 33 kpc) at the 3 _ rms signal to noise level. It is one order of magnitude smaller than the typical size of mini-halos ( 500 kpc), so that it is more likely to be an AGN radio lobe. Source G is cataloged in SIMBAD as a galaxy 2MASX J13590381-4751311. It has the redshift of z=0.074, which is the consistent with the redshift of CIZA1359.

gmw_00007897.name: Source Q
gmw_00007897.overview: Source Q is a galaxy cataloged as 2MASX J13581085-4741243, located near the center of the north subcluster of CIZA1359 [arXiv:2208.04750]. It has a redshift of z=0.074 and is identified as a likely active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.04750].

sources.summary: The paper treats Source Q as a likely AGN radio source near the north subcluster center, cataloged as a galaxy whose redshift matches CIZA1359.

quotes.quote: Similarly to Source G, Source Q is more likely to be an AGN, although it is located near the center of the north subcluster with a size of 60'' 30'' (=90 kpc 45 kpc). Source Q is cataloged in SIMBAD as a galaxy 2MASX J13581085-4741243. Its redshift, z=0.074, is consistent with the redshift of CIZA1359.

gmw_00007898.name: Source R
gmw_00007898.overview: Source R is a 2MASX galaxy located approximately 2 Mpc from the southern subcluster center of CIZA1359 [arXiv:2208.04750]. It exhibits a head-tail radio morphology with a spectral-index gradient that indicates aging from the head to the tail [arXiv:2208.04750].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source R as a 2MASX galaxy near CIZA1359 with head-tail radio morphology, a spectral-index gradient, and a redshift comparable to the cluster.

quotes.quote: Source R is about 2 Mpc away from the southern subcluster center to the south-east and is cataloged in SIMBAD as a galaxy 2MASX J14004272-4804474. We found that it has a radio structure like a head-tail galaxy. There is also a spectral index gradient, with aging from the head to the tail. It has the redshift of z=0.075, which is comparable to that of CIZA1359.

gmw_00007899.name: Source T
gmw_00007899.overview: Source T is a radio source located southeast of the CIZA1359 cluster [arXiv:2208.04750]. It exhibits a head-tail galaxy-like radio structure with an elongated southern component and a faint eastern structure [arXiv:2208.04750].

sources.summary: The paper describes Source T as a head-tail-galaxy-like radio source with elongated components and a 2MASX galaxy at the southern peak at z=0.081.

quotes.quote: Source T is located at the southeast of the CIZA1359 and has a head-tail galaxy-like radio structure. It has an elongated structure to the south and a faint structure to the east. There is no associated source within 30 arcsec in SIMBAD. At the peak flux position of the south component of Source T, there is a galaxy 2MASX J13592976-4757043 in SIMBAD. The redshift of 2MASX J13592976-4757043 is z=0.081, which is located at far side of CIZA1359.

gmw_00007900.name: PGC 043234
gmw_00007900.overview: PGC 043234 is a post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:1609.00010, arXiv:2002.02498] located approximately 90.3 megaparsecs away [arXiv:1507.01598, arXiv:1510.06348]. It appears to be a recent merger remnant [arXiv:1609.00010] that hosts a weak, low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1605.04640, arXiv:1609.00010] and is dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:1601.03331].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VII Zw 211 as ASASSN-14li’s nearby host, places the transient at its nucleus, and characterizes the host as low-mass with weak or absent strong AGN activity.

quotes.quote: A search at the object's position (J2000 RA/Dec = 12:48:15.23/+17:46:26.22) in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 9 SDSS DR9; ahn12 catalog revealed the source of the outburst to be the galaxy (VII Zw 211) at redshift z=0.0206 , corresponding to a luminosity distance of d=90.3 Mpc ( H_0=73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _M=0.27 , _ =0.73 ), and that the ASAS-SN source position was consistent with the center of the host galaxy. Follow-up images obtained on 2014 November 28 with the Las Cumbres Observatory Global Telescope Network (LCOGT) 1-m telescope at McDonald Observatory brown13 and on 2014 November 30 with the UltraViolet and Optical Telescope UVOT; roming05 confirmed the detection of the transient. In order to constrain any offset between the source of the outburst and the nucleus of the host galaxy we first astrometrically aligned an image of the transient taken with the 2-m Liverpool Telescope LT; steele04 with the archival SDSS image of the host galaxy. From this aligned image, we measure an offset of 0.43 0.52 pixels ( 0.17 0.21 arcseconds, or 74.4 91.9 parsecs) between the position of the brightest pixel in the host galaxy in the LT image and the position of the brightest pixel in the SDSS image. This offset is consistent with the source of the outburst being the nucleus of the host galaxy, which provides support for a TDE interpretation of the event.

Examining data from the ROSAT All-Sky Survey, we do not detect the host galaxy with a 3-sigma upper limit of 1.8 10^ 2 counts s ^ 1 , corresponding to 7.5 10^ 14  ergs s ^ 1  cm ^ 2 , in the 0.08-2.9  keV band voges99 , which provides evidence that the galaxy does not host a strong AGN. However, the host galaxy is detected in the Very Large Array Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-cm survey FIRST; becker95 , with 1.4 GHz flux density of 2.96 0.15 mJy, corresponding to a luminosity of L_ 1.4 GHz 2.6 10^ 21  W Hz ^ 1 .

We obtained a good SPS fit (reduced _ ^ 2 =0.6 ), with the following parameters: M_ * =(2.8_ 0.1 ^ +0.1 ) 10^ 9   , age =1.3_ 0.3 ^ +0.2  Gyr, and a 1 upper limit on the star formation rate of SFR 0.9 10^ 2    yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 043234, originally Zw VIII 211, as the host-center galaxy of ASASSN-14li, gives its redshift and distance, rules out a standard active nucleus, and models host ISM absorption.

quotes.quote: Follow-up observations found the transient source to coincide with the center of the galaxy PGC 043234 (originally Zw VIII 211), to within 0.04 arc seconds . This galaxy lies at a red-shift of z = 0.0206 , or a luminosity distance of 90.3 Mpc (for H _0 = 73 km s ^ 1 , _ matter = 0.27 , _ = 0.73 ), making ASASSN-14li the closest disruption event discovered in over 10 years. The discovery magnitudes indicated a substantial flux increase over prior, archival optical images of this galaxy.
Archival X-ray studies rule out the possibility that PGC 043234 harbours a standard active galactic nucleus that could produce bright flaring. PGC 043234 is not detected in the ROSAT All-Sky Survey .

Within SPEX, absorption from the interstellar medium in the Milky Way was modeled using the model ``hot''; a separate ``hot'' component was included to allow for ISM absorption within PGC 043234 at its known red-shift (using the ``reds'' component in SPEX).

sources.summary: PGC 043234 is identified as ASASSN-14li's host at 90 Mpc; its bulge mass constrains the black hole mass, and pre-flare observations set a 132-day disruption window.

quotes.quote: The distance to ASASSN-14li is the exception however: it is tightly constrained by its well identified host galaxy: PGC 043234 (Holoein et al . 2016), at a distance D =90 Mpc. The disc-observer inclination angle is unknown for ASASSN-14li.

The mass of the central black hole is somewhat constrained by previous observations. Using measurements of the host galaxy's bulge mass and standard SMBH-host galaxy scaling relationships, Mendel et al . (2014) derived a black hole mass of M 10^ 6.7 0.6 M_ .

Prior to this date, its host galaxy PGC 043234 had not been observed since the 13th of July 2014, when no TDE was present, as the sun was obscuring this galaxy. This leaves a 132 day window between observations within which the initial disruption could have occurred.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 043234 as ASASSN-14li's old stellar host at z=0.02058, with unusual host-redshift absorption, possible halo gas, and evidence for a pre-outburst AGN.

quotes.quote: The All-Sky Automated Survey for Supernova (ASASSN; spg+14 ) first detected ASASSN-14li in V band images obtained on 2014 Nov 11.65 in the nucleus of the galaxy PGC 043234 ATEL.6777,hkp+16 . Pre-outburst observations from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS; aaa+15 ) indicate that the host galaxy is dominated by an old stellar population ( t_ age 11 Gyr) with a stellar mass _ 10 (M_ * / M _ ) 9.7 and a redshift of z = 0.02058 0.00001 cgw09 .

The lack of common low-ionization absorption features, together with the old host stellar population, indicate that the absorber is unlikely to arise in the cold host ISM. Given that nearly every L_ * galaxy exhibits a circumgalactic medium (GGM) consisting of strong Ly absorption and (frequently) low-ionization absorption pwc+11,ttw+12 , there is a reasonable probability that gas in the halo of the host galaxy contributes to the observed absorption.
The host galaxy does show evidence for a pre-outburst AGN, and so the absorber could result from pre-existing gas in (or possibly expelled from; vas+15 ) the nuclear region.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 043234 as the host galaxy of ASASSN-14li, a faint archival radio emitter whose core showed radio variability after the tidal disruption flare.

quotes.quote: ASASSN-14li is coincident with the nucleus of the galaxy PGC 043234 (12:48:15.2 +17:46:26, J2000), which is a known faint radio emitter with an archival 1.4 GHz flux from the NRAO VLA Sky Survey or NVSS (NVSS 124815+174629, flux S_ 1.4 = 3.2 0.4 mJy, mean time of observations 50105.2 MJD corresponding to 19 years before outburst) and the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty centimeters or FIRST survey (FIRST J124815.2+174626, flux S_ 1.4 = 2.67 0.15 mJy, mean time of observations 51507.6 MJD corresponding to 15 years before outburst). The archival data show no sign of significant radio variability between these two epochs . ASASSN-14li was first observed with the Arcminute Microkelvin Imager Large Array or AMI-LA on 2014-12-23.08 (57014.1 MJD), which detected a radio source coincident with PGC 043234, and consequently ASASSN-14li , yielding a peak flux of 1.93 0.11 mJy at 15.7 GHz.

After observing a factor of two decrease in the AMI-LA light curve of ASASSN-14li during January and February of 2014 we decided it was prudent to obtain radio observations at 1.4 GHz to quantify the radio variability of the core of PGC 043234, which can be compared to the baseline flux values from NVSS and FIRST that predate ASASSN-14li .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC043234 as the galaxy whose center hosted ASASSN-14li, gives its redshift, and notes pre-TDE radio emission suggesting only weak low-luminosity AGN activity.

quotes.quote: The discovery was triggered by an optical flare coincident with the center of the galaxy PGC043234 within 0.04 , prompting it to be a potential TDE flare. Later, the Swift X-ray observations established a new source at the same location. This galaxy is located at z=0.0206 , making ASASSN-14li the closest TDE discovered in over ten years.

Previous works show that \ ( 10^ 39 ) and radio emission do exist in PGC 043234 before the TDE, suggesting that the galaxy may host a low-luminosity AGN. However, the lack of X-ray emission and the W1-W2 color of \ imply that the nuclear activity is rather weak (Holoien et al. 2016).

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 043234 as ASASSN-14li’s nearby post-starburst host, a recent-merger remnant with evidence for a pre-existing low-luminosity AGN and possible binary black hole context.

quotes.quote: ASASSN-14li was discovered by the All-Sky Automated Survey for Supernovae ASAS-SN; shappee14 on 2014 November 22 at the center of the nearby ( z=0.0206 , D=90.3 Mpc) post-starburst galaxy PGC 043234 holoien16 . PGC 043234 appears to be the remnant of a recent merger that likely hosted a low-luminosity Type II AGN prior to the TDE prieto16 .
prieto16 present evidence for a pre-existing AGN in PGC 043234 based on the emission line ratios of diffuse gas near the galaxy.
The host of ASASSN-14li is a post-starburst galaxy and has a morphology suggestive of previous merger activity prieto16 , where the presence of a binary BH would be expected.

sources.summary: PGC043234 is identified as the galaxy spatially coincident with ASASSN-14li; the event lies near its center and host-galaxy scaling constrains the black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Soon after its discovery it became apparent that it exhibited nearly all properties of previously-known TDEs: a spatial position consistent with the host galaxy's center (within 160 parsecs ), a luminosity declining in time with a power-law index of 5/3 as expected from a TDE , a blue optical spectrum with broad Hydrogen and Helium emission lines and a constant optical color unlike any ordinary supernova . In addition to the optical and UV emission, ASASSN-14li also produced an x-ray and a radio synchrotron flare . Owing to these multi-wavelength properties ASASSN-14li has been dubbed the ``Rosetta stone'' for TDEs . The BH mass in ASASSN-14li is constrained to lie in between 10^ 5.8-7.1 M_ using well-studied correlations relating BH mass and host galaxy properties .
A Chandra image shows only a single x-ray point source spatially coincident with the galaxy PGC043234 (SI Fig. fig:EDF2 ).

sources.summary: The ASASSN-14li host is a unique post-starburst galaxy in this sample, with a bright central point source and a blue color gradient.

quotes.quote: The post-starburst host of ASASSN-14li is unique among the four galaxies studied here in being the only post-starburst galaxy, but having a sample with 1/4 post-starburst galaxies is consistent with post-starburst galaxies comprising 12 33 of the TDE host population.
The host galaxy of ASASSN-14li has a bright central point source that can be seen through its diffraction spikes in the optical image.
The blue color gradient (bluer at the center and redder towards higher radii) in the host galaxy of ASASSN-14li is consistent with those seen in most post-starburst galaxies.

gmw_00007901.name: NGC 4776
gmw_00007901.overview: NGC 4776 is a luminous lenticular companion galaxy located near NGC 4778 [arXiv:astro-ph/0606730, arXiv:astro-ph/0701119]. Despite its close proximity, the galaxy shows no signs of interaction or merging [arXiv:astro-ph/0701119], displaying well-behaved, unperturbed kinematics [arXiv:astro-ph/0606730] and hosting a possible low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0701119].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4776 as HCG 62b, a nearby peculiar S0 companion to NGC4778, and cites its regular kinematics as evidence it is not perturbing NGC4778.

quotes.quote: The group is dominated by the pair NGC4778 (also known as HCG 62a) and NGC4776 (HCG 62b), having a projected separation of only 8 Kpc. NGC4778 formerly classified as an E3 galaxy, is now classified as an S0 with a bright compact nucleus, as already noticed by J. Herschel (1811), and subsequently discovered to be a low luminosity AGN ( and ). Both NGC4776 and NGC4761 (HCG62c) are classified as peculiar S0's.

Long slit spectroscopy has been obtained at different position angles. and , positioned the slit along the line joining the nuclei of NGC4778 and NGC4776 (P.A.= 127^ ) and their results lead to the conclusion that the pair NGC4778/4776 is not interacting and the kinematical peculiarities observed in NGC4778 are likely due to an interaction with NGC4761. In fact, the velocity dispersion and rotation curves of NGC4776 are well behaved and appear unperturbed, while the velocity dispersion profile of NGC4778 shows a relatively sharp increase to the SE, suggestive of the presence of a perturber.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4776 as a luminous S0 member near NGC 4778, with a large peculiar velocity, no obvious merger signs, and a central X-ray point source interpreted as a possible low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: One other luminous member NGC 4776 (S0) is located at a projected distance of only 0.4^ west of NGC 4778 with a peculiar velocity of 737 km s ^ 1 . However, it shows no signs of interaction or merging with NGC 4778 (Rampazzo et al. \ 1998).

One of them is detected at the center of NGC 4776 to within 0.5 , which is possibly a low-luminosity AGN hosted in NGC 4776, and one other is identified with a foreground star (HD 111960; Hog et al. 1998).

The authors also showed that the velocity dispersion distribution of the outer halo is strongly distorted in the southwest direction, which cannot be interpreted naturally in terms of a projection effect due to the presence of NGC 4776.

gmw_00007902.name: BCG
gmw_00007902.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy of MACS1206 is a passive galaxy [arXiv:1503.05607] that harbors a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1411.0317]. It exhibits low star-formation activity [arXiv:2004.01786] and likely experienced a strong interaction at least 1.5 billion years ago [arXiv:1403.4979].

sources.summary: The paper includes M1206 among tentative CO detections with low star-formation activity and molecular gas content of a few times 10^10 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Specifically, these were M0329, A1423, M1206, and M2129.

Concerning the remaining sources, three out of four BCGs with tentative detections in CO (i.e., A1423, M1206, M2129) and the 13 BCGs with only upper limits in CO exhibit star formation activity ranging from MS values SFR SFR _ MS down to a few 10^ 3 SFR _ MS . These results suggest that tentative detections in CO as well as non-detections are found quite independently of the star formation activity of the BCG. However, A1423, M1206, and M2129 with tentative detections in CO have low star-formation activity SFR (1-3) M_ /yr, corresponding to low normalized values of (0.009-0.09) SFR _ MS , which are rarely seen associated with cluster galaxies detected in CO (see Fig. fig:mol_gas1 , right).

They include M0329, M1206, and A1423, with M_ H_2 (2-3) 10^ 10 M_ and M_ H_2 /M_ 0.1 . We also tentatively detected M2129, both in CO(1 0) and CO(2 1), which tends to be more gas-rich, with M_ H_2 2 10^ 11 M_ and M_ H_2 /M_ 0.7 . The four BCGs also cover a broad range of star formation activity, ranging from (0.1-3) M_ /yr (M1206, M2129, and A1423) to high levels of star formation 40 M_ /yr (M0329).

sources.summary: The paper finds the BCG+ICL in MACS1206 aligned with the surrounding large-scale structure and argues the BCG experienced a strong interaction at least 1.5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: Matching our BCG/ICL PA estimates, we find 101^ PA_ BCG/ICL 109^ which is similar to this preferential direction, thus suggesting a further connection between the ICL and the infalling direction of the PSBs population. This scenario is also supported by the presence of an elongated large scale structure (LSS) around the cluster whose major axis runs along the NW-SE direction, 15^ PA_ LSS 30^ measured north of west Umetsu2012 . Matching our PA estimates to the same reference system as Umetsu2012 we find 11^ PA_ BCG/ICL 19^ , depending on the assumed BCG+ICL best fit profile. Thus both the BCG and the ICL are oriented along the same axis as that of the LSS, this holds also when comparing the ellipticity of the LSS and of the BCG+ICL. As a consequence the BCG of MACS1206 should have experienced a strong interaction that dates back to at least _ past merger 1.5 Gyr ago, this interaction might involve also the second brightest galaxy and it may has occured along the preferential NW-SE direction.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the MACS J1206.2-0847 BCG as a radio-loud AGN host detected by MUSTANG, models its compact emission, and links its offset to possible ICM sloshing.

quotes.quote: The BCG ( _ J2000 =12^ h 06^ m 12.1^ s , _ J2000 =-08 48 3 ) in \ harbors a radio-loud AGN that is detected by MUSTANG at high significance (S/N >4 ). Using a spatial template derived from the MUSTANG map, we construct a compact source model and allow the amplitude to float in the joint fits with bulk SZE models, in order to account for the degeneracy between the co-spatial positive emission and SZE decrement.

The offset between the pressure profile and the AGN/BCG seems to suggest the ICM is sloshing subsonically, as sloshing should not produce strong pressure discontinuities zuhone2013 . This scenario is supported by the 7 \ offset between the centroid of the X-ray emitting gas and the BCG location (Figure fig:m1206composite ), along with the general E-W elongation of both the surface mass distribution seen in strong-lensing and the X-ray surface brightness. The strong-lensing data in zitrin2012 (reproduced in Figure fig:m1206composite ) reveal a massive component 40 \ to the east of the cluster core. If this subcluster has passed in front of or behind the main cluster's core, it may have induced E-W sloshing that has redirected one or both jets away from the line of sight.

sources.summary: The paper uses the BCG as the MACS1206 center, classifies it as passive despite blended [O II] emission, and locates the main projected density structure on the BCG.

quotes.quote: For the center of MACS1206, we adopted the position of the brightest cluster galaxy [ BCG, R.A.= 12^ h 06^ m 12 15 , Dec.= 08 48 03.3 (J2000) ] .

We note that the BCG is classified as a passive galaxy in spite of the presence of a [ OII ] emission line in the VIMOS spectrum since the HST photometric data suggest that this line can be associated with a blue compact source and/or peculiar features blended with the BCG (see Presotto et al. for the relevant discussion).
When analyzing the whole cluster, the 2D-DEDICA density reconstruction confirms the cluster elongation and shows a main structure peaked on the BCG with a few secondary peaks (at ESE, SE, and WNW).

gmw_00007903.name: NGC 3193
gmw_00007903.overview: NGC 3193 is classified as an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0703646]. Photometric analysis of the galaxy requires the removal of nearby stars, including a bright star close to the galaxy's core that can distort ellipse calculations [arXiv:astro-ph/0703646].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3193 as an elliptical-galaxy reduction example, showing that ARCHANGEL cleans stars and diffraction spikes while preventing a nearby star from biasing ellipse fitting.

quotes.quote: An example of the analysis of an elliptical is found in Figure 1, a 2MASS J image of NGC 3193. The top panel is the raw 2MASS image, the bottom panel is the resulting cleaned image output at the end of the reduction process. The cleaning process efficiently removed all the stars on the frame, including the brighter object on the northern edge of the frame and its diffraction spikes. The star closest to the galaxy core is a problem in two arenas. The first is in the calculation of ellipse, as the inner star would drag the calculated moments off center.

gmw_00007904.name: IC 5271
gmw_00007904.overview: IC 5271 is a disk galaxy that exhibits crossing isophotes [arXiv:astro-ph/0703646]. This isophote crossover represents a real physical transition from the galaxy's bulge to its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0703646].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 5271 as a disk-galaxy example where crossing isophotes trace a bulge-to-disk transition and affect inferred intensities less than off-center ellipse fits would.

quotes.quote: An example of this behavior can be found in Figure 3, the J image of IC 5271. The red ellipses indicate isophote fits that crossover. While flagged as an error, this is in fact the real behavior of the isophotes as one transitions from bulge to disk. The resulting intensities are probably overestimated due to the crossover effect, but this error will be minor compared to the errors that would result from an off-center or overly round ellipse.

gmw_00007905.overview: The object 0036 0113 is a red quasar [arXiv:1712.01851]. Its host galaxy contributes approximately 25 percent of the emission [arXiv:1712.01851], and its hydrogen line is well fitted by a narrow component alone [arXiv:1712.01851].

sources.summary: The paper adds IRTF/SpeX near-infrared spectra for 0036 0113, estimates roughly 25 percent host-galaxy contamination, and finds its H line is fitted by a narrow component despite earlier broad-line reports.

quotes.quote: Additionally, new NIR spectra were obtained for two red quasars (0036 0113 and 1307 + 2338) using the SpeX instrument rayner03 on the NASA Infrared Telescope Facility (IRTF).

We could not detect the Ca II K absorption line for 3 red quasars, and for the remaining 2, we measured EWs of 3.8 , which corresponds to the host galaxy contribution of 25 (0036 0113 and 1307 + 2338), by scaling the results of and .

Among the nine [ O III ] fitted red quasars, the H line of four red quasars (0036 0113, 0915 + 2418, 1307 + 2338, and 1532 + 2415) is well fitted by the narrow component only, although 0036 0113 and 1307 + 2338 were reported to have a broad H component glikman07 .

gmw_00007906.overview: 1307 + 2338 is a red quasar [arXiv:1712.01851]. Its host galaxy contributes approximately 25 percent of the observed light, and its hydrogen line is well fitted by only a narrow component [arXiv:1712.01851].

sources.summary: The paper adds IRTF/SpeX near-infrared spectra for 1307 + 2338, estimates about 25 percent host-galaxy contamination, and fits its H line with only a narrow component despite earlier broad-line reports.

quotes.quote: Additionally, new NIR spectra were obtained for two red quasars (0036 0113 and 1307 + 2338) using the SpeX instrument rayner03 on the NASA Infrared Telescope Facility (IRTF).

We could not detect the Ca II K absorption line for 3 red quasars, and for the remaining 2, we measured EWs of 3.8 , which corresponds to the host galaxy contribution of 25 (0036 0113 and 1307 + 2338), by scaling the results of and .

Among the nine [ O III ] fitted red quasars, the H line of four red quasars (0036 0113, 0915 + 2418, 1307 + 2338, and 1532 + 2415) is well fitted by the narrow component only, although 0036 0113 and 1307 + 2338 were reported to have a broad H component glikman07 .

gmw_00007907.name: SDSS1419+5254
