gmw_00007907.overview: SDSS1419+5254 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.442 [arXiv:2209.03359]. The system has been observed with the James Webb Space Telescope using the F200W and F444W filters [arXiv:2209.03359].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS1419+5254/AEGIS 585 as a z=3.442 quasar host with only two JWST filters, single-PSF F444W fitting, and no SED fit.

quotes.quote: SDSS1419+5254 ( z=3.442 ) falls in CEERS2 and thus has only two \ filters (i.e., F200W and F444W) available at this moment. From AEGIS-X, we also find four quasars with spectroscopic redshifts above z>1.6 . After manually checking, we find that two of them are matched to SDSS quasars After the manual confirmation, we find SDSS1419+5254 and AEGIS 585 are the same system; SDSS1420+5300A and AEGIS 742 are the same system.

After a visual check, we find that only one PSF from the library in the F444W band can produce the clean quasar subtraction for AEGIS 482 and SDSS1419+5254; thus, the uncertainty for these two targets with this filter is not estimated. As shown later, we use 0.4 mag for the error budget for the SED fitting in the F444W bands of these two systems.
Unfortunately, SDSS1419 currently only has observations with F200W and F444W, both above the 4000 \ break. The SED fits are presented in Figure fig:sed_spec with the exception of SDSS1419.

gmw_00007908.name: AEGIS 482
gmw_00007908.overview: AEGIS 482 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:2209.03359]. Its physical size is consistent with the average star-forming galaxy at its respective stellar mass [arXiv:2209.03359].

sources.summary: The paper describes AEGIS 482 as a quasar host whose F444W fit uses one reliable PSF, whose F150W size is inconsistent, and whose final size agrees with star-forming galaxies at its stellar mass.

quotes.quote: After a visual check, we find that only one PSF from the library in the F444W band can produce the clean quasar subtraction for AEGIS 482 and SDSS1419+5254; thus, the uncertainty for these two targets with this filter is not estimated. As shown later, we use 0.4 mag for the error budget for the SED fitting in the F444W bands of these two systems.

As seen in Table table:sample , the fit using /F150W for AEGIS-482 has a size that is very small and inconsistent with the other bands. For sizes, i.e., , we use recipes in vdW+2014 to the adjust the median \ to the rest-frame 5000 . For the control sample, we use the size measurements of individual galaxies at equivalent redshifts (i.e., 1.6<z<3.5 ) and the best-fit relation at z 2.2 in vdW+2014 for star-forming and quiescent galaxies, which are classified using UVJ photometry. In Figure fig:sizemass , we show that most (4/5) quasar hosts are compact with sizes 1 kpc. SDSS1420A is larger due to its high stellar mass. Two quasars (AEGIS 482, SDSS1420A) are in agreement with the average star-forming galaxy at their respective stellar mass, as indicated by the solid blue line.

gmw_00007909.name: GN25
gmw_00007909.overview: GN25 is a high-redshift starburst and active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:0910.2464]. Its spectrum shows signs of moderate X-ray absorption, and up to half of its submillimeter emission may be powered by the active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0910.2464].

sources.summary: The paper reports moderate X-ray absorption in GN25, finds the transmission model preferred over reflection, and allows that an AGN could power up to half of its submillimeter emission.

quotes.quote: A further two sources (GN25 and GN40) show signs of more moderate absorption in their spectra.
Exceptions are GN04 (Southern source), GN25, GN39 (Southern source) and GN40 where the transmission model is better (all with C>5 compared to the other model).

Taking the estimates of the maximum possible contribution allowed by the spectral fits, and the most extreme bolometric correction, we find that GN25 and GN40 might also have as much as 50 per cent of their submm emission powered by the AGN (Fig. bol_lum ).

gmw_00007910.name: GN40
gmw_00007910.overview: GN40 is a high-redshift starburst and active galactic nucleus (AGN) host galaxy [arXiv:0910.2464]. Its spectral data is best fit by a transmission model, showing a low reflection fraction and indicating that the AGN may power up to 50 percent of its submillimeter emission [arXiv:0910.2464].

sources.summary: The paper reports GN40's low reflection fraction, a preferred transmission-model fit, and the possibility that an AGN powers up to half of its submillimeter emission.

quotes.quote: The reflection fractions range from 5 (GN40), to being completely reflection dominated (GN22, GN39-Southern source).
Exceptions are GN04 (Southern source), GN25, GN39 (Southern source) and GN40 where the transmission model is better (all with C>5 compared to the other model).

Taking the estimates of the maximum possible contribution allowed by the spectral fits, and the most extreme bolometric correction, we find that GN25 and GN40 might also have as much as 50 per cent of their submm emission powered by the AGN (Fig. bol_lum ).

gmw_00007911.name: ESO 138-G01
gmw_00007911.overview: ESO 138-G01 is a disky, non-star-forming Seyfert 2 active galaxy [arXiv:1803.00740]. It hosts both old and young stellar populations, which may be the result of a recent minor merger event that funneled lower-metallicity gas to the nucleus [arXiv:1803.00740].

sources.summary: ESO 138-G01 is described as a disky Seyfert 2 with subsolar nuclear metallicity, an inverse ENLR abundance gradient, and evidence for recent low-metallicity gas inflow or minor merger activity.

quotes.quote: This is a disky, non-star forming galaxy that features luminous Seyfert 2 AGN activity. The nuclear gas metallicity is slightly sub-Solar, despite the estimated stellar mass of M_* = 10^ 10.8 M_ Thomas_2017_S7DR2 . In addition we measure an inverse abundance gradient in the ENLR, with abundances reaching up to 1.1 Solar in outer parts of the ENLR. Alloin_1992_ESO138G01 found that this object harbors both an old stellar population and a younger population with an age less than 1 Gyr. These observations could potentially be explained by a recent minor merger event.

In this scenario an infalling smaller galaxy would provide the lower metallicity gas, tidal effects in the merger would funnel the gas to the nucleus, and the relatively unobscured, lower-metallicity Seyfert AGN would result. The radial inflow of accreted gas explains the inverse metallicity gradient, and star formation from the newly accreted gas would explain the younger stellar population.

gmw_00007912.name: SDSSJ0041-0914
gmw_00007912.overview: SDSSJ0041-0914 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1204.3823]. It exhibits a deep NaI absorption feature, high sodium enhancement, and stellar models for the galaxy predict a steep, bottom-heavy initial mass function slope [arXiv:1204.3823].

sources.summary: The paper singles out SDSSJ0041-0914 as an SDSS early-type galaxy with unusually deep NaI absorption, high sodium enhancement, and models predicting a steep IMF slope.

quotes.quote: The XLENS ETG (SDSSJ0912+0029) and an SDSS ETG (SDSSJ0041-0914) appear to require both an extreme dwarf-rich IMF and a high sodium enhancement ( [ Na/Fe ] =+0.4 ).
We examine one system, SDSSJ0041-0914, separately because it has a NaI0.82 feature comparably deep to the XLENS system.

In both cases, the models seem consistent with a Salpeter IMF at the low-dispersion end and a slightly bottom-heavy IMF for the high-dispersion end, if these effects are accounted for, but the models predict a steeper IMF slope of x 3.0-3.5 for both the XLENS galaxy SDSSJ0912+0029 and SDSSJ0041-0914.

gmw_00007913.name: A05
gmw_00007913.overview: The galaxy A05 is classified as an E+A galaxy [arXiv:0903.4719] that features a disk-like major-axis structure [arXiv:0903.4719] and an irregular central color structure [arXiv:0903.4719]. It exhibits a gas-star velocity mismatch suggestive of ionized gas outflows [arXiv:2009.11498] and possesses a non-classical bulge [arXiv:2009.11498].

sources.summary: The paper treats 850 1 I as a bright core sub-mm galaxy without a spectral redshift; photometric redshifts place it outside the cluster, but its large errors leave membership uncertain.

quotes.quote: 0.85 Bright Cluster Core Sources In 2018Coogan no spectral redshifts were detected for sources 850 1 I and 850 1 J (A5 and A4 in 2018Coogan ). When we calculated photometric redshifts for them we found that 850 1 I was not placed in the cluster for either method, both indicating it had a redshift of 2.8.
Combined 850 1 I and 850 1 J contribute 70 of the 870 m flux for the cluster core, meaning that the chance of these galaxies being interlopers is extremely small.

We should note that the errors on 850 1 I are substantial for both EAZY and CIGALE , with both having z > 1 . Whilst there is a chance it could be in the cluster the large error means we cannot say for sure, and therefore the source is excluded from the rest of the analysis.

sources.summary: The paper describes E+A 5 as an E+A galaxy with a somewhat disk-like isophotal profile, disk-like major-axis structure, and irregular central colour structure.

quotes.quote: The exception is the isophotal profile of E+A 2 (and to a lesser extent E+A 5) which appears more typical of a disk i.e. a strong downward concavity in the surface brightness profile.
E+A 2 and E+A 5 reveal disk--like structures along their major axes consistent with the shapes of their isophotal profiles.
Two galaxies have irregular color structure in their centres (E+A 1,E+A 5) and two little structure at all (E+A 4, E+A 9) whilst one galaxy in our sample shows a clear blue core (E+A 6).

sources.summary: The paper describes A05 as a target with gas-star velocity mismatch suggestive of outflows, no central broad wing component, and a low-n bulge model indicating a non-classical bulge.

quotes.quote: For example, A02, A04, A05, A06, A07, and A08 show a radial velocity profile of gas, which is inconsistent with that of stars, suggesting that that ionized gas is under the effect of outflows. The radial profile of gas velocity dispersion also shows a different pattern compared to that of stars. Some of these targets (e.g., A04, A07, A09, and A10) show much higher velocity dispersion of \ than stars at the center, indicating the presence of non-gravitational effects. The different kinematics between gas and stars are expected from the \ emission line profile since all but the three targets (A02, A03, A05) show a broad wing component at the central region in Figure o3fit .

The central region of seven targets were modeled with a n=4 component, while three targets (A04,A05, A09) were modeled with n 0.5, 1.4, and 1.9, respectively, suggesting that the bulge is not a classical bulge.

gmw_00007914.name: A07
gmw_00007914.overview: The galaxy A07 is a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.11498] that exhibits a disturbed morphology and appears to be undergoing an interaction [arXiv:0903.4719]. It features a red core [arXiv:0903.4719] and displays ionized gas outflows indicated by kinematics that differ from its stars [arXiv:2009.11498].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A07 as a previously classified type 1 AGN whose gas radial velocity and central velocity dispersion differ from the stars, consistent with non-gravitational outflow effects.

quotes.quote: Six objects, namely, A01, A03, A04, A07, A08, A10 were previously classified as type 1 AGN by 2006A&A...455..773V . The classification is presumably based on the presence of a broad \ component.

For example, A02, A04, A05, A06, A07, and A08 show a radial velocity profile of gas, which is inconsistent with that of stars, suggesting that that ionized gas is under the effect of outflows. The radial profile of gas velocity dispersion also shows a different pattern compared to that of stars. Some of these targets (e.g., A04, A07, A09, and A10) show much higher velocity dispersion of \ than stars at the center, indicating the presence of non-gravitational effects. The different kinematics between gas and stars are expected from the \ emission line profile since all but the three targets (A02, A03, A05) show a broad wing component at the central region in Figure o3fit .

sources.summary: The paper describes E+A 7 as disturbed and apparently interacting, with a red core and one of the two strongest negative colour gradients.

quotes.quote: Inspection of the g -band images (see column 1, Fig. 1), as indeed the -band images of our sample reveals that six out of ten galaxies in the sample (E+A 1, E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 8, E+A 9, E+A 10) show either tidal bridges or tails or clearly disturbed morphologies with at least three of these apparently currently undergoing an interaction with a companion (E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 9).
A red core is evident in 4 of the 9 galaxies (E+A 2, E+A 7, E+A 8, E+A 10).
Note, however, while the positive gradient galaxies have mild slopes and are not too dissimilar to the flat gradient galaxies the two most striking gradients (E+A 7 and E+A 8) are both negative.

gmw_00007915.name: A08
gmw_00007915.overview: A08 is an E+A galaxy [arXiv:0903.4719] that hosts a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.11498] and contains a bar structure [arXiv:2009.11498]. The galaxy exhibits a disturbed morphology [arXiv:0903.4719], a red core [arXiv:0903.4719], and gas-star kinematic mismatches that suggest the presence of outflows [arXiv:2009.11498].

sources.summary: The paper notes A08 was observed along the minor axis to avoid a prominent bar, was previously classified as a type 1 AGN, and shows gas-star kinematic mismatch suggestive of outflows.

quotes.quote: In advance we determined the position angle (PA) of the slit along the major axis of host galaxy, except for one target (A08), for which the slit was positioned along the minor axis to avoid a prominent bar structure.
Six objects, namely, A01, A03, A04, A07, A08, A10 were previously classified as type 1 AGN by 2006A&A...455..773V . The classification is presumably based on the presence of a broad \ component.
For example, A02, A04, A05, A06, A07, and A08 show a radial velocity profile of gas, which is inconsistent with that of stars, suggesting that that ionized gas is under the effect of outflows.

sources.summary: The paper describes E+A 8 as an E+A galaxy with disturbed morphology or tidal structure, a red core, and one of the two strongest negative colour gradients.

quotes.quote: Inspection of the g -band images (see column 1, Fig. 1), as indeed the -band images of our sample reveals that six out of ten galaxies in the sample (E+A 1, E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 8, E+A 9, E+A 10) show either tidal bridges or tails or clearly disturbed morphologies with at least three of these apparently currently undergoing an interaction with a companion (E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 9).
A red core is evident in 4 of the 9 galaxies (E+A 2, E+A 7, E+A 8, E+A 10).
Note, however, while the positive gradient galaxies have mild slopes and are not too dissimilar to the flat gradient galaxies the two most striking gradients (E+A 7 and E+A 8) are both negative.

gmw_00007916.name: A09
gmw_00007916.overview: A09 is an almost face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2009.11498] that exhibits a disturbed morphology and appears to be currently interacting with a companion [arXiv:0903.4719]. The galaxy hosts a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.11498] and displays a lower stellar velocity dispersion drop at its very center [arXiv:2009.11498].

sources.summary: The paper confirms A09 as a true type 1 AGN with broad lines, describes it as nearly face-on with spiral arms, and finds a central stellar-dispersion drop.

quotes.quote: Based on the \ fitting analysis, we detected a broad component of \ for two targets, A09 and A10, indicating that these objects are type 1 AGN (Figure o3fit ). The FWHM of the broad \ is 1,451 \ and 1,060 , respectively, for A09 and A10. In the case of the broad , we obtained the FWHM of the broad component as 2,226 \ and 1,849 , respectively for A09 and A10. Note that the width of \ is larger than that of .

In the case of A09 and A10, the rotation feature is less clear. For A09, this is probably due to the orientation since the target galaxy is almost face-on while the morphology of the target clearly shows spiral arms. A10 may not have a strong rotation component as expected from the morphology (see Fig. slit ). The radial profile of stellar velocity dispersion generally shows a peak at the center and a radial decrease, which is a typical trend for a gravitational potential of host galaxies while the stellar velocity dispersion at the center is mostly less than 150 . A few targets (i.e., A04 and A09) show a drop with a lower stellar velocity dispersion at the very center, which has been reported in the literature for other galaxies e.g., Kent+90, Bender+94, Pinkney+03, Kang+13 .

sources.summary: The paper describes E+A 9 as disturbed and apparently interacting, with little central colour structure but larger-scale patchiness in its colour distribution.

quotes.quote: Inspection of the g -band images (see column 1, Fig. 1), as indeed the -band images of our sample reveals that six out of ten galaxies in the sample (E+A 1, E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 8, E+A 9, E+A 10) show either tidal bridges or tails or clearly disturbed morphologies with at least three of these apparently currently undergoing an interaction with a companion (E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 9).
Two galaxies have irregular color structure in their centres (E+A 1,E+A 5) and two little structure at all (E+A 4, E+A 9) whilst one galaxy in our sample shows a clear blue core (E+A 6).

In Figure fig:images several galaxies have negative colour gradients becoming bluer with increasing galacto--centric radius (e.g. E+A 7) whilst others have little sign of a gradient at all (e.g. E+A 10) or have large scale patchiness in their colour distribution (e.g. E+A 9).

gmw_00007917.name: A10
gmw_00007917.overview: A10 is a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.11498] that exhibits a disturbed morphology [arXiv:0903.4719] and a red core [arXiv:0903.4719]. The galaxy lacks a strong rotation component [arXiv:2009.11498] and shows a high central gas velocity dispersion driven by non-gravitational effects [arXiv:2009.11498].

sources.summary: The paper confirms A10 as a true type 1 AGN with broad lines, notes weak or unclear rotation from morphology, and finds high central gas velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Based on the \ fitting analysis, we detected a broad component of \ for two targets, A09 and A10, indicating that these objects are type 1 AGN (Figure o3fit ). The FWHM of the broad \ is 1,451 \ and 1,060 , respectively, for A09 and A10. In the case of the broad , we obtained the FWHM of the broad component as 2,226 \ and 1,849 , respectively for A09 and A10. Note that the width of \ is larger than that of .

In the case of A09 and A10, the rotation feature is less clear. For A09, this is probably due to the orientation since the target galaxy is almost face-on while the morphology of the target clearly shows spiral arms. A10 may not have a strong rotation component as expected from the morphology (see Fig. slit ).
Some of these targets (e.g., A04, A07, A09, and A10) show much higher velocity dispersion of \ than stars at the center, indicating the presence of non-gravitational effects.

sources.summary: The paper describes E+A 10 as disturbed and red-cored, with spectral fitting showing little contribution from old stellar templates.

quotes.quote: Inspection of the g -band images (see column 1, Fig. 1), as indeed the -band images of our sample reveals that six out of ten galaxies in the sample (E+A 1, E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 8, E+A 9, E+A 10) show either tidal bridges or tails or clearly disturbed morphologies with at least three of these apparently currently undergoing an interaction with a companion (E+A 4, E+A 7, E+A 9).
A red core is evident in 4 of the 9 galaxies (E+A 2, E+A 7, E+A 8, E+A 10).

As an example of the lack of contribution from the old stellar templates the mean difference (per spaxel) in the ^2/ DOF values for E+A 10 returned by fitting with the different sets of templates is 0.09 .

gmw_00007918.name: companion galaxy just to the east
aliases_local: companion galaxy just to the east
gmw_00007918.overview: This companion galaxy just to the east of IC 2497 is a smaller spiral galaxy with a closely matching redshift [arXiv:1206.3797]. It exhibits a high degree of symmetry and modest luminosity, suggesting it is unlikely to have lost the large mass of neutral hydrogen observed in the system's southern tail [arXiv:1206.3797].

sources.original_name: companion galaxy just to the east
sources.summary: The paper identifies a smaller spiral companion near IC 2497 with matching redshift, symmetry, and modest luminosity, arguing it is associated but unlikely to have supplied the H I tail.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy just to the east lies at a closely matching redshift (section 2.5.2), making it a probable physical companion. However, its high degree of symmetry, and modest luminosity (5.5 times smaller in the band) compared to IC 2497 itself, make it unlikely to have lost the 9 10^9 M _ of H I seen in the curved tail to the south of both galaxies Josza . The symmetry of the companion, and radial-velocity difference near zero, place limits on the kind of encounter it might have undergone with IC 2497.

These can be broadly satisfied if the relative orbit of the companion galaxy is inclined 30-45 ^ to the plane of IC 2497, and if we see the companion as currently moving clockwise on the sky and toward us. Still, the large H I mass in the external features suggests that much or all of it came from IC 2497 or a now-disrupted companion, again at odds with the usual situation in such unequal-mass encounters. Despite its proximity in position and redshift, this smaller spiral companion may thus not be directly relevant to the system's tidal history.

gmw_00007919.name: SDSS J115630.63+500822.1
gmw_00007919.overview: SDSS J115630.63+500822.1 is an extreme starburst galaxy located at a distance of 1200 Mpc [arXiv:1606.04536, arXiv:1910.12882]. It features a compact continuum [arXiv:1606.04536], a star formation rate 40 times higher than the Milky Way [arXiv:1606.04536], and a surrounding 9-kpc shell [arXiv:1910.12882].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J115630.63+500822.1/J1156 as an intense starburst with compact continuum, extended coronal gas emission, high star formation, and spectroscopic outflow diagnostics.

quotes.quote: Our target, SDSS J115630.63+500822.1, is very compact in the continuum but displays \ emission to radii of 23 kpc. The surface brightness profile is well fit by an exponential with a scale length of 7.5 kpc. This is ten times the size of the photoionized gas, and we estimate that about 1/6 the total \ luminosity comes from resonantly scattered continuum radiation.

Basic Properties of J1156 SDSS J115630.63+500822.1 (hereafter J1156) lies at a distance of 1200 Mpc (z=0.235) and was selected to maximize the chances of detecting \ emission. It has a star formation rate 40 times higher than the Milky Way (40 ), and an optical spectrum that is rich with strong emission lines suggestive of active star formation. The SDSS image and spectrum of J1156 are shown in Figure fig:sdss .

sources.summary: The paper compares J1156+5008 to cooling-superwind models as an extreme nearby starburst with a 9-kpc shell and possible central/nuclear emission, while noting some central emission may be spurious.

quotes.quote: Hayes2016 present imaging in \ 1037 of an extreme, nearby starburst, J1156+5008. While the scale of this starburst and resultant emission is much larger than our models, the qualitative features may be consistent with our predictions. There is a clear, 9-kpc shell around the starburst.

In one analysis, a central, 1-kpc region centered on the starburst also shows positive emission. However, the authors suggest that this apparent emission is spurious, based on their lack of confidence in the continuum subtraction method. We note however, that our models do predict strong central \ emission, in addition to the bright shell (Figure fig:R5V250UV ), and perhaps some nuclear emission is not ruled out in this object (M. Hayes, private communication).

gmw_00007920.name: SDSS J0818 + 1722
gmw_00007920.overview: SDSS J0818 + 1722 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1001.3415] with a revised redshift of z=6.02 [arXiv:1110.0539]. Its red-side continuum is poorly fit by standard models, displaying unusually broad and shifted emission-line features [arXiv:2006.10744].

sources.summary: The paper builds on a prior z=6.02 quasar measurement and revises J0818 + 1722's near-zone temperature lower after updating its redshift, adding five absorption lines.

quotes.quote: (hereafter B10) recently side-stepped this difficulty by measuring the IGM temperature in the vicinity of the z=6.02 quasar SDSS J0818 + 1722. Using detailed numerical simulations, B10 demonstrated that the cumulative probability distribution function (CPDF) of Doppler parameters measured from absorption lines in the highly ionised near-zone is sensitive to in-situ photo-heating by the quasar, as well as heating from earlier ionising sources. B10 therefore used the Doppler parameter CPDF to infer the IGM temperature at mean density within 5 proper Mpc of the quasar, (T_ 0 / K)=4.37^ +0.09 _ 0.15 , at 68 per cent confidence.

Although our revised measurement for J0818 + 1722 is formally consistent within the 68 per cent confidence interval, it is systematically lower by 0.13 dex. The first reason for this difference is that in B10 the redshift of J0818+1722 was given as z=6.00 ( Fan06 ). The revised redshift of z=6.02 presented by now extends the region where we fit absorption lines by 855 km s^ 1 , resulting in an additional five absorption lines in the near-zone (yielding a total of 30). These lines lower the median Doppler parameter by 1.7 km s^ 1 , and shift the temperature constraint downward by 0.03 dex.

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J0818 + 1722 to measure z=6 proximity-zone temperatures and constrain the local reionisation history within 33 comoving Mpc.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present the first direct measurement of the IGM temperature around a z=6 quasar by analysing the Doppler widths of absorption lines in the proximity zone of SDSS J0818 + 1722. We use a high resolution ( R= 40 000 ) Keck/HIRES spectrum in combination with detailed numerical modelling to obtain the temperature at mean density, T_ 0 =23 600 ^ 5000 _ 6900 K ( ^ 9200 _ 9300 K ) at 68 (95) per cent confidence assuming a prior probability 13 500 K T_ 0 38 500 K following and reionisation. This enables us to place an upper limit on the redshift of reionisation, z_ H , within 33 comoving Mpc of SDSS J0818 + 1722. If the quasar reionises the in its vicinity, then in the limit of instantaneous reionisation we infer z_ H <9.0 (11.0) at 68 (95) per cent confidence assuming photoheating is the dominant heat source and that reionisation is driven by ionising sources with soft spectra, typical of population II stars. If the and in the IGM around SDSS J0818 + 1722 are instead reionised simultaneously by a population of massive metal-free stars, characterised by very hard ionising spectra, we obtain a tighter upper limit of z_ H <8.4 (9.4) .

sources.summary: The paper finds J0818+1722's red-side continuum is poorly fit by all tested models, with unusually broad and shifted emission-line features; varying redshift or PCA convergence did not fix the mismatch.

quotes.quote: The red side continuum of J0818+1722 is not well fit by any model. This is perhaps concerning since its stranger features (exceedingly broad and shifted C  IV emission line, extended C  III ] and Si  IV lines) are similar to what is seen in the z=7.5 quasar Banados18 . Modifying the redshift by up to z = 0.1 , or using more complex convergence techniques to fit the PCA on the red side, did not alleviate this problem significantly.

gmw_00007921.name: Mrk 331
gmw_00007921.overview: Mrk 331 is a luminous infrared galaxy located at a distance of 75 Mpc [arXiv:1210.5354]. It hosts a starburst region where the emission from a stellar ring is brighter than its nucleus, and its radio emission is concentrated within a sub-kiloparsec region [arXiv:astro-ph/0302377, arXiv:1210.5354].

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 331 with high-velocity starburst clouds, lower-dust optical components, no old-star SED contribution, and ring emission brighter than the nucleus.

quotes.quote: Mrk 331 The continuum SED of Mrk 331 which is shown in Fig. 14 shows that the data are well explained by model M22 (solid lines) with d/g=4 10^ 4 , \ = 500 \ and t=3.3 Myr. However, a model corresponding to t=0.0 and \ = 100 \ better fits the data in the optical range (M3). The corresponding d/g is 10 times lower. As we found for the other galaxies, a model with \ = 200 \ is needed to fit the data in the IR (M18, represented by dash-dot lines). We use a starburst corresponding to t=3.3 Myr, because the one corresponding to t=0.0 Myr shows too high [ OIII ] / [ OI ] line ratios.

The SED of Mrk 331 does not show the emission from old stars. A possible explanation comes from the presence of the ring. In Fig. 15 the flux from different regions are given. It can be noticed that the flux from the ring is higher than the flux from the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 331 as a LIRG radio example, giving its distance scale and showing compact sub-kiloparsec 8.46 GHz emission with a 29.3 mJy total flux.

quotes.quote: As an example, I show in Figure fig1 an 8.46 GHz VLA image of the LIRG Mrk 331. At a distance of D=75 Mpc to Mrk 331, 1 \ corresponds to a linear size of 357 pc. Note that all radio emission is concentrated in a region whose angular size is less that 3 \ in diameter, or less than 1 kpc. The total 8.5 GHz flux density is of 29.3 mJy, corresponding to a monochromatic radio luminosity of 1.6 29 .

gmw_00007922.name: the Emerald
gmw_00007922.overview: The Emerald is a bright, strongly gravitationally lensed dusty star-forming galaxy at redshift z=2.236 [arXiv:1811.11185]. It features a fragmented disk containing two massive star-forming clumps, one of which exhibits a molecular wind signature [arXiv:1811.11185].

sources.summary: The paper studies the Emerald as a bright lensed z=2.236 dusty star-forming galaxy, modeling its magnification, massive clumps, fragmented gas disk, and molecular wind that may recycle gas.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The phase center of the observations was located at =11h27m14.60s and =+42 28 25.0 .

quotes.quote: Received / Accepted We report the discovery of a molecular wind signature from a massive intensely star-forming clump of a few 10^9 M _ , in the strongly gravitationally lensed submillimeter galaxy ``the Emerald'' (PLCK G165.7+49.0) at z=2.236 . The Emerald is amongst the brightest high-redshift galaxies on the submillimeter sky, and was initially discovered with the Planck satellite. The system contains two magnificient structures with projected lengths of 28.5 \ and 21 \ formed by multiple, near-infrared arcs, falling behind a massive galaxy cluster at z=0.35 , as well as an adjacent filament that has so far escaped discovery in other wavebands. We used HST /WFC3 and CFHT optical and near-infrared imaging together with IRAM and SMA interferometry of the CO(4--3) line and 850 m dust emission to characterize the foreground lensing mass distribution, construct a lens model with Lenstool , and calculate gravitational magnification factors between 20 and 50 in most of the source. The majority of the star formation takes place within two massive star-forming clumps which are marginally gravitationally bound and embedded in a 9 10^ 10 M _ , fragmented disk with 20 gas fraction.

One of the clumps shows a pronounced blue wing in the CO(4--3) line profile, which we interpret as a wind signature. The mass outflow rates are high enough for us to suspect that the clump might become unbound within a few tens of Myr, unless the outflowing gas can be replenished by gas accretion from the surrounding disk.

gmw_00007923.name: NGC 3110
gmw_00007923.overview: NGC 3110 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2104.06790, arXiv:1809.04788] interacting with a minor companion to its southwest [arXiv:2104.06790, arXiv:1809.04788]. This interaction has likely shaped its asymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:1809.04788], which host abundant star formation fueled by a large mass of molecular gas [arXiv:1809.04788].

sources.summary: The paper reports SN 2015ca in NGC 3110's northern spiral arm and models the host's multiwavelength SED, finding a disk model with a minor starburst component and tabulating its CCSN rate.

quotes.quote: The discovery image of SN 2015ca 2015ca in NGC 3110 was observed on 2015 March 11; see Fig. fig:ngc3110 . As a reference image a NACO K_s band image from 2010 December 28 was used randriamanakoto2013 . The subtracted image revealed a point source along the northern spiral arm of the galaxy 4.3 \ North and 8.0 \ West from the nucleus, corresponding to a projected distance of 3.5 kpc.

Table tab:sed shows for NGC 3110 the SF rate, starburst age, CCSN rate and the origin of its bolometric luminosity. This is based on modelling the multi-wavelength SED of NGC 3110, using data points available from the literature ranging from optical to submillimetre photometry u2012 , by combining libraries of starburst, AGN torus and disk component models. For more details see herrero2017 and references therein, with the difference that a spheroidal/cirrus component was fitted instead of a disk (Efstathiou et al, in preparation ). We found that the SED of the host galaxy NGC 3110 was best fitted by a disk model with a minor starburst component. Table tab:sed shows the best fit parameters and Fig. fig:sed the best fitting model.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3110 as an interacting barred spiral with bright arms and elongated nucleus, PA and inclination changes, rotating-disc kinematics, pseudo-bulge signature, and dispersion drops.

quotes.quote: NGC 3110 : This SB(rs)b galaxy is interacting with a minor companion located at 40 kpc SW Yuan+10,Larson+16 and presents two bright spiral arms protruding from an elongated bright nucleus. The faint transition between the nucleus and the spiral arms yields abrupt changes in the inclination and PA determination due to loss of S/N. At larger scales, the PA and inclination show a smooth profile. As IC 5179, this galaxy has differences between the brightness of both arms that bias the centre determination at >5 kpc (see Fig. fig:morph_3110 ).

The kinematic analysis reveals a rotating-disc pattern orientated with PA 0 , aligned with the elongated bulge. In addition, the velocity curve shows a steeper profile at <0.5 kpc, consistent with a pseudo-bulge. We observe velocity dispersion drops located in two blobs at 0.4 kpc NW and NE, values expected in SF regions ( 60 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3110 as a barred Sb LIRG interacting with a minor companion, with asymmetric arms, high central star formation, and abundant molecular gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 3110 NGC 3110 is usually classified as a barred Sb galaxy (Table 1). It is located at a distance of D = 75.2 Mpc (1 \ corresponds to 352 pc), and it is interacting with a minor companion located to the SW, which likely contributed to the formation of its two asymmetric spiral arms and a central bar-like feature as seen in Fig. fig1 . The optical diameter is 1 86, or 39.3 kpc.

This galaxy has an overall high SFR of about 20--30 M _ yr ^ 1 , as obtained from IR and H data 2002ApJ...568...88Y,2002ApJS..143...47D , mostly concentrated towards its center. The SF is fueled by a large mass of molecular gas , estimated to be M_ mol 2 10 ^ 10 M _ (Table 1). There is also abundant SF in the two arms of the galaxy based on H maps (Fig. fig2 ), with several bright H 2 knots distributed along them 2004AJ....127..736H,2002ApJS..143...47D and in fact the arms contribute to up to 35 of the FIR flux 2000ApJS..131..413Z .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3110 as a face-on galaxy with spiral-arm regions, bright emission resembling H alpha, enhanced extranuclear line ratios, and deepest silicate absorption at the nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3110 It is a face-on galaxy with numerous \ regions along the spiral arms Hattori04 . The \ emission of this galaxy is especially bright and it resembles that of the H emission. The \ ratio of the extranuclear \ regions is 5 times larger than the nuclear ratio. The deepest silicate absorption is located at the nucleus.

gmw_00007924.name: host of GRB 980703
gmw_00007924.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 980703 is a blue, compact star-forming galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0103058] that is classified as a late-type disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0508197]. It exhibits an asymmetric, somewhat irregular morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0103058, arXiv:astro-ph/0508197] and is highly luminous in both the radio and infrared bands [arXiv:astro-ph/0508197].

sources.summary: The paper finds the GRB 980703 host is a blue, compact star-forming galaxy with an exponential-disk-like profile, high star formation rate, and irregular residual morphology.

quotes.quote: We present deep HST /STIS and ground-based photometry of the host galaxy of the gamma-ray burst GRB 980703 taken 17, 551, 710, and 716 days after the burst. We find that the host is a blue, slightly over-luminous galaxy with V_ gal = 23.00 0.10 , (V R) _ gal = 0.43 0.13 , and a centre that is 0.2 mag bluer than the outer regions of the galaxy. The galaxy has a star-formation rate of 8 - 13 M _ yr ^ 1 , assuming no extinction in the host.

We find that the galaxy is best fit by a Sersic R^ 1/n profile with n 1.0 and a half-light radius of 0 13 ( = 0.72 h_ 100 ^ 1 proper kpc). This corresponds to an exponential disk with a scale radius of 0 22 ( = 1.21 h_ 100 ^ 1 proper kpc). Subtracting a fit with elliptical isophotes leaves large residuals, which suggests that the host galaxy has a somewhat irregular morphology, but we are unable to connect the location of GRB 980703 with any special features in the host. The host galaxy appears to be a typical example of a compact star forming galaxy similar to those found in the Hubble Deep Field North.

sources.summary: The paper says the GRB 980703 host is best fit by an exponential profile, appears as a late-type asymmetric disk, and is radio luminous with high infrared luminosity and star formation.

quotes.quote: With this caveat in mind, we find that the exponential model provides the best fit for the hosts of GRB 970508, GRB 980703 and GRB 990712 (see Table 2 where the ^ 2 values for these galaxies are listed for both fits).
Similarly, the host of GRB 980703, studied by Holland et al. (2001) with STIS on HST, is found to be a late-type disk with asymmetric structures, similar to what we find.
There are also examples of radio luminous hosts, such as GRB 980703, which have total infrared luminosities of > 10^ 12 L _ and star formation rates approaching 1000 M _ yr 1 (Berger et al. 2001).

gmw_00007925.name: host of GRB 990705
gmw_00007925.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 990705 is a large, grand-design late-type spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.8424 [arXiv:1201.4947, arXiv:astro-ph/0508197]. It is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy with obscured star-forming activity [arXiv:1201.4947].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 990705 host as a z = 0.8424 star-forming LIRG detected by Spitzer, with radio limits consistent with infrared-derived star formation rates.

quotes.quote: The GRB 990705 host galaxy is a star-forming galaxy at z = 0.8424 lefl02 . The SFR derived from UV luminosity is SFR(UV) 5--8 lefl02 and 3.31  M_  yr ^ 1 sven10 . The SFR based on the [ O ii ] line is SFR( [ O ii ] ) = 6.96  M_  yr ^ 1 sava09 . The galaxy is detected with Spitzer at 4.5 and 24  m lefl06 . The IR luminosity inferred from the 24- m detection is 1.8^ +2.1 _ 0.6 10^ 11   L_ , classifying the galaxy as a luminous IR galaxy (LIRG).

Although the Spitzer observations indicate that the GRB 990705 host galaxy has obscured star-forming activity lefl06 , our 2 upper limit on SFR is consistent with the SFRs derived from the IR observations after considering their uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 990705 host as a large grand-design late-type spiral, with a scale length comparable to the Galactic scale length and outsized influence on low-redshift host sizes.

quotes.quote: The scale lengths of the hosts also range from very small, 0.14 kpc in the case of GRB 991208, to a value that is comparable to the Galactic scale length: 4.7 kpc for the host of GRB 990705.
For example, the host of GRB 990705 is clearly a ``grand-design'' late-type spiral (Le Floc'h et al. 2002), in agreement with its position in the C-A diagram.
The average GRB host size at z < 1.2 reduces even further to 4.5 1.5 kpc if we remove the GRB 990705 host, which is a large spiral system at z = 0.84 .

gmw_00007926.name: NGC 326
gmw_00007926.overview: NGC 326 is a dumbbell brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:0908.4391] and a prototype of the X-shaped radio galaxy class [arXiv:1907.03274]. Its radio lobes, which lack compact hotspots [arXiv:0811.1907], emanate from the northern component of the double galaxy system [arXiv:0912.3001, arXiv:2004.13025].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 326 as a prototypical X/Z-shaped radio galaxy whose wings, Chandra X-ray structures, and AGN outburst history favor jet-axis reorientation and show interaction with the intracluster medium.

quotes.quote: NGC 326 is one of the most prominent X- or Z-shaped radio galaxies (XRGs/ZRGs) and has been the subject of several studies attempting to explain its morphology through either fluid motions or reorientation of the jet axis. We examine a 100 ks Chandra X-ray Observatory exposure and find several features associated with the radio galaxy: a high-temperature front that may indicate a shock, high-temperature knots around the rim of the radio emission, and a cavity associated with the eastern wing of the radio galaxy. A reasonable interpretation of these features in light of the radio data allows us to reconstruct the history of the AGN outbursts.

B2 0055+26 is a dramatic XRG/ZRG, with wings longer (in projection) than the primary lobes (Figure radio_maps ). The wings are highly collimated over 100 kpc. Its host galaxy is the northern component of the dumbbell NGC 326 colla75 and the entire system resides in the galaxy cluster Zw 0056.9+2636 zwicky68 . Hereafter, we adopt the convention in the literature and refer to the whole system as NGC 326.

sources.summary: New LOFAR observations show NGC 326's X-shaped lobes extend into a complex radio structure, which the paper attributes mainly to hydrodynamical effects from group or cluster merger activity.

quotes.quote: We present new 144-MHz LOFAR observations of the prototypical `X-shaped' radio galaxy NGC 326, which show that the formerly known wings of the radio lobes extend smoothly into a large-scale, complex radio structure. We argue that this structure is most likely the result of hydrodynamical effects in an ongoing group or cluster merger, for which pre-existing X-ray and optical data provide independent evidence. The large-scale radio structure is hard to explain purely in terms of jet reorientation due to the merger of binary black holes, a previously proposed explanation for the inner structure of NGC 326. For this reason, we suggest that the simplest model is one in which the merger-related hydrodynamical processes account for all the source structure, though we do not rule out the possibility that a black hole merger has occurred.

The first winged source to be discovered Ekers+78 , and a prototype of the X-shaped class Wirth+82 , is the nearby radio galaxy B2 0055+26 or 4C 26.03, usually referred to by the name of its optical identification, NGC 326. In this paper we report on new observations of this galaxy with the Low-Frequency Array (LOFAR: vanHaarlem+13 ) that show conclusively that the large-scale structure of the source is generated by hydrodynamical effects, presumably related to bulk motion with respect to the ambient medium.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0055+26 as a dumbbell brightest cluster galaxy with twin large-scale jets, an X-ray peak at the core, and possible parsec-scale extension.

quotes.quote: 0055+26 (NGC326) -- This galaxy is the Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) of the Zwicky cluster 0056.9+2636. It appears as a dumbbell galaxy with two equally bright nuclei with projected separation of 7''. The large scale radio emission shows twin jets along direction P.A. 135 ^ ( and references therein). There is a peak in X-ray emission consistent with the location of the radio galaxy core. In our VLBA image (not shown here), we found nuclear emission with a possible slight extension in the same PA as the kiloparsec-scale jets, with a total flux density of 7.5 mJy.

sources.summary: NGC 326 is a double galaxy whose northern component powers radio lobes; Chandra emission traces both galaxies, empty radio-tail channels, and thermal ISM near the AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 326 ( z=0.048 ) : NGC 326 is a double galaxy with the radio lobes emanating from the northern component; both galaxies show diffuse X-ray ISM emission, and the X-ray emission from the southern galaxy is exclusively thermal. The extent of the Chandra diffuse emission agrees well with the ROSAT data where there is overlap worrall95 . The long tails in the radio map appear to follow empty channels in the Chandra data, and the length of the tails relative to the active lobes provides constraints on backflow formation mechanisms gopal03 . The ISM emission for the northern component is extracted excluding the central point source; the temperature of kT = 0.68 keV agrees precisely with the temperature of the thermal model included in the AGN emission, where a PL component is also required.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 326 as a dumbbell X-shaped radio galaxy whose extended wings and off-nuclear eastern wing foot challenge backflow or cocoon confinement models.

quotes.quote: An earlier explanation, specifically devised for the rare dumbbell galaxies (elliptical galaxies with two nuclei, such as the XRG NGC 326), posited a more gradual reorientation of the large-scale jets during a close passage between two elliptical galaxies (Wirth, Smarr Gallagher 1982).

The difficulty is further underscored by the case of the XRG NGC 326 in which the wings are not only a few times longer than the primary lobes but the ``foot" of the eastern wing is located about 50 kpc from the nucleus, i.e., well outside the ISM of the galaxy (e.g., Murgia et al. \ 2001; Worrall et al., \ 1995). This situation makes it hard to identify a mechanism to ensure confinement of the hypothesized over-pressured cocoon.
We note that this assertion would hold even if the Z-deflections of the two jets occur at vastly different separations from the nucleus, as for instance seen in the XRG NGC 326 (see above).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 326 as an X-shaped G4Jy source, confirms the northern dumb-bell galaxy as the radio core host, and notes a LOFAR plume.

quotes.quote: NGC 326 (4C +26.03) is listed as G4Jy 105 (GLEAM J005823+265214; Figure Xshape b). This source has been observed by Murgia2001 at multiple frequencies using the VLA, and their figure 9 clearly shows that the radio core is associated with the northern member of the dumb-bell galaxy. We dismiss the AllWISE position closest to the centroid position, and confirm that the newly-chosen host-galaxy position is in agreement with the core position (subject to the resolution of AllWISE). A new LOw-Frequency ARray (LOFAR) image of this source reveals that the northernmost tip of the `X' extends into a long plume Hardcastle2019 , a hint of which is seen in the TGSS contours.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC326 as a radio-galaxy test of the code, noting its two optical cores and modeling host rotation from gravitational interaction against its radio map.

quotes.quote: Another test of our code is the radio galaxy NGC326; in particular we refer to the image at 1.4GHz as given in Figure 6 (bottom panel) of murgia : the basic input data as deduced from the radio map are reported in Table data_ngc326 . } In this case, the optical observations with HST reveal two cores, one coincident with the origin of the radio galaxy, and the other at the projected distance of 4.8 kpc . In this case, it is assumed that the rotation of the host galaxy is due to gravitational interaction; the total angle of rotation being _G = 90 ^ . Figure NGC326 reports on the obtained simulation and Figure NGC326_radio_theo reports the radio map superimposed on the simulation ; Table fine_ngc326 gives the data of output from the simulation. }

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 326 as a radio source with hotspot-free main lobes, exceptionally large wings, and a host that is part of a dumbbell galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: NGC 326: Radio images of this source have been presented by mur01 , and it has been noted that both of the pair of main lobes lack compact hotspots at their ends. The wings in NGC 326 are among the most spectacular of such structures: they are more than twice the main source in extent. The host is part of a dumb-bell galaxy pair.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 326 as an XRG with steeper high-frequency wing spectra from particle cooling, an outer-deviation morphology challenging back-flow models, and an X-ray cavity linked to a jet-slewing candidate.

quotes.quote: A detailed analysis of NGC 326 by Murgia2001 further pointed out a similar trend, with wings getting even steeper with higher frequency choices an effect of particle cooling; Fan2008 .

Caveats of the Back-flow Model While this model may be well-suited for explaining the X-shaped morphology in inner deviation sources and in some of the potential outer deviation sources e.g., Cotton2020 , it faces challenges in elucidating the origin of prominent outer deviation sources like NGC 326 Murgia2001 , J1153.9+5848 Bruni2021 , and J1159+5820 Misra2023 .
Later, in a low-exposure X-ray map, a prominent cavity has been associated to the wing structure in NGC 326 Hodges-kluck2012 , which has long been suggested to be another candidate with jet slewing.

gmw_00007927.name: C3
gmw_00007927.overview: C3 is a high-redshift companion galaxy that exhibits an extended gas structure [arXiv:2204.00793]. While its gas structure is extended, the galaxy's line emissions remain spatially unresolved [arXiv:2204.00793].

sources.summary: The paper confirms C3 as a companion galaxy and reports an extended gas structure, unresolved line emission, measured line fluxes, and an upper limit for another line.

quotes.quote: Companion galaxies Our re-analysis of the data confirms the three companion sources originally reported in venemans19 . In addition, we detect the or/and lines in some of the companion galaxies (namely C1, C2, and C3). The , , , and intensity maps of C1, C2, and C3 are shown in Figure companionfig . The spectral line fluxes for companion galaxies are measured in intensity maps through adopting line widths determined from super-high resolution data venemans19 .

As for C3, the intensity map suggests an extended gas structure, while the and emission are not spatially resolved. The extended feature is also observed in the super-high resolution data venemans19 . The and fluxes of C3 are 0.05 0.02 , and 0.07 0.02 . The flux upper limit for C3 is 0.05 .

gmw_00007928.name: X N 81 44
gmw_00007928.overview: X N 81 44 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits extended gas emission [arXiv:2008.01728]. This extended emission is likely ionized by star formation, which is spatially anti-correlated with the galaxy's high-velocity ionized gas outflows [arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: The paper identifies X N 81 44 as an extended-emission SUPER AGN host with excess residuals, a smooth line-centroid gradient, high-velocity ionized gas, and a velocity-gradient outflow structure.

quotes.quote: From Fig. fig:astrometry1 , we see excess residuals at a level of >2 for 6 SUPER targets - X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245 and S82X1905. In summary, from the joint analysis based on the COG and "PSF-subtraction" method we have identified seven sources from our sample which show extended emission. Further analysis on the spectra across the SINFONI FoV will therefore be restricted to the 7 targets, which show extended gas emission with at least one of the methods described above.

A smooth gradient is observed in some of the line centroid maps, e.g. in X N 81 44, X N 66 23, cid 346 and S82X1905, which could be indicative of a rotating disk in the host galaxy or a bi-polar ionized outflow. The observed extreme high velocities in the w_ 80 maps ( 1000 km/s) for most of these galaxies suggest that the kinematics of the ionized gas are also likely affected by the central AGN. For several of our targets, we do detect extended features in emission, which are not produced by wings of the PSF as demonstrated by the analysis performed in the previous sections (e.g. X N 115 23, CID 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245, S82X1905).

From the w_ 80 maps in Figs. fig:flux_velocity1 and fig:flux_velocity2 , it is clear that the ionized outflows detected in these AGN present a diversity of projected structures. In five sources (X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, S82X1905) there is a velocity gradient along a particular axis while for two objects (J1333+1649 and J1549+1245) the maps are more spherically symmetric. Some of the kinematic structures present in the w_ 80 maps could be consistent with a bi-conical outflow, where the inclination of the outflow with respect to the disk of the galaxy and the line-of-sight of the observer could explain the observed velocity maps: i.e. a high inclination of the outflow with respect to the line-of-sight will result in a clear velocity gradient along the outflow axis, with possibly both the blue-shifted and red-shifted part of the outflowing gas detected; a low inclination of the outflow with respect to the line of sight would produce a more uniform and spherically symmetric velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper classifies X N 81 44's extended H emission as star-formation powered and finds a spatial anti-correlation between its unobscured star formation and high-velocity outflows.

quotes.quote: Four targets, X N 160 22, X N 81 44, X N 66 23 and cid 467, have log( /H ) values < 0.7 and with such low /H line ratios, it is less likely for these sources to be ionised by the AGN see the results from low redshift galaxy sample in kauffmann03 . For one of them, X N 81 44, we also have evidence that the and H emission are extended, but are not spatially co-located (Fig. fig:BPT2 , left panel). We would therefore classify the extended emission in these four galaxies as most probably ionized by star-formation.

Based on the analysis presented in Sects. sect3.2 and sect3.3 , two targets fulfil these criteria: X N 81 44 and X N 66 23. Figure fig:HA_OIII_w80 shows the extended H map tracing star formation in these two targets, overlaid by based outflow velocity contours in blue. Only the contours with w_ 80 values above 600 km s ^ 1 are shown in Fig. fig:HA_OIII_w80 . The astrometry of the H-band and K-band images was registered using the AGN continuum peak emission in the respective data cubes. We observe a spatial anti-correlation between the locations of high velocity outflows ( w_ 80 > 600 km s ^ 1 ) and unobscured star formation in X N 81 44 and X N 66 23.

gmw_00007929.name: X N 66 23
gmw_00007929.overview: The active galactic nucleus host galaxy X N 66 23 contains extended ionized gas [arXiv:2008.01728] and shows tentative evidence of extended H-alpha emission that may originate from tidal tails [arXiv:2302.03039]. Its star-formation-tracing H-alpha map is spatially anti-correlated with high-velocity outflows [arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: The paper reports that X N 66 23 lacks a detected H-band BLR component but has extended ionized gas and a line-centroid gradient consistent with disturbed/outflowing kinematics.

quotes.quote: All targets but X N 66 23 show the detection of a BLR component. The non-detection of the BLR component of H in X N 66 23 is possibly a consequence of the short exposure time leading to a low S/N in the spectrum.

A smooth gradient is observed in some of the line centroid maps, e.g. in X N 81 44, X N 66 23, cid 346 and S82X1905, which could be indicative of a rotating disk in the host galaxy or a bi-polar ionized outflow. The observed extreme high velocities in the w_ 80 maps ( 1000 km/s) for most of these galaxies suggest that the kinematics of the ionized gas are also likely affected by the central AGN. For several of our targets, we do detect extended features in emission, which are not produced by wings of the PSF as demonstrated by the analysis performed in the previous sections (e.g. X N 115 23, CID 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245, S82X1905).

Using either of these methods, we find that the ionized gas is extended in X N 81 44, X N 66 23, X N 115 23, cid 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245 and S82X1905. In these targets with extended ionized gas, we constructed the flux and velocity maps and calculated D_ 600 value which is the maximum projected spatial extent of w_ 80 > 600 km/s. The D_ 600 parameter quantifies the spatial extent of the outflow associated with the ionized gas which is consistent with the outflow definition we have adopted throughout this paper i.e. w_ 80 > 600 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper treats X N 66 23 as a tentative blue-shifted extended-H detection, possibly tidal-tail emission, whose star-formation-tracing H map is spatially anti-correlated with high-velocity outflows.

quotes.quote: We confirm the presence of extended H emission in 13 out of the 21 galaxies and 3 galaxies (X N 66 23, cid 166 and J1549+1245) show tentative evidence of extended emission. The detection in X N 66 23 is classified as a tentative one, as the extended emission is observed at a blue-shift of 1200 km s ^ 1 from the expected location of the H line in the spectra.
The extended H detections in X N 66 23 and J1549+1245 may suggest emission from tidal tails.

Based on the analysis presented in Sects. sect3.2 and sect3.3 , two targets fulfil these criteria: X N 81 44 and X N 66 23. Figure fig:HA_OIII_w80 shows the extended H map tracing star formation in these two targets, overlaid by based outflow velocity contours in blue. Only the contours with w_ 80 values above 600 km s ^ 1 are shown in Fig. fig:HA_OIII_w80 . The astrometry of the H-band and K-band images was registered using the AGN continuum peak emission in the respective data cubes. We observe a spatial anti-correlation between the locations of high velocity outflows ( w_ 80 > 600 km s ^ 1 ) and unobscured star formation in X N 81 44 and X N 66 23.

gmw_00007930.name: X N 160 22
gmw_00007930.overview: The active galactic nucleus host galaxy X N 160 22 features extended hydrogen emission to its north [arXiv:2302.03039]. This extended gas is consistent with star formation and exhibits a smooth velocity gradient, while high-velocity material near the nucleus suggests the presence of outflows [arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: The paper describes X N 160 22 as having northward extended H emission, a smooth velocity gradient, star-formation-consistent extended gas, and high-velocity turbulent or outflowing material near the AGN.

quotes.quote: X N 160 22 does not show extended emission, but shows extended H emission towards the north of the AGN location, evident in the PSF-subtracted H channel map in Fig. fig:BPT_PSF and the centroid map in the left panel of Fig. fig:velocity_maps . The centroid map shows a smooth velocity gradient between 60 km s ^ 1 . The v_ 10 and w_ 80 maps show maximum velocities of < 600 km s ^ 1 and > 1000 km s ^ 1 , suggesting the presence of AGN outflows, especially close to the AGN location. Considering that the extended H emission is consistent with star formation, the H velocity maps of X N 160 22 in Fig. fig:velocity_maps could be interpreted as a superposition of a rotating medium (centroid map that shows a smooth gradient) and a turbulent medium ( v_ 10 and w_ 80 maps that show high velocities near the AGN location).

gmw_00007931.name: X N 115 23
gmw_00007931.overview: X N 115 23 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits extended ionized gas emission [arXiv:2008.01728]. This extended emission traces the narrow-line region of the active nucleus [arXiv:2302.03039], which hosts an outflow characterized by a distinct velocity gradient [arXiv:2008.01728, arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: The paper uses X N 115 23 as a resolved SUPER target with BLR-based PSF differences, excess residuals, extended ionized emission, and a velocity-gradient outflow map.

quotes.quote: For the remaining three objects (X N 115 23, cid 346, S82X1905) the PSF profile as traced by the dedicated star observation is narrower than those derived from the profile. These differences between the PSF traced by the BLR emission and the PSF star could be due to the difference in the AO correction, which might change in long exposure observations. Also, as the PSF star observations were performed at the beginning of the 1 hour long science OBs, for these three objects the conditions got relatively worse during the science observations and therefore we consider the PSF profile derived from the BLR the correct one to compare since they trace the conditions simultaneously with the science observations.

From Fig. fig:astrometry1 , we see excess residuals at a level of >2 for 6 SUPER targets - X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245 and S82X1905. In summary, from the joint analysis based on the COG and "PSF-subtraction" method we have identified seven sources from our sample which show extended emission. Further analysis on the spectra across the SINFONI FoV will therefore be restricted to the 7 targets, which show extended gas emission with at least one of the methods described above.

From the w_ 80 maps in Figs. fig:flux_velocity1 and fig:flux_velocity2 , it is clear that the ionized outflows detected in these AGN present a diversity of projected structures. In five sources (X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, S82X1905) there is a velocity gradient along a particular axis while for two objects (J1333+1649 and J1549+1245) the maps are more spherically symmetric. Some of the kinematic structures present in the w_ 80 maps could be consistent with a bi-conical outflow, where the inclination of the outflow with respect to the disk of the galaxy and the line-of-sight of the observer could explain the observed velocity maps: i.e. a high inclination of the outflow with respect to the line-of-sight will result in a clear velocity gradient along the outflow axis, with possibly both the blue-shifted and red-shifted part of the outflowing gas detected; a low inclination of the outflow with respect to the line of sight would produce a more uniform and spherically symmetric velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper finds that X N 115 23's extended H and ionized emission trace the AGN narrow-line region, with blue-shifted central H and high velocities indicating NLR outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: We were able to plot resolved BPT maps for four galaxies, namely X N 115 23, cid 346, J1333+1649 and S82X1905, as extended emission is also detected at the location of extended H as shown in the top two rows of Fig. fig:BPT . The bottom panel in Fig. fig:BPT shows the location of extended emission in these four galaxies in the BPT diagram. In these four galaxies the spatial coincidence of the extended H and emission suggests that we are tracing the NLR rather than the host galaxy disk. That the ionization source for the extended H is the AGN rather than star-formation is further supported by the location of these galaxies in the BPT diagram (bottom panel in Fig. fig:BPT ).

In the case of X N 115 23, the H centroid value at the AGN location appears blue-shifted and the w_ 80 map does not show a centrally peaked profile, but shows a smooth gradient. The velocities in the w_ 80 map reaches values between 850 -- > 1000 km s ^ 1 suggesting that H emission is tracing gas which is part of an outflow in the NLR.

On the contrary, X N 12 26, X N 115 23 and S82X1905 do not have smooth gradients in their centroid maps. The extended H emission in X N 115 23 and S82X1905 is consistent with ionisation by the AGN (Sect. sect3.3 ) and therefore the absence of smooth gradient in the H centroid profile confirms that the H emission is dominated by AGN emission.

gmw_00007932.name: cid 346
gmw_00007932.overview: The active galactic nucleus host galaxy cid 346 features extended ionized gas [arXiv:2008.01728] and broad hydrogen emission [arXiv:2302.03039]. Its observed kinematics combine host galaxy rotation with active galactic nucleus outflows [arXiv:2302.03039], including redshifted gas detected approximately 4 kiloparsecs from the center [arXiv:2008.01728].

sources.summary: The paper finds cid 346 has extended ionized gas, a line-centroid gradient, PSF-subtraction residuals, and spectroastrometric evidence for redshifted gas about 4 kpc from the AGN.

quotes.quote: For the remaining three objects (X N 115 23, cid 346, S82X1905) the PSF profile as traced by the dedicated star observation is narrower than those derived from the profile. These differences between the PSF traced by the BLR emission and the PSF star could be due to the difference in the AO correction, which might change in long exposure observations. Also, as the PSF star observations were performed at the beginning of the 1 hour long science OBs, for these three objects the conditions got relatively worse during the science observations and therefore we consider the PSF profile derived from the BLR the correct one to compare since they trace the conditions simultaneously with the science observations.

A smooth gradient is observed in some of the line centroid maps, e.g. in X N 81 44, X N 66 23, cid 346 and S82X1905, which could be indicative of a rotating disk in the host galaxy or a bi-polar ionized outflow. The observed extreme high velocities in the w_ 80 maps ( 1000 km/s) for most of these galaxies suggest that the kinematics of the ionized gas are also likely affected by the central AGN. For several of our targets, we do detect extended features in emission, which are not produced by wings of the PSF as demonstrated by the analysis performed in the previous sections (e.g. X N 115 23, CID 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245, S82X1905).

There are a couple of targets with notably interesting features from the spectroastrometry analysis. In the case of cid 346, the spectroastrometry method reveals the presence of gas moving at 600 km/s at a distance of 0.3 equivalent to a physical distance of 4 kpc from the AGN location. Similarly, also in the case of S82X1905 we find that the bulk of the gas moving at 300 km/s is located at 4 kpc from the center. We argue that a possible explanation for such extended redshifted emission is that the receding part of the outflow is not obscured by the dust of the host galaxy in these two cases, possibly due to the larger extent of the outflow itself.

sources.summary: cid 346 has broad extended H emission and co-spatial ionised outflow; its velocity field combines host rotation with AGN outflow, though star formation may also affect the extended emission.

quotes.quote: In addition, for cid 346, the width of the extended H emission is 628 45 km s ^ 1 , which is above the limit usually used to distinguish between an outflowing and non-outflowing gas. This suggests that the observed H emission is a part of the NLR outflow. This is also supported by the fact that the ionised outflow is also detected in the line at the same location as the extended H see kakkad20 .

This is also supported by the fact that the H and emission are spatially extended in the same direction and that the extended emission is ionised by the AGN (see BPT map in Fig. fig:BPT ). cid 346 shows a smooth velocity gradient in the centroid map with the major axis orientated roughly along the NE direction and the w_ 80 map peaks at 1300 km s ^ 1 at the AGN location. The large dispersion value cannot be explained purely as ordered rotation and therefore, we infer that the observed H emission is a combination of rotation from the host galaxy and AGN outflows. Although the extended H emission in this galaxy falls under the AGN ionised region in Fig. fig:BPT , the presence of upper limits in the /H ratio could suggest that star formation may play a role in the ionisation and kinematics of the observed extended emission.

gmw_00007933.name: cid 166
gmw_00007933.overview: The high-redshift active galactic nucleus host galaxy cid 166 has been observed near the spectral resolution limit of SINFONI [arXiv:2302.03039]. It features a tentative detection of extended hydrogen-alpha emission with a blue-shifted residual of 700 kilometers per second [arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: The paper classifies cid 166 as a tentative extended-H detection near SINFONI's spectral limit, with a blue-shifted residual and insufficient S/N for pixel-level Gaussian modelling.

quotes.quote: In the case of cid 166, the detection is almost at the limit of the spectral resolution of SINFONI.

Furthermore, the observed residual is blue-shifted by 700 km s ^ 1 from the expected location of the H emission and therefore, the extended emission in cid 166 has been classified as a tentative detection. cid 1205 is the only target among the ones observed with AO and with sufficient S/N for the PSF-subtraction analysis that shows no extended H emission.

Out of these 15 targets, 4 targets (X N 53 3, X N 66 23, cid 166 and cid 1605) did not have sufficient S/N per pixel to constrain the parameters of individual Gaussian components. We note that deriving the PSF-subtracted maps was possible despite the low S/N in these 4 targets as we relied on the channel maps and not on Gaussian fits to verify the presence or absence of residuals.

gmw_00007934.name: J1549+1245
gmw_00007934.overview: J1549+1245 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits extended ionized gas emission [arXiv:2008.01728] and spherically symmetric outflow maps [arXiv:2008.01728]. The system has two nearby line-emitting companion galaxies [arXiv:2305.05173], with tentative extended hydrogen emission suggesting a possible tidal tail from a merger [arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: The paper discusses J1549+1245 as a SUPER AGN with wavelength-dependent continuum shifts, extended ionized emission, PSF-subtraction residuals, and more spherically symmetric outflow maps.

quotes.quote: Further, we noted that the SINFONI observations of target J1549+1245 show a systematic spatial shift in the centroid of the continuum location as a function of wavelength. This could be due to an inaccurate correction of the atmospheric dispersion and/or to a rotation in the grating between the time of the science observation and when the wavelength calibration was obtained. To correct for such spatial shifts, we measure the offset along the X and Y directions as a function of wavelength and the derived offset functions were used to re-align the cubes using the drizzle algorithm fruchter02 .

From Fig. fig:astrometry1 , we see excess residuals at a level of >2 for 6 SUPER targets - X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245 and S82X1905. In summary, from the joint analysis based on the COG and "PSF-subtraction" method we have identified seven sources from our sample which show extended emission. Further analysis on the spectra across the SINFONI FoV will therefore be restricted to the 7 targets, which show extended gas emission with at least one of the methods described above.

From the w_ 80 maps in Figs. fig:flux_velocity1 and fig:flux_velocity2 , it is clear that the ionized outflows detected in these AGN present a diversity of projected structures. In five sources (X N 81 44, X N 115 23, cid 346, S82X1905) there is a velocity gradient along a particular axis while for two objects (J1333+1649 and J1549+1245) the maps are more spherically symmetric. Some of the kinematic structures present in the w_ 80 maps could be consistent with a bi-conical outflow, where the inclination of the outflow with respect to the disk of the galaxy and the line-of-sight of the observer could explain the observed velocity maps: i.e. a high inclination of the outflow with respect to the line-of-sight will result in a clear velocity gradient along the outflow axis, with possibly both the blue-shifted and red-shifted part of the outflowing gas detected; a low inclination of the outflow with respect to the line of sight would produce a more uniform and spherically symmetric velocity field.

sources.summary: J1549+1245 has tentative extended H emission about 9-10 kpc from the AGN, redshifted by roughly 1800 km/s, with ALMA CO evidence for a companion and possible tidal-tail origin.

quotes.quote: We confirm the presence of extended H emission in 13 out of the 21 galaxies and 3 galaxies (X N 66 23, cid 166 and J1549+1245) show tentative evidence of extended emission.

Similarly, the extended emission in J1549+1245 is detected at a distance of 9 kpc from the AGN location and the spectral position of the detection is redshifted by +1800 km s ^ 1 with respect to the expected location of the H line. Although currently available archival optical observations do not suggest the presence of companions, this may be due to their limited spatial resolution. In fact, ALMA CO observations of J1549+1245 have shown the presence of a possible companion south of the AGN location see bischetti21 . Such extreme velocity shifts have been observed in previous CO observations of high redshift X-ray AGN sources e.g., carniani17 . The extended H detections in X N 66 23 and J1549+1245 may suggest emission from tidal tails.

For instance, J1549+1245 shows an extended H component at 10 kpc from the AGN location, but this component is red-shifted by 1800 km s ^ 1 on extracting the spectra (Figure fig:BPT_PSF2 ). Although the presence of large scale outflows cannot be ruled out in this case, the presence of such extended components might also suggest the presence of companions or the AGN host galaxies in the process of a merger. Observations of some of the type 1 SUPER AGN in other wavelengths already suggest the presence of companions. For instance, recent ALMA CO(4-3) observations of J1549+1245 confirmed the presence of a companion at >3 significance see bischetti21 and therefore, the observed extended H could be a part of a tidal tail.

sources.summary: SDSS J154938.71+124509.1 has two nearby line-emitting companion sources within 2.4 arcseconds; the brighter, farther companion is extracted, while the fainter northern source is not visible.

quotes.quote: SDSS J154938.71+124509.1 was observed by and was found to have two line emitting companion sources within 2.4 of the quasar. Only the further companion was extracted on Fig. fig:companions as it is the brighter source. The second fainter companion is located North of the quasar but it is not visible on the image cutout.

gmw_00007935.name: S82X1940
gmw_00007935.overview: The galaxy S82X1940 hosts an active galactic nucleus that ionizes its narrow-line region [arXiv:2302.03039]. This source drives an ionized gas outflow, which is traced by extended hydrogen emission stretching toward the southeast [arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: S82X1940 shows southeast extended H emission with the sample's largest extended-region line width, interpreted as NLR gas ionized by an AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: An exception is S82X1940 shows an extended H emission towards the SE direction from the AGN location (Fig. fig:BPT_PSF2 ). This target displays the largest H line width in the extended region of 784 98 km s ^ 1 . The emission line analysis of this target already shows the presence of an ionised outflow kakkad20 and therefore, the H emission most likely also traces the outflowing gas driven by the AGN. Based on this evidence, we conclude that the observed extended H emission in S82X1940 is part of the NLR ionised by the AGN.
In two galaxies (cid 346 and S82X1940), the width of the H line > 600 km s ^ 1 , suggesting that the H emission is tracing ionised outflow driven by the AGN.

gmw_00007936.name: S82X1905
gmw_00007936.overview: S82X1905 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits extended ionized gas emission [arXiv:2008.01728, arXiv:2302.03039]. This extended emission is dominated by AGN ionization [arXiv:2302.03039], with redshifted gas moving at 300 km/s located approximately 4 kpc from the galactic center [arXiv:2008.01728].

sources.summary: The paper finds S82X1905 has BLR-based PSF differences, extended ionized emission, a line-centroid gradient, a velocity-gradient outflow, and redshifted gas about 4 kpc from the center.

quotes.quote: For the remaining three objects (X N 115 23, cid 346, S82X1905) the PSF profile as traced by the dedicated star observation is narrower than those derived from the profile. These differences between the PSF traced by the BLR emission and the PSF star could be due to the difference in the AO correction, which might change in long exposure observations. Also, as the PSF star observations were performed at the beginning of the 1 hour long science OBs, for these three objects the conditions got relatively worse during the science observations and therefore we consider the PSF profile derived from the BLR the correct one to compare since they trace the conditions simultaneously with the science observations.

A smooth gradient is observed in some of the line centroid maps, e.g. in X N 81 44, X N 66 23, cid 346 and S82X1905, which could be indicative of a rotating disk in the host galaxy or a bi-polar ionized outflow. The observed extreme high velocities in the w_ 80 maps ( 1000 km/s) for most of these galaxies suggest that the kinematics of the ionized gas are also likely affected by the central AGN. For several of our targets, we do detect extended features in emission, which are not produced by wings of the PSF as demonstrated by the analysis performed in the previous sections (e.g. X N 115 23, CID 346, J1333+1649, J1549+1245, S82X1905).

There are a couple of targets with notably interesting features from the spectroastrometry analysis. In the case of cid 346, the spectroastrometry method reveals the presence of gas moving at 600 km/s at a distance of 0.3 equivalent to a physical distance of 4 kpc from the AGN location. Similarly, also in the case of S82X1905 we find that the bulk of the gas moving at 300 km/s is located at 4 kpc from the center. We argue that a possible explanation for such extended redshifted emission is that the receding part of the outflow is not obscured by the dust of the host galaxy in these two cases, possibly due to the larger extent of the outflow itself.

sources.summary: S82X1905 has co-spatial extended H and ionized emission placing it in the AGN-ionized BPT regime; its H centroid lacks a smooth gradient, consistent with AGN-dominated extended emission.
