quotes.quote: We were able to plot resolved BPT maps for four galaxies, namely X N 115 23, cid 346, J1333+1649 and S82X1905, as extended emission is also detected at the location of extended H as shown in the top two rows of Fig. fig:BPT . The bottom panel in Fig. fig:BPT shows the location of extended emission in these four galaxies in the BPT diagram. In these four galaxies the spatial coincidence of the extended H and emission suggests that we are tracing the NLR rather than the host galaxy disk. That the ionization source for the extended H is the AGN rather than star-formation is further supported by the location of these galaxies in the BPT diagram (bottom panel in Fig. fig:BPT ).
In the case of cid 346 and S82X1905, we also do not detect H emission in the extended regions and therefore, a lower limit to the /H line ratio is estimated.

On the contrary, X N 12 26, X N 115 23 and S82X1905 do not have smooth gradients in their centroid maps. The extended H emission in X N 115 23 and S82X1905 is consistent with ionisation by the AGN (Sect. sect3.3 ) and therefore the absence of smooth gradient in the H centroid profile confirms that the H emission is dominated by AGN emission.

gmw_00007937.name: S82X2058
gmw_00007937.overview: S82X2058 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy that exhibits a rotating system [arXiv:2302.03039]. This system shows a smooth H-alpha velocity gradient and is likely undergoing an interaction with a companion galaxy [arXiv:2302.03039].

sources.summary: S82X2058 shows a smooth H velocity gradient and localized velocity-dispersion peak near the AGN, which the paper interprets as rotation probably affected by interaction with another galaxy.

quotes.quote: Lastly, S82X2058 shows a smooth velocity gradient in its H centroid map, but the centroid velocity profile is extended more towards the SE direction from the AGN location. Accounting for the localised peak in the w_ 80 map at the AGN location, this target shows a rotating system probably in an interaction with another galaxy. To summarize the results on the spatially resolved kinematic analysis of the H line: Six galaxies (X N 160 22, X N 81 44, X N 4 48, cid 346, S82X1940 and S82X2058) show smooth velocity gradients in their H centroid maps.
This is not a unique case: from the H kinematic maps, there are indications of companions also in the case of X N 4 48, S82X1940 and S82X2058.

gmw_00007938.name: ATPMN J135340.1-663957
gmw_00007938.overview: ATPMN J135340.1-663957 is a flat-spectrum radio source [arXiv:1301.2386, arXiv:1506.07006] classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1301.2386, arXiv:1506.07006]. It is associated with X-ray and gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1301.2386, arXiv:1202.2625], and has a redshift lower limit of 0.15 [arXiv:1506.07006].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source 1 near J1353.5 6640 as a flat-spectrum radio source with ATPMN and X-ray counterparts, a featureless optical spectrum, and likely BL Lac classification.

quotes.quote: There are two sources within the 99 per cent localisation error ellipse of J1353.5 6640. Source 1 has a spectral index of 0.33, while source 2 has a spectral index of 1.00. Source 1 was also detected in the ATPMN survey :atpmn as J135340.1 663957 with flux densities 43 6 and 28 10 mJy at 4.8 and 8.6 GHz respectively.

There is also a strong X-ray source 1RXS J135341.1 664002 within 7 arcsec of source 1. :tsa observed its optical counterpart with the VLT,, which is within 1.7 arcsec of source 1, and reported a featureless spectrum. This suggests its classification as a BL Lac type. This additional information presents a strong argument in favour of association of source 1 with 2FGL J1353.5 6640.

sources.summary: The paper identifies VASC J1353--66 with 1FHL J1353.0--6642, giving its X-ray flux, radio detections, flat radio spectrum, BL Lac classification, and redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: For 1FHL J1353.0--6642, the restricted X-ray position provides a secure identification with VASC J1353--66. This object, which is listed in the XMM-Newton Slew Survey, has an X-ray 0.2--12 keV flux of 3.9 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . It is detected in radio at various frequencies, including the 36 cm one (flux density of 70.7 mJy, see [ 12 ] ), and shows a flat radio spectrum (see [ 13 ] ), while it is not detected by WISE. The source was optically classified as a BL Lac by [ 13 ] , while [ 14 ] were able to put a lower limit of 0.15 to the source redshift.

sources.summary: The catalogue highlights ATPMN J135340.1-663957 as a new radio gamma-ray association with 2FGL J1353.5-6640, previously known as VASC J1353-66 and tentatively classified as a BL Lac object.

quotes.quote: The unassociated source is 2FGL J1353.5-6640 which lies 1.2 arcmin from ATPMN J135340.1-663957 which has a 4.8 GHz flux density of 43 mJy and a spectral index of 0.8 0.7 .
ATPMN J135340.1-663957, the new association with 2FGL J1353.5-6640, corresponds to a source studied by , VASC J1353-66, in a search for very active stars in the Galaxy.

We have made a new radio ray association between ATPMN J135340.1-663957 and 2FGL J1353.5-6640, a source previously studied by as VASC J1353-66, and tentatively identified them as a BL Lac type object.

gmw_00007939.name: LARS 2
gmw_00007939.overview: LARS 2 is a strong global Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1503.01157]. It is characterized by a low dust content, a very high global Lyman-alpha escape fraction, and neutral-gas kinematics that facilitate this escape [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper presents LARS 2 as a strong global Lyman-alpha emitter with narrow, slightly blueshifted silicon absorption, high escape fraction, low dust content, and neutral-gas kinematics that help Ly escape.

quotes.quote: LARS 2 is another strong global Ly emitter which also locally shows a strong emission profile in the COS aperture. Like LARS 1, it shows a classic P Cygni-like emission profile in Ly , the absorption part of which coincides neatly in velocities with the averaged metal line absorption profile. The silicon absorption lines reveal a relatively narrow profile only slightly blueshifted from the systemic velocity. The four Si ii transitions line up very consistently around what resembles a symmetric Gaussian absorption feature with a shallow, secondary feature on the blue side, which is somewhat deeper for the 1260 transition. A quite strong fluorescent emission is present in these lines and in O i .

LARS 2 is peculiar because of its very high ( = 0.56 ) global Ly escape fraction, more than twice that of nearest competitor, the Green Pea type galaxy LARS 14. This despite a high inferred metal line covering fraction and low outflow velocity; factors which are normally quenching Ly escape. The galaxy does however have low dust content and a narrow velocity range of the neutral medium, both of which ease the escape of Ly .

gmw_00007940.name: LARS 4
gmw_00007940.overview: LARS 4 is classified as a global Lyman-alpha absorbing galaxy [arXiv:1503.01157]. Within the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph aperture, it displays weak but distinct Lyman-alpha emission that is outweighed by absorption [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LARS 4 as a global Lyman-alpha absorber whose COS aperture still shows weak emission, with narrow blue absorption and metal lines that are black at line center.

quotes.quote: LARS 4 is one of the two global Ly absorbers of the sample. In the COS aperture, it shows weak but distinct emission, but even in-aperture, Ly absorption outweighs this. The line shape is very similar to the one of LARS 3, although the blue absorption component is narrower. The averaged LIS metal line is likewise significantly narrower than that of LARS 3, but like LARS 3 completely black at line center for all Si ii lines except at 1304 , which does show a bit of residual intensity in the line center of LARS 4. This can partly be because this line, having the smallest oscillator strength, is not completely opaque, but at least part of it is likely to be due to emission in O i * 1305 fluorescent emission is unusually strong in this spectrum, also for Si ii * 1194.

gmw_00007941.name: LARS 6
gmw_00007941.overview: LARS 6 is a global Lyman-alpha absorbing galaxy that is interacting with a neighboring system [arXiv:1503.01157]. This interaction may have produced a tidal tail of gas extending into the aperture, which exhibits a complex metal absorption profile with three narrow components [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper treats LARS 6 as a global Lyman-alpha absorber with the most complex metal absorption profile, three narrow gas components, and possible interaction-related tidal gas in the aperture.

quotes.quote: LARS 6 This is the second of the two global Ly absorbers, and locally in the aperture it shows a strong, damped absorption profile. The metal absorption profile is the most complex of the LARS galaxies. It consists of three narrow components, one infalling at v 280 km s ^ 1 , one outflowing at v 210 km s ^ 1 , and one static. The static component is the strongest in metal absorption, but the Ly absorption profile seems to be centered around the infalling component. The outflowing component is interesting as the depths of the 1190+1193 doublet suggests that this component is optically thin.

LARSIII show that LARS 6 is in fact interaction with another, neighboring system. One possible explanation for the double-dip absorption profile could be that one of the dips actually comes from a tidal tail of gas from this interacting system extending into the aperture.

gmw_00007942.name: LARS 7
gmw_00007942.overview: LARS 7 is classified as a strong global Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1503.01157]. It exhibits a narrow neutral-gas velocity distribution and a global Lyman-alpha escape fraction of 0.14, making it the faintest of the five strong global emitters in its sample [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 7 as a strong global Lyman-alpha emitter with a narrow P Cygni absorption component, narrow neutral-gas velocity distribution, and relatively faint emission among strong emitters.

quotes.quote: LARS 7 is another one of the strong global Ly emitters and also shows relatively strong emission within the COS aperture. The emission profile is of P Cygni type with a narrow blue absorption component. The strong emission in Ly is peculiar considering that the metal absorption line, like that of LARS 2, is deep and centered close to zero velocity, factors that should act to suppress Ly escape. However, like for LARS 2, the velocity distribution in the neutral gas is narrow, allowing for the red wing of Ly emission to still escape. LARS 7 is also the faintest of the five strong global emitters, with a global Ly escape fraction of 0.14.

gmw_00007943.name: LARS 8
gmw_00007943.overview: LARS 8 is a galaxy that marginally qualifies as a Lyman-alpha emitter [arXiv:1503.01157]. It features a strongly outflowing neutral medium characterized by a broad, high-velocity metal absorption profile and a weak P Cygni profile in its aperture [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper presents LARS 8 as having the sample’s strongest outflowing neutral medium, black broad metal absorption, a marginal Lyman-alpha-emitter classification, and weak P Cygni aperture emission.

quotes.quote: LARS 8 is noteworthy for its strongly outflowing neutral medium. The average metal absorption feature is black at line center, wide, and has the highest velocity of the sample. Despite the strong outflow, the galaxy just barely qualifies as a Ly emitter. Globally, it has a Ly escape fraction of 2 . In the aperture, it displays a P Cygni profile weak in emission and with a broad absorption component suggesting high H i column density in the outflowing medium.

gmw_00007944.name: LARS 9
gmw_00007944.overview: LARS 9 is globally characterized as a weak Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1503.01157]. Within its Cosmic Origins Spectrograph aperture, it exhibits a broad, damped Lyman-alpha absorption profile and two low-ionization state metal-absorption components that suppress the escape of Lyman-alpha radiation [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 9 as a weak global Lyman-alpha emitter but the strongest COS-aperture absorber, with broad damped Ly absorption and two LIS metal-absorption components suppressing escape.

quotes.quote: Globally a weak Ly emitter, LARS 9 is the strongest absorber in the sample measured in the COS aperture. The Ly profile is a broad, damped absorption feature, with significant absorption out to 4000 km s ^ 1 , as is illustrated in Figure fig:broadlines . The LIS average metal absorption profile consists of two components. A strong, saturated static or slowly infalling component centered at v 50 km s ^ 1 , and a shallower component centered at 220 km s ^ 1 . This combination of no outflow, large velocity range and probably high column density (judging from the depth of the metal lines) efficiently suppresses Ly escape.

gmw_00007945.name: LARS 11
gmw_00007945.overview: LARS 11 is a galaxy characterized by a strongly outflowing neutral medium [arXiv:1503.01157]. Despite these outflows, it exhibits a low global Lyman-alpha escape fraction, which may be due to its high dust content and broad metal absorption profile [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 11 as strongly outflowing yet low in global Ly escape, with weak aperture P Cygni emission, broad LIS absorption, high dust content, and noisy Si II constraints.

quotes.quote: LARS 11 is, along with LARS 8, notable for having a strongly outflowing neutral medium while still having a low global Ly escape fraction. In the COS aperture, Lyman emission is slightly more pronounced, forming a weak P Cygni profile. As for LARS 8, the width of the averaged LIS metal absorption profile is quite high, and the dust content of LARS 11 is the second highest of the sample, a combination that could possibly account for the missing Ly output. The Si ii 1190+1193 doublet of LARS 11 falls between the detectors at COS and could not be included in the analysis, meaning that the uncertainties on inferred f_C(v) profile are large, and the assumption of this profile coinciding with the averaged absorption profile must in this case remain an assumption. This is further exacerbated by the spectrum being quite noisy and the estimated measuring errors being large.

gmw_00007946.name: LARS 12
gmw_00007946.overview: LARS 12 is a galaxy characterized as a weak global and aperture Lyman-alpha emitter [arXiv:1503.01157]. It exhibits a weak P Cygni absorption profile alongside a shallow, wide low-ionization state absorption profile [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper treats LARS 12 as a weak global and aperture Ly emitter, showing a weak P Cygni absorption profile, shallow wide LIS absorption, and possible partial transparency.

quotes.quote: Globally, LARS 12 is another weak emitter, and its output in the COS aperture is modest as well. It shows something which in Figure fig:coverfracs3 could look like a skewed double-peak profile,but which, when viewing a broader range of wavelengths, turns out to be a quite classic, weak P Cygni absorption profile. The average LIS profile is relatively shallow and wide. Across most of the profile, Si ii 1304 seems slightly shallower than the rest and 1260 slightly deeper, indicating that the absorbing systems might not be completely opaque. The best fits for f_C(v) also seem to fall a bit below the averaged profile, although the difference is small and full opacity still a good approximation.

gmw_00007947.name: LARS 13
gmw_00007947.overview: LARS 13 is a galaxy characterized as a globally weak Lyman-alpha emitter [arXiv:1503.01157]. It features a modest P Cygni-like emission profile inside the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph aperture, which aligns well with its average low-ionization state absorption profile [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 13 as a weak global emitter with modest P Cygni aperture emission, well-aligned Ly and LIS absorption, and shallow Si II features affected by re-emission.

quotes.quote: LARS 13 is another globally weak Ly emitter with a clear but modest P Cygni-like emission feature inside the COS aperture. The absorption component of this feature aligns particularly well with the averaged LIS absorption profile as seen on Figure fig:coverfracs3 . The averaged LIS absorption profile is relatively wide and everywhere well above zero, centered around v 100 km s ^ 1 . The Si ii absorption profiles again align quite well, except 1304 , which is somewhat more shallow than the rest. Furthermore, this line and 1193 both have a small peak centered at zero velocity which is absent in the two other lines.

gmw_00007948.name: LARS 14
gmw_00007948.overview: LARS 14 is a compact, hot Green Pea type galaxy [arXiv:1503.01157]. It exhibits a low neutral gas column density, an unusually high ionization degree, and a double-peaked Lyman-alpha emission profile [arXiv:1503.01157].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LARS 14 as a Green Pea outlier with compact hot-galaxy properties, low neutral-gas column density, strong double-peaked Ly emission, unusually high ionization, and no evidence against prior non-leakage claims.

quotes.quote: LARS 14 is the second strong outlier in the sample. It is a Green Pea type galaxy GreenPea , a class of compact, hot galaxies with low neutral gas column density. The Ly profile of LARS 14 shows strong emission in a double-peaked profile, the central absorption feature of which is remarkably shallow, with a minimum significantly above continuum level, indicating a low H i column density. This is further supported by the Si iv absorption profile being significantly deeper than the averaged LIS metal line, indicating an ionization degree unusually high for the sample.

This would, in the case of LARS 14, be supported by the overall low values of f_C(v) , with a maximum value of 0.4. However, Heckman2011 ruled out that this galaxy should leak ionizing radiation along our line of sight, and we find no evidence against this claim.

gmw_00007949.name: SBS1325+597
gmw_00007949.overview: SBS1325+597 is an isolated void galaxy [arXiv:1510.07411, arXiv:1008.4616] featuring a red nucleus and a blue, distorted star-forming disk [arXiv:1510.07411, arXiv:1008.4616]. It exhibits a disturbed optical and neutral hydrogen morphology [arXiv:1411.1828], with molecular gas distributed in a rotating ring or torus [arXiv:1510.07411, arXiv:1411.1828].

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS1325+597 as an isolated, gas-rich void galaxy with a red nucleus, bluish star-forming disk, disturbed extended H I, molecular gas in a ring or torus, and moderate star formation.

quotes.quote: SBS1325+597 (IRASF 13254+5945, VGS 34) : This is a gas rich galaxy with a size of D_ 25 11.7 kpc (Table 1) that has been studied as part of the Void Galaxy Survey (here after VGS) kreckel.etal.2011 . The SDSS image of the galaxy shows a compact, red nucleus and a single, faint spiral arm. The SDSS optical spectrum has few emission lines suggesting only moderate star formation and shows no signatures of AGN activity. The HI is extended well beyond the optical radius and the outer isophotes are disturbed, which suggests that the galaxy maybe interacting with a distant companion galaxy.

In SBS 1325+597 the H emisison is distributed over two regions on either side of the galaxy center, which indicates that it is only associated with the inner disk of the galaxy (Figure 3). This distribution agrees well with the CO(1--0) emission profile (Figure 1), which is double horned about the systemic velocity of the galaxy and indicates that the molecular gas is also distributed in a ring or torus about the galaxy center. In the SDSS optical image, it is clear that the galaxy has a red bulge but a bluish star forming disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 34 as a disturbed void galaxy with a kinematically distinct H i protuberance, a blue distorted disk, a small H i companion, and high rotation velocity.

quotes.quote: VGS 34 has a very faint, kinematically distinct protuberance in H i . The disturbed optical morphology also suggests that the target is undergoing some sort of interaction, as the bulge appears extremely red while the farther reaches of the disk appear relatively blue and distorted. The H i extention does align with a small, distant H i detected companion. VGS 34 also has a very large rotational velocity. Like VGS 07, it sits near the center of the void and not near a wall-like configuration.

sources.summary: SBS 1325+597 is a CO-detected void galaxy; its double-horned line profile indicates a rotating molecular disk with H I-like rotation, and it has disturbed optical/H I morphology.

quotes.quote: Of the four detections, SBS 1325+597 has the most striking line profile; it has a double horned structure indicating a rotating disk of molecular gas (Figure 1a). The velocity separation of the peaks is 200 kms^ 1 ; assuming a disk inclination of 59.3 ^ the disk rotation is 116 km s ^ 1 . This is similar to the HI rotation speed from Kreckel et al. (2012).
SBS 1325+597 has a disturbed optical and HI morphology.

gmw_00007950.name: SDSS 143052.33+551440.0
gmw_00007950.overview: SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 is a small, gas-rich disk galaxy [arXiv:1510.07411] with a blue, star-forming nucleus [arXiv:1510.07411, arXiv:1411.1828]. It exhibits a disturbed optical morphology [arXiv:1411.1828], extended hydrogen-alpha emission [arXiv:1510.07411], and an asymmetric carbon monoxide profile that suggests a disturbed gas disk [arXiv:1411.1828].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 as a small gas-rich disk galaxy with a blue, star-forming nucleus, extended H emission, off-center H and CO peaks, and relatively strong SFR.

quotes.quote: SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 (SDSS1430+5514, VGS 44) : This is also a moderate size disk galaxy with a band disk radius of 3.6 kpc and an HI radius < 7 kpc kreckel.etal.2012 . There is no strong bulge or extended disk but the SDSS image reveals a blue nucleus that shows strong H emission in the optical spectrum, indicative of nuclear star formation.

SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 is a small galaxy with a bright nucleus that appears blue in its SDSS optical image. The H emisison peaks close to the center of the galaxy but is extended over the enitire disk (Figure 4). The H luminosity gives a SFR of 0.6 M _ yr ^ 1 (Table 3) which is relatively strong for a galaxy of this size. Both the H flux and the CO line emission appear to peak offcenter from the nucleus. The GALEX NUV emission is also extended over the galaxy.

sources.summary: SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 is a CO-detected void galaxy whose gas disk appears disturbed; its H emission is nuclear, and both optical morphology and CO profile are asymmetric.

quotes.quote: In the other two galaxies (SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 and CG 598) the CO line profile is slightly off center from the systemic velocities of the galaxies, which suggests that their gas disks are disturbed, possibly due to interaction with a companion galaxy (Das et al. 2014, in preparation).

In SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 the H emission is concentrated about the nucleus. In SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 it is concentrated along the bar. The SFR is highest for SDSS 153821.22+331105.1; it is probably triggered by gas flowing along the bar in the galaxy although the emission is surprisingly faint in the disk. 5. Are these galaxies interacting? : SBS 1325+597 has a disturbed optical and HI morphology. SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 also has a disturbed optical morphology and its CO profile is not symmetric about its systemic velocity.

gmw_00007951.name: SDSS 153821.22+331105.1
gmw_00007951.overview: SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 is a blue, face-on barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1510.07411, arXiv:1204.5185] that has a concentrated neutral hydrogen disk with a warp [arXiv:1204.5185]. Its molecular gas and star-forming H-alpha emission are concentrated along the bar [arXiv:1510.07411, arXiv:1411.1828], which may have been triggered by a past interaction [arXiv:1411.1828].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 as an isolated blue barred galaxy whose molecular gas and H emission are concentrated along the bar, driving central star formation and secular evolution.

quotes.quote: SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 (SDSS1538+3311, VGS 57) : This is the only galaxy in our sample that appears to have a bar. It has an extended disk of radius 17.9^ or 8.1 kpc in the SDSS band image and an HI radius < 9 kpc kreckel.etal.2012 . Though the galaxy is relatively blue and the SDSS nuclear spectrum shows strong H emission, other signatures of strong star formation such as [ OI ] emission which is associated with shocks, are lacking. The ongoing star formation is located mainly along the bar.

SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 is the only barred galaxy in our sample. It appears to be an isolated, blue, star forming galaxy with a small bulge and faint spiral arms associated with the bar (Figure 5). The H emission peaks in the nucleus and is extended over the entire bar. This is similar to the molecular gas distribution which is sharply peaked about the systemic velocity of the galaxy (Figure 3) indicating that the molecular gas is concentrated in the center of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes VGS 57 as the survey's most efficient star former, a luminous face-on barred spiral whose concentrated H I disk warps near the optical spiral arms.

quotes.quote: VGS 57 has the highest star formation efficiency of the VGS. It is one of the most luminous (M _r = -20.4) galaxies in our sample with one of the highest SFRs, almost 2 M _ yr ^ 1 . Optically, it appears to be a large, face-on strongly barred spiral, however the H i appears as a fairly concentrated disk with a significant warp at the position of the optical spiral arms. It has a smaller companion with a systemic velocity only 70 km s ^ 1 different and a separation on the sky of 175 kpc.

sources.summary: SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 has centrally peaked CO likely driven into its bar, H emission along the bar, the sample's highest SFR, and a bar possibly triggered by interaction.

quotes.quote: In SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 the gas is centrally peaked (Figure 1b); probably driven into the center by the bar in the galaxy.

In SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 it is concentrated along the bar. The SFR is highest for SDSS 153821.22+331105.1; it is probably triggered by gas flowing along the bar in the galaxy although the emission is surprisingly faint in the disk. 5. Are these galaxies interacting? : SBS 1325+597 has a disturbed optical and HI morphology. SDSS 143052.33+551440.0 also has a disturbed optical morphology and its CO profile is not symmetric about its systemic velocity. SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 has a bar that may have been triggered by an interaction.

gmw_00007952.name: CG 598
gmw_00007952.overview: CG 598 is a large, gas-rich galaxy located in the Bootes void [arXiv:1510.07411]. It features an extended H I gas disk, centrally peaked molecular gas, and strong star formation likely triggered by the accretion of a smaller companion galaxy [arXiv:1510.07411].

sources.summary: The paper describes CG 598 as a large, gas-rich Bootes void galaxy with strong optical star-formation signatures, extended H I, centrally peaked molecular gas, and an accreting companion likely triggering starburst activity.

quotes.quote: CG 598 (IRAS F14575+4228) : This is a distant but large galaxy in the Bootes void that has disk radius of 22.4^ or 26.2 kpc in the k_s band and shows signs of strong star formation in its optical spectrum (e.g. H , H and [ OII ] emission) cruzen.etal.2002 . It is gas rich and the HI gas disk is extended well beyond the optical disk Szomoru et al. (1996). In the SDSS g band image, the galaxy appears to be accreting a smaller galaxy. The interaction probably triggered the burst of star formation.
In both SDSS 153821.22+331105.1 and CG 598, the molecular gas is centrally peaked (Figure 1d and 2b).

gmw_00007953.name: UM 311
gmw_00007953.overview: UM 311 is classified as a blue compact dwarf galaxy located in a crowded field [arXiv:1002.0991]. It is grouped among the most metal-rich galaxies in its class, exhibiting a steep continuum and enhanced polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission [arXiv:1002.0991].

sources.summary: The paper treats UM 311 as a crowded-field BCD whose infrared photometry is probably overestimated, while grouping it among metal-rich BCDs with steep continua and enhanced PAH strength.

quotes.quote: The only exception is UM 311, which is in a crowded field (there are several point sources in close proximity to a spiral galaxy), and thus its photometry is probably contaminated by other sources.

In our low-metallicity BCD sample, with oxygen abundances \ ranging from 7.4 to 8.3, the fraction of PAH emission ranges from 0.1 to 1.6 , with a median (and mean) of 0.5 Because of crowding and source structure, the TIR luminosity in UM 311 may be overestimated; nevertheless even without UM 311, the mean is only slightly larger, 0.57 vs. 0.54 .
The four BCDs with the steepest continua and most enhanced PAH strength are also the most metal-rich galaxies in our sample (Haro 3, Mrk 450, Mrk 1315, and UM 311).

gmw_00007954.name: LBDS 53W069
gmw_00007954.overview: LBDS 53W069 is a high-redshift, early-type radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.43 [arXiv:astro-ph/0402266, arXiv:1011.6122]. It is characterized as a weak radio source with an estimated stellar age of approximately 4.0 Gyr [arXiv:astro-ph/0402266, arXiv:0708.1214].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes LBDS 53W069 as a high-redshift weak radio early-type galaxy, finding age-metallicity degeneracy but somewhat better stellar-age constraints than for LBDS 53W091.

quotes.quote: Age derivation techniques for unresolved stellar populations at high redshifts are explored using the NUV spectrum of LBDS 53W091 ( z=1.55 ) and LBDS 53W069 ( z=1.43 ). The photometry and morphology of these galaxies -- which are weak radio sources -- suggest they are early-type systems, a feature that makes them ideal test beds for the analysis of their ages and metallicities with the use of population synthesis models. In the analysis that is based on simple stellar population models, we find a significant degeneracy between the derived ages and metallicities both in optical+NIR photometric and NUV spectroscopic analyses. This degeneracy is not so strong for LBDS 53W069. However even in this case the stellar age cannot be constrained better than to a range roughly encompassing one third of the age of the Universe at z=1.43 (90 confidence level).

We found a better constraint on the age estimates, giving a range of ages between 2.8 and 4  Gyr at the 3 confidence level for both galaxies, with a metallicity around solar with a 0.5  dex uncertainty. LBDS 53W069 seems to accept models with higher average metallicities.

sources.summary: The paper uses LBDS 53W069 as a 4.0 Gyr old galaxy at z=1.43 in its OHRO age test and reports that NADE accommodates the lower-redshift old galaxies.

quotes.quote: There have been some OHROs discovered, including, the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 and the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 . In particular, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 is an important one, which has been used as a ``cosmic clock'' to constrain cosmological models. Its age is estimated to be 2.0-3.0 Gyr .

We will use three OHROs: the old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 , the old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at z=1.43 and the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at z=3.91 . The ages of the OHROs at z=1.55 and z=1.43 are 3.5 Gyr and 4.0 Gyr, respectively. For the age of the OHRO at z=3.91 , following , we use the lower bound estimate of t_ obj (3.91)=2.0 Gyr. We calculate the age of the universe at different redshifts using the best fit results of the NADE model, n=2.886 and h=0.685 , and then we obtain the values of : (1.55)=1.215 , (1.43)=1.136 , and (3.91)=0.833 , also shown in Table table3 . So, the NADE model can easily accommodate the OHROs at z=1.55 and 1.43, but cannot accommodate the old quasar at z=3.91 .

sources.summary: The paper uses LBDS 53W069 as an old high-redshift galaxy age constraint, noting its 4.0 Gyr age at z=1.43 and finding the agegraphic dark-energy model can accommodate it.

quotes.quote: We investigate the age constraint on the agegraphic dark energy model by using two old galaxies (LBDS 53W091 and LBDS 53W069 ) and the old high redshift quasar APM 08279+5255 . We find that the agegraphic dark energy model can easily accommodate LBDS 53W091 and LBDS 53W069 .
LBDS 53W069 was 4.0 Gyr at z=1.43 .
The constraints given by LBDS 53W091 ( z=1.55 ) and LBDS 53W069 ( z=1.43 ) can be easily satisfied in the agegraphic dark energy model, as many other dark energy models.

sources.summary: The paper treats LBDS 53W069 as a 4.0 Gyr old high-redshift galaxy at z = 1.43 used with other OHROs to test LTB void cosmologies.

quotes.quote: There are some old high redshift objects (OHROs) considered extensively in the literature, for instance, the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55   , the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43   . In addition, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91   is also used extensively.

In the literature, these three OHROs have been extensively used to test various dark energy models (see e.g. ) and modified gravity models (see e.g. ). In the present work, we will use them to test various LTB void models.

Here, we try to test various LTB void models with three OHROs mentioned in Sec.  sec1 , namely the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55   , the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43   , and the 2.0 Gyr old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91   .

sources.summary: The paper uses LBDS 53W069 as a 4.0 Gyr galaxy at z=1.43 and says prior holographic-dark-energy tests could accommodate this old high-redshift object.

quotes.quote: Now, there are some OHROs discovered, for example, the 3.5 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 and the 4.0 Gyr old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 .

In the work of Wei and Zhang , the original holographic dark energy model (neither spatial curvature nor interaction is involved) has been examined by using the three OHROs, the old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 , the old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 , and the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 . It is found in Ref. that the former two OHROs, the old galaxy LBDS 53W091 at redshift z=1.55 and the old galaxy LBDS 53W069 at redshift z=1.43 , can be easily accommodated, but the last one, the old quasar APM 08279+5255 at redshift z=3.91 , cannot be accommodated in the model.

sources.summary: The paper lists 53W069 as a 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy at z=1.43 and estimates its formation redshift under several coupled dark-energy models.

quotes.quote: Not to mention the issues that the 14-Gyr age of the universe obtained by Pont et al. , which is in tension with 13.797 0.023 Gyr , the 3.5-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W091 at z = 1.55 and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 .

Model 1 With the best-fitting parameters in this model, we have: EoR ( 6 z 15 ), for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), from around 379.9 Myr (402.6 Myr) to 1.825 Gyr (1.718 Gyr), which lasts 1.445 Gyr (1.315 Gyr); The seed redshift of J0100+2802, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), z_ seed 9.74 (11.03); If the first star formed at z 20 , then the age of the universe at that time would be, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 272.8 Myr (234.1 Myr); Old globular cluster M92 (NGC 6341) would appear at z 7.53 \ (9.27) with age of 14.0 Gyr for OHD ( H(z) + BAO); For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 3.5-Gyr-old ratio galaxy 53W091 ( z 1.55 ) and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 ( z 1.43 ) are formed at z 4.98 \ (5.40) and z 5.41 \ (5.99) , respectively; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), QSO APM 08279+5255 with age around 2.1 Gyr would have formed at z 9.37 \ (12.32) .

Model 2 With the best-fitting parameters in this Model, we have: EoR ( 6 z 15 ), for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), from around 478.7 Myr (534.1 Myr) to 1.371 Gyr (1.462 Gyr), which lasts 892.4 Myr (927.8 Myr); The seed redshift of J0100+2802, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), z_ seed 17.75 \ (16.46) ; If the first star formed at z 20 , then the age of the universe at that time would be, for OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 337.1 Myr (381.4 Myr); Old globular cluster M92 (NGC 6341) would appear at z 18.19 \ (18.98) with age of 14.0 Gyr for OHD ( H(z) + BAO) ; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), 3.5-Gyr-old ratio galaxy 53W091 ( z 1.55 ) and 4-Gyr-old radio galaxy 53W069 ( z 1.43 ) are formed at z 5.95 \ (6.20) and z 6.85 \ (7.22) , respectively; For OHD ( H(z) + BAO), QSO APM 08279+5255 with age around 2.1 Gyr would have formed at z 92.16 \ (44.83) .

gmw_00007955.name: Leo P
gmw_00007955.overview: Leo P is an extremely gas-rich dwarf galaxy located between 1.5 and 2.0 Mpc from the Milky Way [arXiv:1305.0270]. It is a very low-luminosity system that contains resolved stars and exhibits ongoing star formation [arXiv:1305.0270].

sources.summary: The paper presents Leo P as a newly observed gas-rich dwarf beyond the Local Group, with resolved stars, ongoing star formation, distance constraints, low luminosity, high gas content, and extreme low mass.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the field located at the position of an ALFALFA detection at 10h 21m 45.0s +18d 05m 01s

quotes.quote: In the current paper, we present optical observations of one such object, the gas-rich galaxy AGC 208583 ( = Leo P), discovered by the ALFALFA \ survey (Giovanelli et al. \ 2005; Haynes et al. \ 2011) and described in Giovanelli et al. \ (2013). We have obtained ground-based narrowband H and broadband BVR images of Leo P and made photometric measurements of the resolved stellar population detected in the data. We use these optical data to construct color-magnitude diagrams (CMDs) of the galaxy's stellar component. These CMDs are used to constrain the distance to the galaxy and show that Leo P is likely to be located between 1.5 and 2.0 Mpc from the Milky Way.

If we adopt for this discussion a distance of 1.75 0.25 Mpc, then the picture that emerges is one of a very low-luminosity dwarf galaxy (M _V = 9.3 0.3; diameter = 760 90 pc) that is also extremely gas rich. It has an \ mass of 9.4 10 ^5 M _ and an to-stellar mass ratio M _ HI /M _* 2.6 (the latter number being independent of the distance). It is significantly less luminous than other gas-rich dwarfs like Leo A and Phoenix (M _V = 12.1 and 9.9, respectively; McConnachie 2012), and hence represents the least luminous/massive system in the local universe known to have current star formation.

gmw_00007956.name: RBS 1055
gmw_00007956.overview: RBS 1055 is a radio-quiet quasar located at a redshift of 0.450 [arXiv:1011.4139, arXiv:1501.00913]. Its X-ray spectrum shows very weak intrinsic neutral absorption and features a resolved, redshifted neutral iron K line [arXiv:1011.4139, arXiv:1501.00913].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes RBS 1055 as the most X-ray luminous radio-quiet RBS-QSO in the sample, requiring two power laws and showing no excess neutral absorption plus a resolved neutral Fe K line.

quotes.quote: X-ray Spectra of RBS 1055 We start with the simplest modeling of the 0.2--12 keV data of the most X-ray luminous radio-quiet RBS-QSO: a simple power law (henceforth called the ``1PL'' model); this and all models discussed herein are corrected for Galactic absorption (see Table table:observed ). For all objects, studied in this paper, no significant evidence for neutral absorption in excess of the Galactic column density is found. As the 1PL model is a poor fit ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 1413/1251), we then try two power laws, one modeling the soft, the other the hard energy range (henceforth called the ``2PL'' model). ^2 /d.o.f. improves to 1288/1249. The only residuals left are positive residuals around 6.4 keV in rest frame (4.4 keV in observed frame as shown in Figure rbs1055_single ).

Since a fit with an unresolved component (Gaussian line profile with a line centroid fixed at 6.4 keV and width =0.001 keV) results in a worse fit ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 1277/1247) and the lower limit 95 confidence errors of the measured _ Fe,rest does not include =0.001 keV, we conclude that we have detected a resolved, neutral Fe K line with EW =0.14^ +0.14 _ 0.10 keV (3 uncertainties) in the spectrum of RBS 1055. We exclude the presence of an additional, unresolved component: we add a second narrow Gaussian with energy centroid fixed at 6.4 keV (rest frame) and fixed at 1 eV, but this yields no improvement to the fit. The freely fitted line energy of E=6.25 keV and the broad shape could potentially be caused by the presence of a Compton shoulder to the 6.4 keV Fe line.

sources.summary: The paper identifies QSO B1157--1942 as the lowest-redshift AGN-E13 quasar, fits its X-ray spectrum, and reports weak absorption plus a redshifted iron-line residual.

quotes.quote: QSO B1157--1942 at z =0.450. QSO B1157--1942 is the lowest redshift quasar ( z=0.45 ) in the AGN-E13 sample. They quote a very low intrinsic column density of N_ H (z)<1 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 . \ observed the quasar on Jun 2008. The exposure time is 25 ks and 24 ks for the MOS and pn detectors, respectively.

Fitting the data with an absorbed power -law model ( N_ H ^ Gal =3.2 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 ) , we derive N_ H (z)<6 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 (in line with previous results) and ^2_ red =1.16 (791 dof). The residuals show clear signs of an underlying, redshifted iron line. Adding a free Gaussian component at the quasar redshift, the fit improves to ^2_ red =1.02 (788 dof, F-test probability >8 ).

gmw_00007957.name: RBS 320
gmw_00007957.overview: RBS 320 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1011.4139]. Its X-ray spectrum is best characterized by a two-power-law model and features a detected iron K emission line [arXiv:1011.4139].

sources.summary: The paper finds RBS 320 needs a two-power-law X-ray model, rejects blackbody and partial-covering alternatives, and detects an unresolved Fe K emission line.

quotes.quote: X-ray Spectra of RBS 320 For the analysis of the RBS 320 data (and the remaining RBS objects), we follow the same approach as described in detail for RBS 1055. We will only summarize the most important findings. As for RBS 1055, we also find that a 2PL model is required to characterize the overall shape of the RBS 320 data appropriately ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 934/808) although positive residuals are still present at 6--7 keV (rest frame) where the Fe K line is expected (see Figure rbs320_single ). A PLBB model ( k_ B T = 0.09 0.01 keV, = 2.42 0.02 ) cannot fit the data well ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 1099/808). The best-fit partial covering model incorporating absorption by a gas with a column density of N_ H 2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and a covering fraction of 60 fails to properly model the data ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 1071/808).

Consequently, also in the case of RBS 320 we conclude that an Fe K emission line is detected. Adding a Gaussian component to the 2PL model results in an improved fit (2PLG, ^2 /d.o.f. = 924/806, Table powerlaw ) and yields _ Fe,rest =0.27^ +0.42 _ 0.20 keV for an Fe K line with energy centroid fixed at E=6.4 keV. The line is detected at more than 3 confidence.

gmw_00007958.name: RBS 1124
gmw_00007958.overview: RBS 1124 is a radio-quiet quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.4139]. Its X-ray spectra reveal a variable soft excess, which rules out an origin in a distant region, and a non-varying iron line profile [arXiv:1011.4139].

sources.summary: The paper models RBS 1124 with XMM-Newton and Suzaku spectra, finding two-power-law fits, excluding ionized absorption, observing variable soft excess, and constraining a non-varying Fe line profile.

quotes.quote: X-ray Spectra of RBS 1124 A 2PL model again represented the data better ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 1525/1291) than a 1PL model ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 1660/1293; see Table powerlaw and Figure rbs1124XMM_single ). The PLBB model does not provide a better fit ( k_ B T = 0.14 0.01 keV, =1.88 0.02 , ^2 /d.o.f. = 1580/1291). A partial covering model with a column density of N_ H 4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and a covering fraction of 21 describes the data very well ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 1515/1291). A full-covering and partial-covering absorption model with ionized material can be excluded because they both require very high column densities and ionization parameters ( N_ H 1 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 , log ( /(erg cm s ^ 1 )) = 5 ; see explanation for RBS 320).

Because the soft excess is observed to vary, an origin in a very extended or distant region (greater than 13 light months from the central X-ray source) is ruled out. The detailed relation between the origins and interactions of the soft excess and the hard power law for RBS 1124 remain unclear. However, the parameters for the Fe line profile do not vary between the two observations (line parameters are consistent within the 1 uncertainties), and so we can do a joint fit to achieve better constraints on the Fe line.

gmw_00007959.name: RBS 1883
gmw_00007959.overview: RBS 1883 is a radio-quiet quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1011.4139]. Its X-ray spectrum is well-fit by a two-power-law model, and a partial covering model indicates a high covering fraction [arXiv:1011.4139].

sources.summary: The paper finds RBS 1883 has noisy XMM-Newton data but is better fit by two power laws; partial covering improves the fit, while warm absorber, reflection, and Fe-line constraints remain limited.

quotes.quote: X-ray Spectra of RBS 1883 The observation of RBS 1883 suffers heavily from very high background count rates throughout almost the entire observation. Although the low number of counts does not allow us to study RBS 1883 as detailed as the other RBS QSOs presented in this paper, a 2PL model fits the data much better than a 1PL ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 180/86 versus 119/84; see Table powerlaw and Figure rbs1883_single ). A PLBB model results in a fair fit of the data ( k_ B T = 0.13 0.01 ; ^2 /d.o.f. = 124/84). A partial covering model improves the fit ( ^2 /d.o.f. = 113/84) by finding a very high covering fraction of (82 6) and N_ H,cov = (1.4^ +0.6 _ 0.4 ) 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

The S/N of the data does not allow us to test for a warm absorber in RBS 1883; the ionization parameter and N_ H,warm stay unconstrained. For the same reason we are not able to apply a reflecting disk model to the data. Although no emission line features, e.g., an Fe K line, are visible in the spectra, we included a Gaussian component fixed to an energy of 6.4 keV (rest frame) to give upper limits on the Fe K emission line.

gmw_00007960.name: SDSS J095351.58+012036.1
gmw_00007960.overview: SDSS J095351.58+012036.1 is a polar-ring galaxy candidate located at a photometric redshift of approximately 0.2 [arXiv:2208.12388]. It features a perpendicular polar structure with a flat, ring-like light profile that shows no signs of disturbance [arXiv:2208.12388].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J095351.58+012036.1/J0953 as a new polar-ring galaxy candidate at z~0.2, with a perpendicular, undisturbed polar structure and ring-like light profile needing confirmation.

quotes.quote: During the course of this study, we have discovered a new PRG, identified as SDSS J095351.58+012036.1. Its photometric redshift is estimated as z 0.2 . The polar ring structure in this PRG appears to be almost perpendicular to the disk of its host galaxy without any disturbed features. Therefore, this PRG will provide us with useful information on the formation of such an undisturbed polar structure.

On the other hand, the polar structure has the flatter light profile than the exponential disk; its S e rsic index is 0.47, being much less than 1. This small S e rsic index is consistent with that the polar component has a ring-like structure. Further imaging observation with a higher spatial resolution is necessary to confirm if the polar structure is a ring or not.

gmw_00007961.name: IC 3322A
gmw_00007961.overview: IC 3322A is an edge-on spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 25 Mpc [arXiv:2201.07242]. The galaxy hosts a highly luminous ultraluminous X-ray source whose thermal spectrum is consistent with an undetected, heavily obscured young Type IIn supernova [arXiv:2201.07242].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 3322A as the edge-on host of the sample’s most luminous ULX, arguing its thermal X-ray spectrum and extinction are consistent with an undetected young Type IIn supernova.

quotes.quote: Candidate Type IIn SN in IC 3322A CXOU J122541.70 + 071338.9, detected in IC 3322A (distance of 25 Mpc) on 2018 April 24, is the most luminous ULX in the whole sample. Its X-ray spectrum (Table 3) suggests multi-temperature thermal plasma emission and an intrinsic column density of 2 10^ 22 erg s ^ 1 , for an intrinsic luminosity of 6 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . A spectrum dominated by X-ray thermal plasma at such a high luminosity is very unusual for a ULX. The X-ray spectrum is consistent with that of a young SN; more specifically, of a Type IIn, which explodes in a denser circumstellar medium.

No core-collapse SNe have ever been reported in IC 3322A, but that is not strong evidence against our suggested interpretation. The peak absolute optical brightness of Type IIn SNe is broadly distributed between about 17 mag and 21 mag, with a median value around 19 mag nyholm20 . The distance modulus of IC 3322A is 32 mag. To that, we may add 10 mag of visual extinction, based on the best-fitting value of N_ H 2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 predehl95,guver09,willingale13 . Thus, it is likely that the presumed SN was fainter than 20 mag in the optical, and would have been easily missed by large-area sky searches.

gmw_00007962.name: NGC 4480
gmw_00007962.overview: NGC 4480 is a gas-rich spiral galaxy with an uncertain distance and cluster membership [arXiv:2201.07242]. Its Tully-Fisher distance is estimated at approximately 42 Mpc, which would place it outside the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2201.07242].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4480 as a distance and membership outlier whose gas-rich status and uncertain Tully-Fisher distance affect whether its brightest source enters the high-luminosity ULX census.

quotes.quote: There are only three outliers (NGC 4412, NGC 4416, NGC 4480, labelled in Figure 3) which have Tully-Fisher distances ( 40 Mpc) much larger than their substructure distances (17 Mpc). We do not have elements to decide at this stage which set of distances is more reliable, and whether those three galaxies are Virgo cluster members. The uncertainty in the distance to NGC 4480, in particular, affects our census of the most luminous ULXs, because the most luminous point-like source in that galaxy has either an X-ray luminosity 10 ^ 40 erg s ^ 1 or barely above 10 ^ 39 erg s ^ 1 depending on the choice of distance.

In addition, a 10th ULX exceeded 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , in NGC 4480, if its Tully-Fisher distance of 42 Mpc is correct; however, in that case, NGC 4480 would most likely not be a member of the Virgo cluster. Thus, we omitted that source from subsequent plots and modelling of the cumulative and differential luminosity distributions. For consistency, we also subtracted the SFR and stellar mass of NGC 4480 when we calculated the predicted contribution of HMXBs and LMXBs, and subtracted the projected area of its D_ 25 when we calculated the predicted contribution of foreground and background sources.

gmw_00007963.name: UGC5288
gmw_00007963.overview: UGC 5288 is an isolated, gas-rich dwarf galaxy classified as an Sdm type [arXiv:2304.00724]. It features a low surface brightness, a strong central bar [arXiv:2304.00724, arXiv:1911.11877], and a low star formation rate confined to its central stellar disk [arXiv:2304.00724].

sources.summary: The paper models UGC 5288 as an isolated low-surface-brightness gas-rich barred dwarf, estimating halo spin and concentration and favoring a pseudo-isothermal dark matter halo with core radius 0.23 kpc.

quotes.quote: In this study we investigate a method for estimating the halo spin and halo concentration of low surface brightness (LSB), gas-rich dwarf barred galaxy UGC 5288, by forward modelling disk properties derived from observations - stellar and gas surface densities, disk scale length, HI rotation curve, bar length and bar ellipticity. We combine semi-analytical techniques, N-body/SPH and cosmological simulations to model the DM halo of UGC 5288 with both a cuspy Hernquist profile and a flat-core pseudo-isothermal profile. We find that the best match with observations is a pseudo-isothermal halo model with a core radius of r_ = 0.23 kpc, and halo spin of = 0.08 at the virial radius. Although our findings are consistent with previous core radius estimates of the halo density profile of UGC 5288, as well as with the halo spin profiles of similar mass analogues of UGC5288 in the high-resolution cosmological-magneto-hydrodynamical simulation TNG50, there still remain some uncertainties as we are limited in our knowledge of the formation history of the galaxy.

We searched through several studies of LSBGs in the literature and selected the dwarf LSBG UGC 5288, since it is an isolated galaxy and slightly inclined so that the measured rotation curve is reliable. It is a low luminosity galaxy in the Lynx Cancer void having a large HI disk Van.zee.2000,van.Zee.2004,van.Zee.2006, Kurapati.et.al.2018 , a small bulge (as seen from surface density distribution from WIRCAM data Fingerhut.2010 ), and a low star formation rate (SFR) of 0.0063 M yr ^ 1 Werk.et.al.2011 which is confined to the central part of the stellar disk Van.zee.2000, van.Zee.2004 . The central surface brightness is 22 magnitude/ arcsec^2 in the B band Makarova.1999 i.e., 101 L / pc^ 2 in physical units. So this galaxy is borderline LSB in nature and has been classified as an Sdm galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC5288 as a barred void dwarf whose isothermal halo fit outperformed NFW and whose bar affects velocity-field slope estimates and non-circular-motion interpretation.

quotes.quote: For 2 galaxies, viz. UGC4115 and UGC5288, the isothermal halo gave a better fit compared to the NFW halo. We note that the galaxy UGC5288 has a strong bar.
The difference in slopes from the Hermite velocity field and the first moment map is significant for the galaxy UGC5288, which is a barred galaxy.

In our case, we find no clear evidence for non-circular motions, except in the case of UGC 5288, where there appears to be a strong bar and we indeed find that NFW halo is not a good fit for this galaxy.

gmw_00007964.name: UM 247
gmw_00007964.overview: UM 247 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits a Lyman-alpha residual after point spread function subtraction [arXiv:1502.05132]. This residual reveals a compact excess emission feature located 4 to 8 kiloparsecs north of the quasar [arXiv:1502.05132].

sources.summary: UM 247 is the only quasar in the sample with a significant Ly residual after PSF subtraction; the paper identifies a compact excess 4-8 kpc north with measured line flux and luminosity.

quotes.quote: Small-scale Ly fuzz near UM 247 UM 247 is the only object in our sample with a formally significant residual at Ly after PSF subtraction. To assess whether this residual is real and not an artefact caused by a fault (e.g. an undetected cosmic) in a single exposure, we repeated the stacking of the individual exposures (Sect. sec:stacking ) 5 times, each time with one exposure excluded from the stack. We then performed the empirical PSF subtraction in exactly the same manner as described above (Sect. sec:psf ) for each of those stacks. The feature remained, hence we assert that it is genuine.

This basically unresolved excess emission is located at a distance of d 4-8 kpc (0.5-1 ) north of UM 247. The line has a flux of 5.6 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , corresponding to a Ly luminosity of L_ Ly 2.4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . If the Ly emission of this feature was powered purely by star formation, ignoring ionization of the nearby quasar as well as radiative transfer effects, this would correspond to a star formation rate of 10 -20 M _ yr ^ 1 with the exact value being dependent on the metallicity of the underlying stellar population, see Schaerer2003 . This Ly luminosity is the bright end of the Ly emitter luminosity function for z 2.3 Blanc2011 .

gmw_00007965.name: GDS3073
gmw_00007965.overview: GDS3073 is a compact active galactic nucleus host galaxy at a redshift of approximately 5.56 [arXiv:2006.02451, arXiv:2206.03503]. The system contains an overmassive active black hole [arXiv:2404.04148], exhibits extreme nitrogen enrichment [arXiv:2404.04148], and shows evidence of a possible ongoing merger [arXiv:2206.03503].

sources.summary: The paper presents GDS3073 as a compact z=5.56 GOODS-South AGN candidate; high-ionization emission lines and ALMA CII detection support its active, high-redshift nature.

quotes.quote: GDS3073 The source GDS3073 has sky coordinate RA=03:32:18.92 DEC=-27:53:02.7 and belongs to the CANDELS/GOODS-South field. Its F850LP magnitude is 24.52 and its spectroscopic redshift is z 5.56 raiter10,vanzella10 . GDS3073 has a spatially compact morphology in the ACS HST images and resemble an almost unresolved point-like source, similar to GDN3333. This object is not detected in the ultradeep 7 Msec X-ray image by Chandra down to a flux limit of 10^ 17 erg s^ 1 in the 0.5-2.0 keV band giallongo19 . GDS3073 has been detected by ALMA in CII at 158 m by the ALPINE survey (``ALMA Large Program to INvestigate C+ at Early Times''), as described in alpine .

Strong emission lines of high ionization (NV 1240, NIV ] 1483-1486) are clearly detected in the spectrum, while other high ionization lines (Lyman- , OVI 1032-1038) are barely visible. The CIV line at 1549.1 rest-frame is probably present, but it falls on a sky emission line and it is only barely visible after 20 hours of exposure. In particular, the NV 1240 and OVI 1032-1038 emission lines unambiguously ensure the AGN nature of this object.

sources.summary: The paper measures GS 3073's extreme nitrogen-rich abundance pattern, high Fe/O, and stratified enrichment, linking dense central gas to its overmassive active black hole.

quotes.quote: We report the chemical abundance pattern of GS 3073, a galaxy at z=5.55 which was previously confirmed to host an overmassive active black hole, by leveraging the detection of about 40 emission lines, combining JWST/NIRSpec observations and ground-based (VLT/VIMOS) data. By using rest-frame UV emission lines, which trace high-density ( 10^5 cm ^ 3 ) and highly ionized gas, we could accurately derive an abundance ratio of log(N/O) = 0.46^ +0.12 _ 0.09 . At an estimated metallicity of 0.2 Z_ , this is the most extreme nitrogen-rich object found by JWST thus far, and nearly two orders of magnitude above the (N/O)--(O/H) relation of local galaxies.

We also detected coronal lines, including 6087 and potentially 5303 . We inferred a high Fe abundance of Fe/O 0.1 . The derived Fe abundance indicates the destruction of dust grains in the high-ionization region of this system. Overall, the chemical abundance pattern of GS 3073 is compatible with enrichment by super-massive stars with M_* 1000 M_ , ejecta from asymptotic giant branch (AGB) stars, or winds from Wolf-Rayet (WR) stars, although the WR scenario is less likely.

sources.summary: The paper models GDS J033218.92-275302.7 as a compact z=5.563 emission-line galaxy whose apparent Balmer break can be explained by nebular emission from a hot ionizing source.

quotes.quote: The source GDS J033218.92 275302.7 (hereafter ) was selected for the spectroscopic programme as a band dropout and has a blue colour due to a strong \ emission line falling in the band. It was subsequently studied in the context of SED fitting by sta07 and by wik08 who both conclude, on the basis of its apparently large Balmer break, that it is a high mass, old stellar population. Since the restframe UV images obtained with the ACS on HST show a very small angular size, the implication is that this is an example of an ultradense spheroid at a very early epoch.

GDS J033218.92-275302.7 Two previous studies have attempted to fit the SED of using the apparently large Balmer break to resolve the age-extinction degeneracy. sta07 , using ID = 32 8020, and wik08 , as BBG 5197, both obtain an age of 0.9 Gyr, a stellar mass of close to 10^ 11 M _ \ and zero extinction. The latter authors, who use the two longer wavelength IRAC channels, 5.7 and 8.0 m, note that their fit is worsened by a deviant 5.7 m point.

sources.summary: The paper describes GDS3073 as a compact high-redshift AGN host and possible merger detected in [C II], with archival X-SHOOTER spectra showing Ly and N IV] emission.

quotes.quote: We also included GDS3073 and UVISTA-Z-002 with available X-SHOOTER archival data of the C III ] line and the [ C II ] line data either published in the literature (for GDS3073; 2020A&A...643A...2B ) or available in the ALMA archive (for UVISTA-Z-002; 2021arXiv210613719B ). GDS3073 is a compact ( 0.11 \ kpc ; 2010A&A...513A..20V ), active galactic nuclei (AGN) host galaxy at z 5.56 , with a possible ongoing merger ( 2020ApJ...897...94G ) that has been detected in [ C II ] by ALMA (labeled as CANDELS GOODSS 14; as part of the ALPINE survey ( ALMA Large Program to INvestigate C+ at Early times ; 2020A&A...643A...1L ).

GDS3073 has been observed with FORS2, VIMOS, and X-SHOOTER spectrographs at the VLT telescope and several UV rest-frame line detection have been reported, including N V ] \ 1240 and O VI \ 1032-1038 , confirming the AGN nature of GDS3073 ( 2010A&A...513A..20V ; 2020ApJ...897...94G ). We recover a very bright Ly emission detection with a S/N 22 and N IV ] line emission with a S/N 3.9 . For the C III ] line, we place an upper limit on the velocity integrated flux, assuming FWHM_ C III ] = FWHM_ [ C II ] , where the FWHM_ [ C II ] = 230 16 \ km \ s^ 1 derived by .

gmw_00007966.name: 5C4.74
gmw_00007966.overview: 5C4.74 is a distant background Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy with two distinct radio lobes [arXiv:1002.0594]. The galaxy has no known optical identification, and its redshift remains undetermined [arXiv:1002.0594].

sources.summary: The paper treats 5C4.74 as a distant background FRII radio source with two lobes and reports its unknown redshift, absent optical identification, and lobe polarizations.

quotes.quote: 5C4.74 The source 5C4.74 consists of 5C4.74a and 5C4.74b, the two radio lobes of a FRII radio source. Its redshift is unknown, and no optical identification has been found, either with a Coma cluster member (Miller et al. 2009) nor with a background radio source. From this we conclude that it is a distant background source. The northeastern lobe has a fractional polarization of 28 and 35 respectively at 4.535 and 8.465 GHz, while the southwestern lobe is less polarized ( 19 at 4.535 GHz and 23 at 8.465 GHz). In Fig. fig:74_pol the radio emission is shown at 4.535 and 8.465 GHz.

gmw_00007967.name: 5C4.127
gmw_00007967.overview: 5C4.127 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=1.374 [arXiv:1002.0594]. In addition to its bright nucleus, the source features a weak east-west extension spanning 16 arcseconds, or approximately 136 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1002.0594].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 5C4.127 as a z=1.374 QSO with a bright nucleus, weak east-west extension, and measured fractional polarization in the extension and nucleus.

quotes.quote: 5C4.127 5C4.127 is a QSO located at z=1.374 (Veron-Cetty Veron, 2001). Observations presented here show that in addition to a bright nucleus the source has a weak extension in the E-W direction of 16 '' ( 136 kpc) at both of the observing frequency bands. The extended component has a mean fractional polarization of 13 at 4.535 GHz and 14 at 8.465 GHz, while the nucleus is polarized at the 3 level. In Fig. fig:127_pol radio contours of the source and polarization vector images are shown.

gmw_00007968.name: 5C4.42
gmw_00007968.overview: 5C4.42 is an optically unidentified background radio galaxy classified as an FRII-type source [arXiv:1002.0594]. It features a weakly polarized core and two lobes extending 25 arcseconds in the southwest and northeast directions [arXiv:1002.0594].

sources.summary: The paper describes 5C4.42 as an optically unidentified background FRII radio source with a weakly polarized core, two lobes, and measured lobe polarization.

quotes.quote: 5C4.42 5C4.42 is a FRII-type radio source. Redshift information is not available in the literature and no optical identification has been found. The same arguments explained above for the source 5C4.74 let us conclude that it is a background radio source. The source is composed by a weakly polarized core and two lobes that extend for 25 '' in the southwest and northeast directions. The lobes show a mean fractional polarization of 13 at both 4.535 GHz and 8.465 GHz. In Fig. fig:42_pol radio contours and vector polarization images of the source are shown.

gmw_00007969.name: 5C4.152
gmw_00007969.overview: 5C4.152 is a background Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy with no known redshift [arXiv:1002.0594]. The galaxy is composed of a core and two lobes that extend 28 arcseconds in a north-south direction [arXiv:1002.0594].

sources.summary: The paper describes 5C4.152 as a background FRII radio galaxy with no known redshift, a core, north-south lobes, and measured lobe polarization.

quotes.quote: 5C4.152 5C4.152 is a FRII type Radio Galaxy. No redshift is available in the literature for this source. The same arguments explained above for the source 5C4.74 let us conclude that it is a background radio source. It is composed of a core having a fractional polarization of a few percent and two lobes that extend for 28 '' north-south. The lobes show a mean fractional polarization of 13 4.535 GHz and 15 at 8.275 GHz. In Fig. fig:152_pol radio contours and vector polarization images of the source are shown.

gmw_00007970.name: PAndAS-48
gmw_00007970.overview: PAndAS-48 is a faint, diffuse, and moderately flattened stellar system located in the outskirts of the M31 halo [arXiv:1304.7826]. It is composed of an ancient, metal-poor stellar population, with physical characteristics that place it between an extended globular cluster and a faint dwarf satellite galaxy [arXiv:1304.7826].

sources.summary: The paper presents PAndAS-48 as an ancient, metal-poor, diffuse M31 satellite whose size, luminosity, flattening, and stellar population leave its dwarf-galaxy versus cluster status ambiguous.

quotes.quote: We present Hubble Space Telescope imaging of a newly-discovered faint stellar system, PAndAS-48, in the outskirts of the M31 halo. Our photometry reveals this object to be comprised of an ancient and very metal-poor stellar population with age 10 \ Gyr and [ Fe / H ] 2.3 . Our inferred distance modulus (m-M)_0=24.57 0.11 confirms that PAndAS-48 is most likely a remote M31 satellite with a 3D galactocentric radius of 149^ +19 _ 8 \ kpc. We observe an apparent spread in color on the upper red giant branch that is larger than the photometric uncertainties should allow, and briefly explore the implications of this.

Structurally, PAndAS-48 is diffuse, faint, and moderately flattened, with a half-light radius r_h=26^ +4 _ 3 \ pc, integrated luminosity M_V=-4.8 0.5 , and ellipticity =0.30^ +0.08 _ 0.15 . On the size-luminosity plane it falls between the extended globular clusters seen in several nearby galaxies, and the recently-discovered faint dwarf satellites of the Milky Way; however, its characteristics do not allow us to unambiguously class it as either type of system. If PAndAS-48 is a globular cluster then it is the among the most elliptical, isolated, and metal-poor of any seen in the Local Group, extended or otherwise. Conversely, while its properties are generally consistent with those observed for the faint Milky Way dwarfs, it would be a factor 2-3 smaller in spatial extent than any known counterpart of comparable luminosity.

gmw_00007971.name: B2 1040+31A
gmw_00007971.overview: B2 1040+31A is a radio galaxy that originates from the largest galaxy in a close triple system [arXiv:0912.3001]. This system is embedded within a large region of intragroup medium, and the host galaxy exhibits bright X-ray emission that is primarily thermal [arXiv:0912.3001].

sources.summary: B2 1040+31A is described as a radio galaxy in a close triple system, embedded in intragroup gas, with bright X-ray host emission interpreted mainly as thermal.

quotes.quote: B2 1040+31A ( z = 0.036 ) : The radio galaxy emanates from the largest galaxy in a close triple system fanti77 which itself is contained within a large region of intragroup medium. The larger IGM is in agreement with worrall00 , but for the relevant medium we use a smaller region of IGM which is bright against the large region and centered on the system. In the DSS image the host galaxy cannot be isolated from its companions, so we use the SDSS image alone. In the X-ray band, the host galaxy is bright; whether it is AGN emission is unclear based on the spectrum. Because the character of the emission does not change much outside the PSF, we assume we are seeing primarily thermal emission.

gmw_00007972.name: 3C 192
gmw_00007972.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 192 is a high-excitation, narrow-line Fanaroff-Riley II source [arXiv:1508.03287, arXiv:astro-ph/9912477] that exhibits a winged, X-shaped double-lobe morphology [arXiv:0912.3001, arXiv:0908.4391]. It features two-sided VLBA jets [arXiv:0908.4391], moderate ultraviolet excess likely powered by its active nucleus [arXiv:1508.03287], and mid- to far-infrared dust emission [arXiv:1508.03287].

sources.summary: 3C 192 is treated as a winged galaxy resembling X-shaped radio galaxies, with enhanced X-ray emission near the lobes and uncertain thermal or power-law origin.

quotes.quote: 3C 192 ( z=0.060 ) : 3C 192 is formally a ``winged'' galaxy cheung07 , but the morphology is otherwise very similar to X shaped radio galaxies. Enhanced emission is detected in an elliptical region encompassing the primary radio lobes, but a smaller enhancement we identify with the ISM exists near the AGN. It is also possible that this is the central region of the IGM, but it appears compact. Moreover, the ``IGM'' is consistent with being fit only by a power law ( = 2.5 ) and is in good spatial agreement with the primary lobes. It may also be fit by a thermal model with kT 0.9 and Z < 0.1 ; if it is indeed thermal emission, the IGM is in perfect agreement with capetti02 .

sources.summary: 3C 192 shows moderate UV excess, HEG emission lines, probable AGN-powered UV emission, FRII radio morphology, and MIR/FIR dust.

quotes.quote: 3C 192. This source has a moderate UV excess ( XUV=1.3 ). The optical spectrum shows the strong emission lines typical of a HEG. No signs of star formation are apparent, and AGN emission producing the UV excess is more probable. We also note that 3C 192 is an FRII radio source. MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mixture of dust with temperatures of 60 K ( M_d = 1.6 10^4 M_ ) and 140 K ( M_d = 3.5 10^2 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 192 as the sample's narrow-line FRII outlier, probably affected by HST resolution or line contamination that makes its optical flux over-bright.

quotes.quote: The one narrow-line FRII in the sample, 3C 192, lies some way off the correlation. This may be an artefact of the lower resolution of the HST observations (Table ss ), or it may indicate a difference between FRIs and FRIIs.

The only narrow-line FRII object in the sample is 3C 192, which is an outlier on all the correlations involving optical data, since its optical flux is over-bright relative to its radio or X-ray flux density. There is likely to be a significant amount of line contamination in the HST flux of this object, and it was observed with the WFC rather than the PC, reducing our ability to remove background emission. Nevertheless, it is possible that the anomalous behaviour of 3C 192 is an indication of a difference between FRI and FRII objects.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0802+24 as an X-shaped double-lobed radio source with extended narrow-line emission and two-sided VLBA jets aligned with the kiloparsec structure.

quotes.quote: 0802+24 (3C 192) -- On large scale, this radio source shows an 'X' symmetric double-lobe structure which extends 200 arcsec at 8.35 GHz, showing bright hotspots at the end of the lobes ( ). Extended narrow line emission has been detected in this galaxy. In our VLBA image (Fig. f0802.1 ), the source appears two-sided, with jets at the same orientation as the kiloparsec-scale jets (P.A. 80 ^ ). The core flux density is 2.1 mJy and the total flux density is 3.6 mJy. The correlated VLBI flux density is 50 of the arcsecond core flux.

sources.summary: The paper reports no unresolved near-infrared core for 3C 192, severe IRAC starlight contamination, and no silicate absorption despite high extinction from other methods.

quotes.quote: Despite the fact that we have not detected an unresolved core source in 3C 192, 3C 321 and 3C 405, in these cases we have estimated an upper limit to the 2.05 m fluxes applying the same procedure explained above (fitting a PSF plus a S ersic profile with galfit ), and then we fixed the free parameters of the S ersic profile to subtract a PSF only using a trial and error approach, until an unphysical hole appeared owing to over-subtraction.
We have excluded 3C 192 because its IRAC fluxes are severely contaminated by starlight.
For instance, in 3C 98 and 3C 192 no silicate absorption is detected, however, a large amount of extinction is derived based on the other four independent techniques.

sources.summary: The paper adds a thermal MEKAL component to model 3C 192, obtaining good fits and noting that its X-ray spectrum had not previously been discussed.

quotes.quote: 3C 192 When this source was fitted with the standard two-component power-law model there were prominent residuals in the range 0.5-1.0 keV, suggesting the addition of a thermal component. We obtained good fits by adding a MEKAL model with fixed kT = 0.5 keV and 0.5 solar abundance. To our knowledge the X-ray spectrum of this source has not been discussed elsewhere.

gmw_00007973.name: 3C 315
gmw_00007973.overview: 3C 315 is an X-shaped radio galaxy with edge-darkened lobes that lack compact hotspots [arXiv:0811.1907]. The galaxy features a small, 8-kiloparsec inner edge-brightened double radio source at its center [arXiv:0811.1907], while its active galactic nucleus is only weakly detected [arXiv:0912.3001].

sources.summary: For 3C 315, the AGN is weakly detected while large-scale diffuse IGM emission is thermal; the authors find no strong evidence that radio activity shaped it.

quotes.quote: 3C 315 ( z=0.108 ) : The AGN is detected at a significance of 5.07 (see Appendix A.3 for methods), but there are too few counts to make a spectrum. The diffuse IGM stands out against the background on large scales and is fit well by thermal models and not by power law models. No ISM is detected, but we can use the galaxy for comparison to the IGM independently. It is possible that this is not real IGM, as we suspect in 3C 192, but the alignment of the radio galaxy and the hot atmosphere is not as good as in 3C 192, nor is it quite as eccentric. Thus, there is no strong evidence that the radio galaxy created the observed X-ray morphology, but the exposure is very short.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C315 as a well-studied X-shaped radio source with edge-darkened lobes, no compact hotspots, and a small inner edge-brightened double aligned with the main source.

quotes.quote: 3C315: This is a well studied X-shaped radio source lea84 with clearly edge-darkened lobes, although the northern lobe has a warm-spot located somewhat away from the end. There are no compact hotspots in either of the two main lobes. Apart from the relaxed appearance for the lobes along the main axis and the lack of hot spots, which are unusual in X-shaped radio sources, 3C315 appears to possess a relatively small, inner edge-brightened double radio source at the centre as in the previous two cases presented above. The inner-double is about 8 kpc in extent and is closely aligned with the axis of the main source (3CRR Atlas 1 , eds. J. P. Leahy, A. H. Bridle, R. G. Strom).

gmw_00007974.name: 3C 83.1B
gmw_00007974.overview: 3C 83.1B is a head-tail radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.018 [arXiv:0912.3001]. It features a southern X-ray edge and northern twin tails, which are likely shaped by interactions between its interstellar medium and the intracluster medium [arXiv:0912.3001].

sources.summary: 3C 83.1B is described as a head-tail galaxy with a southern X-ray edge, central ISM temperature measurements, and radio emission curtailed to the south.

quotes.quote: 3C 83.1B ( z=0.018 ) : The Chandra data from this head-tail galaxy was published by sun05 who argue that there is a distinct southern edge to the X-ray emission which otherwise shares the ellipticity and position angle of the optical isophotes. They apply a deprojection analysis to the ISM data and find that the central ISM has a temperature of kT = 0.45 keV, in agreement with our non-deprojected fit to the central PSF emission with kT = 0.48 keV. sun05 also find that the LMXB contribution to the diffuse gas is on the order of 5 and they show that the southern edge in the X-ray data cannot be produced by ICM pressure, but argue that the edge is a sign of ISM/ICM interaction and that if the galaxy is moving south very rapidly, the long twin tails to the north are naturally explained. The radio emission is likewise curtailed to the south.

gmw_00007975.name: 4C +32.25
gmw_00007975.overview: 4C +32.25 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.053 [arXiv:0912.3001]. Its kiloparsec-scale radio structure features curved jets, extended hotspots, and primary and secondary lobes that may be formed by a precessing jet [arXiv:2402.12521].

sources.summary: The paper maps 4C +32.25 with uGMRT, finding curved jets, extended hotspots, primary and secondary lobes, and precessing-jet models for its kiloparsec radio structure.

quotes.quote: 4C 32.25 Our total intensity maps of 4C 32.25 at 400 MHz and 1250 MHz are presented in Figure gmrt4c32.25 . The high resolution offered by uGMRT at low frequencies and enhanced sensitivity with the bandwidth upgrade enabled us to map the detailed structure of the diffuse emission in both the primary and the secondary lobes. We can distinctly spot the core, the jet towards the east, and the counterjet towards the western side, as inferred from the brightness ratio. The jets are not perfectly linear and possess a significant curvature. Our maps also reveal the extended nature of both hotspots, a feature not witnessed commonly in radio galaxies.

The complex radio structure observed at kiloparsec-scale radio images of 4C +32.25 are essentially formed by brighter east and west radio lobes with hotspots at their ends and fainter and more extended north and west wings (e.g., Parma1985 ). We modeled these radio structures in terms of a precessing jet with a constant speed of 0.033 and a period of about 87.5 Myr.

sources.summary: 4C +32.25 has a detected point source but diffuse emission only as an upper limit, so the authors cannot perform their geometric analysis.
