quotes.quote: 4C +32.25 ( z=0.053 ) : The point source is detected with a significance of 8.52 with S = 20.7^ +9.4 _ 6.3 counts in the detect cell. The diffuse emission within a = 24^ region (much larger than the ISM) has an upper limit unabsorbed thermal luminosity of 4 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 at 1.0 keV, but it does not appear to be centrally concentrated on the chip. Our geometric analysis is not possible in this case, especially since the ISM is the medium of interest given the scale of the radio emission.

gmw_00007976.name: 4C +48.29
gmw_00007976.overview: The active galactic nucleus 4C +48.29 is an extended, X-shaped radio source [arXiv:1411.0361] located at a redshift of z=0.052 [arXiv:0912.3001]. It exhibits a double-peaked H I profile near its systemic velocity [arXiv:1411.0361] and displays barely distinguishable diffuse emission [arXiv:0912.3001].

sources.summary: 4C +48.29 has a weakly detected AGN, barely distinguishable diffuse emission, and an upper limit attributed to faint IGM rather than concentrated ISM.

quotes.quote: 4C +48.29 ( z=0.052 ) : We detect 19 counts in the detect cell (positioned around the core of radio emission) and detect the AGN with a significance of 7.56 and S = 17.6^ +8.9 _ 5.7 counts in a 95 confidence interval. The diffuse emission in a = 24^ region is barely distinguishable from the background and we measure an unabsorbed thermal luminosity upper limit of L = 5 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . We attribute this to the faint IGM as opposed to the ISM due to the scale and lack of central concentration.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4C +48.29 as an extended X-shaped AGN with a double-peaked H I profile near systemic velocity, likely containing systemic and blueshifted H I components.

quotes.quote: 4C +48.29 This extended AGN is an X-shaped radio source Jaegers1987, Mezcua2011, Landt2010 . We detect a double-peaked \ profile close to the systemic velocity. Before Hanning smoothing, the two peaks are more separated, suggesting the presence of two \ components (one at the systemic and one blueshifted).

gmw_00007977.name: 3C 197.1
gmw_00007977.overview: 3C 197.1 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.128 [arXiv:0912.3001]. It features a long northern wing comparable in spatial extent to its active lobe, though it lacks a symmetric southern wing [arXiv:0912.3001].

sources.summary: 3C 197.1 has a long northern wing and ambiguous XRG classification; the authors compare it to classical XRGs but exclude it for redshift and classification uncertainty.

quotes.quote: 3C 197.1 ( z=0.128 ) : 3C 197.1 has a long northern wing which is comparable in spatial extent to the active lobe in a 1.5 GHz image neff95 , but there is no obvious southern wing which could be described as symmetric about the central AGN. In the 5 GHz image of neff95 extensions in the southern lobe appear to be essentially symmetric about the jet axis. 3C 197.1 is included in the saripalli09 list of XRGs, but its inclusion in our sample is questionable. We discuss it here because of the other ``XRGs'' listed in saripalli09 and not in cheung07 , 3C 197.1 bears the most similarity to the classical XRGs listed in cheung07 . We do not include it in our sample due to its relatively high redshift and ambiguity of classification.

gmw_00007978.name: 3C 52
gmw_00007978.overview: 3C 52 is a classical X-shaped radio galaxy located at a redshift of 0.285 [arXiv:0912.3001]. It features highly collimated secondary lobes and a diffuse atmosphere that is larger than the radio galaxy itself [arXiv:0912.3001].

sources.summary: 3C 52 is a higher-redshift classical XRG with collimated secondary lobes and a Chandra-detected diffuse atmosphere whose hot-gas nature requires deeper observations.

quotes.quote: 3C 52 ( z=0.285 ) : 3C 52 is a classical XRG with highly collimated secondary lobes in the 1.5 GHz image alexander87 and excluded from our sample on the basis of much higher redshift than our other sources. The Chandra image is an 8 ks snapshot which clearly detects the central point source and also a diffuse atmosphere larger than the radio galaxy itself (with a radius of about 50 arcsec centered on the galaxy). This atmosphere is in good agreement geometrically with the capetti02 relation (i.e. the major axis of the ellipsoidal atmosphere is coaligned with the active lobes), but proving the presence of a hot atmosphere spectroscopically is difficult. An isothermal apec model requires T > 7 keV and is otherwise poorly constrained; a power law with = 1.5 fits the spectrum well. Since the emission region is large compared to the radio galaxy, it seems likely this is hot gas, but a much deeper observation is necessary to establish this.

gmw_00007979.name: NGC 6753
gmw_00007979.overview: NGC 6753 is a massive, early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2003.13889, arXiv:astro-ph/0501355] featuring a nuclear ring-lens, an intermediate spiral region, and an outer pseudoring [arXiv:astro-ph/0501355]. This outer ring contains numerous H II regions and exhibits ongoing star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0501355], while the galaxy is surrounded by a diffuse X-ray halo [arXiv:2003.13889, arXiv:1602.00689].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6753 as a massive spiral with a deeply imaged X-ray halo whose emission and central hot-CGM metallicity are below the simulations' predictions.

quotes.quote: Existing deep imaging of a handful of individual X-ray halos is capable of probing the S_ X values of our stacks, as in the cases of massive spirals (NGC 1961, anderson16 ; NGC 6753, bogdan17 and the sample of li17 ). The emission at 50 kpc from these halos is several times less than that of the high-mass high-sSFR stacks from both simulations. These galaxies were targeted based on being X-ray-bright and massive late-types. If they are representative of the galaxies in our eROSITA simulated stacks, the observations suggest that both EAGLE and TNG over-predict extended emission from high-mass star-forming galaxies in general.
We also find that the metallicity of the central hot CGM of EAGLE galaxies is generally higher than the 0.1 derived for NGC 1961 and NGC 6753.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6753 as an early-type ringed spiral whose inner line-strength rise and 45-arcsec bump align with color changes and an outer star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 6753 - (R')SA()b NGC 6753 has a nuclear ring-lens surrounded by a pseudoring, an intermediate spiral region, and finally an outer pseudoring CBB96 . The arms are thin and have only small to moderate current star formation rate SB94 . We find that increases steeply for this early-type galaxy in the inner 10 arcsec. Eskridge et al. (2003) found a similar rise in the (NUV -- I_ 814 ) colour over this radial range. We find evidence for a bump in at about 45 arcsec that seems to correspond to an outer ring of ongoing star formation, which contains many H ii regions.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6753 as an external comparison: diffuse X-ray emission and bolometric luminosity are reported, but its high stellar mass makes it only an upper limit for the corona model.

quotes.quote: Anderson11 and Bogdan13 detect diffuse X-ray emission around NGC 1961 and NGC 6753. For NGC 1961, Anderson11 find a luminosity of 4 10^ 40 between 0.6 and 2.0 keV within 50 kpc of the galaxy. This was revised by Anderson15 to 9 10^ 40 . Bogdan13 estimate a bolometric luminosity of 6 10^ 40 inside an annulus of 23-70 kpc for NGC 1961 and NGC 6753. However, since these galaxies are much more massive, with stellar masses an order of magnitude higher than the MW, they serve as upper limits for our current work.

gmw_00007980.name: NGC 6935
gmw_00007980.overview: NGC 6935 is a highly circular spiral galaxy featuring a boxy inner ring, tightly wound spiral arms, and a small, bright nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0501355]. Although it is separated from NGC 6937 by only 246 arcsec, the two galaxies do not appear to be interacting [arXiv:astro-ph/0501355].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 6935 as a circular spiral with an inner ring, tightly wound arms, and radial Mg/Fe trends that decline in the disk and inner 10 arcsec.

quotes.quote: NGC 6935 - (R)SA()a This galaxy is highly circular with a boxy inner H ring, tightly wound spiral arms, and a small bright nucleus (Crocker, Baugus Buta 1996). It is separated from NGC 6937 by 246 arcsec (Sandage Bedke 1994) but the pair do not appear to be interacting. As mentioned above, there is good agreement between our central Fe5270 value and that from Idiart et al. (1996), whereas we obtain a slightly higher value for . The strengths of both indices show clear radial declines in the galactic disk and maxima at around 10 arcsec. Both indices decrease in the inner 10 arcsec, while Fe5270 drops significantly at a radius of few arcsec.

gmw_00007981.name: NGC 7412
gmw_00007981.overview: NGC 7412 is classified as a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0501355]. It features a stellar bar with a length of approximately 30 arcseconds, along which the V-I and V-R colors remain nearly constant [arXiv:astro-ph/0501355].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7412 as a barred spiral with fairly flat line-strength gradients and nearly constant bar colors, while noting sky-background uncertainty.

quotes.quote: NGC 7412 - SAB(s) The radial line-strength gradients for NGC 7412 are fairly flat and featureless: possible evidence of smoothing due to the presence of the bar with length 30 arcsec (Saraiva Schroeder et al. 1994) . Photometry results from these authors show that the V-I and V-R colours are almost constant along the bar. Unfortunately, our estimated gradients are particularly uncertain for this galaxy due to large uncertainties in the background sky level.

gmw_00007982.name: SDSS J1543-00
gmw_00007982.overview: SDSS J1543-00 is a type-2 quasar and ultraluminous infrared galaxy undergoing a major merger [arXiv:1403.0724]. The system contains approximately 9.6 billion solar masses of molecular gas, which is consistent with other galaxies of similar infrared luminosity [arXiv:1403.0724].

sources.summary: The paper confirms CO(1-0) in SDSS J1543-00, describing the quasar as a ULIRG-type QSO2 in a major merger with about 9.6e9 solar masses of molecular gas.

quotes.quote: CO(1-0) detection is confirmed in one object (SDSS J1543-00) with L'_ CO =(1.2 0.2) 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 (Table 2). HST images of this quasar show that it is undergoing a major merger event (Villar Mart n et al. 2012). Assuming =0.8 M _ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 ) ^ 1 , the implied molecular gas mass is (9.6 1.6) 10 ^9 M _ , which is consistent with molecular gas content of other systems with similar L_ IR . A 1-Gaussian fit of the line profile implies FWHM_ CO =576 102 km s ^ 1 with a velocity redshift relative [ OIII ] 5007 of 128 47 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00007983.name: SDSS J023219.52+002106.8
gmw_00007983.overview: SDSS J023219.52+002106.8 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:0801.1264]. It is characterized as a soft X-ray-loud source that exhibits little intrinsic soft X-ray absorption [arXiv:0801.1264].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J023219.52+002106.8 as one of three soft X-ray-loud BALQSOs, analyzes ROSAT/SDSS data for the trio, and finds little intrinsic soft X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: Our in depth inspection of the SDSS spectra of these 1744 AGNs has identified approximately 44 AGNs as BALQSOs. This section describes the data for the three candidate BALQSOs that are soft X-ray loud, SDSS J023219.52+002106.8, SDSS J101949.75+450256.0 and SDSS J132229.66+141007.3 (corresponding to the WGA counterparts 1WGA J0232.3+0021, 1WGA J1019.7+4502 and 1WGA J1322.4+1410, respectively). Hereafter, the SDSS designations will be abbreviated as 0232, 1019 and 1322.

X-ray spectra of all three sources were binned so that there were at least 30 counts per fitting bin. Spectral redistribution matrices and ancillary response files were retrieved from the ROSAT archive. XSPEC version 11.3 was used to fit the 0.2 -- 2.4 keV energy spectra with an absorbed ( phabs ) powerlaw ( powerlaw ) model. Confidence contours in the (photon index)-- N_ H plane were generated for the models. The confidence contours indicate that N_ H < 2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 with >99 confidence.

gmw_00007984.name: SDSS J101949.75+450256.0
gmw_00007984.overview: SDSS J101949.75+450256.0 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:0801.1264]. It is classified as a soft X-ray-loud source that exhibits little intrinsic soft X-ray absorption [arXiv:0801.1264].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J101949.75+450256.0 as one of three soft X-ray-loud BALQSOs, analyzes ROSAT/SDSS data for the trio, and finds little intrinsic soft X-ray absorption.

This section describes the data for the three candidate BALQSOs that are soft X-ray loud, SDSS J023219.52+002106.8, SDSS J101949.75+450256.0 and SDSS J132229.66+141007.3 (corresponding to the WGA counterparts 1WGA J0232.3+0021, 1WGA J1019.7+4502 and 1WGA J1322.4+1410, respectively). Confidence contours in the (photon index)-- N_ H plane were generated for the models. The confidence contours indicate that N_ H < 2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 with >99 confidence.

gmw_00007985.name: SDSS J132229.66+141007.3
gmw_00007985.overview: SDSS J132229.66+141007.3 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:0801.1264]. X-ray spectral analysis of the source indicates that it is soft X-ray-loud and exhibits little intrinsic soft X-ray absorption [arXiv:0801.1264].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J132229.66+141007.3 as one of three soft X-ray-loud BALQSOs, analyzes ROSAT/SDSS data for the trio, and finds little intrinsic soft X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: Our in depth inspection of the SDSS spectra of these 1744 AGNs has identified approximately 44 AGNs as BALQSOs. This section describes the data for the three candidate BALQSOs that are soft X-ray loud, SDSS J023219.52+002106.8, SDSS J101949.75+450256.0 and SDSS J132229.66+141007.3 (corresponding to the WGA counterparts 1WGA J0232.3+0021, 1WGA J1019.7+4502 and 1WGA J1322.4+1410, respectively). Hereafter, the SDSS designations will be abbreviated as 0232, 1019 and 1322.

gmw_00007986.name: GN-108036
gmw_00007986.overview: GN-108036 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy identified as a z-dropout [arXiv:1107.3159]. Spectroscopic observations confirm a redshift of z = 7.213 based on the detection of an asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:1107.3159].

sources.summary: The paper confirms GN-108036 as a z=7.213 Ly-emitting z-dropout, with repeated DEIMOS detections and asymmetric-line evidence supporting the Ly interpretation.

quotes.quote: For one of the targets in GOODS-N, GN-108036, we detect an emission line at about 9980 (Figure fig:2d1dspectra_GN108036 ). We confirm the line detection in three independent DEIMOS observations, performed in 2010 and 2011, with different configurations. In 2010, we obtained the spectrum using mask GNZD1B, the 830 lines mm ^ 1 grating, and the OG550 filter (first two-dimensional spectrum in the top panel in Figure fig:2d1dspectra_GN108036 ). In the 2011 run, we used a different set up with the 600 lines mm ^ 1 grating, the GG455 filter, and a different mask (HDF11C) to locate the spectrum on a different position on the DEIMOS CCD (second one in the top panel in Figure fig:2d1dspectra_GN108036 ). The HDF11D data were obtained over two nights of observing, and the line is detected in independent reductions of each night's data, as well as in the combined spectrum.

The S_w values of the three z dropouts are estimated to be S_w = 12.6 0.4 for SDF-63544, S_w = 8.6^ +0.6 _ 0.7 for SDF-46975, and S_w = 4.1 0.7 for GN-108036, which means that all the three lines have an asymmetric profile with a sharp decline on the blue side and a long tail on the red side, as is commonly seen in Ly at high redshifts e.g., shimasaku2006,kashikawa2006 .

Thus, we conclude that all of the detected lines are redshifted Ly . The redshifts derived from the Ly emission centroids are z_ spec = 6.965 for SDF-63544, z_ spec = 6.844 for SDF-46975, and z_ spec = 7.213 for GN-108036.

gmw_00007987.name: SDF-46975
gmw_00007987.overview: SDF-46975 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:1107.3159]. Spectroscopic observations have identified a Lyman-alpha emission line with an asymmetric profile, placing the galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.844 [arXiv:1107.3159].

sources.summary: The paper detects SDF-46975 as a z=6.844 Ly-emitting z-dropout, using line central wavelength, asymmetric skewness, and lack of lower-redshift line counterparts.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:2d1dspectra shows a prominent emission line in the spectra of SDF-63544 and SDF-46975. We fit Gaussian profiles to these lines using the IDL |MPFIT| routine markwardt2009 http://www.physics.wisc.edu/ craigm/idl/idl.html , and find the emission lines correspond to the observed central wavelengths 9683 (SDF-63544) and 9536 (SDF-46975) The best-fit central wavelengths are slightly shorter ( 3 ), if we fit an asymmetric Gaussian profile in which the standard deviation on the blue side is smaller than that on the red side, as a better approximation of the observed profiles but with an additional free parameter. .

The S_w values of the three z dropouts are estimated to be S_w = 12.6 0.4 for SDF-63544, S_w = 8.6^ +0.6 _ 0.7 for SDF-46975, and S_w = 4.1 0.7 for GN-108036, which means that all the three lines have an asymmetric profile with a sharp decline on the blue side and a long tail on the red side, as is commonly seen in Ly at high redshifts e.g., shimasaku2006,kashikawa2006 . empirically set S_w = 3 as a critical value to distinguish Ly emission from other emission lines for galaxies at z>5.7 see also, shimasaku2006 . Our objects have higher S_w than the criterion, which would suggest that the detected lines are Ly .

Thus, we conclude that all of the detected lines are redshifted Ly . The redshifts derived from the Ly emission centroids are z_ spec = 6.965 for SDF-63544, z_ spec = 6.844 for SDF-46975, and z_ spec = 7.213 for GN-108036.

gmw_00007988.name: J105528.80+312411.3
gmw_00007988.overview: J105528.80+312411.3 is a low-redshift analogue of 3000-break quasars [arXiv:1601.00544] classified as an iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:1501.00091]. The object exhibits strong broad iron emission [arXiv:1601.00544], nearly saturated magnesium absorption [arXiv:1501.00091, arXiv:1601.00544], and strong spectral variability [arXiv:1501.00091, arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper treats FBQS J105528.8+312411 as a strongly variable OFeLoBAL quasar with ambiguous, overlapped Fe absorption, saturated Mg BALs, and Fe multiplets identified in difference spectra.

quotes.quote: As shown in Figure 3, the BALs of Fe 2 multiplets are ambiguous or severely overlapped in FBQS J105528.8+312411 and J140800.4+345125. The spectral variability in these two cases is deepening overall below Mg 2 and blueward. It is difficult to resolve the absorption multiplets based on their single observations, but it is possible to identify strong variations of the iron multiplets in their difference spectra.

The triple-epoch spectra of FBQS J105528.8+312411 reveal that the Mg 2 BALs are nearly saturated and exhibit no shift. The absorption structures are somewhat similar to those of FBQS J140806.2+305448 and J152350.4+391404. We present the identification of the ultraviolet and optical Fe 2 multiplets in the difference spectra.

The Fe 2 UV144-149 and UV158-164 multiplets in four OFeLoBALs (with the exception of FBQS J105528.8+312411) that are considered in this work have depths that are nearly identical to those of the troughs of Fe 2 UV1 and UV2+3, and in three cases, considerable absorption troughs of Fe 2 Opt6+7 and Opt8 are evident.

sources.summary: The paper describes J105528.80+312411.3 as a low-redshift analogue of 3000-break quasars, with broad iron emission, Mg II absorption, uncertain redshift, and spectral variability.

quotes.quote: (1) \ J105528.80+312411.3 Originally discovered by the FIRST Bright Quasar Survey (FBQS; White et al. ). Appears to be a low- z version of the prototypical 3000 \ break quasars (Hall et al. ). Strong broad iron emission and associated Mg ii absorption at z_ abs =0.492 . Emission redshift only roughly estimated to z_ em 0.5 . Comparison of the SDSS spectra from three epochs indicates strong spectral variability at _ rest < 3400 \ (Zhang et al. ).

gmw_00007989.name: J140025.53-012957.0
gmw_00007989.overview: J140025.53-012957.0 is a low-ionization broad absorption line (LoBAL) quasar [arXiv:1601.00544]. Its emission redshift, determined from oxygen and hydrogen lines, matches the absorption redshift measured from magnesium II [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J140025.53-012957.0 as similar to J105528.80+312411.3, with matching emission and absorption redshifts and inclusion in a LoBAL mid-infrared sample.

quotes.quote: (2) \ J140025.53-012957.0 Quite similar to J105528.80+312411.3 ( 1). Emission redshift from [ O ii ] 3728, H , and [ O iii ] 4960.3, 5008.2 in agreement with the absorption redshift from Mg ii . Belongs to the LoBAL quasar sample studied by Lazarova et al. ( ) in the MIR.

gmw_00007990.name: J091613.59+292106.2
gmw_00007990.overview: J091613.59+292106.2 is a quasar with a spectrum that decreases shortwards of its peak before increasing at shorter wavelengths [arXiv:1601.00544]. This spectral feature may be caused by Galactic dust reddening or the superimposition of two quasars at a similar redshift [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper treats J091613.59+292106.2 as a peculiar sample-A quasar whose spectrum may combine an ordinary quasar component with a 3000-break residual.

quotes.quote: J091613.59+292106.2 ( 4) is the only quasar in sample A where the decrease in the SDSS spectrum shortwards of the peak turns into an increase at short wavelengths. In Paper I, we argued that this feature is perhaps due to reddening by Galactic dust with its characteristic 2175 \ trough at the redshift of the quasar. However, the agreement between the de-reddened spectrum and the quasar composite is rather poor at _ obs 4400 \ (Fig. fig:091613_diff , top).

Alternatively, the observed spectrum might be the superimposition of the spectra of two quasars at approximately the same redshift (Fig. fig:091613_diff , bottom). The part at _ obs 4600 is well matched by the unreddened SDSS quasar composite and might thus represent an ordinary quasar. The residual from subtracting this component from the observed spectrum of J091613.59+292106.2 is similar to a typical 3000 \ break quasar spectrum.

gmw_00007991.name: J010540.75-003313.9
gmw_00007991.overview: J010540.75-003313.9 is a highly reddened quasar with an emission redshift of z = 1.179 [arXiv:1101.4928, arXiv:1601.00544]. It is classified as a prototypical 3000-break quasar that displays broad iron emission, associated Mg II absorption, and variable absorption troughs [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper treats FBQS J0105-0033 as an uncertain BALQSO: it lacks obvious absorption, may show Fe II emission, is highly reddened, and appears at most weakly polarized.

quotes.quote: Of the seven sources found in Hall et al. (2002), four of them are uncertain in their classification as BALQSOs: FBQS J0105-0033, FBQS J2204+0031, FBQS J0127+0114, and SDSS J0338+0056. The first of these two, FBQS J0105-0033 and FBQS J2204+0031, show no obvious absorption (the redshifts are too low for C 4 to be seen in the spectrum), but may show Fe 2 emission which correlates with the presence of BALs (Boroson Meyers 1992). Both sources are highly reddened as well. Becker et al. (2001) has classified these objects as BL Lacartae (BL Lac) sources, though they do note that the classification is uncertain.

FBQS J0105-0033, FBQS J2204+0031, SDSS J0338+0056 These three sources are uncertainly classified as BALs (see 2), and none appear to be convincingly intrinsically polarized. FBQS J0105-0033 has a continuum polarization of 0.31 and a maximum ISP of 0.32 , and FBQS J2204+0031 has p=0.22 and a maximum ISP of 0.50 .

sources.summary: The paper treats J010540.75-003313.9 as a prototypical 3000-break quasar, with BOSS iron and [O II] emission, Mg II absorption, and variable absorption troughs.

quotes.quote: (5) \ J010540.75-003313.9 One of the two prototypical 3000 \ break quasars discovered by Hall et al. ( ). BOSS spectrum displays broad iron emission features near 4000 \ and 4200 \ and the [ O ii ] 3728 emission line. Emission redshift z = 1.179 in good agreement with the redshift of the resolved associated Mg II absorption. Deviation of the BOSS spectrum from the SDSS spectrum at _ obs 5400 \ probably indicates variable absorption troughs (Fig. fig:two_spectra , Sect. sec:disc_a ).

gmw_00007992.name: J152928.53+133426.6
gmw_00007992.overview: J152928.53+133426.6 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.23 [arXiv:1601.00544]. It exhibits a magnesium II doublet structure within a deep absorption trough, alongside weak neon III emission and mid-infrared colors typical of quasars [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper says J152928.53+133426.6 was misclassified by SDSS, has quasar-like WISE colors, Mg II doublet structure, and weak [Ne III] emission.

quotes.quote: (7) \ J152928.53+133426.6 Targeted by SDSS as a high- z quasar and wrongly classified by the pipeline of SDSS DR12 as a galaxy at z=0.578 . WISE colour index w1-w2 = 1.4 mag typical for quasars. Two peaks inside the deep absorption trough at 6200 \ separated by 16 \ can be identified with the Mg ii 2796.3, 2803.5 doublet at z = 1.23 . Weak [ Ne iii ] 3870 emission is indicated at the same redshift, but at low S/N.

gmw_00007993.name: J220445.27+003141.8
gmw_00007993.overview: J220445.27+003141.8 is a highly reddened quasar [arXiv:1101.4928] that is classified as one of the prototypical 3000-break quasars [arXiv:1601.00544]. The object exhibits associated magnesium II and iron II absorption [arXiv:1601.00544], but it is not convincingly intrinsically polarized [arXiv:1101.4928].

sources.summary: The paper treats FBQS J2204+0031 as an uncertain BALQSO: it lacks obvious absorption, may show Fe II emission, is highly reddened, and appears weakly or not intrinsically polarized.

quotes.quote: Of the seven sources found in Hall et al. (2002), four of them are uncertain in their classification as BALQSOs: FBQS J0105-0033, FBQS J2204+0031, FBQS J0127+0114, and SDSS J0338+0056. The first of these two, FBQS J0105-0033 and FBQS J2204+0031, show no obvious absorption (the redshifts are too low for C 4 to be seen in the spectrum), but may show Fe 2 emission which correlates with the presence of BALs (Boroson Meyers 1992). Both sources are highly reddened as well. Becker et al. (2001) has classified these objects as BL Lacartae (BL Lac) sources, though they do note that the classification is uncertain.

FBQS J0105-0033, FBQS J2204+0031, SDSS J0338+0056 These three sources are uncertainly classified as BALs (see 2), and none appear to be convincingly intrinsically polarized. FBQS J0105-0033 has a continuum polarization of 0.31 and a maximum ISP of 0.32 , and FBQS J2204+0031 has p=0.22 and a maximum ISP of 0.50 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J220445.27+003141.8 as the second prototypical 3000-break quasar, with Mg II/Fe II absorption and faint BOSS peaks including [O II].

quotes.quote: (10) \ J220445.27+003141.8 One of the two prototypical 3000 \ break quasars discovered by Hall et al. ( ). Continuum similar to J010540.75-003313.9 ( 5). Associated Mg ii and Fe ii absorption at z = 1.335.

Emission redshift uncertain. Hall et al. ( ) derived z_ em = 1.353 from cross-correlation with J010540.75-003313.9 . BOSS spectrum displays two faint peaks at 8800 , where the blue peak was identified here with [ O ii ] 3728 at z = 1.353 .

gmw_00007994.name: J111541.01+263328.6
gmw_00007994.overview: J111541.01+263328.6 is a broad-absorption line quasar with an uncertain emission redshift estimated at 1.355 [arXiv:1601.00544]. Its spectrum displays two absorption troughs from magnesium II and aluminum III, alongside a double magnesium II emission peak [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper reports J111541.01+263328.6 has Mg II and Al III absorption troughs, a double Mg II emission-peak interpretation, and uncertain emission redshift.

quotes.quote: (11) \ J111541.01+263328.6 The (noisy) SDSS spectrum displays two absorption troughs from Mg ii and Al iii at z_ abs = 1.327 . Identification of the double emission peak at the red edge of the 6500 \ trough with Mg ii 2796.3, 2803.5 yields z_ em = 1.355 . Emission redshift uncertain.

gmw_00007995.name: J161836.09+153313.5
gmw_00007995.overview: J161836.09+153313.5 is a quasar with a revised redshift of 1.322 based on its magnesium II emission peak [arXiv:1601.00544]. Alternatively, a systemic redshift of 1.359 is suggested by a narrow oxygen II emission line, with high-velocity broad absorption lines likely contributing to its spectrum [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper describes J161836.09+153313.5 as an independently rediscovered source with revised redshift, SDSS spectral similarity, possible [O II] systemic redshift, and high-velocity BAL contribution.

quotes.quote: (12) \ J161836.09+153313.5 Another source discovered by Plotkin et al. and independently in Paper I. In earlier SDSS data releases identified as high- z quasar due to a confusion of the peak with Lyman . Carballo et al. ( ) give a revised value z = 1.322 based on the identification of the peak with Mg ii .

SDSS spectrum similar to J160827.08+075811.5 ( 6). If the narrow emission line at _ obs 8800 \ is [ O ii ] 3730, the systemic redshift is z_ em = 1.359 and the peak of the spectrum is at shorter wavelengths than Mg ii . The system of narrow Mg ii and Fe ii absorbers at z_ abs = 1.158 make it plausible that high-velocity BALs contribute to the absorption.

gmw_00007996.name: J152423.18+115312.0
gmw_00007996.overview: J152423.18+115312.0 is a quasar that lacks clearly identified emission or absorption lines [arXiv:1601.00544]. Its redshift has been estimated by fitting the SDSS composite spectrum and comparing it to the similar source J130941.35+112540.1 [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper says J152423.18+115312.0 resembles J130941.35+112540.1, lacks clearly identified lines, and has a redshift estimated from composite-spectrum comparisons.

quotes.quote: (14) \ J152423.18+115312.0 Similar to J130941.35+112540.1 ( 14). No clearly identified emission or absorption line. Redshift estimated from fitting the SDSS composite at the red side of the peak and by the comparison with J130941.35+112540.1 ( 13) at the blue side.

gmw_00007997.name: J215950.30+124718.4
gmw_00007997.overview: J215950.30+124718.4 is a quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.514 [arXiv:1601.00544]. Its spectrum shows strong [O II] and weak [Ne III] emission lines, alongside slightly redshifted Ca H+K absorption and overlapping trough absorption [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper describes J215950.30+124718.4 as independently discovered, with BOSS [O II]/[Ne III] emission, Ca H+K absorption, and strong partial coverage by trough absorption.

quotes.quote: (15) \ J215950.30+124718.4 Discovered by Plotkin et al. and independently in Paper I. BOSS spectrum reveals strong [ O ii ] 3730 and probably weak [ Ne iii ] 3870 emission at z_ em = 1.514 as well as slightly redshifted Ca H+K absorption. Similar to J134246.24+284027.5 ( 8) but stronger partial coverage of the continuum source by overlapping trough absorption.

gmw_00007998.name: J134408.32+283932.0
gmw_00007998.overview: J134408.32+283932.0 is a 2MASS quasar that exhibits strong, narrow Mg II and Fe II absorption [arXiv:1601.00544]. The galaxy lacks [O II] coverage and displays radio spectral properties suggestive of young radio jets [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper says J134408.32+283932.0 has strong Mg II/Fe II absorption, lacks [O II] coverage and other emission lines, and is a 2MASS quasar with young-radio-jet properties.

quotes.quote: (17) \ J134408.32+283932.0 Similar to J085502.20+280219.6 ( 16), strong narrow Mg ii and Fe ii absorption at z_ abs = 1.767 . At this redshift, the [ O ii ] 3730 is not covered by the BOSS spectrum. No other emission line is seen. Known as a 2MASS quasar with radio spectral properties suggestive of young radio jets (Geogakakis et al. ).

gmw_00007999.name: J110511.15+530806.5
gmw_00007999.overview: J110511.15+530806.5 is a strongly reddened narrow-line quasar [arXiv:1601.00544] that exhibits deep absorption in the optical and ultraviolet domains [arXiv:2210.10438]. This absorption can be modeled as two distinct clouds [arXiv:2210.10438], and the object displays an associated magnesium II absorption line [arXiv:1601.00544].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J110511.15+530806.5 as a peculiar distant quasar whose deep optical/UV absorption is explained with absorbers affecting black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we apply the continuum-fit method of BH mass estimation to the peculiar quasar SDSS J110511.15+530806.5 (hereafter J1105), which exhibits deep absorption in the optical/UV domain. The source was first identified by Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) Data Release 6 obtained on September 17, 2007. The BH mass in this quasar reported by Shen2011qsocatalog is M_ BH = 11.205 solar masses, indicating that this object is a behemoth with an enormous mass. Notwithstanding, indicated that the mass may be erroneous, and these virial masses for individual objects have to be interpreted with caution Shen_2008MBHbiases . Considering all of this and taking the conclusions of our previous paper Marculewicz2020 into account, we used a phenomenological model of disk emission with a hot corona and warm skin affected by absorbing gas to fit the continuum spectrum of J1105 that was obtained by the SDSS Data Release 7 SDSSDR7 to constrain the BH mass in this quasar.

The second step in our analysis aims to explain the deep absorption that is visible in the optical/UV spectrum of J1105. We modeled this unusual absorption and discuss eventual scenarios of the origin of the absorbing material in this peculiar quasar.

Absorbing clouds in J1105 Our result indicates that the deep absorption in J1105 could be explained by two Gaussian functions, which may describe absorption on two different clouds. All fitting parameters in the rest frame are given in Table tab:observed_energy_rest_wave . The minima of the Gaussian functions for J1105 in the rest frame are at 1516 10 , and 1992 20 \ with = 332 69 and 375 64 , receptively. The absorption described by the first gabs(1) dominates and has a strength of nearly 90 of the whole recess. The origin of this deep absorption in the J1105 is unclear.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J110511.15+530806.5 as a strongly reddened narrow-line quasar with multiple narrow emission lines, associated Mg II absorption, and unusually steep reddening.

quotes.quote: (19) \ J110511.15+530806.5 Strongly reddened narrow-line quasar. BOSS spectrum displays strong narrow emission lines from Si iv , C iv , Al iii , C iii , and Mg ii and an associated Mg ii absorption line. Reddening steeper than in the SMC, perhaps even steeper than for IRAS 14026+4341 (Sect. sec:disc_b ).

gmw_00008000.name: VCC0170
gmw_00008000.overview: VCC0170 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy in the Virgo Cluster that exhibits stellar rotation and a flat velocity dispersion [arXiv:2007.05021]. It contains central HI gas and is actively forming stars in its inner regions [arXiv:2007.05021, arXiv:2308.16768], hosting the youngest stellar populations in its sample [arXiv:2207.06977].

sources.summary: The paper measures VCC0170 as a rotating Virgo dE with flat stellar velocity dispersion, central star-forming nebular emission, HI gas, and possible stripping-driven asymmetry.

quotes.quote: The velocity map of VCC 0170 shows rotation with V_ max 18 km s ^ 1 in the outskirts of the galaxy. Like other members of our sample, VCC0170 has a flat velocity dispersion as a function of radius. The stacked image from the MUSE cube, the velocity map, and velocity dispersion map of VCC0170 are presented in the lower panels of Fig. VCC0170Kin .

The detection of nebular emission lines in the central part of VCC0170 is consistent with its blue colour, which is likely due to the presence of young massive stars. While this galaxy was originally classified as a late-type galaxy by , it was recognized as a dE member of Virgo by , who also pointed out an irregularly-shaped distribution of the blue colour in the central part of VCC0170. HI emission also is observed within the central part of this galaxy, indicating a M_ HI M_ bar ratio of less than one percent 2005Gavazzi, 2006Lisker, 2006LiskerII .
Using the "Baldwin, Phillips, and Terlevich" (BPT) diagram 1981Baldwin , we confirm that the nebular emission from this irregularly shaped region is due to star formation.

sources.summary: VCC0170 is the sample dE with central nebular emission and active star formation, with young metal-rich central populations linked to ram-pressure-triggered star formation during Virgo infall.

quotes.quote: In what follows, we describe some features specific to each dE: VCC0170: As explained in B20, this dE is the only member of our main sample for which we detected nebular emission lines in its central regions. In B22 we showed that this dE experienced a peak in its star formation history at the time of its accretion onto Virgo ( 2-3 Gyr ago), possibly triggered by ram pressure acting during the early phases of accretion. Ram pressure can compress gas in the galaxy's inner disk and initiate or enhance an episode of star formation, mainly in the inner regions of infalling galaxies . The presence of young and more metal-rich stellar populations in the central regions of VCC0170 and the fact that this dE is still actively forming new stars in its center (see B20), supports the picture presented above.

From this work, we conclude that: Except for VCC0170, VCC0407 and VCC1833, the main sample dEs show flat age radial profiles with negligible fluctuations in value (-0.02 < _ Age < 0.12). Among the exceptions here VCC0170 is still actively forming stars in its inner regions and the other two show traces of recent star formation episodes.

sources.summary: The paper finds VCC0170 has the youngest stellar population in the sample, agrees with prior SMAKCED measurements, shows ongoing star formation, and experienced strong recent mass growth.

quotes.quote: The youngest stellar populations, with an average age = 2.10 Gyr and a mean [ M/H ] = 0.61 dex, are detected in VCC0170, while the oldest stellar populations are observed in VCC1896 and VCC2019.
In detail, reported a light-weighted age of 2.0 0.4 Gyr (compared to 2.10 0.20 in this study) and [ M/H ] of -0.50 0.10 dex (compared to -0.61 0.04 dex derived here) for VCC0170.
According to Fig. cumulative_prof VCC0170, where B20 detected ongoing star formation, has experienced the most drastic mass growth ( 70 percent) since 4 Gyr ago.

gmw_00008001.name: VCC0794
gmw_00008001.overview: VCC0794 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2207.06977, arXiv:2308.16768]. It features an old, metal-poor, and alpha-enhanced stellar population that formed rapidly before the galaxy was quenched and accreted [arXiv:2207.06977, arXiv:2308.16768].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC0794 as the most alpha-enhanced sample dwarf, old and metal-poor, with a short early star-formation history and evidence for quenching before Virgo infall.

quotes.quote: In the case of VCC0794, the most enhanced dE in our sample with [ /Fe ] = 0.43 dex, the desired SSP family is constructed by linearly extrapolating the available SSP models beyond their maximum value of 0.4 dex.
Similarly, they reported a light-weighted age of 7.8 1.8 Gyr (compared to 9.90 0.40 in this study) and [ M/H ] of -0.8 0.1 dex (compared to -0.73 0.02 dex obtained here) for VCC0794.

In Section Results:Integrated values , we show that VCC0794 is the most enhanced member of our sample. The spectral indices of this dE point to old ( 10 Gyr) and metal-poor ( 0.73 dex) stellar populations (Table Integrated values ). In the top right panel of Fig. ligh-mass-fractions , this particular dE shows a peak in its stellar light fraction profile at the age of 9 Gyr, which is consistent with our previous results and endorses the fact that this dE has been accreted onto Virgo already old and quenched. The profile of this particular dE indicates that the bulk of its stellar population formed in a rather short time interval ( 4 Gyr), which can explain its high value of [ /Fe ] .

sources.summary: VCC0794 is described as the most metal-poor and alpha-enhanced sample galaxy, with homogeneous old, metal-poor, enhanced populations attributed to rapid early star formation before Virgo accretion.

quotes.quote: VCC0794: Our analysis in B22 shows that VCC0794 is the most metal-poor and enhanced galaxy of our sample (See Table 2 of that study). The homogeneous distribution of old, metal-poor, and enhanced stellar populations across this dE confirms our results in B22.

Below we compare the results of this work with those of B22: Maps of light-weighted stellar population properties for three dEs (namely VCC0794, VCC1896 and VCC2019) presented in Section Results_1 indicate the presence of old, metal-poor and enhanced stars all over these galaxy. Hand in hand with their SFH profiles, shown in Fig. SFH profiles , our results support B22's interpretations that the high [ /Fe ] and low [ M/H ] in these three dEs can be due to their rapid star formation and its early cessation which has taken place, long before their accretion onto Virgo and possibly due to to the effect of the parent group.

gmw_00008002.name: VCC1896
gmw_00008002.overview: VCC1896 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy in the Virgo Cluster that contains a bright central bar and two faint spiral arms [arXiv:2007.05021, arXiv:2308.16768]. The galaxy is dominated by old stellar populations [arXiv:2308.16768], having experienced a short period of star formation that ended before it was accreted as a quenched system [arXiv:2207.06977].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC1896 as a Virgo dE with the sample's largest kinematic misalignment, faint spiral arms, and a bright bar relevant to its evolution.

quotes.quote: VCC1896 with = 82.7 ^ 3.1 ^ has the highest offset between its photometric and kinematic axis. Taken at face value, VCC1896 would look like a dE with minor axis rotation. reported the existence of two rather faint spiral arms for VCC1896 and a bar aligned with the outer photometric minor axis of this galaxy.

These results might explain the existence of the faint spiral arms in VCC1896. Due to their low surface brightness, the faint spiral arms of VCC1896 are not detected in our MUSE data cube; therefore, they are not present in the kinematic maps of this dE. We only detected the bright bar of this dE in its MUSE cube. While the presence of the bar explains the high offset between kinematic and photometric PA, it may also be a morphological feature relevant to the evolution of VCC1896. The bar in the central region of VCC1896 can either be a pristine structure that formed and developed through standard processes or a tidal feature that formed as a result of interactions with the environment e.g., see 2005MastropietroII .

sources.summary: VCC1896 has metal-rich, less-enhanced populations along its major axis, old stars across the galaxy, and MUSE maps of a bright bar associated with V-shaped abundance profiles.

quotes.quote: VCC1896: The [ M/H ] and [ /Fe ] maps of this dE show the presence of more metal-rich and less enhanced stellar populations along VCC1896's photometric major axis. Meanwhile, the age map of this dE shows no particular variations as the old stellar populations dominate the entire galaxy. Deep photometric observations of VCC1896 2006Lisker,2009Lisker,2021Michea proved the presence of a central bar and two faint spiral arms. Here, given the depth and spatial coverage of our MUSE data, we can only map the bright bar of VCC1896, which is extends over the VCC1896's photometric major axis.

The distribution of [ M/H ] and [ /Fe ] resembles a V-shape profile (with the maximum on the photometric major axis) that is similar to what (Fig.14 of their paper) measured for nearby massive barred galaxies (with M _ > 10 ^ 10 M _ ) also predicted in Auriga hydrodynamical cosmological simulations by 2017Grand . The bar's potential can trap stars in quasi-periodic or periodic orbits, based on their initial kinematics or that of the gas they formed from e.g., 2017Fragkoudi . showed that bars of massive galaxies trap young and intermediate-age stars ( < 8 Gyr) in elongated orbits, shaping a V-shape profile, which we could also detect in the [ M/H ] and [ /Fe ] maps of this low-mass dE with log(M _ /M _ ) = 8.9.

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC1896 as one of the oldest sample dwarfs, with short early star formation and evidence it was already quenched when accreted into Virgo.

quotes.quote: The youngest stellar populations, with an average age = 2.10 Gyr and a mean [ M/H ] = 0.61 dex, are detected in VCC0170, while the oldest stellar populations are observed in VCC1896 and VCC2019.
On the contrary, two of the dEs in the right-hand panels of Fig. ligh-mass-fractions (namely VCC0794 and VCC1896) exhibit a shorter star formation activity that culminated about 8 - 10 Gyrs ago.
Our results indicate that the remaining three dEs in our sample (namely VCC0794, VCC1896 and VCC2019) were accreted onto Virgo already quenched.

gmw_00008003.name: PG 2112 + 059
gmw_00008003.overview: PG 2112+059 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094] that exhibits long-term optical variability, brightening by 0.18 magnitudes in the V-band between 1992 and 1996 [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094]. In the X-ray band, the active nucleus has been observed in a deep minimum state [arXiv:0709.2412, arXiv:0912.4456] with spectra showing a weak iron fluorescent line [arXiv:0912.4456].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG 2112+059 in a deep X-ray minimum, finding a tenfold flux drop, strong 3-6 keV excess emission, and spectra explainable by absorption or disk reflection.

quotes.quote: We analyse a 75ks XMM-Newton observation of PG 2112+059 performed in November 2005 and compare it with a 15ks XMM-Newton observation taken in May 2003. PG 2112+059 was found in a deep minimum state as its 0.2-12 keV flux decreased by a factor of 10 in comparison to the May 2003 observation. During the deep minimum state the spectra show strong emission in excess of the continuum in the 3-6 keV region. The excess emission corresponds to an EW = 26.1 keV whereas its shape resembles that of heavily absorbed objects.

The spectra of both observations of PG 2112+059 can be explained statistically by a combination of two absorbers where one shows a high column density, N_ H 4.5 10^ 23 cm^ 2 , and the other high ionisation parameters.

The spectra can also be explained by a continuum plus X-ray ionised reflection on the accretion disk, seen behind a warm absorber. The ionisation parameter of the high state ( 5.6 erg cm s^ 1 ) is higher than the ionisation parameter of the deep minimum state ( 0.2 erg cm s^ 1 ), as expected for a stationary absorber.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG 2112+059 in a low X-ray state, comparing new XMM-Newton spectra with archival data and modeling absorption or ionized disk reflection scenarios.

quotes.quote: Our observational aim is to perform a long X-ray observation of the quasar PG 2112+059 in its low or minimum state. Starting form this very peculiar emission state, we intend to constrain the intrinsic emission mechanism by comparing new and old data, corresponding to different source states. XMM-Newton successfully detected the minimum state of PG 2112+059 during a short snapshot observation and performed a long follow-up observation. The high signal-to-noise spectra are modelled assuming different emission scenarios and compared with archival spectra taken by XMM-Newton and Chandra. The PG 2112+059 X-ray spectra acquired in May 2007 allowed the detection of a weak iron fluorescent line, which is interpreted as being caused by reflection from neutral material at some distance from the primary X-ray emitting source.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PQSJ211452.57+060742.9 as the remaining true redshift discrepancy, comparing a published z=0.457 with its MARZ redshift from broad MgII and H lines and assigning a 1000 km/s uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The remaining true discrepancy is PQSJ211452.57+060742.9, or 2MASS J21145258+0607423. The published redshift value for this QSO is 0.457 2017AJ....154..114O , compared to our MARZ redshift of 0.4608, derived from two very broad emission lines we identified as MgII and H . state the wavelength covered in the Keck spectrum to be 3805 5976 , and thus, they would have detected only the broad MgII line for a redshift estimate. A redshift uncertainty of 1000 km s ^ 1 is therefore appropriate for this target.

sources.summary: The paper reports PG 2112 + 059 as the only monitored RQQSO with detected long-term variability, brightening by 0.18 magnitudes between 1992 and 1996.

quotes.quote: The data also reveal no night to night or longer term variability for any of the sources, with the sole exception of PG 2112 + 059 . This source brightened by 0.18 magnitudes in the V-band between September 1992 and June 1996 (Table three-quasars and Fig. PG2112+059 ).

The only source in this sample of RQQSOs to display variability is PG 2112 + 059 . It was observed during 1990, 1992 and 1996 and was found to have brightened by 0.18 magnitudes between September 1992 and June 1996 (Table three-quasars ).

gmw_00008004.name: PG 2302 + 029
gmw_00008004.overview: PG 2302 + 029 is a radio-quiet quasar with a redshift of 1.044 and a B magnitude of 16.03 [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094]. Its spectrum reveals emission peaks identified with Fe III multiplets, and no variability was detected during observing runs in 1991 or 1992 [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094].

sources.summary: The paper notes PG 2302 + 029 has Fe III emission peaks, redshift z=1.044, B magnitude 16.03, and no detected variability in two observing runs.

quotes.quote: PG 2302 + 029 reveals emission peaks in its spectrum which have been identified with Fe iii multiplets (Wampler, 1986). This source has a redshift z=1.044 and m_ B =16.03 . No variability was detected during the 1991 or 1992 observing runs.

gmw_00008005.name: PG 1416 129
gmw_00008005.overview: PG 1416 129 is a radio-quiet quasar located at a redshift of z=0.129 that has been classified as a broad absorption line quasar candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094]. The source exhibits variability in its high-energy X-ray flux on a timescale of days and has varied by a factor of 4.5 over a ten-year period at radio frequencies [arXiv:astro-ph/9803094].

sources.summary: The paper presents PG 1416 129 as an unusual radio-quiet quasar/BAL candidate with X-ray variability, unusual VLA radio properties, and possible compact synchrotron-core behavior.

quotes.quote: The second unusual RQQSO is PG 1416 129 at z=0.129 . It has been classified (Turnshek Grillmair 1986; Ulrich 1988) as a broad absorption line quasar (BALQSO). The value of R is 1.1 and similar to IRAS 13349 + 2438 it lies near the top of the upper band for RQQSOs which is also heavily populated with BALQSOs (Francis et al. 1995). This source has a soft x-ray excess (de Kool Meurs 1994) and the hardest spectral index of any source in the energy range 2--20 keV when observed with GINGA (Williams et al. 1992). Staubert Maisack (1996) detected this bright RQQSO at energies above 50 keV with the OSSE telescope on CGRO and the flux was found to be variable on a timescale of days during the 14 day observation.

VLA observations of PG 1416 129 reveal unusual results for a RQQSO: (i) the radio source consists of an unresolved core coincident with an extended component that is assumed not to be an unrelated background source (Kellermann et al. (1994), (ii) the source varied by a factor of 4.5 over a period of ten years and could have been more variable given the very limited monitoring at radio frequencies and (iii) the radio spectrum is consistent with a flat or inverted spectrum (Barvainis et al. 1996). No VLBI observations have been reported for PG 1416 129 but one of the other variable RQQSOs PG 1216 + 069 was detected with essentially all of its flux in the VLBI core and the high brightness temperature limit confirmed the self-absorbed synchrotron hypothesis for the flat spectrum component (Barvainis et al. 1996). This component may well be a scaled-down version of the radio sources observed in radio loud quasars and future VLBI observations of PG 1416 129 may also reveal a similar component.

gmw_00008006.name: FCC 215
gmw_00008006.overview: FCC 215 is a faint dwarf galaxy located within the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1403.0589]. It exhibits a very blue optical color that suggests recent star formation, as well as an unusually high dust-to-stars ratio [arXiv:1403.0589].

sources.summary: The paper singles out FCC 215 as a faint Fornax dwarf with an unusually high dust-to-stars ratio, marginal SPIRE detections, a poor flat SED fit, cluster velocity, and blue color suggesting recent star formation.

quotes.quote: From 185 dwarf galaxies identified in the FCC, only 11 were detected in the 250 band, and only FCC 215 was detected in 3 or more Herschel bands. FCC 215 has a very high dust-to-stars ratio (approximately (M_ Dust /M_ Stars ) = -1 ) and a very faint optical magnitude ( m_ bt 19 ), making it an interesting object worthy of further inspection. FCC 215 has a dust mass of _ 10 (M_ dust /M_ ) = 5.2 and a stellar mass of _ 10 (M_ stars /M_ )= 6.5 .

It is just detected in the 3 SPIRE bands at S/N 5. The SED fit is quite poor with ^ 2 _ dof=3 = 9.94 . The SED appears very flat, which may indicate it is a background galaxy with a synchrotron component.

However, it is listed in NED as having a velocity of 1964 km s ^ 1 , which places it inside the cluster. Its optical colour is very blue, B-R = 0.36, suggesting that the galaxy is undergoing/has undergone an episode of recent star formation.

gmw_00008007.name: DA 240
gmw_00008007.overview: DA 240 is an early-type giant radio galaxy [arXiv:1501.00407, arXiv:2208.02130] classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II source [arXiv:2310.13783]. It is situated within a galaxy group [arXiv:1501.00407] and exhibits parsec-scale bipolar jets oriented close to the sky plane [arXiv:2208.02130].

sources.summary: The paper studies the giant FR II radio galaxy DA 240, deriving 326-608 MHz spectral-index maps and finding synchrotron radiation dominant across most of the source except near the central core.

quotes.quote: Here we investigate the giant radio galaxy DA 240, which is a FR II source.

We calculated spectral indices between 326 and 608 MHz, over the whole area of DA 240, and we show how they change over this area in Fig. fig05 . From the colorbar in Fig. fig05 we can read the values of radio spectral index , and we can notice that over the area of DA 240 it ranges from 0 to positive values, meaning this: > 0 corresponds to non-thermal mechanism of radiation while < 0 corresponds to thermal mechanism of radiation.

For the first time we give spectral index map of DA 240 between 326 and 608 MHz. We show that synchrotron radiation is the dominant emission mechanism over the majority of the area of the source, except in the central core.

sources.summary: The paper presents DA 240 as a giant radio galaxy whose strong polarization and relatively symmetrical lobes trace a surrounding galaxy group, with companion galaxies described as weak radio sources.

quotes.quote: As was clear from early WSRT observations of DA 240, 3C 236 and NGC 6251 (Figure 2), giant radio galaxies are strongly polarized at 608 MHz willis74 .

However, in the case of DA 240 peng04, chen11a , the relatively symmetrical lobes coincide with a galaxy group. The group members evenly reside along the major axis of the lobes while the isolated host galaxy lies in the middle (Figure 3).
In several groups (like DA 240, ; and 4C 73.08, ) we find that a large fraction of the companion galaxies are weak radio sources.

sources.summary: The review presents DA240 as an early-type giant radio galaxy discovered in WSRT work, later found to show parsec-scale bipolar jets and near-sky-plane orientation.

quotes.quote: The existence of such large sources was highlighted in the mid-1970s by willis74 who presented observations of 3C236 and DA240 with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT; Hogbom74 ) with its high sensitivity and suitable resolution. GRSs may be associated with both radio galaxies and quasars; those associated with galaxies and quasars are hereinafter referred to as GRGs and GRQs, respectively. Similarly, we refer to smaller ( < 0.7 Mpc) sized radio sources, radio galaxies and radio quasars as SRSs, SRGs, and SRQs, respectively. Both 3C236 and DA240 are associated with early-type galaxies.
The GRG DA240 in their sample showed pc scale bipolar jets, and hence they infer it to be oriented close to the sky plane.

gmw_00008008.name: NGC 3456
gmw_00008008.overview: NGC 3456 is a galaxy located at a distance of 62.9 Mpc [arXiv:2306.11961]. The galaxy contains segmented H II regions and hosted the Type Ic supernova SN 2018ie [arXiv:2306.11961].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3456 as a MUSE example host galaxy, noting its 62.9 Mpc distance, SN Ic 2018ie, observational coverage, H map, and oxygen-abundance map.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:NGC3456 shows the MUSE RGB image composition of the galaxy NGC 3456, located at 62.9 Mpc. The galaxy hosted SN Ic 2018ie, whose position is marked by the yellow star. The top panel in the right column of Figure fig:NGC3456 shows the section of the galaxy covered by the MUSE observation. The middle panel in the right column of Figure fig:NGC3456 shows the H map of the galaxy, and the bottom panel shows the resulting oxygen abundance in the D16 index for the segmented H ii regions in the galaxy.

gmw_00008009.name: host of LGRB 020819B
gmw_00008009.overview: The host of LGRB 020819B is a large spiral galaxy with a diameter of approximately 7 arcseconds on the sky [arXiv:1211.7068]. The long gamma-ray burst occurred on a small blob at the edge of the spiral, which may be an outlying star-forming region or a satellite galaxy [arXiv:1211.7068].

sources.summary: The paper describes the LGRB 020819B host as a large spiral with the burst placed on an edge blob, using host-center and local metallicity constraints.

quotes.quote: The host of LGRB 020819B is a large spiral Jakobsson,pregraham with a diameter of about 7" on the sky. The LGRB was dark, but a radio position with an error circle of 1" diameter Jakobsson places it on a small ``blob" at the edge of the spiral (this could either be an outlying star-forming region of the spiral or possibly a satellite). The metallicity of the center of the host has been well determined Levesque020819B . However, a problem with the blue chip of the spectrograph on the same observing run, meant that a full R _ 23 metallicity could not be obtained for the "blob" under the location of the burst. However, the [ N II ] /H diagnostic can be used to give a rough estimate of the local metallicity Levesque020819B .

gmw_00008010.name: IGRJ00333+6122
gmw_00008010.overview: IGRJ00333+6122 is an active galactic nucleus with a compact, unresolved morphology at both 45-arcsecond and 1-arcsecond resolutions [arXiv:2005.08569]. The source exhibits faint high-frequency fluxes and a steep radio spectral index of 1.25 [arXiv:2005.08569].

sources.summary: The paper reports that IGRJ00333+6122 remains compact and unresolved from NVSS to 1 arcsec JVLA resolution, with faint high-frequency fluxes and a steep spectrum.

quotes.quote: IGRJ00333+6122 The only existing work comprising this source is the 1.4 GHz NVSS characterisation made by our group in Panessa2015 . The flux density at 1.4 GHz is 9.5 mJy with a compact, unresolved morphology at the 45 arcsec resolution. Even at the 1 arcsec resolution level of our map the source remain unresolved, with flux densities smaller that 690 , with a steep spectral index 1.25 0.4 .

gmw_00008011.name: J1228+5017
gmw_00008011.overview: J1228+5017 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.262 [arXiv:2108.08521]. Its late-type host galaxy displays an elongated structure that may trace star formation [arXiv:2108.08521], which is also supported by its low-frequency radio spectrum [arXiv:2312.02326].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1228+5017 as an NLS1 with modest steep-spectrum JVLA emission, elongated 1.6 GHz structure, and a late-type host whose extended radio emission may trace star formation.

quotes.quote: J1228+5017 J1228+5017 is an NLS1 at z = 0.262. It did not have any prior radio detections before the detections at Metsähovi at 37 GHz. Its integrated JVLA flux densities are very modest: (0.97 0.04) mJy, (0.29 0.01) mJy, and (0.19 0.01) mJy, at 1.6, 5.2, and 9.0 GHz, respectively, indicating a steep spectrum up to 9.0 GHz (Fig. fig:lcx-spectra ). Its 1.6 GHz radio map (Fig. fig:J1228-radio a) shows an elongated structure toward west, with a maximum extent of 18.1 kpc. The 5.2 and 9.0 GHz maps(Figs. fig:J1228-radio and e) do not show signs of this structure, suggesting a steep spectrum.

The host galaxy of J1228+5017 was found to be a late-type galaxy (Björklund et al. in prep.). Also the host shows an elongated structure toward west and the 1.6 GHz radio emission traces this structure (Fig. fig:hosts a). This might be pure chance, or indicate that the extended radio emission actually originates from the host galaxy, in which case it would be produced by star formation related processes.

sources.summary: The paper says J1228+5017 is detected by JVLA and LOFAR, unresolved in JVLA bands, has apparent 144 MHz extension caused by a nearby galaxy, and has a spectrum compatible with star formation.

quotes.quote: SDSS J122844.81+501751.2 J1228+5017 is detected with the JVLA in all other bands except the Q band, and it is also detected by LOFAR at 144 MHz (Table tab:jterf ). It is not properly resolved in any JVLA band (Figs. j1228x j1228q ). In the 144 MHz radio map it seems to be extended toward north-west, but upon closer inspection the extended part turns out to be a nearby galaxy that can also be seen in optical images. The radio spectrum, shown in Fig. j1228spect , has a constant slope of 0.7 from 144 MHz to X band, above which the slope flattens considerably (Table tab:j1228spind ). The low-frequency spectral index is consistent with the characteristic star formation activity spectral index of 0.7 and the flux density levels could be explained by star formation 2020berton2 .

gmw_00008012.name: J1522+3934
gmw_00008012.overview: J1522+3934 is a nearby narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2108.08521] hosted by an interacting spiral disk galaxy undergoing an evolved merger [arXiv:2108.08521, arXiv:2312.02326]. The active galactic nucleus drives relativistic jets [arXiv:2108.08521] and exhibits extended radio emission that is perpendicular to the host galaxy [arXiv:2312.02326].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1522+3934 as a nearby NLS1 with relativistic jets, steep JVLA emission, extended 1.6 GHz structure, and an evolved merger host that may relate to jet reorientation.

quotes.quote: J1522+3934 J1522+3934 is a nearby NLS1 at z = 0.077. It was detected in FIRST with a flux density of 2.52 mJy, which is comparable to its optical flux density, implying that no enhanced activity was expected from the AGN. However, so far its maximum flux density observed at 37 GHz has been 1.43 Jy, unquestionably confirming the presence of relativistic jets 2018lahteenmaki1 . Its integrated JVLA flux densities are (3.18 0.07) mJy, (0.40 0.02) mJy, and (0.35 0.02) mJy at 1.6, 5.2, and 9.0 GHz, respectively, consistent with a steep spectral index (Fig. fig:lcx-spectra ). At 1.6 GHz it shows considerable symmetric extended emission in east/south-east - west/north-west direction (Fig. fig:J1522-radio a).

J1522+3934 is a late-type galaxy clearly in a merger, and it has also been confirmed with spectroscopy 2017jarvela1 . Taking into account the small projected separation of the nuclei it seems to be an evolved merger, and the galaxies can be expected to affect, for example, the gas dynamics of each other. Like mentioned before, this can be the cause behind the jet re-oriention in J1522+3934.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1522+3934 as a nearby disk-galaxy merger with resolved radio emission perpendicular to the host, likely AGN-dominated optically thin radio emission, and record 37 GHz flux.

quotes.quote: SDSS J152205.41+393441.3 J1522+3934 is a nearby source ( z = 0.077) that resides in a disk galaxy that is merging with a non-active galaxy 2018jarvela1 . It shows almost symmetrical resolved emission on west/north-west and east/south-east sides of the nucleus from 144 MHz to Ku band, and is detected up to Ka band (Table tab:jterf and Figs. j1522x j1522ka ). Interestingly, the extended radio emission is perpendicular to the host galaxy, indicating that it does not originate from the star formation activity in the host 2021jarvela1 . To explain the 37 GHz flaring in J1522+3934 the jet emission needs to be relativistically boosted and thus the jet needs to point close to our line of sight.

The radio spectrum of J1522+3934, in Fig. j1522spect , has a very stable slope around 0.7 from 144 MHz all the way to the Ka band (Table tab:j1522spind ). The VLASS points seem to deviate from this which is surprising considering that the Quick Look flux densities should underestimate the real flux densities. Overall the spectrum seems to be consistent with optically thin radio emission and we can assume its predominant origin to be the AGN. J1522+3934 has the record 37 GHz flux density among our sources at 1430 mJy, whereas the other detections are much more modest.

sources.summary: The paper finds J1522+3934 agrees with FIRST at 1.6 GHz, has excess radio emission over star-formation predictions, and is hosted by an interacting spiral galaxy.

quotes.quote: Two of the sources, J1522+3934 and J1641+3454, have been already observed in the FIRST survey at 1.6 GHz in B configuration, which measured flux densities of 1.88 0.14 and 2.43 0.13 mJy, respectively. The integrated flux density we measured at 1.6 GHz was 2.03 0.16 and 2.52 0.10 mJy, respectively, results well in agreement with those from the FIRST.

The radio flux density of J1522+3934 is almost an order of magnitude higher than what the W4 flux density predicts, and additionally its W4 flux density value has a variability flag, suggesting the jet contribution might not be insignificant at 22 m. However, despite the possible contribution of the jet its radio flux density is higher than expected, indicating that star formation is not a sufficient explanation for the whole radio emission.

It is also worth noting that the 1.6 GHz map of J1522+3934 of Fig. fig:J1522 (left) has a nice counterpart in the infrared morphology of the source Jarvela18 . This source, indeed, is hosted by a spiral galaxy which is interacting with a close companion, and the diffuse radio emission is well aligned with the two nuclei, possibly because it originates in a starburst induced by the ongoing interaction.

gmw_00008013.name: NGC 7632
gmw_00008013.overview: NGC 7632 is a ring galaxy located within a loose galaxy group [arXiv:1202.2713]. The galaxy is distorted and bent toward other group members, while nearby interacting galaxies to its south also appear strongly distorted with much absorbing material [arXiv:1202.2713].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7632 as a ring galaxy in a loose group, distorted toward other group members, with nearby interacting galaxies also strongly distorted.

quotes.quote: NGC 7632 is a member of a loose galaxy group, containing multiple galaxies. This is a ring galaxy, which is observed to be distorted and bent towards the other group members 1981ApJS...46...75A . The interacting galaxies located south of the NGC 7632 also appear strongly distorted with much absorbing material 1981ApJS...46...75A .

gmw_00008014.name: 3C 277.3
gmw_00008014.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 277.3, also known as Coma A, is a smooth elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2111.01615, arXiv:0908.4391] classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II source [arXiv:1811.02844]. It features a double-lobed radio structure [arXiv:1811.02844, arXiv:1602.00505] surrounded by a large emission-line nebula [arXiv:2111.01615] and rich in cool gas [arXiv:1602.00505].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA polarization and Faraday-rotation observations of Coma A, modeling an external Faraday screen and describing its redshift, FRII classification, and two-lobed radio morphology.

quotes.quote: This paper presents broadband VLA polarization and Faraday rotation observations of the radio galaxy Coma A (3C 277.3) from 1 to 4 GHz, including archival VLA observations at 4.9 and 15 GHz. Through broadband polarization model-fitting, we find that an external Faraday screen with a turbulent magnetic field provides an appropriate description to most of the data. By combining the polarization and Faraday rotation results with previous H observations, we identified the H emitting gas as the Faraday screen responsible for the observed Faraday depolarization. We were able to derive the magnetic field strength in the H emitting gas, finding typical field strengths of 1 G, which is consistent with studies of the intra-group medium local to other radio galaxies.

Coma A Coma A is a radio galaxy at redshift z=0.0857 Bridle1981 corresponding to a comoving radial distance of 371.6 Mpc and angular scale of 1.659 kpc / Wright2006 using the values of H_0= 67.74 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _M = 0.3089 , and _ = 0.6911 from the Plank 2015 results Planckcoll2016 . The source has two distinct radio lobes and an angular size of 45 in the north-south direction. Coma A is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley II galaxy in . The northern lobe shows a bright hotspot, and the southern lobe has a bright plume expanding into a lobe structure.

sources.summary: VLT/MUSE observations show Coma A has a large emission-line nebula around its radio source, star-forming knots up to 60 kpc out, shocked filaments, and likely jet-triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: We present observations obtained with the VLT/MUSE optical integral field spectrograph of the radio source 3C277.3, located at a redshift of 0.085 and associated with the galaxy Coma A. An emission line region fully enshrouds the double-lobed radio source, which is 60 kpc 90 kpc in size. Based on the emission line ratios, we identified five compact knots in which the gas ionization is powered by young stars located as far as 60 kpc from the host. The emission line filaments surrounding the radio emission are compatible with ionization from fast shocks (with a velocity of 350-500 ), but a contribution from star formation occurring at the edges of the radio source is likely. Coma A might be a unique example in the local Universe in which the expanding outflow triggers star formation throughout the whole radio source.

Coma A is an elliptical galaxy with a total absolute magnitude (measured from the 2MASS images) of M _K =-25.12 with no signs of recent interactions capetti00,madrid06 . The central stellar velocity dispersion derived from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectrum is _s=196 7 , corresponding to a mass of the supermassive black hole of M _ BH 1.6 10^8 M _ when the relation between M _ BH and _s from gutelkin09 is adopted. The active nucleus has optical emission line ratios typical of high-excitation galaxies (HEGs, buttiglione10 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 277.3 as a small double-lobed radio galaxy behind Coma, rich in cool gas and classified as jet-cloud interacting because of an optical knot beside the bent jet.

quotes.quote: 3C 277.3 is a relatively small double-lobed radio galaxy (total size about 65 kpc), studied in detail by vanbreugel . In the northern lobe there is a prominent hotspot but no obvious jet, whereas in the southern lobe two radio knots delineate a dramatic bend in the jet through a projected angle of about 40^ , but there is no hotspot. While also known as Coma A, \ lies behind the Coma cluster and is offset from its centre by about 75 arcmin, outside the boundary of cluster X-ray emission. The entire \ system is rich in cool gas emitting in a number of optical lines vanbreugel and cold hydrogen is seen in absorption at 21 cm morganti . The H emission-line gas forms a system of large-scale arcs and filaments which extend almost as far perpendicular as parallel to the radio axis, and which appear to bound the radio lobes tadhunter .

3C 277.3's designation as a jet-cloud interacting radio galaxy is due to the bright optical emission-line knot that lies adjacent to the large bend in the southern jet, and with which the jet is interacting vanbreugel, si, tilak .

sources.summary: The paper describes 1251+27B as a smooth elliptical host with diffuse lobes, brightness enhancements, and a possible two-sided VLBA structure aligned with the kiloparsec jet.

quotes.quote: 1251+27B (3C 277.3) -- The radio and optical properties of 3C 277.3 are described in detail by . The elliptical host galaxy of 3C 277.3 is very smooth and regular; on kiloparsec-scales, the radio source extends < 60 kpc and it has two wide and diffuse lobes with a few brightness enhancements (southern jet and northern hot spot). In our VLBA image (Fig. f1251.1 ), this source shows a possible two-sided structure with total flux density 10.4 mJy and a jet orientation quite similar to the kiloparsec-scale jet ( PA 20 ^ ). The core flux density is 6.2 mJy and only 10 of the arcsecond flux is lost on the mas scale.

sources.summary: The paper notes missing IRAC coverage for 3C 277.3, uses WISE photometry, and finds short-wavelength starlight contamination makes IRAC AGN detection uncertain.

quotes.quote: Unfortunately there are no IRAC data available for 3C 277.3 and 3C 433 (See Table table:merger ).

We have used WISE Wright:2010 data for 3C 277.3 and 3C 433 (there are no IRAC data for these two sources) at 3.4 , 4.6 , 12 , and 22 m (bands W1, 2, 3 and 4, respectively), achieving an angular resolution of 6.1 , 6.4 , 6.5 and 12.0 arcsec in the four bands.

This is not the case for 3C 277.3, because its SED shows a substantial starlight contribution at 3.4 and 4.6 m, suggesting that its AGN would not be clearly detected at 3.6 and 4.5 m with IRAC, and making it uncertain that the AGN would be detected at 5.8 and 8.0 m in this object.

gmw_00008015.name: GAMA 485885
gmw_00008015.overview: GAMA 485885 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits a classic spiral gas distribution [arXiv:1903.03121]. Its gas rotates approximately regularly around a kinematic center, showing a peaked central velocity dispersion and a potential small warp in its kinematic position angle [arXiv:1903.03121].

sources.summary: The paper uses GAMA 485885 to demonstrate recovery of a classic spiral gas distribution, regular rotation with a possible small warp, and central velocity-dispersion peak.

quotes.quote: For GAMA 485885, we see the ability of our approach to resolve a classic spiral gas distribution. The 2D residuals between the convolved model and data exhibit significant differences on scales less than the FWHM _ PSF . The 2D maps for the LoS gas kinematics suggest that the gas is approximately regularly rotating around a kinematic centre, potentially with a small warp in the kinematic position angle. The H gas velocity dispersion is peaked within the centre of the galaxy as expected for most regularly rotating galaxies that have been affected by beam smearing.

gmw_00008016.name: GAMA 220371
gmw_00008016.overview: GAMA 220371 is a galaxy characterized by a clumpy hydrogen gas profile [arXiv:1903.03121]. Despite the presence of resolved gas clumps in its northeastern, southeastern, and southwestern regions, the galaxy exhibits approximately regular gas rotation [arXiv:1903.03121].

sources.summary: The paper describes GAMA 220371 as having a clumpy H gas profile that the model fits adequately, with resolved clumps and approximately regular gas rotation.

quotes.quote: We show 2D maps for GAMA 220371 in Fig. fig:example_sample1 . This galaxy has a clumpy H gas profile. We are still able to construct an adequate model to the data using our approach. The 2D residual maps for the H flux show greater differences for three clumps in the North-East, South-East, and South-West regions. However, the general structure of the clumps is reasonably well resolved. The maps for the gas kinematics suggest an approximately regularly rotating galaxy.

gmw_00008017.name: GAMA 30890
gmw_00008017.overview: GAMA 30890 is a disk galaxy that exhibits star formation [arXiv:1903.03121]. It displays asymmetries and substructure in its line-of-sight hydrogen gas kinematic maps, including a slight velocity warp and localized velocity dispersion differences [arXiv:1903.03121].

sources.summary: The paper uses GAMA 30890 as an asymmetric gas-kinematics example, partially recovering H gas structure, residual flux substructure, velocity warp, and dispersion differences.

quotes.quote: An example posterior sample for GAMA 30890 is shown in Fig. fig:example_sample2 . This galaxy exhibits asymmetries and substructure in the LoS H gas kinematic maps. We are able to partially recover the H gas kinematics despite only introducing asymetries in the gas H gas distribution.

Some substructure in the residuals remain with a patch of H gas flux that is lower in the convolved model compared to data. There is also a slight warp in the LoS velocity profile as a function of radius, and differences in the velocity dispersion on the order of 5 km s ^ 1 . However, the convolved model still performs reasonably well at resolving the gas flux and kinematics.

gmw_00008018.name: ScUVLG143417
gmw_00008018.overview: ScUVLG143417 is a disturbed, asymmetric galaxy containing two bright cores embedded in a fainter structure [arXiv:0906.1012]. The system exhibits disturbed kinematics suggestive of recent merger activity [arXiv:0906.1012], with its northwestern companion showing anomalously strong molecular emission likely driven by shocks [arXiv:1911.07854].

sources.summary: The paper describes ScUVLG143417 as a disturbed, asymmetric galaxy with two bright cores, little ordered velocity structure, high dispersion, and signatures consistent with recent merger activity.

quotes.quote: ScUVLG143417 This galaxy's morphology differs from ScUVLG092600, with two bright cores (separated by < 1 kpc) embedded in a fainter, disturbed (irregular and asymmetric) galaxy. We cannot detect the fainter arms very clearly in the Pa- data. However, the central region surrounding the brightest two star-forming knots does appear kinematically disturbed -- lacking structure in velocity and velocity dispersion. While a face-on disk could explain the absence in velocity structure, lack of structure in makes that scenario less plausible.

Using the integrated spectrum, we find = 86 3 km s ^ 1 . With no observed velocity gradient, we can only place an upper limit: v/ < 0.7, using v _c=( v _ max v _ min )/2 and no inclination correction.
