This case represents disturbed kinematics with multiple cores and underlying faint features, suggestive of recent merger activity.

sources.summary: J1434 has a broader second Pa component and spatially offset molecular emission; its northwestern companion shows anomalously strong H2/Pa, interpreted as possible merger-driven shocks.

quotes.quote: For J0150, J0213, and J1434, the second Pa component has a broader line width than the first.
The distribution of H _2 1-0 ro-vibrational emission in J1434 is concentrated toward the northwest source where the Pa line is weak.
For J1434, H _2 /Pa in its northwestern companion source is anomalously strong, which can be interpreted as evidence for merger-driven shocks.

gmw_00008019.name: HCG 16d
gmw_00008019.overview: HCG 16d is a nearly edge-on luminous infrared starburst galaxy morphologically similar to M82 [arXiv:1910.03420, arXiv:1509.08468]. It exhibits shock excitation driven by a starburst outflow [arXiv:1910.03420, arXiv:1509.08468], alongside a discrepancy between its neutral hydrogen and optical disc kinematics [arXiv:1910.03420].

sources.summary: The paper describes HCG 16d as an M82-like LIRG with starburst-driven shock/LINER signatures, ambiguous nuclear activity, central H I absorption, and H I kinematics disconnected from the optical disc.

quotes.quote: HCG 16d is a LIRG, classified as S0-a, and is morphologically similar to M82. It contains a central source classified as both a LINER and as a LINER/Seyfert 2 double nucleus Ribeiro+1996,deCarvalho+1999 with optical line ratios, or as an AGN in X-rays observations with XMM Turner+2001 . However, HST observations show that the suggested double nucleus is instead a group of star clusters Konstantopoulos+2013 and Chandra X-ray observations indicate that the hard X-rays might originate solely from X-ray binaries, not an AGN OSullivan+2014a,Oda+2018 , while IFU observations suggest that the LINER line ratios in the optical may arise from shock excitation driven by the on-going starburst Rich+2010 .

When considering the kinematics of the gas in HCG 16d there is an apparent contradiction between \ and H . In \ there is only a weak velocity gradient in approximately the North-South direction, almost perpendicular to the optical major axis (Figure fig:HCG16d_moms ). In contrast, the existing H rotation curve of the inner regions of the galaxy Rubin+1991,Mendes+1998,Rich+2010 shows significant rotation ( V_ rot 100 ) and has its major axis almost aligned with the optical disc, that is, approximately perpendicular to the slight gradient in the \ emission.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes this nearly edge-on HCG 16 starburst as showing outflow-driven shocks in emission-line maps and post-starburst E+A spectral regions.

quotes.quote: IRAS F02072-1025 (NGC 839) This system is described in great detail in Rich10 . It is a nearly edge-on starburst galaxy in a compact group (HCG 16). Our emission line maps trace the shocks associated with the starburst-driven outflow and we see evidence of a post-starburst E+A spectrum in portions of the galaxy.

gmw_00008020.name: NGC 848
gmw_00008020.overview: NGC 848 is a barred spiral starburst galaxy physically connected to the Hickson Compact Group 16 by a 160 kpc tidal tail [arXiv:1910.03420]. Despite this connection, the galaxy maintains a normal global neutral hydrogen content and a mostly regular gas distribution [arXiv:1910.03420].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 848 as a barred spiral starburst connected to HCG 16 by a 160 kpc H I tail, while retaining normal global H I content and a mostly regular gas distribution.

quotes.quote: NGC 848 NGC 848 is a barred spiral galaxy (SBab) that is separated from the rest of the group by about 10 , or around 160 kpc, and is another galaxy in the group undergoing a starburst Ribeiro+1996,deCarvalho+1999 . Although this galaxy was not included in the original Hickson catalogue, once spectra were obtained of the galaxies it was immediately noticed that it was at the same redshift as the core group (Table tab:optprops ) and therefore likely associated Ribeiro+1996 . However, it was not until there were \ interferometric observations that the physical connection, in the form of a 160 kpc tidal tail, was discovered Verdes-Montenegro+2001 . Despite its connection to this enormous tidal feature (visible in Figure fig:mom0 ) the galaxy itself has an entirely normal global \ content (Table tab:HIprops ). This alone indicates that the gas in the tail probably did not (for the most part) originate from NGC 848, but from somewhere else in the group, especially as the total \ mass in the tail is approximately the same as the \ mass of NGC 848.

The \ distribution in the galaxy itself (Figure fig:NGC848_moms ) appears mostly regular, with a centroid coincident with the optical centre and a mostly uniform velocity field. However, the kinematic major and minor axes are not quite perpendicular, which is probably due to the presence of the bar e.g. Bosma1981 . The \ gas is also slightly extended towards the NW and SE, i.e. in the direction of the tidal tail, while in the optical image the spiral arms appear very loosely wrapped (Figure fig:NGC848_moms ), probably indicating that the outer disc is beginning to be unbound.

gmw_00008021.name: PGC 8210
gmw_00008021.overview: PGC 8210 is a small spiral galaxy that is considered part of the Hickson Compact Group 16 structure [arXiv:1910.03420]. It exhibits an undisturbed optical disk and a normal neutral hydrogen gas content, suggesting it has not yet experienced any meaningful interaction with the group [arXiv:1910.03420].

sources.summary: The paper treats PGC 8210 as a smaller spiral likely associated with HCG 16 but not yet meaningfully interacting, with undisturbed optical and H I structure and normal gas content.

quotes.quote: PGC 8210 The final galaxy detected within the primary beam of the VLA observations is PGC 8210. This is another spiral galaxy but it is considerably smaller and less massive than the core members of the group. Its B-band luminosity is almost 1 dex lower than the galaxies in the core group and its \ mass is considerably less than all but HCG 16b, which is extremely \ deficient. Because the velocity of PGC 8210 is coincident with that of the group it is assumed to be part of the same structure, or at least about to join it.

However, as shown in Figure fig:PGC8210_moms the \ distribution appears undisturbed (with the caveat that it is hardly larger than the beam), as does its optical disc, and its total \ content is entirely normal. Therefore, it seems very unlikely that this galaxy has had any meaningful interaction with the group to date.

gmw_00008022.name: NW clump
gmw_00008022.overview: The NW clump is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located at the end of a tidal feature [arXiv:1910.03420]. It features a blue, low-surface-brightness optical counterpart, which suggests that in situ star formation is occurring within the clump [arXiv:1910.03420].

sources.summary: The paper favors the NW clump as a tidal dwarf candidate at the end of a tidal feature, with high H I column density and a blue low-surface-brightness optical counterpart.

quotes.quote: The NW clump has the smallest \ mass of the three clumps, but the highest column density, peaking at over 2.45 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 . The moment 1 map does not show clear evidence for rotation, however, it is barely 2 beams across and any potential signature of rotation may be confused with gas in the NW tail. TDGs are expected to be found at the tip of tidal tails as this clump is e.g. Bournaud+2004 and unlike the other two clumps there is a clear, but very LSB optical counterpart visible in the DECaLS image at RA = 2h 9m 26s , Dec = -10^ 7 \ 9 \ (top left panel of Figure fig:clump_moms and right panel of Figure fig:HCG16a_moms ) and it is also just visible in GALEX Galaxy Evolution Explorer, Martin+2005 .

The blue colour of this optical counterpart suggests that it is the result of in situ SF, not old stars which might have been stripped along with the gas. This strengthens the case for this candidate TDG, but there remains the possibility that this is a gas-rich LSB dwarf which is having its \ stripped, rather than a new galaxy that has formed as a result of the tidal interactions in the group. While it is not possible, with the current information, to rule out either of these hypotheses, we favour the TDG interpretation because this dwarf appears at the end of an enormous tidal feature and it seems implausible that either such a small galaxy stripped this gas from the other (much more massive) group members, or that it could have originally been sufficiently gas-rich to be the origin of the observed tidal gas.

gmw_00008023.name: G136604
gmw_00008023.overview: G136604 is a strong gravitational lens galaxy [arXiv:2009.09493] that serves as the brightest cluster galaxy of ACO 1364 [arXiv:1512.00462]. It is surrounded by a dense environment containing at least 12 companion galaxies within one arcminute [arXiv:1512.00462].

sources.summary: SDSS J1143-0144 has two large arcs and is the brightest cluster galaxy of ACO 1364, where the dense environment and envelope complicate single-Sersic modelling.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1143-0144 exhibits two large arcs with radii 2.4 and 1.7 ^ , respectively. The latter is very faint compared to its environment, complicating the surface brightness modelling. The larger and more pronounced (45 ^ ) tangential arc has a small radial arc as a counterpart. Both the radial arc and the brighter spots in the faint arc have been masked out for the photometric model. We use this doubly imaged configuration as an input for the lens reconstruction.

The lens galaxy is known to be the brightest cluster galaxy of ACO 1364, with at least 12 companions within 1 ^ . The high density environment and extended low surface brightness envelope often found around cD galaxies SGJ07 explains why with increasing radius, a single S ersic profile does not provide a good fit anymore.

sources.summary: The paper presents G136604 as a strong lens found by both LinKS and GalaxyZoo, with high but discrepant scores and Einstein-radius estimates compared against the known SLACS lens value.

quotes.quote: The two candidates (G136604 and G124486) common to both LinKS machine learning and GalaxyZoo citizen science fall within the overlap of the parameter space occupied by the two methods' samples in terms of stellar mass and redshift, as shown in Figure fig:big_plot . Table overlap_table compares these two candidates, and their KiDS cutouts are shown in Figure fig:mac_zoo_overlap . Their high scores make these two of the most promising candidates; however, it is worth noting the disagreement between scores given to G136604 by each method. This candidate scored very highly in the LinKS catalog but barely passed GalaxyZoo score selection criteria.

For G136604, which is one of the two overlaps between the LinKS machine learning and GalaxyZoo citizen science candidate catalogs, the true Einstein radius Bolton08 lies between the SIS estimate from the relation and the thin-lens estimate, while the SIS estimate from underestimates the true value by around half an arcsecond. The SIS estimate utilizing the true source redshift and the relation most closely approximates the true Einstein radius.

G136604 was identified in both the LinKS machine learning and GalaxyZoo citizen science selections (KiDS cutout shown also in Figure fig:mac_zoo_overlap , left) and was a known strong lens from the SLACS survey slacs7,slacs10,Barnabe11,Bolton12,Czoske12,Shu15 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSSJ1143-0144 as a case study where the MGE lens-light model succeeds better than three Sersic profiles and captures central point-like emission.

quotes.quote: Taking SDSSJ1143-0144 as a case study, the left and middle panels of Fig. fig:example_norms compare the normalized residuals of the `` 6 30 + 1 10 '' MGE lens light model and the 3 S ersic lens light model. The three S ersics model shows significant correlated residuals and therefore fails to capture angular complexity inherent in the light distribution of the lens galaxy, which the MGE model fits successfully.

Fig. fig:example_norms compares the normalized residuals for SDSSJ1143-0144 using an MGE lens light model with (left panel) and without (right panel) this set of 10 Gaussians. Omitting them leads to the presence of minor dipole residuals in the central region of the lens galaxy's light distribution.

gmw_00008024.name: SDSSJ1226
gmw_00008024.overview: SDSSJ1226 is a strongly lensed, dusty star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 1.60 [arXiv:2110.05537]. It exhibits Milky Way-like, low-excitation molecular gas and an extended, main-sequence star-formation mode [arXiv:2110.05537].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSSJ1226 as a strongly lensed z=1.60454 ultra-bright dusty star-forming galaxy with Milky Way-like low CO excitation and an extended main-sequence star-formation mode.

quotes.quote: Based on observations with the IRAM 30 m and Yebes 40 m telescopes, we report evidence of the detection of Milky Way-like, low-excitation molecular gas, up to the transition CO( J=5-4 ), in a distant, dusty star-forming galaxy at z_ CO =1.60454 . WISE J122651.0+214958.8 (alias SDSSJ1226, the Cosmic Seahorse ), is strongly lensed by a foreground galaxy cluster at z=0.44 with a source magnification of =9.5 0.7 . This galaxy was selected by cross-correlating near-to-mid infrared colours within the full-sky AllWISE survey, originally aiming to discover rare analogs of the archetypical strongly lensed submillimeter galaxy SMM J2135-0102, the Cosmic Eyelash. We derive an apparent (i.e. not corrected for lensing magnification) rest-frame 8-1000 m infrared luminosity of L_ IR =1.66^ +0.04 _ 0.04 10^ 13 L _ and apparent star-formation rate SFR _ IR =2960 70 M _ yr ^ 1 .

SDSSJ1226 is ultra-bright at S_ 350 m 170 mJy and shows similarly bright low- J CO line intensities as SMM J2135-0102, however, with exceptionally small CO( J=5-4 ) intensity. We consider different scenarios to reconcile our observations with typical findings of high- z starbursts, and speculate about the presence of a previously unseen star-formation mechanism in cosmic noon submillimeter galaxies. In conclusion, the remarkable low line luminosity ratio r_ 5,2 =0.11 0.02 is best explained by an extended, main-sequence star-formation mode -- representing a missing link between starbursts to low-luminosity systems during the epoch of peak star-formation history.

gmw_00008025.name: SDSS 1027
gmw_00008025.overview: SDSS 1027 is a hierarchical merging system at a redshift of z=0.066 [arXiv:1104.3391, arXiv:1109.0710]. While initially identified as a triple active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1104.3391, arXiv:2402.02107], recent observations classify the central nucleus as a retired galaxy with no active galactic nucleus or star formation activity [arXiv:2304.09968].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1027+1749 as a hierarchical merger hosting three emission-line obscured AGN nuclei whose stellar components may soon merge into an interacting triple SMBH system.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery of a kpc-scale triple AGN, SDSS J1027+1749 at z=0.066 , from our systematic search for hierarchical mergers of AGNs. The galaxy contains three emission-line nuclei, two of which are offset by 450 and 110 km s ^ 1 in velocity and by 2.4 and 3.0 kpc in projected separation from the central nucleus. All three nuclei are classified as obscured AGNs based on optical diagnostic emission line ratios, with black hole mass estimates M_ 10^8 M_ from stellar velocity dispersions measured in the associated stellar components. Based on dynamical friction timescale estimates, the three stellar components in SDSS J1027+1749 will merge in 40 Myr, and their associated SMBHs may evolve into a gravitationally interacting triple system in 200 Myr.

Here, we report our initial results on the discovery of a triple AGN in a hierarchical merging system, SDSS J1027+1749. Shown in Figure fig:sdssimg , the galaxy contains three nuclei as seen in its SDSS image, with B and C at projected separations of 3.0 and 2.4 kpc from A. The nuclei A and B have SDSS spectra. We have conducted optical slit spectroscopy for all three nuclei.

sources.summary: The paper revises the central source SDSS 1027 from a triple-AGN candidate to a retired or fake AGN galaxy, with WHAN and Chandra data showing no current AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Since there are very few of them confirmed as such, we present the results of a new study of the triple-AGN candidate SDSS 1027 (center nucleus) through observations with GTC \ Integral Field Unit. 1D and 2D analysis of the line ratios of the three nuclei allow us to locate them in the EW( ) vs. / \ diagram. The central nucleus is found to be a retired galaxy (or fake AGN).
Chandra archival data confirm that the neighbors have X-ray (0.5-2) keV and (2-7) keV emission, whereas the center nucleus shows no X-ray emission.
The WHAN diagram shows that 1027, previously identified as a LINER by L11, is instead a retired galaxy that does not have activity produced by either AGN or SF.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J1027 as a triple AGN candidate with FUV SFR below an IR-based literature value and extreme color/extinction among massive distant AGN hosts.

quotes.quote: It is possible that the nuclei of other two galaxies with AGN (e.g., MRK 789 or SDSS J102700.40+174901.0) have contributed to the UV emission. However, those are not AGN dominant and the nuclei are not resolved enough to perform the mask analysis.
The triple AGN candidate SDSS J1027 in our sample has a SFR _ FUV = 10.55 0.62 M _ yr ^ 1 whereas foord2021b reported SFR _ IR as 18.2 1.3 M _ yr ^ 1 .
We did notice that two of the AGN-hosted galaxies (viz., SDSS J1019, SDSS J1027) show extreme color as well as extinction. However, these are the most distant as well as massive systems.

sources.summary: The review describes SDSSJ1027+1749 as a z=0.066 kpc-scale triple AGN with three emission-line nuclei, all classified as obscured AGNs.

quotes.quote: Recently, discovered a kpc-scale, triple AGN, SDSSJ1027+1749, at z = 0.066. The system contains three emission-line nuclei, two of which are offset by 2.4 and 3.0 kpc in projected separation from the central nucleus. All three objects are classified as obscured AGNs based on optical diagnostic emission line ratios, with black hole mass M_ BH 10^8 M_ estimated from stellar velocity dispersions.

gmw_00008026.name: southeastern nucleus
gmw_00008026.overview: The southeastern nucleus is a strong active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2304.09968]. It features a rotating disk, extended ionized gas, and a collisional ring with star-forming knots likely formed by a minor merger with its northern companion [arXiv:2304.09968].

sources.summary: The paper classifies the southeastern nucleus as a strong Sy2 AGN with X-ray emission, a rotating disk, extended ionized gas, and a collisional ring likely shaped by the northern companion.

quotes.quote: The neighbors are found to be a strong AGN (southeastern nucleus, ) compatible with a Sy2 galaxy, and a weak AGN (northern nucleus, ) compatible with a LINER2. We find evidence that the neighbors constitute a dual AGN system (Sy2-LINER2) with a projected separation of 3.98 kpc in the optical bands.
The \ velocity map shows that the northern nucleus has an \ emission with a velocity offset of 500 , whereas the southeastern nucleus has a rotating disk and \ extended emission at kpc scales.

A collisional ring with knots is observed in the HST images of the southeastern nucleus. These knots coincide with star formation regions that along with the ring are predicted in a head-on collision. In this case, the morphology changes are probably due to a minor merger that was produced by the passing of the northern through the southeastern nucleus.

gmw_00008027.name: northern nucleus
gmw_00008027.overview: The northern nucleus is a weak active galactic nucleus classified as a LINER2 [arXiv:2304.09968]. It is in a late minor merger phase with a southeastern nucleus, moving with a velocity offset of 500 km/s relative to its neighbor [arXiv:2304.09968].

sources.summary: The paper classifies the northern nucleus as a weak LINER2 AGN with X-ray emission, a large velocity offset, and a likely minor-merger encounter with the southeastern nucleus.

quotes.quote: The neighbors are found to be a strong AGN (southeastern nucleus, ) compatible with a Sy2 galaxy, and a weak AGN (northern nucleus, ) compatible with a LINER2. We find evidence that the neighbors constitute a dual AGN system (Sy2-LINER2) with a projected separation of 3.98 kpc in the optical bands.
The \ velocity map shows that the northern nucleus has an \ emission with a velocity offset of 500 , whereas the southeastern nucleus has a rotating disk and \ extended emission at kpc scales.

The kinematical analysis presented in this work shows that the Northern nucleus is moving with respect to the South-Eastern nucleus with a speed of 500 . This large velocity suggests that both AGN are in a late minor merger phase.

gmw_00008028.name: IXO 33
gmw_00008028.overview: IXO 33 is a quasar with a spectrum that rises slightly toward red wavelengths [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290]. Its spectrum is characterized by wide Balmer lines and narrow forbidden oxygen emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IXO 33 as a quasar-like optical counterpart of a ULX source, with a red-rising spectrum and Balmer plus forbidden oxygen emission lines.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the results of new observations of six other cases, all of which also turn out to be quasars at higher redshift than their parent galaxies. We took spectroscopy in January 2004 for the optical counterparts of sources (following the notation of the CP02 catalogue) IXO 33, 45, 58, 69, 71 and 84.

Spectra of four of the objects (IXO 45, 58, 69 and 71) are rather flat while the other two (IXO 33 and 84) rise slightly towards red wavelengths. The main lines in the spectra of objects IXO 33 and 71 are wide Balmer lines (H _ and H _ ) and narrow forbidden OII ( 3727 ) and OIII ( 4959, 5007 ) lines, while the spectra of the other four (objects IXO 45, 58, 69 and 84) are dominated by the wide emission line of Mg II (2798 ). All these features are characteristic of quasar spectra. The position of the main emission lines allows unambigous determination of the redshifts of the objects.

gmw_00008029.name: IXO 45
gmw_00008029.overview: IXO 45 is a quasar-like object identified as the optical counterpart of an ultraluminous X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290]. Its optical spectrum is relatively flat and is dominated by a broad magnesium II emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IXO 45 as a quasar-like optical counterpart of a ULX source, with a flat spectrum dominated by broad Mg II emission.

All these features are characteristic of quasar spectra. The position of the main emission lines allows unambigous determination of the redshifts of the objects.

gmw_00008030.name: IXO 58
gmw_00008030.overview: IXO 58 is a quasar-like object that was identified as the optical counterpart of an ultraluminous X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290]. Its optical spectrum is relatively flat and is dominated by a broad Mg II emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IXO 58 as a quasar-like optical counterpart of a ULX source, with a flat spectrum dominated by broad Mg II emission.

All these features are characteristic of quasar spectra. The position of the main emission lines allows unambigous determination of the redshifts of the objects.

gmw_00008032.name: IXO 71
gmw_00008032.overview: IXO 71 is a quasar characterized by a relatively flat optical spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290]. Its spectrum displays wide Balmer emission lines alongside narrow forbidden oxygen lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IXO 71 as a quasar-like optical counterpart of a ULX source, with a flat spectrum and Balmer plus forbidden oxygen emission lines.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the results of new observations of six other cases, all of which also turn out to be quasars at higher redshift than their parent galaxies. gmw_00008033.name: IXO 84
gmw_00008033.overview: IXO 84 is a quasar-like object identified as the optical counterpart of an ultraluminous X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290]. Its optical spectrum rises slightly toward red wavelengths and is dominated by a broad magnesium II emission line [arXiv:astro-ph/0502290].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IXO 84 as a quasar-like optical counterpart of a ULX source, with a red-rising spectrum dominated by broad Mg II emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the results of new observations of six other cases, all of which also turn out to be quasars at higher redshift than their parent galaxies. We took spectroscopy in January 2004 for the optical counterparts of sources (following the notation of the CP02 catalogue) IXO 33, 45, 58, 69, 71 and 84.
