gmw_00008034.name: SDSS J0859+4239
gmw_00008034.overview: SDSS J0859+4239 is a luminous low-ionization broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 1.5 [arXiv:1710.08563]. Near-infrared spectroscopy reveals strong Balmer absorption features and redshifted absorption that may indicate an inflow of high-density gas [arXiv:1710.08563].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0859+4239 as a luminous z~1.5 LoBAL quasar with strong Balmer absorption, redshifted H absorption possibly tracing inflow, and derived neutral hydrogen column density.

quotes.quote: We here present the discovery of two luminous LoBAL QSOs at z 1.5 which show strong Balmer absorption features in their near-IR spectra, namely SDSS J085910.40+423911.3 and SDSS J101927.37+022521.4, hereafter SDSS J0859+4239 and SDSS J1019+0225. Both objects have been observed as part of the sample presented in Schulze:2017 (hereafter S17). In S17 we obtained near-IR spectroscopy, covering H , H and H , for a sample of 12 LoBAL QSOs at z 1.5 and 10 at z 2.3 . The targets were selected from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) quasar catalog Schneider:2010 , showing continuous broad absorption in Mg 2 or Al 3 as measured by Allen:2011 using the balnicity index BI; Weymann:1991 . We identified the two Balmer absorption systems within these objects.

In SDSS J0859+4239 we find redshifted absorption by 500 km s ^ 1 with an H rest-frame equivalent width of 7 . The redshifted absorption could indicate an inflow of high density gas onto the black hole, though we cannot rule out alternative interpretations.

We estimate the covering fractions and optical depth of the absorber and derive neutral hydrogen column densities, N_ H I 1.3 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 for SDSS J1019+0225 and N_ H I 9 10^ 17 cm ^ 2 for SDSS J0859+4239 .

gmw_00008035.name: SDSS J0839+3805
gmw_00008035.overview: SDSS J0839+3805 is a high-redshift Balmer-absorption quasar located at a redshift of z=2.32 [arXiv:1710.08563]. Its spectrum displays hydrogen absorption with a blueshift of 520 km/s, alongside tentative detections of helium absorption [arXiv:1710.08563].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J0839+3805 as a high-redshift Balmer-absorption quasar comparison, noting UV spectral similarity to SDSS J0859+4239, H absorption, blueshift, and tentative He I absorption.

quotes.quote: In the bottom panel of Fig. fig:spec_uv we show in addition a BOSS spectrum (taken in 2010) for SDSS J083942.11+380526.3 (hereafter SDSS J0839+3805), the first high- z Balmer absorption quasar discovered Aoki:2006 . Interestingly, we find a close resemblance in the rest-frame UV spectra between SDSS J0839+3805 and SDSS J0859+4239. This might indicate similar physical conditions of the absorbing gas in both objects. SDSS J0839+3805 does show H absorption with a rest equivalent width of 8 , comparable to SDSS J0859+4239, and a blueshift of 520 km s ^ 1 , while the absorption feature in SDSS J0859+4239 is redshifted by about the same amount. Since SDSS J0839+3805 is at z=2.32 , its BOSS spectrum does not cover the He 1 ^* lines, but Aoki:2006 report a tentative discovery of He 1 ^* 3889 in their J band spectrum, though at low significance.

gmw_00008036.name: 4FGL J1219.0+3653
gmw_00008036.overview: 4FGL J1219.0+3653 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus located at z ~ 3.59 [arXiv:2010.12907]. It is identified as the first gamma-ray emitting BL Lacertae object discovered beyond a redshift of 3 [arXiv:2010.12907].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4FGL J1219.0+3653 as the first gamma-ray BL Lac beyond z=3, supported by featureless GTC spectra, a Lyman break, and blazar-like radio association.

quotes.quote: Here we report the identification of the first ray emitting BL Lac object, 4FGL J1219.0+3653 (J1219), beyond z=3 , i.e., within the first two billion years of the age of the Universe. The optical and near-infrared spectra of J1219 taken from 10.4 m Gran Telescopio Canarias exhibit no emission lines down to an equivalent width of 3.5 supporting its BL Lac nature. The detection of a strong Lyman- break at 5570 , on the other hand, confirms that J2119 is indeed a high-redshift ( z 3.59 ) quasar. Based on the prediction of a recent BL Lac evolution model, J1219 is one of the only two such objects expected to be present within the comoving volume at z=3.5 .

Among the 10 most distant ( z 3 ) ones, 9 are classified as FSRQs and one object, 4FGL J1219.0+3653 (hereafter J1219), associated with the radio source NVSS J121915+365718, is flagged as a blazar of uncertain type (BCU Blazar candidates of uncertain type or BCUs are ray emitting sources whose multi-wavelength properties are similar to blazars, e.g., a flat radio spectrum, but lack spectral characterization needed to classify them as a BL Lac or FSRQ 2015ApJ...810...14A . ). The only available Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectrum of J1219 exhibits the Lyman- cut-off at 5570 , thus supporting its high-redshift nature. More importantly, it does not show any broad emission lines, thus hinting it to be a candidate BL Lac object. The identification of a ray emitting BL Lac beyond z=3 can have major implications on the cosmic evolution of relativistic jets.

gmw_00008037.name: NSA 28616
gmw_00008037.overview: NSA 28616 is a secure ultraviolet-variable active galactic nucleus identified through both infrared criteria and BPT diagnostics [arXiv:2205.00547]. The galaxy's spectral energy distribution model shows an active galactic nucleus contribution fraction of 0.90 [arXiv:2205.00547].

sources.summary: The paper treats NSA 28616 as a secure UV-variable AGN, selected by IR criteria and BPT diagnostics, with a high AGN fraction in its SED model.

quotes.quote: NSA 28616 is selected by both IR criteria and NSA 631637 is only selected by the criteria of . Interestingly, the vast majority of our highly variable sources are not selected by WISE color-color diagnostics. NSA 28616 is also selected as a BPT AGN based on log( [ O 3 ] / H ) vs log( [ N 2 ] / H ). In addition, NSA 28616 has significant AGN contribution to its overall SED ( f_ AGN = 0.90).
The top panel shows the SED of NSA 28616. With a high AGN fraction ( f_ AGN = 0.90) and narrow lines placing it in the AGN region of the BPT diagram, this is a secure UV-variable AGN.

gmw_00008038.name: NSA 58917
gmw_00008038.overview: NSA 58917 is a low-mass dwarf galaxy that exhibits variability [arXiv:2205.00547]. Its spectral energy distribution is dominated by star-forming emission templates and shows very little contribution from an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2205.00547].

sources.summary: The paper describes NSA 58917 as a low-mass variable galaxy whose SED has little AGN-template contribution and is dominated by star-forming emission templates.

quotes.quote: The SED model of NSA 58917 has AGN fraction of 17.3 and only 3.1 of iterations contains an AGN template. This dwarf galaxy's SED contains high contribution of star-forming emission from the SFG and IRR templates.

Low-mass galaxies Six galaxies of the selected variable population have stellar masses less than 10^ 10 M _ (NSA 58917, 208702, 631480, 631492, 631637, and 649438). The AGN fraction at 15 m splits this group into either virtually none ( f_ AGN <20 , NSA 58917, 208702, and 649438) or significant ( f_ AGN >90 , NSA 631480, 631492, 631637) AGN contribution.

gmw_00008039.name: NSA 208702
gmw_00008039.overview: NSA 208702 is a low-mass dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2205.00547]. It exhibits ultraviolet variability and is classified as a composite galaxy on the standard BPT diagram, indicating a mixture of starburst and active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2205.00547].

sources.summary: The paper lists NSA 208702 among low-mass UV-variable galaxies with little SED AGN contribution, composite BPT classification, and selection that optical spectroscopy would have missed.

quotes.quote: Low-mass galaxies Six galaxies of the selected variable population have stellar masses less than 10^ 10 M _ (NSA 58917, 208702, 631480, 631492, 631637, and 649438). The AGN fraction at 15 m splits this group into either virtually none ( f_ AGN <20 , NSA 58917, 208702, and 649438) or significant ( f_ AGN >90 , NSA 631480, 631492, 631637) AGN contribution. Only NSA 589117 and 208702 have emission line spectra for testing optical spectroscopic diagnostics: NSA 589117 was categorized as starburst and NSA 208702 is categorized as a composite galaxy in the standard BPT diagram. The lack of SDSS spectra for the remaining four low-mass galaxies and the results for NSA 589117 and 208702 demonstrate that spectroscopic selection would have missed these sources, whereas our use of UV variability included them in our population.

gmw_00008040.name: NSA 631637
gmw_00008040.overview: NSA 631637 is a low-mass dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass of 10^8.19 solar masses [arXiv:2205.00547]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus, which contributes a large fraction of 93 percent to its spectral energy distribution [arXiv:2205.00547].

sources.summary: The paper describes NSA 631637 as a low-mass dwarf galaxy selected by WISE criteria, without SDSS spectroscopy, and with a large AGN contribution to its SED.

quotes.quote: NSA 28616 is selected by both IR criteria and NSA 631637 is only selected by the criteria of . Interestingly, the vast majority of our highly variable sources are not selected by WISE color-color diagnostics.
NSA 631637 did not have SDSS spectroscopy. NSA 631637 is a dwarf galaxy, having a mass of 10^ 8.19 M _ with a large AGN contribution ( f_ AGN = 0.93) to its SED.

Low-mass galaxies Six galaxies of the selected variable population have stellar masses less than 10^ 10 M _ (NSA 58917, 208702, 631480, 631492, 631637, and 649438). The AGN fraction at 15 m splits this group into either virtually none ( f_ AGN <20 , NSA 58917, 208702, and 649438) or significant ( f_ AGN >90 , NSA 631480, 631492, 631637) AGN contribution.

gmw_00008041.name: VCC 1386
gmw_00008041.overview: VCC 1386 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0511015]. The galaxy hosts a system of globular clusters that are old and metal-poor, whereas the galaxy body itself contains a younger, more metal-rich stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0511015].

sources.summary: The paper studies VCC 1386 as a Virgo dwarf elliptical whose globular clusters are old and metal poor, while the galaxy body is younger and more metal rich.

quotes.quote: Our target, VCC 1386, is a M _ V = 16.25 ( L _ * +5) dE with a system of globular cluster candidates, 13 of which we obtain spectroscopy for using the Keck I telescope.

Our main conclusion is that some of the globular clusters surrounding VCC 1386 contain old and metal poor stars, in contrast to the dwarf itself, which contains a younger and more metal rich luminosity weighted stellar population.
This suggests that VCC 1386 contains multiple star formation episodes, and some globulars in this system are as old as, or older than, those in giant ellipticals.

gmw_00008042.overview: The galaxy 11020-1902 is the highest-mass compact galaxy in its sample [arXiv:2309.12394]. It is compatible with the mass-size relation of z~2 galaxies, making it a strong candidate for a relic galaxy that has experienced wet interacting processes [arXiv:2309.12394].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 11020-1902 as the highest-mass compact galaxy in its sample, compatible with a z=2 mass-size relation and a strong relic candidate.

quotes.quote: Only the most massive galaxy, 11020-1902, is compatible with a z 2 relation, making it the best candidate for a relic galaxy in this sample.
This galaxy is also the one with the highest mass in our sample, 11020-1902, which followed the mass-size relation of z 2 galaxies in Figure fig:Mass radius .
However, if the `late-bloomer' suffers wet interacting processes, these would trigger star-forming events, increasing the value of _ 90 , as we see for 11020-1902.

gmw_00008043.name: NGC 4136
gmw_00008043.overview: NGC 4136 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy that contains a weak central bar [arXiv:1902.09892, arXiv:astro-ph/0409622]. It is a possible member of the Coma I association or the CVnII group [arXiv:1902.09892, arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4136 as a distance and nucleus example: its nucleus is inconsistent with an 8 Mpc Hubble distance, but fits at Virgo distance and may belong to Coma I.

quotes.quote: For example, the Sc galaxy NGC 4136 with the radial velocity V_ LG =560 km/s and the apparent magnitude B=11.9^m has a distinct unresolved nucleus. With a Hubble distance of 8 Mpc, its absolute magnitude, 17.7^m , would not correspond to the presence of a noticeable nucleus (see Fig. 6). However, NGC 4136 is located in the ``Local Velocity Anomaly'' region known as Coma I (Kashibadze et al. 2018). Being actually at the Virgo cluster distance, 17 Mpc, this galaxy with M_B=-19.3^m does not seem an exception to the general pattern. Thus, NGC 4136 is a possible new member of the peculiar association Coma I numbering about 30 members.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4136 is a nearly face-on possible CVnII group member with a weak central bar and uncertain orientation and rotation measurements.

quotes.quote: According to Allsopp (1979), the galaxy is a possible member of the CVnII group. In the central part ( <15 ) there is a small bar weakly recognizable in our continuum image (Fig. ngc4136 a), but clearly seen on the CCD images given in The Digital Atlas of Nearby Galaxies by Frei et al. (1996). The galaxy is seen almost face-on, and as a result, the parameters i and PA_0 are poorly determined (see the dispersion of the estimates in Table orpar ). For this reason, the absolute value of the rotation velocity is rather uncertain. The ``troughs'' at the rotation velocity (Fig. ngc4136 d) may be just artifacts.

gmw_00008044.name: Horologium II
gmw_00008044.overview: Horologium II is a stellar system with a density center located at right ascension 49.138 and declination -50.008 [arXiv:2112.04689]. Because the system shows no evidence of mass segregation, it is most likely classified as a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2112.04689].

sources.summary: The paper adopts a revised center for Horologium II, finds no evidence for mass segregation at that position, and concludes that the system is most likely a dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: There is some confusion about the correct density centre of Horologium II. kimjerjen2015c give the position of the density centre as RA=49.13375, DEC=-50.01806, while munozetal2018 find RA=49.1077, DEC=-50.0486 with significant errors in both right ascension and declination. The stars selected as members in our HST data show a clear concentration around RA=49.138, DEC=-50.008 and we therefore adopt this position as the centre of Horologium II. With that position, we obtain R_ H,Bright /R_ H,Faint =1.05 0.11 , i.e. no evidence for mass segregation. No further information is available for the system, however given the lack of mass segregation we conclude that Horologium II is most likely a dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00008045.name: Pegasus III
gmw_00008045.overview: Pegasus III is a dwarf galaxy characterized by a large half-light radius and an elongation of the system [arXiv:2112.04689]. It is a strongly dark matter dominated system that exhibits a low metallicity, a metallicity spread, and no evidence of mass segregation [arXiv:2112.04689].

sources.summary: The paper favors Pegasus III as a dwarf galaxy because it is large, elongated, metal-poor, possibly dark-matter dominated, shows metallicity spread, and lacks mass segregation in the authors' data.

quotes.quote: The most detailed study of Pegasus III was done by kimetal2016 , who obtained Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy and Magellan/IMACS photometry of Pegasus III. They derived a large half-light radius of 54 pc and a significant elongation of the system, something that we also see in our HST photometry. Based on eight stars, they also determined a low metallicity and a stellar velocity dispersion of =5.4_ 2.5 ^ +3.0 km/sec, indicating a strongly dark matter dominated system.

Their photometry also indicated a significant metallicity spread in the system, which again would speak for a dwarf galaxy nature of Pegasus III. Our results are compatible with this conclusion since we find no evidence for mass segregation of Pegasus III. Taking all evidence together, it seems most likely that Pegasus III is a dwarf galaxy, although a tidally disrupted star cluster might still be a possible solution.

gmw_00008046.name: IC 2039
gmw_00008046.overview: IC 2039 is an early-type E-S0 galaxy [arXiv:2009.14731] that is structurally dominated by a disk [arXiv:2012.10306] and shows clear signatures of interaction with IC 2038 [arXiv:2009.14731]. It exhibits diffuse, smooth emission with centrally enhanced equivalent width [arXiv:2009.14731], as well as inner far-ultraviolet emission without corresponding H-alpha regions [arXiv:2012.10306].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2039 as an E-S0 interaction partner with diffuse smooth emission, no H-alpha regions, and a centrally enhanced equivalent width.

quotes.quote: IC 2038/IC 2039 The two galaxies are classified Scd (Type=7.0 0.5) and E-S0 (Type=-3.1 0.7), respectively. According to Cattapan2019 the galaxies show clear signatures of interaction.
In IC 2039 the \ emission is diffuse and smooth, without \ regions.
NGC 1553, IC 2039 and NGC 1533 show higher EW values at the centre, 1, 1, and 1.5, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 2039 as the faintest galaxy in the substructure, fitting it with a disk-like Sersic profile and detecting inner FUV emission without corresponding H-alpha regions.

quotes.quote: IC 2039 In FUV IC 2039 is the faintest galaxy of the sub-structure (Figure comparison_mag ). We best fit luminosity profile of IC 2039 (top right panel of Figure Tricromia_IC2038 ) with a single S ersic law with an index n=1.45 0.18 indicating that it is basically composed of a disc. The average ellipticity is =0.19 0.05 and the position angle PA =104.1^ 8.7^ to compare with optical values of 0.15 0.07 and 124.3 ^ from HyperLeda .
In IC 2039 the FUV emission is detected in the inner regions where no \ regions have been detected by Rampazzo et al. (2020) .

gmw_00008047.name: NGC 1581
gmw_00008047.overview: NGC 1581 is an early-type E-S0 galaxy that features an inner ring with small, bright H-alpha regions near its center [arXiv:2009.14731]. It is a likely companion of NGC 1566, from which it may have acquired gas during a past peri-galactic passage [arXiv:2009.14731].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1581 as an E-S0 companion of NGC 1566, with an inner ring, small H-alpha regions, and possible gas acquisition during passage.

quotes.quote: NGC 1581 This is an E-S0 galaxy (Type=-2.9 0.8 according HyperLeda ). Figure NGC1581 shows an inner ring both in the continuum and in the \ images. Small, bright \ regions are visible near the galaxy centre and on the inner ring, which is brighter in the west region of the galaxy. NGC 1581 is likely a companion of NGC 1566 Kendall2015,Oh2015 (see Discussion ).

Kendall2015 and Oh2015 suggest that NGC 1566 and NGC 1581 form a physical pair of galaxies, although their projected separation in terms of galaxy size, R _ proj /R _ 25 , is 9.7 kpc. This pair, with a relative mass ratio of 0.05 and a radial velocity difference of 96 , has high tidal parameter (a measure of the strength of the tidal interaction) according to Kendall2015 . The arm structures of NGC 1566 (Figure NGC1566 ) still show signature of an encounter that might have occurred with NGC 1581 Oh2015 , while NGC 1581 does not show obvious signatures of interaction (see Figure NGC1581 ). However, NGC 1581, which shows \ regions in the inner ring, could have acquired the gas from NGC 1566 during its peri-galactic passage.

gmw_00008048.name: GB6 J1542+6129
gmw_00008048.overview: GB6 J1542+6129 is an intermediate synchrotron peaked blazar [arXiv:2202.04363, arXiv:2112.06232] that is classified as a masquerading BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2202.04363]. The source has been associated with neutrino excesses and potential neutrino flares detected in IceCube data [arXiv:2112.06232].

sources.summary: The paper argues GB6 J1542+6129 is an IceCube-associated IBL consistent with a masquerading BL Lac, with uncertain redshift, high radio/gamma-ray power, and radio properties resembling PKS/TXS.

quotes.quote: GB6 J1542+6129, an IBL Giommi_2021 , has then appeared twice in two years in a list of 3 - 3.3 IceCube associations and is therefore also worth investigating. The redshift of this object is not available: all we know is that 0.34 z 1.76 Shaw_2013 , which translates into P_ 1.4GHz = 10^ 26.4 0.7 W Hz ^ 1 and L_ /L_ Edd = 10^ 0.3 0.9 (for M_ BH = 6.3 10^8 M_ , i.e. assuming the host galaxy to be a typical giant elliptical: Section sec:astro_data ). Both values are perfectly consistent with a masquerading BL Lac classification (Section sec:source_char ). From the data given by we derive T_ B = 1.7 - 3.6 10^ 11 K, _ app = 0.8 - 3.1 , = 4.2 - 8.7 , and = 2.3 - 5.0 for the adopted redshift range. and are therefore also relatively small, which suggests that GB6 J1542+6129 shares similar radio properties with the other two sources.

sources.summary: The paper treats GB6 J1542+6129 as a bright IHBL blazar associated with IceCube point-source and flare searches and similar SEDs to other candidates.

quotes.quote: A search for point sources in the IceCube 10 year data collection, which includes events with energy typically 1 TeV, found an excess of neutrinos at the 2.9 level from the direction of the local ( z = 0.004 ) Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1068 and a 3.3 excess in the northern sky due to significant p-values in the directions of NGC 1068 and three blazars: TXS 0506+056, PKS 1424+240, and GB6 J1542+6129 2020PhRvL.124e1103A .

A recent investigation aimed at the detection of flare neutrino emission in the 10 year IceCube data set reported a time-dependent neutrino excess in the northern hemisphere at the level of 3 associated with four sources: M87, a giant radio galaxy, TXS 0506+056 and GB6 J1542+6129, two IBL blazars, and NGC 1068.

A very recent search for neutrino flare emission in the IceCube 10 year data set Abbasi2021 reported possible flares at the level of 3 from NGC 1068, two IHBL blazars TXS 0506+056, GB6 J1542+6129, and M87, a giant radio galaxy with properties similar to that of IHBL objects that was already noticed as a possible counterpart of the track IceCube-141126A Giommidissecting .

gmw_00008049.name: host galaxy of GRB 180418A
gmw_00008049.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 180418A is a faint galaxy detected in the r, i, and z bands [arXiv:2012.09961]. It is located at an angular offset of only 0.16 arcseconds from the optical afterglow position of the gamma-ray burst [arXiv:2012.09961].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint galaxy coincident with GRB 180418A's afterglow as the host, measuring its position, magnitude, small offset, and low chance-coincidence probability from late-time imaging.

quotes.quote: In Gemini imaging at t 289 days, we identify a faint galaxy at RA= 11 20 29.21 and Dec= + 24 55 58.73 (J2000), coincident with the Chandra and Gemini afterglow positions (Figure fig:optag ). We perform aperture photometry using the IRAF/ phot package as previously described and measure a brightness of = 25.69 0.21 mag. The galaxy is at an angular offset from the optical afterglow position of 0.16 0.04'' . Using this offset and the band magnitude (Table tab:gb_obs ), we calculate the probability of chance coincidence following the methods of bkd02 to be P_ cc =1.4 10^ 3 .

The low value of P_ cc , coupled with the fact that there are no other detectable >3 sources within 7.5'' of the afterglow position to 26 mag, solidifies this source as the host galaxy. The host galaxy is detected in riz bands, and the photometry results are in Table tab:gb_obs . We also obtain a deep upper limit with MMT/Binospec observations in g band of g 25.7 mag.

gmw_00008050.name: JADES-GS-z6-0
gmw_00008050.overview: JADES-GS-z6-0 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2402.05996]. Although a 2175 Å UV-bump detection was previously reported for the galaxy, a subsequent full-spectrum fit resulted in a non-detection of this feature [arXiv:2402.05996].

sources.summary: The paper notes a reported 2175 A UV-bump detection in JADES-GS-z6-0, but its own DAWN full-spectrum fit finds no significant bump and flags pipeline or model differences as possible causes.

quotes.quote: Witstok et al. (2023) reported a UV bump detection (with a 4 ) in the JADES-GS-z6-0 galaxy, achieved by fitting an excess attenuation at 2175 A with a Drude profile. However, applying our method to the full spectrum of JADES-GS-z6-0 from DAWN resulted in a non-detection, with a UV bump strength of B = 0.239 0.213 or c_4 = 0.032_ 0.027 ^ +0.038 .

For s00717 the data reduction was performed with the standard pipeline , whereas JADES-GS-z6-0 was reduced by a custom version of the pipeline . The data in this work are reduced with the MSAEXP custom code . The different pipeline versions have different sampling, resulting in slightly different flux densities, uncertainties, and channel widths, which can create inconsistencies (for s00717 and JADES-GS-z6-0), as well as various fitting procedures involving different models (for JADES-GS-z6-0).

gmw_00008051.name: NGC 1302
gmw_00008051.overview: NGC 1302 is an early late-type galaxy that possesses a bar, an inner ring, and an outer ring [arXiv:2304.00515]. The galaxy features an outer neutral hydrogen ring with mild tail-like extensions, which may indicate gas accretion or interaction [arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1302 as a barred ring galaxy with a prominent outer HI ring, mild tail-like extensions, and possible interaction or gas accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 1302: The optical DSS image and the 1 velocity map for NGC 1302 is shown in the top right panels of Fig. fig:mosaic1 . The 1 contours are overlaid on top of the DSS optical image. It is a more early late-type galaxy and is observed to posses a bar, an inner ring as well as an outer ring. In find that this galaxy also shows a very prominent outer 1 ring corresponding to the outer ring observed in the optical image. The both the 1 contours and the velocity map, we see that the 1 distribution does not appear to be too disturbed. However, we do find evidence of tail like extensions towards the North-East and South-West ends of the galaxy. This may be alluding to some form of interaction or gas accretion associated with the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1302 as a clear evolved-bar case, using radial velocity-dispersion profiles and elevated bar-disk dispersion differences as evidence for vertical bar growth.

quotes.quote: Clear instances of evolved bars are the ones in NGC 1302, NGC 1317 and NGC 5850. Their _z radial profiles resemble Fig(s). 5(b) and 6(d). These galaxies have _ z, bar equal to 100, 145 and 60 Km/s. Although the latter value is clearly much lower, this is a result from the fact that NGC 5850 is of a later morphological class than the former galaxies. In fact, _z 30 Km/s in the three cases, an evidence that these bars have vertically grown up from their parent disks.

gmw_00008052.name: NGC 1440
gmw_00008052.overview: NGC 1440 is a galaxy featuring a vertically thick, evolved bar structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0504646]. Its nucleus likely hosts a compact, unresolved nuclear star cluster with stellar-like light and very little emission [arXiv:2308.03913].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1440 as an evolved, vertically thick bar case despite low _z values, attributing the apparent contradiction to early-type bulge contamination.

quotes.quote: The behavior of _z in NGC 1387 and NGC 1440 shows a relatively steep fall from the center outward in both axes, and their _z values are low, presumably indicating young bars. However, their values for _ z, bar are too high to be the case of young bars. These are certainly vertically thick and evolved bars, resembling the schema of Fig. 6(). These are typical cases where the early--type bulge presents a very high velocity dispersion. The low values of _z may likely be explained by light contamination in the bar minor axis from the bulge and the bar even at the outermost spectra.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1440 as having stellar-like unresolved nuclear light, probably a compact nuclear star cluster whose Sersic radius is only an upper limit.

quotes.quote: On the other hand in NGC 2775 and NGC 1440 the nuclear light appears to be stellar; specifically, the nuclear sources in both have very similar colors to the surrounding galaxy light, and the nuclear spectra ho1995 shows very little emission.
Therefore it is likely these two galaxies host compact NSCs that we just cannot resolve with HST.
The best-fit S ersic r_e are used here as upper limits; for NGC 2775 and NGC 1440 these are 3.87 pc and 4.65 pc or 0 042 and 0 048.

gmw_00008053.name: NGC 2665
gmw_00008053.overview: NGC 2665 is a barred galaxy that features a characteristic R1-type outer stellar ring [arXiv:0910.0757]. This ring exhibits dimples near the direction of the bar's major axis, giving the structure a shape reminiscent of a figure eight [arXiv:0910.0757].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2665 as an R1 outer-ring example, emphasizing bar-axis dimples and a figure-eight ring shape that the manifold model reproduces qualitatively.

quotes.quote: NGC 2665, ESO 325-28 and ESO 507-16, shown in Fig. fig:family (second to fourth column of the top row), are prime examples of these three types of outer rings. Note that NGC 2665 includes a characteristic feature of R_1 rings. Namely at or near the direction of the bar major axis, the ring approaches the ends of the bar. The features thus formed are called dimples Buta86, Buta.Crocker91, Buta95 and give to these outer rings a characteristic shape reminiscent of the number 8.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 2665 as a doubtful case where uncertain outer minor-axis dispersions may hide a young bar behind high bar dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 2665 is a similar case, and the very low and uncertain _z estimates in the farthest points along the bar minor axis may be occulting a young bar behind a high value for _z . In fact, the _z estimates along the bar minor axis do not agree in the outermost points from each side of the galaxy center. Good spectra taken at farther galactocentric distances certainly avoid and clarify these doubtful cases.

gmw_00008054.name: NGC 5383
gmw_00008054.overview: NGC 5383 is a regular-disk galaxy [arXiv:1907.03574] that hosts a recently formed, vertically thin bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0504646]. The galaxy exhibits an irregular metallicity distribution, including a lower-metallicity region along its spiral arm [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: NGC 5383 is a regular-disk Type Ia host with a central H-flux exception and an irregular metallicity map containing a lower-metallicity spiral-arm region.

quotes.quote: According to Fig.  fig.SNIa-havd , the H velocity gradient of the host galaxies of all the four Type Ia SNe 2007sw (UGC 7228), 2006iq (PGC 1380172), 2007R (PGC 21767) and 2005cc (NGC 5383) are smooth without irregularities. There are some spaxels that have a higher in the host galaxies and they distribute in the outer regions of the field of view. We infer that this may be caused by the low signal-to-noise (S/N) for the spaxels in the outer region. Roughly, the host galaxies of all the SNe Ia only have one peak of and the peaks locate at the center of the host galaxies. Therefore, all the SNe Ia host galaxies here are almost RD.
From Fig.  fig.SNIa-havd , we can see that the H flux of these four galaxies have the highest value in the center except for the host galaxy of SN 2005cc (NGC 5383).

The host galaxy of 2005cc (NGC 5383) shows an irregular distribution of metallicity. There is a spindly region where presents apparently lower metallicity, which is believed to be where the spiral arm locates.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5383 as having a recently formed, vertically thin bar, with low bar velocity dispersion and radial profiles matching expected young-bar behavior.

quotes.quote: One can say with a certain degree of confidence that NGC 1326, NGC 4394 and NGC 5383 harbor recently formed bars. Their results resemble what we would expect based on Fig(s). 5(a) and 6(a) (for NGC 1326 and NGC 5383) and 6(b) (for NGC 4394). Moreover, _ z, bar < 40 Km/s and _z < 10 Km/s (although we do not have this parameter for NGC 5383), that in fact point to these bars as being still a vertically thin structure, belonging to the plane of the disk.

gmw_00008055.name: IRAS F18341-5732
gmw_00008055.overview: IRAS F18341-5732 is an isolated galaxy that is not currently undergoing any interactions [arXiv:1204.5520, arXiv:1509.08468]. Its nucleus is dominated by a LINER and an aging stellar population, while star formation occurs in knots along its spiral arms and where the arms meet the bar [arXiv:1204.5520, arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: IRAS F18341-5732 is described as an isolated galaxy with LINER-dominated nuclear spectra, an aging stellar population, and bar/spiral-arm star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18341-5732 (IC 4734) Like IRAS F17222-5953, our second isolated galaxy is in the vicinity of a few other luminous galaxies, but is not yet undergoing any interactions. Our nuclear spectra are dominated by a LINER combined with an aging stellar population. The strongest sights of star formation are where the bar in this galaxy meet the spiral arms evidenced by the two strong clumps of HII-region like points seen in the line-ratio and metallicity. imaging by Dopita02 shows further knots of star formation along the spiral arms and our nuclear spectra also show signs of an aging stellar population in the nucleus of IC 4734.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4734 as isolated, not yet interacting, with LINER-like nuclear spectra, an aging stellar population, and bar-arm star-forming clumps.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18341-5732 (IC 4734) Like IRAS F17222-5953, this isolated galaxy is in the vicinity of a few other luminous galaxies, but is not yet undergoing any interactions. Our nuclear spectra are dominated by a LINER combined with an aging stellar population. The strongest sites of star formation are where the bar in this galaxy meet the spiral arms, evidenced by the two strong clumps of region like spaxels seen in the line-ratio and metallicity. imaging by Dopita02 shows further knots of star formation along the spiral arms and our nuclear spectra also show signs of an aging stellar population in the nucleus of IC 4734.

gmw_00008056.name: IC 4687
gmw_00008056.overview: IC 4687 is a luminous infrared starburst galaxy [arXiv:1601.02427, arXiv:1504.02724] that is part of an interacting triplet, undergoing a close merger with IC 4686 and a wider merger with IC 4689 [arXiv:1204.5520, arXiv:1509.08468]. The galaxy features a slightly inclined disc with a spherical nucleus [arXiv:2104.06790], and its circumnuclear region is dominated by several star-forming clumps [arXiv:1504.02724].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 4687 as a local luminous infrared starburst, finding efficient 250 pc-scale star formation, high infrared luminosity, a weak possible AGN, and weak interaction with IC 4686 and IC 4689.

quotes.quote: We analyze the spatially resolved (250 pc scales) and integrated star formation (SF) law in the local luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) IC 4687. This is one of the first studies of the SF law on a starburst LIRG at these small spatial scales. We combined new interferometric ALMA CO(2--1) data with existing HST NICMOS Pa narrowband imaging and VLT SINFONI near-IR integral field spectroscopy to obtain accurate extinction-corrected SF rate (SFR) and cold molecular gas surface densities ( _ gas and _ SFR ). We find that IC 4687 forms stars very efficiently with an average depletion time ( t_ dep ) of 160 Myr for the individual 250 pc regions.

In particular, we study the local ( d =74 Mpc; 345 pc arcsec ^ 1 ) luminous IR galaxy (LIRG) IC 4687. This galaxy has an IR luminosity of 10 ^ 11.3 , which corresponds to an integrated SFR of 30 yr ^ 1 Pereira2011 . Although, the energy output of IC 4687 is dominated by SF, a weak AGN with a contribution < 5 \ to the total IR luminosity, is possibly present AAH2012a . IC 4687 forms part of an interacting group together with IC 4686 and IC 4689, which are 10 and 20 kpc away from IC 4687, respectively.

sources.summary: IC 4687 is used as a star-forming LIRG prototype whose Br emission traces circumnuclear clumps, with diffuse H2 and FeII following the clumpy Br distribution.

quotes.quote: IC 4687: prototype of LIRG dominated by star formation IC 4687 can be considered as the prototype of star-forming LIRG with its Br emission (Fig.2 left upper panel) dominated by several circumnuclear star-forming clumps, and a weak Br nucleus, which is also identified as weak Pa emitter on HST imaging AlonsoHerrero:2006p4703 . The H _2 emission (Fig.2 central upper panel) appears very diffuse and distributed over the entire circumnuclear region, with an above-average nucleus, and presenting the highest surface brightness. Finally, the [ FeII ] emission (Fig.2 right upper panel) follows that of the Br clumpy light distribution.

The overall distribution of the line ratios in the [ FeII ] 1.64 m/Br H _2 2.12 m/Br plane (Fig. figure:IC4687 , lower left panel) is such that the mode of the line ratios is outside the previously identified region for star-forming galaxies Riffel:2013MNRAS429 but with a long tail towards low [ FeII ] 1.64 m/Br (0.6) H _2 /Br ratios (down to 0.15), characteristic of star-forming regions dominated by young, ionizing stars as in M82 ( ForsterSchreiber:2001ApJ552 ; see also Fig. figure:prot ). The bright circumnuclear Br regions (blue points/regions in Fig. figure:IC4687 ) identify young star-forming regions with [ FeII ] /Br and H _2 /Br values well below 1. The nuclear region (red points/regions in Fig. figure:IC4687 ) appears as a weak and resolved ionizing source (as traced by the Pa and Br lines), with no evidence of an AGN ( AlonsoHerrero:2006p4703 , AlonsoHerrero:2012p744 , PereiraSantaella:2011fw ), and is identified with evolved, supernova dominated, star-forming regions (Pereira-Santaella et al. in prep).

The diffuse, line-emitting gas (white points/region in Fig. figure:IC4687 ) has [ FeII ] /Br ratios in between those of young and supernovae dominated star-forming regions, while the H _2 /Br ratio is more consistent with that of supernovae dominated regions.

sources.summary: IC 4687 is classified as a close merger in a triplet, interacting with IC 4686 and IC 4689, with HST morphology and IFU metallicities tracing gas from multiple systems.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18093-5744 N (IC 4687) We classify this galaxy as a close merger; in fact it is a member of a triplet. IC 4687 is undergoing a close merger with the less massive starburst IC 4686, classed as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy by Kovo99,Fernandes04 . IC 4687 itself is a is also in a wide merger with the equally massive spiral IC 4689. The archived Hubble Space Telescope images of IC 4687 show a complex morphology tangled up with IC 4686: gas and dust from IC 4687 appear to be obscuring the less massive system. Our IFU data covers the entirety of IC 4686/4687 and the metallicities we measure are consistent with the expected metallicties in the outskirts of IC 4687 as extrapolated from the gradient we present in this paper as well as a low-metallicity, flattened gradient in IC 4686.
The kinematic information from our data also indicates that we are indeed seeing gas from both systems.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4687 as part of an interacting system, spatially resolved in spectral mapping, with line emission associated with nuclear regions and locally enhanced line ratios.

quotes.quote: IC 4687 is part of an interacting system (IC 4686/IC 4687). Only IC 4687 was observed in the spectral mapping mode. In the emission line map it is not possible to isolate any region of interest, however the emission is spatially resolved. We detect the \ line, that seems to be associated to the nuclear \ regions seen in the Pa image. Two of these regions have slightly larger \ ratio than the rest of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4687 as a close merger with IC 4686 and a wider merger with IC 4689, with complex morphology, obscuring gas and dust, and mixed gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18093-5744 N (IC 4687) We classify this galaxy as a close merger; in fact it is a member of a triplet. IC 4687 is undergoing a close merger with the less massive starburst IC 4686, classed as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy by Kovo99,Fernandes04 . IC 4687 itself is a is also in a wide merger with the equally massive spiral IC 4689. The archived hst images of IC 4687 show a complex morphology tangled up with IC 4686: gas and dust from IC 4687 appear to be obscuring the less massive system. Our IFU data cover the entirety of IC 4686/4687 and the metallicities we measure are consistent with the expected metallicties in the outskirts of IC 4687 as extrapolated from the gradient we present in this paper as well as a low-metallicity, flattened gradient in IC 4686.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4687 as part of an interacting group, with uncertain PA/inclination from faint arms and bright regions, a spherical nucleus, inclined disc, and arm-related dispersion structure.

quotes.quote: IC 4687 : This galaxy is part of an interacting group together with IC 4686 and IC 4689, which are at 10 kpc and 20 kpc SW. Its faint arms along with the presence of bright regions in the FoV yield large uncertainties in the estimation of the PA and inclination. Despite this, one can observe a well-defined spherical nucleus ( i 20 , <0.4 kpc) and a slightly inclined disc ( i 40 ). The velocity dispersion map shows a flat distribution, with lower values along the arm structure likely produced by the SF regions populating the arms. The isovelocity lines in the nuclear region of this galaxy are nearly parallel to the minor axis, yielding a less restricted inclination determination, which is biased towards low values by DiskFit .

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4689 as a less massive, intensely star-forming spiral whose metallicity gradient is already flattened despite less disturbed morphology.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18093-5744 S (IC 4689) This spiral galaxy is slightly less massive and luminous than IC 4687 and less well-observed, though it is still intensely star forming Howell10 . It is less morphologically disturbed than the other two interacting galaxies IC 4686/4687, though its gradient is quite flattened already according to our observations Rich12 . Although it is widely separated from IC 4687, we include it as part of the closely interacting system of IRAS F18093-5744.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4686 as a compact Wolf-Rayet starburst with a strong moderate component and possibly unresolved overlapping kinematics.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18093-5744 C (IC 4686) IC 4686 is a compact strongly star-bursting galaxy with Wolf-Rayet features in its spectrum Kovo99,Fernandes04 . As noted in Rich12 , this galaxy shows a strong moderate component with lower line ratios. This may be due to the very compact nature, causing an overlap in the kinematics that may require a higher-component line fit or higher resolution spectroscopy than have been considered in this thesis.

gmw_00008057.name: IC 4689
gmw_00008057.overview: IC 4689 is a spiral galaxy that is part of the closely interacting IRAS F18093-5744 system [arXiv:1204.5520]. Although it is less morphologically disturbed than its system companions, it is intensely star-forming and exhibits a flattened gradient [arXiv:1204.5520].

sources.summary: IC 4689 is characterized as a less massive, intensely star-forming spiral with less morphological disturbance than IC 4686/4687 but an already flattened gradient.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18093-5744 S (IC 4689) This spiral galaxy is slightly less massive and luminous than IC 4687 and less well-observed, though it is still intensely star forming Howell10 . It is less morphologically disturbed than the other two interacting galaxies IC 4686/4687, though its gradient is quite flattened already according to our observations. Although it is widely separated from IC 4687, we include it as part of the closely interacting system of IRAS F18093-5744.

gmw_00008058.name: NGC 4449B
gmw_00008058.overview: NGC 4449B is a dwarf satellite galaxy undergoing accretion by the dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 4449 [arXiv:1603.04656]. It exhibits an S-shaped morphology characteristic of a disrupting satellite on its first passage near its host [arXiv:1603.04656].

sources.summary: The chapter identifies NGC 4449B with the d1228+4358 feature as a disrupting dwarf satellite being accreted by NGC 4449, with S-shaped morphology and first-passage tidal debris.

quotes.quote: One of the many probable dwarf galaxies dwarf galaxies discovered by karachentsev2007 was an elongated feature near the dwarf irregular galaxy NGC 4449 NGC 4449 in Digitized Sky Survey (POSS-II) plates (denoted as object ``d1228+4358'' in their catalog).

Using deep, wide-field imaging around NGC 4449 NGC 4449 , MD2012 definitively identified the Karachentsev et al. feature as a dwarf galaxy dwarf galaxiessatellites of undergoing accretion by NGC 4449 (see Figure n4449 ). This new dwarf galaxy was also seen by rich2012 in a similar deep-imaging survey, which revealed the dwarf (dubbed NGC 4449B) and its S shaped morphology that is characteristic of disrupting satellites.

The morphology, size, luminosity, and surface brightness profile of the newly discovered stream/dwarf, along with evidence of tidal features in the NGC 4449 halo and outer disk, was suggested by rich2012 to result from a dwarf (NGC 4449B) that is on its first passage, and passed near the center of its host 10^8 yr ago.

gmw_00008059.name: NGC 5387
gmw_00008059.overview: NGC 5387 is a Milky Way-like spiral galaxy that has experienced a recent accretion event [arXiv:1603.04656]. This interaction produced a red stellar stream and a blue, star-forming overdensity where the stream crosses the galaxy's disk [arXiv:1603.04656].

sources.summary: The chapter describes NGC 5387 as a Milky-Way-like galaxy with a recent accretion event, a red stellar stream, and a blue star-forming overdensity where the stream crosses the disk.

quotes.quote: One example of a burst of star formation in a Milky Way-like galaxy with an associated recent accretion event is seen in NGC 5387 NGC 5387 note that NGC 5387 can be considered a Milky Way analog based on its properties, but contains about an order of magnitude less mass than the MW beaton2014 . This system shows a low surface-brightness surface brightness feature in SDSS Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) imaging, that was explored with deeper imaging by Beaton et al., who found a stellar stream extending over at least an entire orbital wrap, with median surface brightness of 24.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 in R band (see Figure n5387 ). This stream has a redder color than the typical stellar populations of NGC 5387, and contains a total stellar mass of 6 10^8 M_ . Coincident with the position of the stream's crossing of the NGC 5387 disk is a ``blue overdensity'' that is not only blue in optical colors, but is producing a large FUV flux as well. Evidence from the FUV flux as well as follow-up spectra obtained by beaton2014 suggests a very recent ( 8 Myr ago) star formation event of total stellar mass 2.5 1.3 10^7 M_ .

gmw_00008060.name: UGC11648
gmw_00008060.overview: UGC11648 is classified as a low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:2305.01127]. Analysis of its rotation curve indicates that its dark matter halo strongly supports a multistate model, despite a poor fit to the observational data [arXiv:2305.01127].

sources.summary: In the low-surface-brightness rotation-curve analysis, UGC11648 is an exception: the multistate fit is poor and its likelihood value increases slightly, yet the Bayes-factor comparison strongly supports the multistate case.

quotes.quote: Although we obtain an acceptable parameter inference for all the data sample according to the gray contours, we can observe that rotational curves for galaxies UGC11648 and UGC11748 the multistate case does not fit the data too well.

As for the 2 L value, it is noticed that for all galaxies is bigger for the excited state ( _ 210 ) and the multistate case smaller than the ground state, except for galaxy UGC11648 with a small increase.
Some galaxies stand out, UGC11616 with a (B_ 12 ) =-12.40 that supports the ground state and, galaxies UGC11648 and UGC11454 strongly supporting the multistate case.

gmw_00008061.name: UGC11748
gmw_00008061.overview: UGC11748 is classified as a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:2305.01127]. Its observed rotation curve is poorly fitted by a multistate dark matter halo model [arXiv:2305.01127].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC11748 as a poor rotation-curve fit that does not follow the multistate model behavior, while a plus-marker Bayes-factor entry shows a larger value for the multistate case.

quotes.quote: Although we obtain an acceptable parameter inference for all the data sample according to the gray contours, we can observe that rotational curves for galaxies UGC11648 and UGC11748 the multistate case does not fit the data too well.

Galaxies with an asterisk ( ) have smaller (B_ 12 ) for the _ 210 case and those (UGC11748) with a plus marker ( + ) have bigger (B_ 12 ) for the multistate case, that doesn't appear in the figure and can be consulted in table tab:model_comparison .
Although, there are some galaxies like UGC11648 and UGC11748, that are barely fitted but clearly they don't follow the model behavior.

gmw_00008062.name: LAE J1044 0130
gmw_00008062.overview: LAE J1044 0130 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 5.687 [arXiv:astro-ph/0312228]. It is identified as a probable superwind galaxy, displaying a triangular emission-line morphology and a broad red-wing emission profile that suggest active superwind activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0312228].

sources.summary: The review describes LAE J1044 0130 as a z=5.687 probable superwind galaxy with a truncated Ly emission profile, broad red wing, and triangular emission-line morphology.

quotes.quote: LAE J1044 0130 is identified as a probable superwind galaxy at z=5.687 0.002 . Its emission line profile is strongly truncated at wavelengths blueward shortward the line peak while shows red-wing emission. The observed broad line width, FWHM (full width at half maximum) 340 km s ^ 1 as well as the red wing emission suggest that this object is experiencing the superwind activity. The emission-line morphology appears to show a triangle shape. This may be also interpreted in terms of the superwind activity.

gmw_00008063.name: LAE J1044 0123
gmw_00008063.overview: LAE J1044 0123 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.655 [arXiv:astro-ph/0312228]. It has an estimated star formation rate of 13 solar masses per year and a lower-limit dynamical mass of 2 billion solar masses [arXiv:astro-ph/0312228].

sources.summary: The review describes LAE J1044 0123 as a z=5.655 star-forming dwarf galaxy with modest Ly velocity dispersion, star formation, and an estimated lower-limit dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: LAE J1044 0123 is identified as a star forming galaxy at z=5.655 0.002 with a star formation rate of 13 h_ 0.7 ^ 2 M_ yr ^ 1 . Remarkably, the velocity dispersion of Ly emitting gas is only 22 km s ^ 1 . Since a blue half of the Ly emission could be absorbed by neutral hydrogen gas, perhaps in the system, a modest estimate of the velocity dispersion may be 44 km s ^ 1 . Together with a linear size of 7.7 h_ 0.7 ^ 1 kpc, we estimate a lower limit of the dynamical mass of this object to be 2 10^9 M_ . Therefore, LAE J1044 0123 seems to be a star-forming dwarf galaxy (i.e., a subgalactic object or a building block).

gmw_00008064.name: RX J0057
gmw_00008064.overview: RX J0057 is a quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.2 [arXiv:1501.02095]. It exhibits a high bolometric active galactic nucleus luminosity, small optical obscuration, an Eddington ratio of around 0.1, and is undetected in FIRST radio observations [arXiv:1501.02095].

sources.summary: RX J0057 is presented as a z~2.2 QSO with small optical obscuration, high bolometric AGN luminosity, no FIRST detection, and an Eddington ratio around 0.1.

quotes.quote: From the SED shapes and template fits, we see some differences in the properties of the objects in our sample: RX J0057 and RX J1249 have similar redshifts z 2.2 , small optical obscurations, the highest bolometric AGN luminosities and a preference for the first torus model in . RX J0941 and RX J1218 have again similar redshifts z 1.8 , higher optical obscurations, the lowest bolometric AGN luminosities and also allow for the empirical and the second torus models. Finally, RX J1633 has the highest redshift z=2.8 , small optical obscuration, intermediate bolometric AGN luminosity and a strong preference for the third torus model in . This results in a higher torus contribution at longer wavelengths, intuitively corresponding to a higher probability of further reprocessing of the direct emission at larger distances and lower temperatures.

Additional evidence for a (relative) higher inclination of RX J1633 comes from its radio morphology in FIRST images, which shows two diffuse blobs roughly symmetrical with respect to the optical position of the QSO, in contrast with the rest of our objects, which are either not detected (RX J0057 and RX J1218) or pointlike (RX J0941 and RX J1249). The host galaxies of RX J0941 and RX J1218 seem to be growing much faster than their black holes, both in absolute terms and compared to the other objects. This is because their AGN are less luminous but have similar SFR to the rest. The difference in AGN luminosities between the objects could be ascribed to the redshift, since those further away are also more luminous, as expected in a flux-limited sample, but this is belied by the intermediate luminosity of the highest redshift object RX J1633, which is also the one with the most discordant torus properties. In any case, with such a small sample it is impossible to assess the significance and implications of the differences observed.

From Table discu , three of our QSOs have Eddington ratios around 0.1 (RX J0057, RX J0941 and RX J1218), one has a value around 0.6 (RX J1249) and the last one is closer to unity (RX J1633), although with large error bars, specially in the last two. This range of values is compatible with that found by , who have studied a sample of 58000 Type 1 QSO, also finding that _ EDD 0.1 are significantly rarer at z 4 compared to z 2 , while objects with _ EDD 0.1 are found similarly in both redshift ranges. This is also similar to what we see in our sample, where the four objects with z 2 are compatible with _ EDD 0.1 , while RX J1633 at z=2.8 shows some preference for higher values of _ EDD . They conclude that Type 1 quasars radiating near the Eddington limited are extremely rare, suggesting that Type 1 quasars violating the Eddington limit do so only for a very brief period of time. RX J1633 could be in this situation.

gmw_00008067.name: RX J1249
gmw_00008067.overview: RX J1249 is a quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.2 [arXiv:1501.02095] that exhibits a pointlike radio morphology [arXiv:1501.02095]. It is classified as a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1204.6048] and displays absorption line variability on a rest-frame timescale of eight days [arXiv:1204.6048].

sources.summary: The paper reports CIV BAL variability in 1246 0542 on an 8-day rest-frame timescale, with two variable velocity intervals, repeated changes across epochs, and a cloud-motion interpretation placing gas near 1 pc from the SMBH.

quotes.quote: In our dataset, we found variability down to a t of 8 days in 1246 0542 between the 2010.13 and 2010.20 observations. Fig. sp1246 shows the CIV region of the spectra for these two observations as bold and thin lines, respectively. As indicated by shaded bars in Fig. sp1246 , there are two separate velocity intervals of variability between the two spectra, and the flux difference is at least 8 in each interval. We also overplot an earlier (2007.04; thin dashed curve) and a later (2010.35; bold dashed curve) epoch in Fig. sp1246 , which shows that there was variability in these intervals in other epochs as well.

If the short time-scale variations in 1246 0542 were due to cloud movements and a 10 change in line strength over 8 days indicates the cloud crossed 10 of the continuum source in that time, then the transverse speed is 27 000 km/s. If we assume the transverse speed is related to the Keplarian rotation speed, then the outflowing gas is located 1 pc from the central SMBH, probably inside the radius of the broad emission-line region (Paper 3).

sources.summary: RX J1249 is presented as a z~2.2 QSO with small optical obscuration, among the highest AGN luminosities, pointlike FIRST morphology, and an Eddington ratio around 0.6.

quotes.quote: From the SED shapes and template fits, we see some differences in the properties of the objects in our sample: RX J0057 and RX J1249 have similar redshifts z 2.2 , small optical obscurations, the highest bolometric AGN luminosities and a preference for the first torus model in . In any case, with such a small sample it is impossible to assess the significance and implications of the differences observed.

sources.summary: RX J1633 is described as the highest-redshift QSO in the sample, with intermediate AGN luminosity, a preferred cold torus model, diffuse FIRST morphology, and near-Eddington accretion.

gmw_00008069.overview: 0811+5007 is a compact broad-absorption line quasar that exhibits a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:1507.00998]. The source displays flux variability, has been detected in X-rays without clear neutral hydrogen absorption, and has recently shown broad absorption line disappearance [arXiv:1507.00998].

sources.summary: The paper reports 0811+5007 as a compact BAL quasar with a flat radio spectrum, flux variability, X-ray detection without clear neutral absorption, and reported BAL disappearance.

quotes.quote: Simple linear models have been fitted to the spectra of four our sources by dipompeo2011 revealing a flat spectral index ( < 0.5 ) in the cases of 0811+5007 and 0856+4242, and a steep spectral index for resolved sources 0842+0631 and 1040+0555.

Three of the BALQSOs from the VLBI sample, namely the 0811+5007, 1401+5208, and 2107-0620, also show flux variability and are listed in Table variable . A wide range of viewing angles is estimated in their case. We have already mentioned that the lack of X-ray detection of 2107-0620 can be caused by the presence of the shielding gas wang2008 . There are no X-ray observations of 1401+5007, but the 0811+5007 has been detected in X-rays showing no clear evidence for X-ray absorption from neutral hydrogen. As an explanation, wang2008 suggested that if absorption is present in this source it must be more complex or that the X-rays come from the radio jet outside the shielding gas. Interestingly, the broad absorption line disappearance has recently been reported in 0811+5007 filiz2012 .

gmw_00008070.overview: The active galactic nucleus 2107-0620 is a compact broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1507.00998]. It remains unresolved at 5 GHz, exhibits flux variability, and displays X-ray weakness that may be caused by shielding gas [arXiv:1507.00998].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2107-0620 as an unresolved compact BAL quasar, unlike most of the VLBI sample, with flux variability and X-ray weakness suggesting shielding gas.

quotes.quote: We detected all the objects, but three of them (0856+4242, 2107-0620, 2238+0016) still remain unresolved at 5 GHz. The resolved ones show one-sided morphology probably indicating the core-jet type of structure (Fig. images ), typical for quasars. The small linear sizes ( 1 kpc ) of the observed quasars and their high luminosity (Table sample ) may indicate the young age of these AGNs.
The one exception is the unresolved source 2107-0620. The low value of log R_ I and its X-ray weakness wang2008 may imply that this source is viewed close to the plane of the accretion disk.

Three of the BALQSOs from the VLBI sample, namely the 0811+5007, 1401+5208, and 2107-0620, also show flux variability and are listed in Table variable . A wide range of viewing angles is estimated in their case. We have already mentioned that the lack of X-ray detection of 2107-0620 can be caused by the presence of the shielding gas wang2008 .

gmw_00008071.overview: The active galaxy 0847+1824 is classified as an extreme changing-look object that demonstrated a Seyfert type transition of magnitude 3 [arXiv:1603.00035]. It exhibits extended emission offset from the galaxy center, which may indicate an ongoing merger [arXiv:1603.00035].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0847+1824 as an extreme changing-look object whose broad H line became hard to identify, shifted strongly in Seyfert type, and may involve off-center emission.

quotes.quote: For three other objects (0932+0405, 0847+1824, and 0831+0521), the broad H component could not be accurately identified in the Keck spectra. These four objects are excluded from any discussion involving the H line fitting.
One object (0847+1824) demonstrated a type transition of magnitude 3.

Objects 0847+1824 and 1038+4658 show extended emission in the SDSS multi-color images, offset from the galaxy center (Fig. appendix:sdss ), which might indicate the presence of an ongoing merger. While we cannot exclude the possibility that these are off-center AGNs, it is statistically unlikely to have off-centered AGNs in such a small sample, and follow-up spectroscopy would be needed to test such a scenario further. We note in passing that 0847+1824 seems to show a small kinematic offset between its narrow and broad H line (with the broad H line being blue-shifted by 100km s ^ 1 ), which is, however, most likely mimicked by the asymmetric broad line profile.

gmw_00008072.name: J202151+423847
gmw_00008072.overview: J202151+423847 is a double-lobed candidate radio galaxy with two possible infrared host associations [arXiv:2108.01505]. Its radio morphology reveals inner knots that may represent old bipolar jets or indicate a double-double radio galaxy structure [arXiv:2108.01505].

sources.summary: The paper describes J202151+423847 as a double-lobed candidate with possible infrared host associations, inner radio knots, old bipolar jets, and possible double-double radio-galaxy morphology.

quotes.quote: J202151+423847 ( 10). There are two possible associations at infrared wavelengths for this source: WISEA J202150.84 + 423846.9 and 2MASS J20215131 + 4238521, coincident with the position of the geometric centre. Besides, the radio image at 610 MHz reveals two knots in-between the IR sources and the lobes (see Figs. fig:galaxitas2 and fig:IR+radio ). These knots could be part of common bipolar jets (an unseen weak bridge) formed time ago, of an AGN with much activity in the past. The morphology could be also indicative of DDRG, as two double radio galaxies that share a centre.

gmw_00008073.name: J202919 + 441341
gmw_00008073.overview: J202919 + 441341 is a radio source with a complex intensity distribution across both observed radio bands [arXiv:2108.01505]. It is likely a double-lobed radio galaxy where projection effects play an important role, and it has no known associations at other wavelengths [arXiv:2108.01505].

sources.summary: The paper treats J202919 + 441341 as a complex double-lobed source with no multiwavelength associations, likely affected by projection, and unlikely to be a one-lobe/core system.

quotes.quote: J202919 + 441341 ( 27). There are no associations at other wavelengths. The intensity distribution at both radio bands is complex. We may be looking at a double-lobed source, where projection effects play an important role, see Fig. fig:galaxitas3 . The possibility that we were looking at just one lobe (to the north) and that the southern component is the core seems unlikely, since this latter source do not present the flat spectral index expected from cores.

gmw_00008074.name: J203201 + 413730
gmw_00008074.overview: J203201 + 413730 is a radio galaxy candidate with a morphology reminiscent of a double-double radio galaxy [arXiv:0705.3801, arXiv:2108.01505]. The system features an optically thick compact core that likely acts as its central engine, alongside northern and southern radio lobes [arXiv:0705.3801, arXiv:2108.01505].

sources.summary: The paper interprets NVSS J203201+413722 as a likely double-double radio galaxy, identifying an optically thick compact core as the probable central engine.

quotes.quote: A detailed GMRT view of this double-lobed source inside the Whipple ellipse is presented in the left panel of Fig. rgal . Its morphology is clearly reminiscent of a double-double radio galaxy which has experienced different epochs of jet activity (see e.g. ). The same field is also shown at 6 cm from our multi-configuration VLA observations.

The most interesting issue about NVSS J203201+413722 is revealed by the detection of a compact component in the 6 cm map located at a J2000.0 position 20^h32^m 01 96 and +41^ 37^ 34 3 (error 0 5 in each coordinate) and with a flux density of 1.1 0.2 mJy. The fact that we do not see it at 20 nor 49 cm, above four times the local rms noise, implies an optically-thick spectral index of about +0.4 . This is possibly due to synchrotron self absorption. have reported marginal Chandra X-ray detections in the vicinity of NVSS J203201+413722 and their source B is consistent with this optically-thick component. All these facts, together with the central symmetric location of the component with respect to the lobes, strongly suggests that we have identified the true location of the central engine powering the rest of the non-thermal jet source.

sources.summary: The paper describes J203201 + 413730 as a radio-galaxy candidate with several radio counterparts, previous VLA/GMRT study, marginal Chandra association, compact core, and named lobes.

quotes.quote: J203201 + 413730 ( 29). For this source, there are several counterparts at radio frequencies. Is also known as NVSS J203201+413722 and is unresolved. studied it in detail; they proposed it as a radio galaxy which might suffered different episodes of jet activity (see Fig. fig:galaxitas4 ). The authors observed the field using the VLA C+D array configuration at 5 GHz as well as the GMRT at 610 MHz. These observations, along with a marginal Chandra X-ray detection in the vicinity of 29 reported by , made it a radio galaxy candidate with an optically-thick compact core Marti-2007 . The two lobes of 29 were also reported by at their two observing bands; they named them as SBHW-217 ( S_ 350MHz =85 5 mJy, SC_ 1400MHz =36 5 mJy) -northern lobe- and SBHW-218 ( S_ 350MHz =122 35 mJy, SC_ 1400MHz =39 5 mJy) -southern lobe-, where the quoted integrated fluxes SC at 1400 MHz were derived from images convolved to the 325 MHz beam.

gmw_00008075.name: J203529 + 403201
gmw_00008075.overview: J203529 + 403201 is a candidate radio galaxy that lacks detections at other frequencies [arXiv:2108.01505]. Its two lobes are separated by 45 arcseconds and exhibit significantly different spectral indices, suggesting the southwest component may be a bright extragalactic hotspot [arXiv:2108.01505].

sources.summary: The paper presents J203529 + 403201 as a candidate lacking other-frequency detections, with very different lobe spectral indices and a possible extragalactic hotspot interpretation.

quotes.quote: J203529 + 403201 ( 37). There are no detections at other frequencies at its position; see Fig. fig:galaxitas5 .The spectral indices of both lobes, separated 45 arcsec, are quite different; in particular the one associated with SW lobe, which is close to zero, see Table long . A possible scenario as an extragalactic source, is that the SW component is a bright hotspot, with its flat-spectrum emission being the reason why the spectral index is so different from the NE component.

gmw_00008076.name: J201841 + 431844
gmw_00008076.overview: J201841 + 431844 is an active galactic nucleus-like source that features two highly collimated radio jets extending across 7 arcminutes [arXiv:2108.01505]. A central knot located between the lobes indicates a possible nucleus, though the source lacks known infrared or radio counterparts [arXiv:2108.01505].

sources.summary: The paper describes J201841 + 431844 as an AGN-like double-lobed source with highly collimated jets, a possible nucleus knot, and no infrared or radio counterpart coverage.

quotes.quote: J201841 + 431844 ( 41) shows emission that resembles two highly collimated jets extending along 7 arcmin on the sky. It presents a knot in between the two lobes, indicative of a possible nucleus. The source is not covered by our 325 MHz mosaic image and lacks of infrared or radio putative counterparts; see Fig. fig:galaxitas6 . Notwithstanding, the morphology of the object is typical AGN-like sources.

gmw_00008077.name: HG J2012
aliases_external: HG J2012 / VT J201229.90-170556.32 host galaxy
gmw_00008077.overview: HG J2012 is a green-valley host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.053 [arXiv:2310.03795]. It is classified as a Balmer-strong galaxy with a low star-formation rate and a slightly older stellar population than E+A galaxies [arXiv:2310.03795].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HG J2012 as the z=0.053 host galaxy of VT J2012, a Balmer-strong green-valley system with low star formation.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy properties An optical image from the DECam Legacy Survey of HG J2012 is shown in the panel (a) of Figure fig:J2012_sum . It is at a redshift z=0.053 , or a luminosity distance d_L = 244 Mpc. The host galaxy spectrum (contaminated by transient features) is shown in panel (b). From the H _A absorption and an upper limit on the H EW, this galaxy is a balmer strong galaxy, which have slightly older stellar population than E+A galaxies.

From an SED fit performed in Paper I, the host galaxy has a stellar mass M_*/M_ = 9.90_ 0.09 ^ 0.32 . The 3 upper limit on the star-formation rate is SFR /(M_ yr ^ 1 ) < 1 . This host is consistent with the green valley.

gmw_00008078.name: host galaxy of GRB 181123B
gmw_00008078.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 181123B is a high-redshift emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2007.03715]. Spectroscopic observations of the host reveal a redshift of z = 1.754 [arXiv:2007.03715], and it has an estimated stellar mass of 1.7 billion solar masses [arXiv:2007.03715].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 181123B host, measures z=1.754 from spectroscopy, and describes it as a typical SGRB host with stellar mass, age, and luminosity estimates.

quotes.quote: Observations with Gemini-North starting at 9.1 hr after the burst reveal a faint optical afterglow with i 25.1 mag, at an angular offset of 0.59 0.16 '' from its host galaxy. Using grizYJHK observations, we measure a photometric redshift of the host galaxy of z = 1.77^ +0.30 _ 0.17 . From a combination of Gemini and Keck spectroscopy of the host galaxy spanning 4500-18000 , we detect a single emission line at 13390 , inferred as H at z = 1.754 0.001 and corroborating the photometric redshift. The host galaxy properties of GRB 181123B are typical to those of other SGRB hosts, with an inferred stellar mass of 1.7 10^ 10 M_ , mass-weighted age of 0.9 Gyr and optical luminosity of 0.9L^ * .

gmw_00008079.name: GN4 5461276
gmw_00008079.overview: GN4 5461276 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a grism redshift of approximately 6.513 [arXiv:2111.14863]. It has been identified as a tentative Lyman-alpha emission galaxy, though the detection of this emission line is possibly spurious and requires further observational validation [arXiv:2111.14863].

sources.summary: The paper reports GN4 5461276 as a tentative z=6.513 Ly emission candidate, notes possible spuriousness, and describes significant features in two position angles and combined spectra.

quotes.quote: We performed emission-line search as described in Section 2.2.1, which results in no convincing Ly detection s from the entire sample except for one tentative detection from a z_ grism = 6.513 0.005 galaxy (GN4 5461276). Although we the detection of Ly emission is possibly spurious from the galaxy with given data, it is worth presenting the promising features of the detected emission line here as the physical properties of the emission line would be rather extreme if confirmed. Further observations are required for validating the nature of the detected emission line. We display the individual 2D spectra obtained from 4 PAs with the HST H band stamp of GN4 5461276 in Figure fig:PAs where we detect significant emission-line features from 2 (PA=93 and PA=173) of the 4 PAs. The all PA combined 1D and 2D spectra are shown in Figure fig:lae_spectra , which shows a formal > 4 detection of the emission line.

gmw_00008080.name: GN2 23734
gmw_00008080.overview: GN2 23734 is a high-redshift galaxy identified as a tentative continuum-break candidate [arXiv:2111.14863]. It has a tentative grism redshift of z = 6.2, with its continuum detected at greater than four-sigma significance redward of a possible break [arXiv:2111.14863].

sources.summary: The paper presents GN2 23734 as a tentative z=6.2 continuum-break candidate detected at >4 significance redward of a possible break in combined G102 and G140 spectra.

quotes.quote: It results in one continuum candidate from a z_ grism = 6.2^ +2.5 _ 0.8 galaxy. The continuum is detected at > 4 at wavelengths redward of a possible continuum break. Figure fig:continuum shows the grism spectra of GN2 23734, combining both of the G102 and G140 spectra.
Although the grism continuum-fit spectrum seems comparable to the best-fit SED, we consider it tentative given the current noise level in the grism spectrum.

gmw_00008081.name: HETDEX J115031.93+504850.4
gmw_00008081.overview: HETDEX J115031.93+504850.4 is an active galactic nucleus at redshift z ~ 2.24 with extremely high equivalent width emission [arXiv:2210.12679]. Its Lyman-alpha emission extends to 85 kpc, displaying asymmetric blue and red peaks that suggest the presence of galactic outflows [arXiv:2210.12679].

sources.summary: The paper presents HETDEX J115031.93+504850.4/J1150+5048 as a z 2.24 high-EW AGN whose Ly emission extends 85 kpc and whose asymmetric blue/red peaks indicate outflows.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we introduce an AGN (HETDEX J115031.93+504850.4, shortened to J1150+5048 in this paper) with extremely high EW ( EW_ Ly +N V,rest 921 ) at z 2.24 from the HETDEX survey.

The Ly emission of J1150+5048 extends to 85 kpc. J1150+5048 lies well within the scattered region of the correlation between the radius and the Ly luminosity in Figure 13 of . The extended emission-line region can be explained either by central-driven outflows or by inflows from the circumgalactic medium. The blue peak of the Ly emission is always weaker than the red peak. This suggests that the near side is more heavily scattered than the far side, and the gas flows are outflows rather than inflows.

gmw_00008082.name: NGC5985
gmw_00008082.overview: NGC5985 is a bright Seyfert galaxy with an apparent magnitude of V = 11.0 [arXiv:astro-ph/9812144]. Multiple companion galaxies and high-redshift quasars are aligned along the extension of its minor axis [arXiv:astro-ph/9812144].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC5985 as a central Seyfert whose minor axis allegedly aligns with nearby quasars and companion galaxies, arguing their redshifts and positions indicate a physical family.

quotes.quote: There was NGC5985, only 36.9 arcmin away on the skyThe chance of finding a Seyfert as bright as V = 11.0 mag. this close to the z = .81 quasar was less than 10^ 3 .

But five of these six quasars fall on a line through the Seyfert, with the dwarf galaxy along the same line. What is the probability that three or four objects would accidentally align within a degree or so of as straight line through the Seyfert? Conservatively this can be computed to be of the order of 10^ 4 to 10^ 5 . But the most astonishing result of all is that if one looks up the position of the minor axis in NGC5985 it turns out, as shown in Figs. 1 and 2, to be a line that looks as though it were drawn through the positions of the objectsJust a simple visual evaluation of Fig. 1 would lead to the conclusion that the configuration was physically significant.

In Fig. 2 I have enlarged the central regions of Fig. 1 and plotted all the bright, NGC galaxies in the region. It is apparent that these NGC galaxies, which are fainter than NGC5985 as companions should be, are almost exactly aligned along the WNW extension of the minor axisTaken together with the dwarf companion on the other side of the minor axis, this leaves no doubt that in the case of NGC5985 the companion galaxies and quasars are aligned exactly along the same minor axis. This then constitutes another proof that these objects of variously higher redshift have some physical relation to the low redshift,central galaxy.

gmw_00008083.name: RGB 1745+398
gmw_00008083.overview: RGB 1745+398 is a BL Lacertae object located near the center of a moderately massive galaxy cluster [arXiv:0804.0502, arXiv:0711.1158]. The source exhibits a highly distorted Fanaroff-Riley class I radio morphology with sharp jet bends [arXiv:0711.1158].

sources.summary: The paper studies RGB 1745+398 as a BL Lac object in a rich lensing cluster, measuring nearby galaxy redshifts, cluster redshift, velocity dispersion, mass, and richness.

quotes.quote: The BL Lac object RGB 1745+398 lies in an environment that makes it possible to study the cluster around it more deeply than the environments of other BL Lac objects. The cluster centered on the BL Lac works as a strong gravitational lens, forming a large arc around itself. The aim of this paper is to study the environment and characteristics of this object more accurately than the environments of other BL Lac objects have been before.

The redshifts of nine galaxies in the field were measured to be near the redshift of the BL Lac object, confirming the presence of a cluster. The average redshift of the cluster is 0.268, and the velocity dispersion 470^ +190 _ 110 km s ^ 1 . The mass of the cluster is M_ 500 = 4^ +3 _ 2 10^ 14 M_ which implies a rather massive cluster. The richness measurement also suggests that this is a rich cluster: the result for covariance function is B_ gb = 600 200 Mpc ^ 1.77 , which corresponds to Abell richness class 1 and which is consistent with the mass and velocity dispersion of the cluster.

sources.summary: The paper places 1743+398 near a galaxy cluster center, describes its distorted FRI morphology, and reports a nearly southward VLBI jet with a longitudinal-field sheath.

quotes.quote: 1743+398 This BL Lac object lies close to the center of a moderately massive galaxy cluster Nilsson99 .
