Also, both GRQs have small inclination angles resulting in less dust contamination of the central AGN. We compared the modelled inclination angles with lobe-to-core ratio and obtained the smaller value of inclination for the higher R parameter, as it is expected.

gmw_00008140.name: J0209+075
gmw_00008140.overview: J0209+075 is a face-on spiral galaxy featuring a luminous bulge and loose spiral arms [arXiv:2210.11724]. These arms harbor several star-forming clumps, and the southwestern arm appears disjoint from the rest of the galaxy [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0209+075 as a face-on galaxy with a luminous bulge, loose spiral arms containing star-forming clumps, a disjoint southwestern arm, and a nearby foreground star.

quotes.quote: J0209 + 075 : This is a face-on galaxy with a luminous bulge and loose spiral arms, which harbor several star-forming clumps. The southwestern arm looks disjoint from the galaxy, indicating a low stellar density in the inter-arm region. The bright point-like source northwest of the galaxy is a foreground star identified by SDSS.

gmw_00008141.name: J0219+015
gmw_00008141.overview: J0219+015 is a local galaxy that features a bulge and dust structures, including a dust lane [arXiv:2210.11724]. Its associated radio source is classified as winged or X-shaped [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0219+015 as a local galaxy with a fairly prominent bulge, prominent dust structures and a dust lane, while its radio source is classified as winged or X-shaped.

quotes.quote: Six galaxies (J0209+075, J0806+062, J1128+241, J1328+571, J1646+383, J1656+640) have spiral features, and seven (J0219+015, J0847+124, J0855+420, J0914+413, J0926+465, J0956+162, J1633+084) have prominent dust lanes with dimensions comparable to that of the entire galaxy, which are common in spiral galaxies and are regarded as signatures of edge-on spiral disks Holwerda_2019_AJ_158_103 .
J0219 + 015 : This local ( z_ spec =0.04 ) galaxy has a fairly prominent bulge and prominent dust structures. The radio source is classified as winged or X-shaped Yang_2019_ApJS_245_17 .

gmw_00008142.name: J0806+062
gmw_00008142.overview: J0806+062 is a galaxy that features several spiral arms and a luminous bar-like region [arXiv:2210.11724]. It may have a genuine companion galaxy located to its northeast, and a star identified by SDSS and Gaia lies to its east [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0806+062 as a spiral-armed galaxy with a luminous bar-like region, a possible northeastern companion, and a nearby eastern star identified by SDSS and Gaia.

quotes.quote: J0806 + 062 : This galaxy shows several spiral arms and a luminous bar-like region. A galaxy to the northeast with z_ phot =0.11 0.08 might be a genuine companion. A star to the east of the galaxy with m_g=20.7 mag has been identified by SDSS and Gaia https://irsa.ipac.caltech.edu/cgi-bin/Gator/nph-dd Gaia_DR3 .

gmw_00008143.name: J0847+124
gmw_00008143.overview: J0847+124 is a nearly edge-on disk galaxy featuring a long dust lane with complicated substructures [arXiv:2210.11724]. It hosts a central radio core and asymmetric double radio lobes, of which the northern lobe is luminous while the southern lobe is barely detected [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0847+124 as a nearly edge-on galaxy with a long structured dust lane, asymmetric double radio lobes, and a radio core coincident with the galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0847 + 124 : This nearly edge-on galaxy has a long dust lane with complicated substructures. The double radio lobes are asymmetric; while the northern lobe is luminous, the southern one is only barely detected. A radio core coincides with the galaxy.

gmw_00008144.name: J0855+420
gmw_00008144.overview: J0855+420 is a massive, edge-on late-type galaxy bisected by a 10 kpc dust lane [arXiv:2210.11724]. It hosts a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio structure featuring double lobes with a total projected length of 400 kpc [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0855+420 as a massive edge-on late-type galaxy bisected by a 10 kpc dust lane, hosting an FR I radio structure with large projected lobes.

quotes.quote: J0855 + 420 : This edge-on late-type galaxy, bisected by a prominent, 10 kpc dust lane, has a large stellar mass ( M_*=1.2 10^ 11 M_ ). The radio structure has an FR I morphology because the hotspots are much closer to the galaxy than the lobes. The double lobes have a total length of 400 kpc and a width of 100 kpc (projected). The lobes have sinuous shapes near the hotspots.

gmw_00008145.name: J0901+164
gmw_00008145.overview: J0901+164 is a massive spiral galaxy that features arms and star-forming clumps [arXiv:2210.11724]. The galaxy is associated with an eastern radio lobe that has a tail extending to the southwest [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0901+164 as a massive spiral with prominent arms and star-forming clumps, an eastern radio-lobe tail, and nearby sources considered in chance-alignment estimates.

quotes.quote: J0901 + 164 : A massive spiral with M_* = 8 10^ 10 M_ , this galaxy has prominent arms and star-forming clumps. The eastern radio lobe has a tail to the southwest instead of toward the direction of the other radio lobe. We crossmatched the peak coordinate of the tail with the AllWISE catalog Wright_2010_AJ_140_1868 but found no source. A spiral galaxy with z_ phot =0.22 0.05 and a prominent bar is located southeast of the main object. Another faint object with z_ phot =1.35 0.25 is located to the northwest of the main object and close to the center of the radio contours. To obtain a conservative estimate of the chance-alignment probability, we regard this faint object as a possible radio source.

gmw_00008146.name: J0903+432
gmw_00008146.overview: J0903+432 is a faint, very massive edge-on disk galaxy that features a dust lane [arXiv:2210.11724]. The galaxy has radio lobes oriented perpendicular to its plane, though there is a high probability that these lobes are a chance alignment [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0903+432 as a faint, very massive edge-on galaxy with a prominent dust lane, perpendicular radio lobes, and a high chance-alignment probability.

quotes.quote: J0903 + 432 : This is a faint ( m_g=20.7 mag), very massive ( M_*=2 10^ 11 M_ ), edge-on galaxy with a prominent dust lane. The radio lobes are perpendicular to the galaxy plane. It has a high probability of being chance-aligned with the radio lobes because the number density of galaxies of this magnitude is large.

gmw_00008147.name: J0926+465
gmw_00008147.overview: J0926+465 is an edge-on galaxy that features a dust lane spanning 20 kpc in length [arXiv:2210.11724]. In addition to its two radio lobes, the galaxy exhibits faint radio emission that coincides directly with its main body [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0926+465 as an edge-on galaxy with a 20 kpc dust lane and faint radio emission coincident with the galaxy, among prominent dust-lane hosts.

quotes.quote: Six galaxies (J0209+075, J0806+062, J1128+241, J1328+571, J1646+383, J1656+640) have spiral features, and seven (J0219+015, J0847+124, J0855+420, J0914+413, J0926+465, J0956+162, J1633+084) have prominent dust lanes with dimensions comparable to that of the entire galaxy, which are common in spiral galaxies and are regarded as signatures of edge-on spiral disks Holwerda_2019_AJ_158_103 .
J0926 + 465 : This is an edge-on galaxy with a dust lane 20 kpc long. In addition to the two lobes, faint radio emission also coincides with the galaxy.

gmw_00008148.name: J0941+312
gmw_00008148.overview: J0941+312 is a faint, edge-on galaxy located at a photometric redshift of approximately 0.366 [arXiv:2210.11724]. It is situated at the center of radio emission, with associated radio lobes that would span a physical dimension of 1.4 Mpc [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0941+312 as a low-confidence faint edge-on galaxy near radio emission; if associated, its radio lobes would span a physical dimension of 1.4 Mpc.

quotes.quote: J0941 + 312 : We classified this faint edge-on galaxy as a low-confidence object. However, it is located in the center of radio emission, and no other sources are detected nearby. The radio lobes have an angular size of 2 5 . If they are associated with the galaxy, which has z_ phot =0.366 0.037 , the physical dimension would be 1.4 Mpc, similar to J2345 0449 Bagchi_2014_ApJ_788_174 .

gmw_00008149.name: J0956+162
gmw_00008149.overview: J0956+162 is an edge-on galaxy that features a 15 kpc dust lane [arXiv:2210.11724]. It hosts intense radio emission from its nucleus and possesses two radio lobes located roughly equidistant from the galactic center [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0956+162 as an edge-on galaxy with a prominent 15 kpc dust lane, strong nuclear radio emission, roughly symmetric lobes, and an unrelated eastern object.

quotes.quote: J0956 + 162 : This edge-on galaxy with a prominent ( 15 kpc) dust lane has intense radio emission from the nucleus, with peak intensity reaching 1.1 mJy beam ^ 1 . The two radio lobes are roughly equidistant from the center of the galaxy. We find an object with z_ phot =0.46 0.08 in the direction of the eastern lobe, 2 offset from the radio peak; it is unlikely to be associated with the radio lobe because of its small size ( 0 91 0 04 ) and faintness ( m_ F475W =22.5 0.1 mag ).

gmw_00008150.name: J0958+561
gmw_00008150.overview: The disk galaxy J0958+561 is characterized by a bulge and an extended disk [arXiv:2210.11724]. Its nuclear region is complicated, containing a radio core and a compact source that may represent a secondary nucleus [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0958+561 as having a prominent bulge, extended disk, complicated nuclear region, a possible secondary nucleus, and a radio core coincident with the galaxy.

quotes.quote: J0958 + 561 : The galaxy has a prominent bulge and an extended disk. Its nuclear region is complicated in the HST image. The GALFIT residuals reveal a compact source near the center, which might be a candidate secondary nucleus (see Figure fig:galfit_all ). In addition to the two lobes, a radio core coincides with the galaxy.

gmw_00008151.name: J1136+125
gmw_00008151.overview: J1136+125 is a low-mass galaxy with a peculiar morphology, outer distortions, and a modest star formation rate [arXiv:2210.11724]. It is associated with a background active galactic nucleus and quasar at a redshift of z = 0.76, which exhibits core-dominated radio emission [arXiv:2405.00784].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1136 + 1252 as a confirmed background AGN/quasar at z=0.76, with compact core-dominated radio emission detected by EVN and VLBA non-detection attributed to scintillation.

quotes.quote: More recently, among the parent sample of 13 radio sources in dwarf galaxies, J1220 + 3020 and J1136 + 1252 have been confirmed as AGN based on spectroscopic data. In particular, J1220 + 3020 shows the presence of coronal line [ Fe X ] and enhanced [ O I ] emission Molina21 , while J1136 + 1252 is associated with an optical counterpart in deep Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) imaging at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 0.76 (Sturm et al. 2024, in prep. ), pointing to a background quasar origin.

We note that in addition to the PRSs in our sample, we also detected the background source J1027 + 0112 and the background AGN J1136 + 1252 in our EVN observations. J1136 + 1252 has a physical size of < 9.86 pc at a redshift of z = 0.76 (Sturm et al. 2024, in prep. ), indicating that the radio emission is core-dominated. Interestingly, J1136 + 1252 is not detected with VLBA at 9 GHz. A potential explanation is refractive scintillation of the compact core due to small-scale irregularities in the interstellar medium. Indeed, given the Galactic latitude of the source, variations in the flux density of up to 60 at higher frequencies are expected Walker98 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J1136+125 as a low-mass peculiar galaxy with outer distortions and modest star formation, but not a likely radio-source host because a nearby point source may be responsible.

quotes.quote: J1136 + 125 : This is a low-mass galaxy ( M_*=2 10^9 M_ ) with a peculiar morphology and distortions in its outer regions. It has a star formation rate of 0.4 M_ year ^ 1 according to GSWLC-2. We do not consider it a likely host for the radio source. We note that there is a bright point source with m_ F475W = 22.1 mag located only 1 \ west of the galactic center, which should be investigated further as a possible quasar candidate responsible for the radio lobes.

gmw_00008152.name: J1328+571
gmw_00008152.overview: J1328+571 is a low-mass, low-redshift barred galaxy that contains at least two arms and numerous star-forming regions [arXiv:2210.11724]. The galaxy hosts an intermediate-mass black hole and features a double-lobed, extended radio jet structure whose center is offset from the galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1328+571 as a low-mass, low-redshift barred galaxy with star-forming regions, an intermediate-mass black hole, extended double-lobed radio emission, and an offset lobe center.

quotes.quote: J1328 + 571 : This low-mass ( M* = 8 10^8 M_ ), low-redshift ( z_ phot =0.032 0.029 ) galaxy has many star-forming regions, at least two prominent arms, and a strong bar. With an estimated BH mass of 10^5 M_ , it qualifies as one of the very few intermediate-mass BHs known to have strong radio emission, and the first of its kind to have a double-lobed, extended jet structure. suggest that intermediate-mass BHs may not have sufficient time to sink into the galactic center by dynamical friction. This may explain why the center of the radio lobes is offset from the galactic nucleus by 4 0 ( 2.5 kpc).

gmw_00008153.name: J1349+454
gmw_00008153.overview: The galaxy J1349+454 is classified as a disk galaxy [arXiv:2210.11724]. It is categorized within a low-confidence sample of double-lobed radio sources because observations have detected only a single radio lobe [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper assigns J1349+454 to the low-confidence sample because only one radio lobe is detected, so it fails the double-lobed source definition pending further observations.

quotes.quote: J1349 + 454 : We assign this galaxy to the low-confidence sample because only one lobe is detected. It does not satisfy our sample definition of double-lobed radio sources. Further observation may help to verify the existence of the other lobe.

gmw_00008154.name: J1354+465
gmw_00008154.overview: J1354+465 is a late-type galaxy with a stellar mass of 5 billion solar masses [arXiv:2210.11724]. It is a strong candidate for a radio association due to its close proximity to the center of two radio lobes [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1354+465 as a late-type galaxy with stellar mass 5e9 solar masses, low-confidence classification, and a strong candidate radio association needing better spectroscopy and radio imaging.

quotes.quote: J1354 + 465 : This is a late-type galaxy with a stellar mass of 5 10^9 M_ . Although classified into the low-confidence sample, it is still a strong candidate for radio association due to its proximity to the center of two radio lobes. Further high-resolution radio observations and optical spectroscopy to secure a better redshift would help to determine the optical-radio association.

gmw_00008155.name: J1509+515
gmw_00008155.overview: J1509+515 is a spiral galaxy characterized by two grand-design spiral arms and a long bar extending 10 kpc [arXiv:2210.11724]. It is considered a strong candidate radio galaxy with a stellar mass of 5 × 10^11 solar masses [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1509+515 as an enormous stellar-mass galaxy with grand-design spiral arms and a 10 kpc bar, considered low-confidence but still a strong candidate.

quotes.quote: J1509 + 515 : This galaxy has two grand-design spiral arms, a long bar that reaches 10 kpc, and an enormous stellar mass of 5 10^ 11 M_ . We consider it a low-confidence object because it is offset from the center of the radio lobes. However, we regard this galaxy as still a strong candidate, given its unusually large stellar mass.

gmw_00008156.name: J1633+084
gmw_00008156.overview: J1633+084 is a massive, edge-on disk galaxy that features a large-scale dust lane [arXiv:2210.11724]. The galaxy hosts a radio core and two compact, symmetrically aligned radio lobes despite having an unexpectedly small pseudo bulge [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1633+084 as a massive edge-on galaxy with a large dust lane, unexpectedly small pseudo bulge, radio core, and compact aligned sources treated as radio lobes.

quotes.quote: J1633 + 084 : This massive ( M_* = 2 10^ 11 M_ ), edge-on galaxy has a prominent, large-scale dust lane. It is quite unexpected that a galaxy this massive would have such a small bulge ( B/T=0.11 ), which is likely a pseudo bulge given its very small \ index ( n_ bulge =0.37 ). In addition to the two lobes, a prominent radio core is coincident with the optical galaxy. Since both radio lobes are barely resolved, one might suspect that they are two unrelated point sources instead of radio lobes. However, the chance is extremely small for three unrelated sources to be so well aligned. We estimate the probability that three random radio sources with a projected distance smaller than 50 \ to be aligned by chance into a straight line with an accuracy of 5 . Since the FIRST survey has 220,000 resolved sources spanning 10,000 deg ^2 of the sky with S/N > 10 Banfield_2015_MNRAS_453_2326 , the total number of such alignments is given by Edmunds_1981_NAT_290_481 : N= 2 3 d^ 3 n^ 3 P, where is the sky coverage of the survey, d is the maximum distance between the three sources, n is the source surface density, and P is the alignment accuracy. For this object, we find N=0.8 . Moreover, the actual probability is likely much lower since we find no optical counterparts for the two radio lobes. Therefore, it is unlikely that the three sources have no physical association, and we consider the two compact, symmetrically aligned radio sources to be radio lobes.

gmw_00008157.name: J1646+383
gmw_00008157.overview: J1646+383 is a radio galaxy that features a dust lane with a peculiar, arc-like shape [arXiv:2210.11724]. A K-type star is projected against its radio contour, but this star is unlikely to be associated with the galaxy's large-scale radio emission [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1646+383 as a galaxy with an arc-shaped dust lane and argues that a projected nearby star is not responsible for the large-scale radio emission.

quotes.quote: J1646 + 383 : The galaxy has a prominent dust lane with a peculiar, arc-like shape. Curiously, we find a star projected against a radio contour, located northeast of the galaxy at ( , ) = (16^ h 46^ m 29.7^ s , +38^ 31 ^ 36 10 ). The parallax measurement from Gaia of 0.44 0.05 mas rules out the possibility that it is a quasar, and its SDSS colors ( u-g=1.7 , g-=0.6 ) are consistent with those of a K-type star Covey_2007_AJ . Thus, it is unlikely to be associated with the large-scale, intense radio emissions. Moreover, its Gaia proper motion of v_ N,E =(-3.3 0.1,2.1 0.1) mas year ^ 1 suggests that it is moving from northeast to southwest toward, instead of away from, the center of the radio lobes.

gmw_00008158.name: J1656+640
gmw_00008158.overview: J1656+640 is an extremely massive spiral galaxy with a star formation rate of 10 solar masses per year [arXiv:2210.11724]. A radio core is located at its galactic nucleus, and three other low-redshift galaxies are situated nearby [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1656+640 as an extremely massive star-forming spiral with a radio core at the nucleus and nearby low-redshift galaxies unlikely to be distant giant ellipticals.

quotes.quote: J1656 + 640 : This extremely massive ( M_* = 3 10^ 11 M_ ) spiral galaxy, classified as star-forming from its narrow emission lines Baldwin_1981_PASP_93_5 , has a star formation rate of 10 M_ year^ 1 according to the GSWLA-2 catalog. A radio core coincides with the galactic nucleus, with an intensity slightly above 3 . We note three other galaxies near the radio center, which are relatively low-redshift objects with z_ phot <0.3 , not likely distant giant elliptical galaxies.

gmw_00008159.name: J1721+262
gmw_00008159.overview: J1721+262 is a spiral galaxy featuring a luminous bulge and an extended disk [arXiv:2210.11724]. The galaxy coincides with a radio core and hosts two radio hotspots, with an extended tail emanating from the eastern hotspot [arXiv:2210.11724].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1721+262 as a galaxy with a luminous bulge, extended spiral disk, nearby projected star, roughly symmetric radio hotspots, an eastern tail, and a radio core.

quotes.quote: J1721 + 262 : The galaxy has a luminous bulge and an extended disk with spiral arms. A star, identified by SDSS and Gaia, is projected to the south. The two radio hotspots have similar projected distances to the galaxy, with an extended tail emanating from the eastern one. A radio core coincides with the galaxy.

gmw_00008160.name: J1023 + 3243
gmw_00008160.overview: J1023 + 3243 is a galaxy that has been studied as a potential dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1605.09188]. Although Very Large Array observations detected two radio components in the source, Very Long Baseline Array observations failed to detect any compact, high radio power features [arXiv:1605.09188].

sources.summary: J1023+3243 was undetected by the authors with VLBA, but prior VLA observations found two radio components; the paper says its VLBA data cannot strengthen the dual-AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: The fourth source in our sample, J1023 + 3243 , which was not detected with VLBA in our experiment, was however successfully observed and imaged by with the VLA Unfortunately do not list the date of their observations. According to the NRAO archive, within their program, the observations of J1023 + 3243 took place on 2012 October 13 and 2013 January 5.

The only overlapping source with our sample is J1023 + 3243. used two subbands within the X band, the lower one centered at 8.5 GHz, the upper one at 11.5 GHz. They detected two radio components in J1023 + 3243 when combining the observations from the two subbands by averaging across all spectral channels.
Thus we cannot strengthen the conclusion of that J1023 + 3243 is a dual AGN, since we did not detect any compact (on VLBA scales), high radio power feature in this source.

gmw_00008161.name: J1155 + 1507
gmw_00008161.overview: J1155 + 1507 is a merging galaxy system located at a redshift of z=0.287 [arXiv:1605.09188]. High-resolution radio observations reveal an elongated structure spanning 30 milliarcseconds, with emission components that likely originate from one of the merging galaxies [arXiv:1605.09188].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1155+1507 as the most distant and faintest detected source, with elongated radio emission in a merging system where the VLBA components likely arise in one merging galaxy.

quotes.quote: J1155 + 1507 is the most distant ( z=0.287 ) and the faintest of the three detected sources. It has an elongated structure of 30 mas (Figure fig:J1155 ).

For J1155 + 1507, the position angle of the two emission components given by is 137 3 . The position angle of the radio structure (Figure fig:J1155 ) is 121 10 , nearly perpendicular the position angle derived from optical spectroscopy, thus they are probably not related. According to the high-resolution image of J1155 + 1507 obtained with the NIRC2 camera on Keck II/LGSAO with a broadband Kp ( 1.948-2.299 m) filter by see also 1155_Fu_NIRimage , this source is a merging system with a component separation of 2.5 kpc.

The resolved stellar components were enveloped by the spatially extended [ O 3 ] emission. concluded that although the extended NLR is clearly responsible for the double-peaked line profiles, it is impossible to decide whether both or just one AGN powers the NLR. In our VLBA image, the detected two components are separated by 70 pc, and connected with continuous emission structure; their radio emission most probably originate in one of the two merging galaxies detected in the near-infrared image 1155_Fu_NIRimage .

gmw_00008162.name: J2104 0009
gmw_00008162.overview: J2104 0009 is a galaxy that was initially identified as a dual active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1605.09188]. However, near-infrared imaging reveals only a single spatial structure, suggesting its double-peaked spectral lines are instead driven by radio jets and outflow kinematics [arXiv:1605.09188].

sources.summary: The paper treats J2104 0009 as a dual-AGN candidate whose optical emission offsets exceed the radio structure; near-infrared imaging and radio alignment suggest jet-driven outflows rather than resolved kpc dual AGN.

quotes.quote: J1155 + 1507 was one of the 3 candidate dual AGN selected by based on the adaptive optics images of see also 1155_Fu_NIRimage , while J2104 0009, J2304 0933, and J1023 + 3243 were selected on the basis that their emission features aligned with the major axis of the host galaxy. determined the spatial extent and position angle of the two emission components in their sample. The angular projected spatial offsets of the narrow-line emission features are 0 55 , 0 28 , and 0 84 in the case of J1155 + 1507, J2104 0009, and J2304 0933, respectively. These separations are an order of magnitude (in the case of J1155 + 1507, and J2304 0933), or more than 2.5 times (in the case of J2104 0009) larger than the size of the radio structures detected in our VLBA observations.

J2104 0009 and J2304 0933 were imaged in the near-infrared with the NIRC2 camera (PI: Keith Matthews) enhanced by Laser Guide Star Adaptive Optics system on the Keck II Telescope Keck II/LGSAO, Keck_LSGAO . J2104 0009 was observed with a broadband H ( 1.337-1.929 m) filter, J2304 0933 was observed with a broadband Kp ( 1.948-2.299 m) filter. The images were published by . Both sources show single spatial structures. concluded that the double-peaked narrow-line emissions in these sources are likely not caused by kpc-separation dual AGN but are due to NLR kinematics, outflows, jets, rings of star formation, or a close pair of AGN which cannot be resolved in the near-infrared. The good coincidence between the double emission components and the radio structure in our VLBA images (Figs. fig:J2104 , fig:J2304 ) suggest that the radio jets, although on much larger scales than mapped here, do play a role in producing the emission regions responsible for the double-peaked spectral lines.

gmw_00008163.name: J2304 0933
gmw_00008163.overview: J2304 0933 is a nearby radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.032 [arXiv:1605.09188]. High-resolution VLBA observations reveal a complex linear radio structure aligned broadly with the host galaxy's plane, though star-formation emission is considered an unlikely source for this radio activity [arXiv:1605.09188].

sources.summary: J2304 0933 is the closest and brightest target, showing a complex linear VLBA radio structure whose components align broadly with optical features and the host plane, with star-formation emission considered unlikely.

quotes.quote: J2304 0933 is the brightest and the closest source ( z=0.032 ) in our sample. At the high resolution of the VLBA, it exhibits a complex linear radio structure oriented roughly east--west with a size of almost 70 mas (Figure fig:J2304 ). The source seems to be decomposed into 5 or 6 different features along the direction of the minor axis of the restoring beam. However, they cannot be fitted individually, the best fitted model consists of two slightly extended circular Gaussian brightness distribution components, D1 and D2 (Table tab:comp ).

In J2304 0933 (Figure fig:J2304 ) the position angle of D2 is 105 6 which is broadly consistent (modulo 180 ) with the position angle of 66 3 given by . Thus in these two sources the VLBI-detected radio features, similarly to the optical emission features, are located along the plane of the host galaxy. Radio emission aligned with the plane of the host galaxy might indicate that it originates from star formation rather than AGN jet activity. However, in Sect. res , we showed that the high radio powers in J2104 0009 and the star formation rate estimate for J2304 0933 render unlikely that these radio emission features originate from SNRs or SNR complexes.

gmw_00008164.name: NGC 5972
gmw_00008164.overview: NGC 5972 is a faded active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.05915] that hosts a large-scale double radio source [arXiv:1612.06006]. The galaxy features an extended emission-line region [arXiv:2208.05915], an arcsecond-scale nuclear bubble [arXiv:2208.05915], and an eastern emission-line loop [arXiv:1612.06006].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5972 as a faded Voorwerpjes AGN with an EELR, nuclear bubble, extended soft X-ray emission, and multi-temperature ionized gas used to examine feedback after a recent luminosity decline.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The centroid of the near-IR emission, shown by the red cross in Figure fig:bulge , is located at R.A 15:38:54.1551 decl +17:01:34.274.

quotes.quote: We present Chandra X-ray Observatory observations and Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph spectra of NGC 5972, one of the 19 "Voorwerpjes" galaxies. This galaxy contains an Extended Emission Line Region (EELR) and an arc-second scale nuclear bubble. NGC 5972 is a faded AGN, with EELR luminosity suggesting a 2.1 dex decrease in L _ bol in the last 5 10^ 4 yr.

We detect broadband X-ray emission from NGC 5972 coincident with an arc-second scale bubble-like structure seen in HST [ O iii ] imaging which contains the central engine. Simulations of the Chandra PSF show that the soft emission (0.5-1.5 keV) is spatially extended out to 2 5 in the same direction as the bubble. Spectral modelling shows that the soft emission is compatible with a combination of photo-ionized and collisionally ionized gas. Our best model suggests two populations of collisionally ionized gas with temperatures of 0.80 keV and 0.1 keV. From this model we have been able to estimate other physical parameters, including the effects of feedback on the ISM.

sources.summary: NGC 5972 is treated as a large-scale double radio-source host with nuclear IFU observations, significant velocity structure, dust-lane double/asymmetric peaks, and an eastern emission-line loop lacking a clear velocity signature.

quotes.quote: These include NGC 5252 with its well-documented set of ionization cones ( PrietoFreudling , morse ), and the large-scale double radio source hosts NGC 5972 VCV2001 and Mkn 1498 Huub .

As listed in Table tbl-ifudata , we have observations centered on the nuclei of Mkn 1498 and NGC 5972, and sets of 2 overlapping offset fields for UGC 7342 and 11185; the table includes the requested offsets from the galaxy nucleus to the observed IFU center point.

NGC 5972 shows significant velocity structure, and a nearly constant ionization level (Fig. fig-ngc5972ifu ). It shows double and asymmetric peaks near the dust lane, which can occur strictly from obscuration in a continuous velocity field, and in the inner part of the emission-line loop to its east. Overall, the emission-line loop to the east of the nucleus shows no discernible velocity signature; it does have narrower line profiles than its surroundings, perhaps because its stronger emission dominates the more diffuse gas elsewhere along the line of sight.

gmw_00008165.name: UGC 7342
gmw_00008165.overview: UGC 7342 is an active galactic nucleus with ionizing gas filaments and a lower derived luminosity than previously estimated [arXiv:1612.06006]. Its inner regions exhibit double-peaked emission lines, indicating localized outflows or non-rotational motions that depart from the overall rotation pattern [arXiv:1612.06006].

sources.summary: For UGC 7342, WISE data lower the inferred AGN luminosity and strengthen the energy deficit; IFU spectra reveal double-peaked emission-line regions consistent with localized outflow or non-rotational motions.

quotes.quote: WISE data show that much of the mid- and far-IR flux attributed initially to UGC 7342 at z=0.047 comes instead from a background starburst system at z=0.069 (as identified in Paper I). The AGN luminosity we derive for UGC 7342 is correspondingly lower, and the energy deficit for ionizing its gas filaments becomes even stronger.

As seen in Fig. fig-lineprofiles , UGC 7342 shows substantial regions with double emission-line peaks, likely evidence of outflow in at least one component. We examine this in more detail by fitting sets of 2 Gaussian [ O III ] profiles where this improves the fit, constraining them to have equal widths for consistency across a range of signal-to-noise ratios. Similar behavior is seen in H and H , but H is weaker and H is affected by overlap with [ N II ] components.

We are left with a complex empirical picture for kinematics in the inner few kpc of UGC 7342. Emission-line components suggest outflow, or at least motions departing from the overall rotation pattern by as much as 260 km s ^ 1 , in regions of diffuse, low-surface-brightness emission perpendicular to the main emission regions, with the strongest off-rotational emission in the outer parts of a bright cloud, part of which also shows blue wings beyond a simple 2-component line profile.

gmw_00008166.name: z6 3
gmw_00008166.overview: z6 3 is a strongly lensed, quintuply imaged young galaxy at a redshift of z=6.072 [arXiv:2402.18543]. High-resolution observations reveal a smooth, rotating cool gas disk that is gravitationally unstable and contains at least 15 individual star-forming clumps [arXiv:2402.18543].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on z6 3, a strongly lensed z=6.072 galaxy image, resolving clumps, a rotating gas disk, disk instability, and robust lensing constraints from its multiple images.

quotes.quote: Here we report high-resolution observations of a strongly lensed and quintuply imaged, low-luminosity, young galaxy at z=6.072 (dubbed ), 930 million years after the Big Bang. Magnified by gravitational lensing, the galaxy is resolved into at least 15 individual star-forming clumps with effective radii of r_ e 10--60 parsec (pc), which dominate 70 of the galaxy's total flux. The cool gas emission unveils a smooth, underlying rotating disk characterized by a high rotational-to-random motion ratio and a gravitationally unstable state (Toomre Q 0.2--0.3), with high surface gas densities comparable to local dusty starbursts with 10^ 3-5 solar mass ( M_ ) per pc ^ 2 . These gas properties suggest that the numerous star-forming clumps are formed through disk instabilities with weak feedback effects.

The extended arc structure consists of a pair of two multiple images, and thus a total of five multiple images have been spectroscopically confirmed, referred to as z6 1 , z6 2 , z6 3 , z6 4 , and z6 5 , respectively . In this paper, we focus on the results obtained from z6 3 , dubbed `` '', while results from the other multiple images will be presented in a separate paper . The sky positions, morphology, and flux ratios among the five multiple images provide stringent constraints on the lens model, which makes the magnification estimate for these sources robust even in the high magnification regime (Section 4).

gmw_00008167.name: SDSS J171420.38+414815.7
gmw_00008167.overview: SDSS J171420.38+414815.7 is a core extremely red quasar located at a redshift of z=2.34 [arXiv:2001.03379]. Spectroscopy of the active galaxy reveals broad [O III] emission lines and a tentatively broader, redshifted H-beta line [arXiv:2001.03379].

sources.summary: The paper adds SDSS J171420.38+414815.7 to the core-ERQ sample, reporting GTC/EMIR spectroscopy and broad [O III] with tentative broader, redshifted H.

quotes.quote: We also include the core-ERQ SDSS J171420.38+414815.7 at z= 2.34 (J1714+4148 hereafter) that we observed with the 10.4m Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC) (see Sect. observations ). Measurement of the UV line ratios We have measured UV line ratios for the sample of core-ERQ using the BOSS optical spectra. As a guidance, we show in Table ratios the line ratios for the four core-ERQ in and for J1714+4148.

From this analysis, we propose two tentative results: the [ OIII ] lines are broad, with a most probable range 1700 FWHM _ OIII < 2300 km s ^ 1 (100 FWHM _ OIII ^ obs < 130 . The fits suggest that H is broader than [ OIII ] and is redshifted, although we cannot discard that this is an artificial result due to the noise of the H profile. Tentatively, 2000 FWHM _ H 3000 km s ^ 1 and 900 v _ H [ OIII ] 1300 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00008168.name: J0834+0159
gmw_00008168.overview: J0834+0159 is a high-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1711.01269] located at a redshift of z=2.59 [arXiv:1711.01269]. It is classified as a core extremely red quasar [arXiv:2001.03379] and exhibits ultraviolet broad-line emission features [arXiv:1711.01269] with exceptionally low ultraviolet line ratios [arXiv:2001.03379].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0883448 as its only high-redshift source at z=2.59, with UV broad-line emission features and an SED fit the authors consider inappropriate.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:HiZQSO shows the spectrum of J0883448, our only high-redshift source (z=2.59) . We conclude that J0883448 is a high redshift source as we are able to identify the emission features to be SiII 1309 , SiIV 1400 , CIV 1549 , and CIII 1909 . However, we note that the SED fit for this object, presented in Figure fig:SEDFit , is likely inappropriate. The existing rest-frame UV broad lines in the spectrum do not originate primarily from starlight, as suggested by the SED. It may be of interest to examine the SED of this object in more detail in the future.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0834+0159, with J2215-0056, as a core-ERQ with among the lowest UV line ratios, best matched by dense, metal-rich photoionization models.

quotes.quote: J0834+0159 and J2215-0056. They have among the lowest UV line ratios of core-ERQ. Based on their location in the diagrams NV/CIV vs. NV/HeII, NV/CIV vs. CIV/CIII ] , CIV/HeII vs. CIV/CIII ] , the optimum models have the following parameters (see Fig. 1 in Silva2019 ): Secondary N and primary C models. Models with n 10 ^ 6-8 cm ^ 3 and 3-5 Z_ are consistent with the location of the two objects in all diagrams mentioned above. These models are also consistent with NIV ] /CIV 0.3 measured in these two objects. No adequate secondary N and secondary C models are found.

gmw_00008169.name: J1232+0912
gmw_00008169.overview: J1232+0912 is an active galactic nucleus classified as an extremely red quasar [arXiv:2001.03379]. The object exhibits extreme emission line ratios, which strongly suggest the presence of high-density and high-metallicity gas [arXiv:2001.03379].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1232+0912, with J2323-0100, as an extreme core-ERQ whose strong NV and large CIV/HeII ratios imply dense and/or high-metallicity gas.

quotes.quote: J1232+0912 and J2323-0100. Their line ratios are rather extreme. NV is very strong compared with QSO2: NV/CIV 2 and NV/HeII 10, while QSO2 show in general NV/CIV < 1 and NV/HeII < 10. CIV/CIII ] 5 is also at the high end of the range of values spanned by QSO2. There are no optimum models within the parameter space explored by that can explain all line ratios simultaneously. On the other hand, the high NV ratios and large CIV/HeII strongly suggest high densities n> 10 ^6 cm ^ 3 and/or high metallicities Z 4 Z_ .

gmw_00008170.name: Spock galaxy
aliases_external: Spock arc / HFF14Spo host galaxy image
gmw_00008170.overview: The Spock galaxy is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.0054 [arXiv:2304.09222]. Its lensed image forms an elongated, merging arc that contains four observed transients interpreted as microlensed supergiant stars [arXiv:2304.09222].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the z=1.0054 lensed Spock galaxy, interpreting four transients as microlensed supergiant stars in a caustic-crossing arc.

quotes.quote: Then we focus on a previously known lensed galaxy at z=1.0054 , nicknamed Spock, which contains four previously reported transients. We interpret these transients as microcaustic crossings of SG stars and compute the probability of such events. Based on simplifications regarding the stellar evolution, we find that microlensing (by stars in the intracluster medium) of SG stars at z=1.0054 can fully explain these events.

The elongated arc from the Spock galaxy is shown in the centre of Fig. Fig_SpockCritCurves . The lensed image forms a long arc perpendicular to the main axis and the critical curve of the cluster. A third image (with a much smaller magnification 3.5 and shown in the upper-right corner of Fig. Fig_Arcs ) is found in the northern part of the cluster (and outside the field of view shown in Fig. Fig_SpockCritCurves ). The elongated arc is in fact a merging image showing only a small portion of the Spock galaxy.

gmw_00008171.name: SDSS J1019
gmw_00008171.overview: SDSS J1019 is a distant, massive active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:2402.02107]. It exhibits extreme color and extinction, and it has been shown to possess a high far-ultraviolet star formation rate [arXiv:2402.02107].

sources.summary: The paper masks SDSS J1019 nuclear emission, reports it has the highest FUV SFR, and groups it among distant massive AGN hosts with extreme color and extinction.

quotes.quote: Later on, we also masked the nuclei of SDSS J101920.83+490701.2 (see section global_SFR ).
SDSS J1019 showed the highest FUV SFR. Here, we have re-estimated the SFR after masking the nuclei.
We did notice that two of the AGN-hosted galaxies (viz., SDSS J1019, SDSS J1027) show extreme color as well as extinction. However, these are the most distant as well as massive systems.

gmw_00008172.name: GMP2923
gmw_00008172.overview: GMP2923 is a faint, blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1005.3874] that overlaps in the line of sight with the kinematically independent galaxy GMP2945 [arXiv:1202.1044]. It is associated with an ionized gas cloud characterized by two knotty, straight streams [arXiv:1202.1044] and a detached emission structure [arXiv:1005.3874].

sources.summary: GMP 2923 has an EIG with two knotty straight streams, a detached root extending to 50 kpc, and a monotonically increasing velocity field.

quotes.quote: GMP 2923 and GMP 3071 The EIG around GMP 2923 is shown in Figure 4d in YY10. Two galaxies --- GMP 2923 and GMP 2945 --- overlap in the line of sight near the EIG (Figure 3). We measured the radial velocity of the early-type galaxy GMP 2945 with the same slit mask as that used for the EIG; it is 6500 km s ^ 1 , much slower than those of the EIG and GMP 2923. Thus GMP 2945 is kinematically independent from the EIG. The EIG of GMP 2923 is characterized by two knotty straight streams (see Figure 4d in YY10). The opening angle of the streams is 20 degrees. The root of the streams is detached from the galaxy.

The kinematics of both the GMP 2923 and GMP 3071 EIGs also reveal a monotonically-increasing pattern. Figure 13 and 14 show the velocity fields of the EIGs of the two galaxies. The acceleration rates are 10 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 and 25 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 for GMP 2923 and GMP 3071, respectively.

sources.summary: GMP2923 is identified as the cloud parent rather than nearby GMP2945 and is classified among faint, blue dwarf parents with detached emission clouds.

quotes.quote: In this study, we used the spectroscopic result indicating that four clouds had similar redshifts to their respective parent galaxies (GMP2559, GMP2910, GMP2923, and GMP4060). In Figure fig:postage2923 , GMP2945, which lies just to the west of GMP2923, apparent to be the parent of the clouds, but GMP2945 had a markedly different redshift (z=0.0207) from the clouds (z 0.028). We identified GMP2923(z=0.0291) as the parent and GMP2945 as a chance overlap.

3 detached \ clouds: GMP2923, GMP3016, GMP4060, and GMP4232 have no apparent \ emission in the parent galaxy, only outside the galaxy. The color of the parent is very blue ( g-=0.27-0.30 ), and the magnitude is very faint ( > 16.8). The four galaxies are in fact the four least luminous galaxies among the parents.

gmw_00008173.name: GMP3779
gmw_00008173.overview: GMP3779 is a blue spiral galaxy [arXiv:2207.06624, arXiv:astro-ph/0109316] that exhibits core star formation and blobby, extended emission connected to its core [arXiv:1005.3874]. It displays an asymmetric gas distribution [arXiv:2207.06624, arXiv:astro-ph/0109316] with one-sided radio tails extending southwest beyond the optical host due to ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2207.06624, arXiv:astro-ph/0109316].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 58 as a blue Sa disk with asymmetric H I, southwest radio tails beyond the optical host, and RPS classification.

quotes.quote: 14 (j): Mrk 58 (a.k.a. KUG 1256 + 279) The optical host is a blue disk Sa galaxy KentandGunn . It shows a very asymmetric gas distribution; most of the H I gas is lies to the west side, while the east appears depleted MazzarellaandBalzano . The radio emission at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is one-sided showing asymmetric tails toward the southwest, extending beyond the optical host galaxy with the 550--850 MHz image showing smaller extent than 250--500 MHz image. The source is also classified as RPS galaxy see Sec. tab:tab3-rps Chenetal . The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 0 74 ( 20.7 kpc). The integrated spectral index is (400--700 MHz) = 0.76 0.11, and it agrees with S_ 1.4 GHz = 3.29 0.15 mJy, within uncertainties Chenetal .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 58 as a gas-deficient blue Sb disk projected on Coma X-ray emission, with offset asymmetric gas likely shaped by ram pressure.

quotes.quote: Mrk 58 This Sb, blue disk galaxy is projected onto the X--ray emission, some 20 ( 400 kpc) SW of NGC 4874, in the zone lying between the main cluster and the SW group. It is gas deficient by a factor of 3. Its map (Fig. 7) displays a considerably asymmetry, with the gas swept from the NE. The is observed at an offset position of 12 (4 kpc) SW from the optical disk. The observed distribution could be explained if we consider the supersonic velocity of Mrk 58 relative to the cluster, 1575 (the sound speed in Coma is estimated around 1460 by Stevens 1999); under these conditions the interaction with the ICM may produce ram pressure stripping to enhance the density behind the galaxy (Stevens 1999).

sources.summary: GMP3779 is one of the few parents with detected HI gas and is classified as having core star formation plus blobby extended emission connected to the core.

quotes.quote: Although the survey covered the entire field of this study, there were only three parents in which HI gas was detected (GMP2374, GMP2559, and GMP3779).
2 connected \ clouds without disk star formation: GMP2910, GMP3071, and GMP3779 exhibit core star formation and extended \ emission connected to the core. The extended clouds have a blobby structure.

gmw_00008174.name: GMP3816
gmw_00008174.overview: GMP3816 is a disturbed barred spiral galaxy in the Coma Cluster that exhibits a jellyfish morphology [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:2310.20417]. The galaxy features one-sided, northwest-directed tails with extraplanar star formation driven by ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2310.20417, arXiv:2207.06624].

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC4858 as the strongest outlier from the aging model, with excess 144 MHz and 1.5 GHz flux possibly linked to extraplanar star formation in its stripped tail.

quotes.quote: While the vast majority of flux-density measurements are well reproduced by the aging model (see Fig. fig:model_offsets ), the galaxy in the sample with the largest deviations from the aging model predictions is NGC4858. This is visible in the spectrum plots in Fig. fig:NGC4858_spec where two clear offsets between the model and data are visible. First, at = 0 the model significantly under-predicts the observed 144 MHz flux density, and second, at = 14 and = 21 the model under-predicts the observed 1.5 GHz flux density. The S/N of the deviations at 1.5 GHz are 5.4, 3.1, respectively.

NGC4858 is one of the poster children in the Coma Cluster for ongoing star formation in a ram pressure stripped tail smith2010,yagi2010 . Qualitatively, extra emission from ongoing star formation could potentially explain the offsets between the data and model in Fig. fig:NGC4858_spec . Synchrotron emission from recent star formation will have a flat spectral index, and thus when superimposed with steep-spectrum, aged plasma, the relative contribution from star formation will be most apparent at the high-frequency end. Therefore extra-planar star formation could, at least partially, account for the 1.5 GHz excess while only making a relatively small contribution at low frequencies.

sources.summary: GMP 3816/NGC 4858 is a disturbed barred spiral with jellyfish morphology, fan-like tails and knots, variable H-to-UV ratios, HI deficiency, and a nearby projected elliptical.

quotes.quote: GMP 3816 = NGC 4858 This disturbed barred spiral presents a ``jellyfish'' morphology in the GALEX image and in the MegaCam data (especially u band). Several tails and knots, are seen in a broad fan-like distribution, extending 0.5 arcmin (15 kpc) to the north-west, away from cluster centre. There are strong variations in H vs-UV flux ratio among the various filaments; in particular the brightest UV structure is not coincident with the strong central H feature. The galaxy is deficient in HI (Def _ HI >1.1 ), based on the upper limit to the gas mass published by Gavazzi et al. (2006). The galaxy is observed close in projection (0.6 arcmin or 20 kpc) to a large elliptical, GMP 3792, with 1500 \ radial velocity difference.

It is possible that these galaxies are physically interacting, although their relative velocity is quite large compared to the characteristic internal velocities, and hence strong mutual interactions are unlikely.

sources.summary: GMP3816 is a blue parent galaxy with galaxy-wide star formation, emission beyond the galaxy, and inclusion among UV-asymmetric gaseous-stripping systems.

quotes.quote: 1 connected \ clouds with disk star formation: GMP2374, GMP3271, and GMP3816 show galaxy-wide star formation and some regions of \ emission have flown out of the galaxies entirely. GMP3271 and GMP3816 are blue, while GMP2374 is as red as early-type galaxies and lies on the tip of the color-magnitude relation.

Their observed field overlapped with our field, and there were six galaxies in common (GMP2559, GMP2910, GMP3016, GMP3816, GMP4060, and GMP4232). It is striking that the UV asymmetric galaxies in our fields are only the six. This suggests that GSE galaxies have extended \ clouds with high probability.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4858 as an interacting Sb/RPS galaxy with one-sided northwest radio tails, a 22.1 kpc projected extent, and a steep spectrum.

quotes.quote: 13 (i): NGC 4858 The optical host is an Sb showing interaction with NGC 4860 (at z = 0.02645), which is located to the northeast BothunandDressler . The radio emission at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is one-sided, showing asymmetric tails toward the northwest, extending beyond the optical host galaxy with the 550--850 MHz image showing smaller extent than the 250--500 MHz image. The source is also classified as the RPS galaxy see Sec. tab:tab3-rps Chenetal . The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 0 79 ( 22.1 kpc). The integrated spectral index is steep, (400--700 MHz) = 1.07 0.10, and it agrees with S_ 1.4 GHz = 8.73 0.13 mJy, within uncertainties Chenetal .

gmw_00008175.name: GMP4060
gmw_00008175.overview: GMP4060 is a faint blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1005.3874] with a post-starburst spectrum [arXiv:1006.4867] and a non-star-forming disk [arXiv:2111.01140]. It features a ram-pressure-stripped tail [arXiv:2111.01140] containing extended filaments [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:1202.1044] and star-forming fireball knots [arXiv:1005.3874, arXiv:1006.4867].

sources.summary: GMP4060 has similar-redshift clouds, may be associated with GMP4017 cloud structure, and is classified as a faint blue dwarf with detached fireball emission.

quotes.quote: Note on GMP4017 clouds The \ clouds of GMP4017 lie along the line of the clouds of GMP4060, and are slightly elongated in the north-south direction (Figure fig:GMP4017a ). This implies that they may not be associated with GMP4017, but with GMP4060. If the GMP4017 clouds were in fact an extension of the GMP4060 clouds, the length of the GMP4060 cloud would be 144 kpc. As the redshift of GMP4017(0.0280) is similar to that of GMP4060(0.0292), it is difficult to identify the physical association from spectroscopy.

3 detached \ clouds: GMP2923, GMP3016, GMP4060, and GMP4232 have no apparent \ emission in the parent galaxy, only outside the galaxy. The color of the parent is very blue ( g-=0.27-0.30 ), and the magnitude is very faint ( > 16.8). The four galaxies are in fact the four least luminous galaxies among the parents. GMP3016 and GMP4060 show fireball features: \ emission following knots of young blue stars Yoshida2008 .

sources.summary: RB199 is described as an E+A Coma galaxy with an 80 kpc extended ionized-gas structure, star-forming knots and filaments, and steadily increasing filament velocities.

quotes.quote: RB199 The B , R_ C and H images of RB199 (fireball) were shown in our previous work (YY08). The characteristics of the EIG of RB199 can be summarized as follows: (1) The EIG consists of a number of bright star-forming knots, elongated blue filaments and more extended H emitting gas. Total length of the EIG is about 80 kpc.

Figure 5 shows the velocity field of the EIG of RB199. Relative radial velocities of the EIG filaments with respect to the systemic velocity of RB199 increase with the distance from the nucleus at almost a constant rate of 7 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 . The relative radial velocity of the most distant cloud of the EIG, H cloud 2 (YY08), is roughly consistent with the extrapolation of the line of constant increase, which suggests that this cloud is really a part of the EIG.

sources.summary: The paper describes GMP 4060 as a Coma dwarf with a ram-pressure-stripped H-emitting tail, a non-star-forming disk, and some of the strongest morphology-linked kinematic disturbances in the sample.

quotes.quote: Two Coma galaxies (GMP 4060 and GMP 2923) are known to exhibit spectacular tails of material stripped by the ram pressure of the hot intracluster medium with H emission suggesting current star formation. Their discs, unlike most other galaxies in the the Subaru SuprimeCam H survey of the Coma cluster, are not starforming.

GMP 4060, GMP 2640 and GMP 4188 have noticeable kinematical disturbances localised in the area of significant morphological irregularities. Detailed analysis of high-resolution HST images for the brightest galaxy in our sample, GMP 2640, revealed a large number of luminous star clusters in its central part whose asymmetric distributions on the sides of the slit causes the non-uniform slit illumination and, hence, affects the radial velocity measurements. Disturbances of kinematics profiles of GMP 4060 and GMP 4188 are also of the same origin.

GMP 4060 and GMP 3640 exhibit the most noticeable disturbances. However, they are substantially less significant in the Rc band compared to the bluer bands ( V and B ) and, therefore, they are likely caused by different stellar population properties (i.e. younger stellar age) rather than variations in the stellar mass density which would significantly affect the gravitational potential.

sources.summary: GMP 4060 has disturbed merger-remnant-like morphology, a post-starburst spectrum, optical tidal debris, a broad GALEX fan, and star-forming fireball knots in extended filaments.

quotes.quote: GMP 4060 This galaxy, discussed extensively by Yoshida et al. (2008), has a disturbed optical morphology suggestive of a merger remnant, and a post-starburst spectrum (Poggianti et al. 2004). There is some tidal stellar debris prominent in the optical images, to the west of the galaxy. In the GALEX images, the more spectacular feature is a broad fan of emission extending 1 arcmin (30 kpc) south of the galaxy, with several bright knots. Yoshida et al. used deep broadband and H imaging to reveal a complex network of filaments extending up to 2.7 arcmin 80 kpc from the galaxy. Embedded within these filaments are bright star-forming knots, which Yoshida et al. refer to as `fireballs', which in several cases are co-located with the UV emission peaks.

gmw_00008176.name: GMP4017
gmw_00008176.overview: GMP4017 is a galaxy with a redshift of 0.0280 [arXiv:1005.3874]. It features slightly elongated clouds that may be physically associated with the nearby galaxy GMP4060, though no HI emission has been detected around GMP4017 [arXiv:1005.3874].

sources.summary: GMP4017 has elongated clouds aligned with GMP4060; the paper questions their association, notes similar redshift, and reports no detected HI emission.

quotes.quote: Note on GMP4017 clouds The \ clouds of GMP4017 lie along the line of the clouds of GMP4060, and are slightly elongated in the north-south direction (Figure fig:GMP4017a ). This implies that they may not be associated with GMP4017, but with GMP4060. If the GMP4017 clouds were in fact an extension of the GMP4060 clouds, the length of the GMP4060 cloud would be 144 kpc. As the redshift of GMP4017(0.0280) is similar to that of GMP4060(0.0292), it is difficult to identify the physical association from spectroscopy.

HI observations may uncover the origin of the clouds of GMP4017. However, no HI emission has been detected around GMP4017 by previous deep HI surveys BravoAlfaro2000,BravoAlfaro2001 , with rms per channel of 0.20-0.40 mJy beam ^ 1 .

gmw_00008177.name: GMP4156
gmw_00008177.overview: GMP4156, also known as NGC 4853, is a red giant galaxy that may host a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1005.3874, arXiv:2207.06624]. It features detached emission clouds [arXiv:1005.3874] and exhibits slight low-frequency radio extensions toward the north that project up to 7.3 kpc [arXiv:2207.06624].

sources.summary: GMP4156 has detached emission superimposed on a red giant parent; its central excess and high line ratio may indicate a weak AGN.

quotes.quote: The clouds of GMP4017 and GMP4156 are also detached. However, the emission is superimposed on the stellar component of the parent galaxy, and the parents are red. The clouds near the two giant galaxies could be different from those near the other four dwarfs. We classified GMP2923, GMP3016, GMP4060, and GMP4232 as 3 blue dwarfs, and GMP4017 and GMP4156 as 3 red giants.

The central R- \ excess of GMP4156 was partly due to the saturation of our R-band image and red color of stellar components, although \ emission existed in the SDSS spectrum of 3 arcsec aperture. The SDSS spectrum showed a high [ NII ] / \ ratio of 0.85, which may indicate a weak active galactic nucleus (AGN).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4853 as an RPS galaxy with slight northward low-frequency radio extension beyond the optical host and a measured integrated spectrum.

quotes.quote: (e): NGC 4853 (a.k.a. GMP 4156) The 250--500 MHz band image shows slight extensions in radio emission toward the north, beyond the extent of the optical host, whereas, the extent in the 550-250 MHz image is similar to the extent of the optical host. The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 7.3 kpc. The integrated spectral index is (400--700 MHz) = 0.77 0.12, which agrees, within uncertainties with the measurement at 1.4 GHz see Table tab:tab3-rps , Chenetal .

gmw_00008178.name: GMP4232
gmw_00008178.overview: GMP4232 is a faint, very blue dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1005.3874] that is classified as a distorted possible spiral [arXiv:1006.4867]. It is a confirmed member of the Coma cluster [arXiv:1005.3874] and exhibits an emission tail [arXiv:1005.3874] with ultraviolet and u-band filaments to its southeast [arXiv:1006.4867].

sources.summary: GMP4232 is a confirmed Coma member with an emission tail, classified as a faint blue dwarf with detached clouds and included among UV-asymmetric stripping galaxies.

quotes.quote: Two of the three galaxies, GMP3016 and GMP4232, are members, which is confirmed by Hectospec spectroscopy, and had \ tails as shown in Fig 4.

3 detached \ clouds: GMP2923, GMP3016, GMP4060, and GMP4232 have no apparent \ emission in the parent galaxy, only outside the galaxy. The color of the parent is very blue ( g-=0.27-0.30 ), and the magnitude is very faint ( > 16.8). The four galaxies are in fact the four least luminous galaxies among the parents.

Their observed field overlapped with our field, and there were six galaxies in common (GMP2559, GMP2910, GMP3016, GMP3816, GMP4060, and GMP4232). It is striking that the UV asymmetric galaxies in our fields are only the six.

sources.summary: GMP 4232 is a distorted possible spiral with southeast UV and u-band filaments, a nearby S0 with similar velocity suggesting possible tidal interaction, and coincident H emission.

quotes.quote: GMP 4232 This is a distorted possibly spiral galaxy with an apparent trail to the south-east in the GALEX imaging. The MegaCam u band image shows a fan of faint filaments/knots 0.5 arcmin (15 kpc) south-east of the galaxy; beyond this the GALEX ``trail'' is probably spurious, caused by aligned unrelated galaxies. GMP 4232 is close in projection and radial velocity to an edge-on S0 (GMP 4255, at 0.6 arcmin or 20 kpc, with 290 \ radial velocity difference), so tidal interaction is a possibility. The INT H imaging shows one bright knot and some faint extended emission to the SE, coincident with the UV features.

gmw_00008179.name: Q0420-388
gmw_00008179.overview: Q0420-388 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1510.00029]. The distribution of surrounding Lyman-alpha emitters suggests a bi-conical illumination geometry from the quasar, which constrains its lifetime and opening angle [arXiv:1510.00029].

sources.summary: The paper uses emitters around Q0420-388 to test quasar illumination geometry, deriving lifetime/opening-angle constraints while warning that fluorescence is uncertain.

quotes.quote: Constraining the lifetime and opening angle of quasars using fluorescent Lyman emission: the case of Q0420-388 Elena Borisova 1,* , Simon J.

The azimuthal asymmetry in the distribution matches quite well that expected for a bi-conical illumination from the quasar. If we assume that these high equivalent width sources are being fluorescently illuminated, then we can already set significant constraints on the quasar lifetime and opening angle. On the other hand, we do not see some of the expected signatures at the current survey limits, including a radial fall off in luminosity or EW. Furthermore, the most distant sources would require the quasar to have been significantly brighter in the past if most of the is fluorescently reradiated quasar emission.

On the assumption that these high equivalent width objects are fluorescent emitters with emission boosted by the quasar emission, as assumed also by Trainor Steidel (2013) in their similar study, we have applied our model to derive constraints on the opening angle and lifetime of the quasar emission. The existence of two emission line sources at high projected distances of 15 pMpc requires a lifetime of at least 15 Myr for an opening angle of 60 or more, increasing to more than 40 Myr if the opening angle is reduced to a minimum 30 . The overall distribution of these sources across the field gives best fit lifetimes in the range 20 < < 50 Myr for opening angles in the range 90 < < 40 .

gmw_00008180.name: Coma P
gmw_00008180.overview: Coma P is an extremely faint dwarf irregular galaxy with a low optical surface brightness [arXiv:1801.06211]. Located in the Local Volume, it exhibits complicated neutral hydrogen gas dynamics and intriguing patterns of recent star formation [arXiv:1801.06211].

sources.summary: Coma P is presented as an extremely faint dwarf irregular with unusual HI content, low surface brightness, complex gas kinematics, and recent but weak star formation.

quotes.quote: We have presented new HI spectral line imaging of the curious Local Volume dwarf irregular galaxy Coma P (AGC 229385). This source is unique in many respects. It has an extremely low optical surface brightness, complicated HI gas dynamics, and very intriguing patterns of recent star formation. An understanding of the physical properties of this galaxy is important for galaxy evolution in the low-mass regime.

This galaxy is one of three HI line sources detected by ALFALFA at recessional velocities between 1282 and 1343 km s ^ 1 and in close angular proximity to each other. Remarkably, the new HST imaging presented in S. Brunker \ (2017, in preparation) has shown that Coma P is much closer than the original Virgocentric flow model of masters05 predicted (D 25 Mpc). At D = 5.50 0.28 Mpc, the total HI mass is M _ HI = (3.48 0.35) 10 ^ 7 , reshaping and complicating our interpretive framework for this already enigmatic source.

gmw_00008181.name: galaxy D
gmw_00008181.overview: Galaxy D is a giant elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.01 [arXiv:astro-ph/0006382]. It acts as a gravitational lens for the quasar MG2016+112 and is associated with a coherent cluster structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0006382].

sources.summary: The paper identifies galaxy D as a giant elliptical at z 1.01 between the quasar images, partly lensing MG2016+112 but requiring an associated massive cluster.

quotes.quote: Deep imaging revealed the existence of a giant elliptical galaxy at z 1.01 named D and located between the 3 images. It is identified as partly responsible for the multiple lensing of the quasar, although its mass is insufficient to explain the complex nature of the lensed images of MG2016+112. Detailed modeling requires another source of lensing mass, such as the mass of a rich cluster, centered on galaxy D .

An excess of galaxies around z=1 is, however, clearly seen and corresponds to the redshift of galaxy D already identified in Schneider et al. \ schneider86a . Two other concentrations are suspected which are also spatially coherent at z 0.66 and z 0.82 , with a somewhat lower significance. Most of our results on the multiple images of the radio source MG2016+112 are presented in Yamada et al. \ yamada00 who study in detail the emission-line properties of the images B and C. Note that this is the first spectrum of C observed in the optical (Fig. b1 ), after its identification as a third image by its Ly emission and its radio emission . The higher resolution of our spectrograph compared to the spectrum of B presented in Schneider et al. \ schneider85 allows to resolve the main emission lines and then to study the physics of the emitting regions in the source. Schneider et al. schneider86a claimed that 2 objects, labeled A1 and B1, detected close to the triple radio-source, were at the same redshift as the lensed source. They identified them from a narrow-band image of the field, centered on Ly redshifted to 3.26. A close look at our deep I and R images confirms the detection of B1 but not A1. B1 was positioned in one slit of Mask 2 and its spectrum (Fig. b1 ) confirms its redshift at z= 3.269 , similar but different from MG2016+112. Note however that the emission-line properties of B1 are quite different from those of B, with only Ly in emission. SiIV and CIV may be detected in absorption but with a lower confidence. If true, these lines are representative of a young stellar component in the source, rather than an active nucleus. In our spectroscopic sample, 9 objects have a redshift z 1 , with 8 new spectroscopic identifications (Fig. cluster-z1 ). This confirms that a coherent gravitational structure is associated with object D, previously suspected as the main lens .

gmw_00008182.name: NGC7785
gmw_00008182.overview: NGC7785 is an elliptical galaxy characterized by a de Vaucouleurs spheroid stellar mass distribution [arXiv:1807.10317]. The galaxy is surrounded by an extended X-ray halo that reaches out to a radius of 32 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1807.10317].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC7785 as an elliptical galaxy with a 32 kpc X-ray halo and de Vaucouleurs spheroid; the emergent-gravity fit is fair but struggles in the inner region.

quotes.quote: In particular, Memola et al.(2009) have built the mass model of two elliptical galaxies NGC7052 and NGC7785. Chandra X-ray Observatory has observed these sources, discussed in . Observations show that- NGC7052 and NGC7785 have an extended x-ray halo out to 16 \ kpc and 32 \ kpc respectively. The stellar mass distribution is well described by de Vacouleurs spheroid with the half-light radius R_e = 7.7 \ kpc and R_e = 5.6 \ kpc respectively.

The best fit models have: M_ sph =10^ 11.4 M_ for NGC7052 and M_ sph 10^ 11.9 M_ for NGC7785. The value of spheroid mass is similar to the one found in dark matter halo scenario. The fit is excellent for NGC7052 and fairly good for NGC7785. However, concerning the NGC7785, dark halo plus spheroid model have shown the same difficulties in fitting the inner-most region.

gmw_00008183.name: M-7977-09102
gmw_00008183.overview: M-7977-09102 is a giant galaxy located at a distance of 251.4 Mpc [arXiv:2108.11264]. It has a close satellite galaxy, and an area of enhanced gas velocity dispersion is located at the edge of M-7977-09102 near this companion [arXiv:2108.11264].

sources.summary: The paper describes M-7977-09102 as a giant MaNGA galaxy with a close satellite; its enhanced gas-velocity-dispersion spot lies on the edge near that satellite and shows intermediate BPT spectra.

quotes.quote: This is a giant galaxy of mass 10 ^ 10.935 M_ at a distance of 251.4 Mpc. The M-7977-09102 galaxy has a close satellite (SDSS J221119.18+121116.0) of mass 10 ^ 9.489 M_ . The separation between M-7977-09102 and its satellite galaxy on the sky plane is 14 Kpc (that is comparable to the optical radius of M-7977-09102, R_ 25 = 12.8 Kpc) and the separation in the line-of-sight velocity is d V_ los 230 km/s (that is comparable with the variation of the V _ los across the image of M-7977-09102, see panel b of Fig. figure:m-7977-09102 ).

The field of view of the MaNGA measurement covers partly the satellite. The enhanced gas velocity dispersion takes place in the region at the edge of M-7977-09102, which is close to the satellite. The emission lines in the spectra of the spaxels within the spot of the enhanced gas velocity dispersion are single-peaked, see panels f2 and f3 of Fig. figure:m-7977-09102 . This evidences against a false enhancement of the lines width due to two separate lines (M-7977-09102 and satellite). Some spaxels in the spot show the spectra of intermediate BPT type.

gmw_00008184.name: M-8080-09101
gmw_00008184.overview: M-8080-09101 is an emission line galaxy that is interacting with a close satellite companion [arXiv:2108.11264]. This interaction has produced enhanced gas velocity dispersion on the facing sides of both galaxies, as well as oxygen abundance patterns that suggest gas exchange [arXiv:2108.11264].

sources.summary: The paper describes M-8080-09101 as interacting with a close satellite, with enhanced gas velocity dispersion on both facing sides and abundance patterns suggesting gas exchange.

quotes.quote: The M-8080-09101 galaxy has a close satellite (SDSS J030922.02-002440.5) of mass 10 ^ 9.489 M_ . The separation between the M-8080-09101 galaxy and its satellite in the sky plane is comparable to the optical radius of M-8080-09101, and the separation in the line-of-sight velocity is comparable to the variation of the V _ los across the image of M-8080-09101, see panel b of Fig. figure:m-8080-09101 .

The enhanced gas velocity dispersion can be seen in both, the region of M-8080-09101 which is close to the satellite, and the region of the satellite which is close to M-8080-09101. The lines in the spectra of the spaxels with enhanced gas velocity dispersion are single-peaked, see panels f2 and f3 of Fig. figure:m-8080-09101 . The gas-phase oxygen abundance in the satellite is lower by a factor of around three as compared to the oxygen abundance in M-8080-09101 (panel of Fig. figure:m-8080-09101 ).

However, the oxygen abundance at the edge of the satellite close to M-8080-09101 is higher than in the rest of the satellite. This can be an indicator of the gas exchange between M-8080-09101 and its satellite.

gmw_00008185.name: M-8244-09101
gmw_00008185.overview: M-8244-09101 is a massive galaxy located at a distance of 241.6 Mpc [arXiv:2108.11264]. It features an off-centered spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion and lower oxygen abundance, which may indicate the projection of a satellite galaxy [arXiv:2108.11264].

sources.summary: The paper describes M-8244-09101 as a massive MaNGA galaxy with an off-centered enhanced-dispersion spot, lower oxygen abundance at that radius, and evidence consistent with projected-satellite interaction.

quotes.quote: This galaxy has a mass of 10 ^ 11.201 M_ , and it is at a distance of 241.6.7 Mpc. There is a spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion at a galactocentric distance of around 15 Kpc ( 0.8 of the optical radius R_ 25 , panels d1 and d2 of Fig. figure:m-8244-09101 ). The gas-phase oxygen abundance in the spot is lower in comparison to the abundance at this radius (panels d1 and d2 of Fig. figure:m-8244-09101 ). The lines in the spectra of the spaxels with enhanced gas velocity dispersion are single-peaked, see panels f2 and f3 of Fig. figure:m-8244-09101 .

The spots of enhanced in four other galaxies (M-8244-09101, M-8568-12703, M-8716-12703, and M-8984-12705) are located within the optical radius, at the fractional radii from 0.6 to 0.8 optical radius. The spots of the enhanced in three of those galaxies are related to the bright spots in the photometric B band (see images for those galaxies in Fig. figure:images , and the maps of the surface brightness across the images for those galaxies in Fig. figure:m-8244-09101 , Fig. figure:m-8568-12703 , and Fig. figure:m-8984-12705 , as well as the comments for those galaxies above). This could be an indicator of a projection of a satellite on the galaxy.

gmw_00008186.name: M-8568-12703
gmw_00008186.overview: M-8568-12703 is a galaxy located at a distance of 221.2 Mpc with a stellar mass of 10^10.469 solar masses [arXiv:2108.11264]. It features an off-centered spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion and surface brightness, which may indicate a projected satellite interaction [arXiv:2108.11264].

sources.summary: The paper describes M-8568-12703 as a MaNGA galaxy with a large off-centered enhanced-dispersion spot visible in brightness and velocity maps and interpreted as possible projected-satellite interaction.

quotes.quote: This galaxy has a mass of 10 ^ 10.469 M_ and it is at a distance of 221.2 Mpc. There is a large spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion at a galactocentric distance of around 11 Kpc ( 0.8 of the optical radius R_ 25 , panels d1 and d2 of Fig. figure:m-8568-12703 ). The spot is also revealed on the surface brightness and the line-of-sight velocity maps. The lines in the spectra of the spaxels with enhanced gas velocity dispersion are single-peaked, see panels f2 and f3 of Fig. figure:m-8568-12703 .

The spots of enhanced in four other galaxies (M-8244-09101, M-8568-12703, M-8716-12703, and M-8984-12705) are located within the optical radius, at the fractional radii from 0.6 to 0.8 optical radius. The spots of the enhanced in three of those galaxies are related to the bright spots in the photometric B band (see images for those galaxies in Fig. figure:images , and the maps of the surface brightness across the images for those galaxies in Fig. figure:m-8244-09101 , Fig. figure:m-8568-12703 , and Fig. figure:m-8984-12705 , as well as the comments for those galaxies above). This could be an indicator of a projection of a satellite on the galaxy.

gmw_00008187.name: M-8716-12703
gmw_00008187.overview: M-8716-12703 is a galaxy with a stellar mass of 10^9.770 solar masses located at a distance of 174.5 Mpc [arXiv:2108.11264]. It features an off-centered spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion that exhibits controversial properties resembling both a central active galactic nucleus and an extranuclear LINER [arXiv:2108.11264].

sources.summary: The paper treats M-8716-12703 as unusual: its off-centered enhanced-dispersion spot has AGN-like radiation and controversial properties resembling both M-8984-12705 central AGN and extranuclear LINER behavior.

quotes.quote: This galaxy has a mass of 10 ^ 9.770 M_ and it is at a distance of 174.5 Mpc. There is a spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion at a galactocentric distance of around 6 Kpc ( 0.7 of the optical radius R_ 25 , panels d1 and d2 of Fig. figure:m-8716-12703 ). The configuration of the radiation distribution in the spot resembles the configuration of the radiation distribution in a circumnuclear AGN, that is the innermost region of the AGN-like radiation is surrounded by a ring of radiation of the intermediate type (panel e of Fig. figure:m-8716-12703 ). The spot is not exhibited appreciable on the surface brightness and the line-of-sight velocity maps. The lines in the spectra of the spaxels with enhanced gas velocity dispersion are single-peaked, see panels f2 and f3 of Fig. figure:m-8716-12703 .

Thus, the properties of the off-centered AGN-like spot in the galaxy M-8716-12703 are controversial. From one hand, the radiation distribution configuration of the off-centered spot in the galaxy M-8716-12703 is similar to that of the central AGN in the galaxy M-8984-12705, in both cases the innermost region of the AGN-like radiation is surrounded by a ring of radiation of the intermediate type. On other hand, the relation between the gas velocity dispersion and the [ N ii ] 6584/H line ratio for individual spaxels in the spot in M-8716-12703, is similar to that of the extranuclear LINER in the galaxy M-8984-12705, and differs strongly from that for the central AGN in the galaxy M-8984-12705.

gmw_00008188.name: M-8984-12705
gmw_00008188.overview: M-8984-12705, also known as NGC 5251, is an isolated S0-a galaxy hosting a central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2108.11264]. The galaxy features a bright hydrogen-alpha flux ring, extranuclear LINER-type regions, and an off-centered spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion with reduced oxygen abundance [arXiv:2108.11264].

sources.summary: The paper identifies M-8984-12705 as isolated S0-a NGC 5251, with an off-centered enhanced-dispersion spot, reduced oxygen abundance, a bright H-flux ring, central AGN, and extranuclear LINER regions.

quotes.quote: Also known as NGC 5251, this galaxy is an isolated S0-a (HyperLeda database) galaxy of mass 10 ^ 11.292 M_ at a distance of 158.3 Mpc.
