There is a spot of enhanced gas velocity dispersion at a galactocentric distance of around 12 Kpc ( 0.6 of the optical radius R_ 25 , panels d1 and d2 of Fig. figure:m-8984-12705 ). The gas-phase oxygen abundance in the spot is reduced in comparison to the abundance at this radius (panel of Fig. figure:m-8984-12705 ). The spot is also a prominent feature on the surface brightness map.

A prominent feature in the distribution of the H flux in the image of the galaxy M-8984-12705 is the presence of a bright ring. Panel b in Fig. figure:xbpt-sv shows the locations of the spaxels with spectra of different BPT types (the AGN-like, H ii region-like, and intermediate) on the image of the galaxy M-8984-12705. A comparison between panel a and panel b in Fig. figure:xbpt-sv shows that the spaxel spectra are H ii region-like in the areas of high star formation rate, and that the spectra are intermediate or AGN-like types correspond to areas of low H flux. The galaxy M-8984-12705 hosts the AGN at its center and extranuclear LINERs-type regions.

gmw_00008189.name: M-9031-06104
gmw_00008189.overview: M-9031-06104 is a galaxy with a stellar mass of $10^{10.019} M_{\odot}$ located at a distance of 185.1 Mpc [arXiv:2108.11264]. It has a close satellite galaxy, and an enhanced gas velocity dispersion is observed at the edge facing this companion [arXiv:2108.11264].

sources.summary: The paper describes M-9031-06104 as a MaNGA galaxy with a close satellite, similar line-of-sight velocities, and an enhanced gas-velocity-dispersion spot at the satellite-facing edge.

quotes.quote: This galaxy has a mass 10 ^ 10.019 M_ and it is at a distance of 185.1 Mpc. The M-9031-06104 galaxy has a close satellite. The separation between M-9031-06104 and its satellite exceeds slightly the optical radius of M-9031-06104 ( R_ 25 = 8.5 Kpc), the line-of-sight velocities of M-9031-06104 and its satellite are close to each other, Fig. figure:m-9031-06104 . The field of view of the MaNGA measurement covers partly the satellite. The enhanced gas velocity dispersion takes place at the edge of M-9031-06104 which looks to the satellite. The emission lines in the spectra of the spaxels with enhanced gas velocity dispersion are single-peaked, see panels f2 and f3 of Fig. figure:m-9031-06104 .

gmw_00008190.name: HSC J1152+0047
gmw_00008190.overview: HSC J1152+0047 is a quadruply lensed source located at a redshift of 3.76 [arXiv:1608.06288]. It is most likely a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus, though it may instead be an unusually compact Lyman-break or Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1608.06288, arXiv:1909.00120].

sources.summary: The paper reports HSC J115252+004733 as a z=3.76 quadruply lensed source, probably a low-luminosity AGN though a compact bright galaxy remains possible, with unusual flux-ratio behavior.

quotes.quote: We report the serendipitous discovery of a quadruply lensed source at z_ s =3.76 , HSC J115252+004733, from the Hyper Suprime-Cam (HSC) Survey. The source is lensed by an early-type galaxy at z_ l =0.466 and a satellite galaxy. Here, we investigate the properties of the source by studying its size and luminosity from the imaging and the luminosity and velocity width of the Ly- line from the spectrum. Our analyses suggest that the source is most probably a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus (LLAGN) but the possibility of it being a compact bright galaxy (e.g., a Lyman- emitter or Lyman Break Galaxy) cannot be excluded. The brighter pair of lensed images appears point-like except in the HSC i band (with a seeing 0 5 ).

The source appears point-like in all bands except i band where only the brighter pair of lensed images suggest extendedness in the tangential direction. This could suggest that we are detecting emission from the underlying AGN host galaxy. Alternatively, if there is no AGN, then the source (with _ eff 0.2 kpc) is one of the most compact galaxies at z 4 .

sources.summary: The paper treats G1 as a probable satellite of the main lens galaxy, too faint for spectroscopy, with a photometric redshift consistent with being gravitationally bound to galaxy G.

quotes.quote: A second galaxy (G1) located very close to image C, is probably a satellite of the main lens galaxy G and is likely to perturb the lens potential. The lens galaxy G is fit with a double Sersic model and the companion galaxy G1 is fit with a PSF model. The flux of image C is likely contaminated by the emission from satellite galaxy (G1) in the reddest ( Y ) band where G1 becomes more prominent (see fig:fxrat and tab:phot ).

The companion galaxy G1 is aligned with the fainter pair and fell on the second slit but was too faint to be detected. As mentioned earlier, we do not have a spectrum of G1 but our photometric redshift estimate ( z_ l G1 =0.46 0.15 ) suggests that G1 is a gravitationally bound satellite of galaxy G. Galaxy G is allowed to have ellipticity whereas galaxy G1 is assumed to have spherically symmetric mass distribution since G1 is less massive and the contribution from the quadrupole moment of its mass distribution is not expected to be significant.

sources.summary: The paper uses HSC J1152+0047 as a prior HSC quadruply lensed source, emphasizing that it is unusually compact compared with other z~4 LBGs or LAEs and comparable to HSC J0904--0102.

quotes.quote: Together with a previously discovered SuGOHI lens, HSC J1152+0047, that is similarly compact, we believe that the HSC Survey is extending LBG studies down to smaller galaxy sizes.

In fig:shibuya15 , we show another quad, lens HSC J1152+0047 henceforth, referred to as J1152 More+17 , also discovered serendipitously from HSC. The J1152 quad also happens to be unusually compact compared to the rest of the LBGs or Lyman Alpha Emitters (LAEs) at z 4 .

Overall, we did not find any lensed LBGs in the literature as compact as our source and J1152. Similar to J1152, our lens system is also located in the footprint of other surveys such as the KiDS Kuijken+19 , PanSTARRS Chambers+16 , and SDSS DR15 Aguado+19 but is barely detectable or identifiable as a lens owing to the shallower depth of those surveys coupled with the faintness of the lens system (see panels on the left in fig:shibuya15images ).

gmw_00008191.name: NGC 7507
gmw_00008191.overview: NGC 7507 is a massive, bright elliptical galaxy located in the field [arXiv:1305.1335, arXiv:astro-ph/0501090]. It features a central dust lane [arXiv:1305.1335] and a counter-rotating outer stellar halo [arXiv:1412.3402], and it is considered a candidate dark-matter-free galaxy [arXiv:1412.3402, arXiv:2311.12598].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7507's stellar halo kinematics, finding counter-rotation, merger-remnant signatures, and dynamics consistent with little or no dark matter compared with CDM expectations.

quotes.quote: NGC 7507 has a two component stellar halo, with the outer halo counter rotating with respect to the inner halo, with a kinematic boundary at a radius of 110'' ( 12.4 kpc). The velocity dispersion profile exhibits an increase at 70'' ( 7.9 kpc), reminiscent of several other elliptical galaxies. Our best fit models are those under mild anisotropy which include 100 times less dark matter than predicted by CDM, although mildly anisotropic models that are completely dark matter free fit the measured dynamics almost equally well. Our MONDian models, both isotropic and anisotropic, systematically fail to reproduce the measured velocity dispersions at almost all radii. The counter rotating outer halo implies a merger remnant, as does the increase in velocity dispersion at 70'' .

Our Jeans models are completely consistent with a no dark matter scenario, however, some dark matter can be accommodated, although at much lower concentrations that predicted by CDM simulations. This indicates that NGC 7507 may be a dark matter free elliptical galaxy. Whether NGC 7507 is completely dark matter free or very dark matter poor, this is at odds with predictions from current CDM cosmological simulations.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7507 as a massive field elliptical with a very poor globular-cluster system, low dark matter content, dust, and possible past gas-rich infall.

quotes.quote: Although not completely isolated, NGC 7507 is a massive field elliptical galaxy with an apparently very low dark matter content. We determine the richness, the colour distribution, and the structural properties of the GCS of NGC 7507. We perform wide-field Washington photometry with data obtained with the MOSAIC II camera at the 4m-Blanco telescope, CTIO.

Dust in NGC 7507 Morphologically, NGC 7507 has, except for a central dust lane spa85 , a smooth appearance without obvious shells or tidal structures (the feature reported by tal09 had not been confirmed by sal12 ).
The existence of dust in an otherwise morphologically undisturbed galaxy could be evidence of a past merger and is perhaps related to the existence of the intermediate population of GCs.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7507 as a key isolated elliptical comparison, emphasizing that improved modeling made it a candidate dark-matter-free or pure-baryonic exception needing fuller velocity-field data.

quotes.quote: In the sample of gerhard01 , the isolated elliptical NGC 7507 was the most dark matter dominated galaxy. In strong contrast, salinas12,lane15 using much more complete data, were able to produce models of NGC 7507 that did not require any dark matter. All these data let the ''pure baryonic'' galaxy NGC 7507 so far appear as a not-understood exception. At the isolated ellipticals end, NGC 7507 stands still as a candidate galaxy without dark matter. Perhaps the observation of a full velocity field will bring clarity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7507 as a very bright, low-redshift putative parent whose surrounding quasar cone is argued to align with Karlsson peak redshifts.

quotes.quote: It is clear that the quasars in the field form a rather dense cone-like distribution having at its apex the very bright and very low redshift object NGC 7507 and the companion galaxies that make up its group (Arp/Madore 2309-284). If NGC 7507 is taken as the putative parent then, according to the basic hypothesis and since this object has an extremely small redshift at z=0.005 , we should expect to find a strong bias to the Karlsson peaks in the raw data. In Figure 6 the observed redshifts are written next to each quasar. Filled circles indicate redshifts falling within the 50 probability circle around the Karlsson peaks (that is, within a region 0.022 centred on each peak on the _ 10 (1+z) axis), and open circles indicate redshifts falling outside the 50 circle. It is clear that the quasars stretching up and to the NW from NGC 7507 are almost all near the Karlsson peak values.

But in addition to the high probabilities for the realities of the periodicity, the physical association of these quasars with the bright galaxy is reinforced because it is possible to draw a cone shaped perimeter, with NGC 7507 at one end, which contains 15 quasars, only one of which does not fall near a Karlsson peak . It seems that this would strongly support the ejection origin for the excess number of quasars from this very low redshift (z = .005), bright galaxy.

gmw_00008192.name: HIPEQ1303+03
gmw_00008192.overview: HIPEQ1303+03 is a massive low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:0809.1434]. It is characterized as an anaemic spiral with a blue color, a modest total luminosity, and a substantial neutral hydrogen content [arXiv:0809.1434].

sources.summary: The paper describes HIPEQ1303+03 as a massive low-surface-brightness anaemic spiral with substantial dynamical mass, modest luminosity, significant H I content, and blue color.

quotes.quote: Our second class-type is the low surface brightness galaxy of the more massive kind - typically an anaemic spiral like HIPEQ1303+03 (UGC08153) shown in Fig. 12b (other good examples are HIPEQ1228+02 and 2337+00). It truly is low SB with a _ 50 (g) = 24.3 and yet its dynamical mass is 10 ^ 10.9 M _ . Formally speaking there are even more massive LSBGs in the sample but their discs are so extremely dim that estimating an accurate axis-ratio, necessary to calculate a dynamical mass, often becomes problematic. The total luminosity is 0.5 L _ * [ M _ g = -18.8 ] and the Hydrogen content is log _ 10 M _ H I = 9.0 [ M _ H I /L _ B = 0.77 ] . The colour is blue, (g ) = 0.27, as is commonly the case with LSBGs (McGaugh et al. 1994) but there are also some reddish ones in the survey.

gmw_00008193.name: HIPEQ0821 00
gmw_00008193.overview: HIPEQ0821 00 is a gas-rich irregular galaxy [arXiv:0809.1434]. It is characterized by a low luminosity, a very blue color, and a moderately high surface brightness [arXiv:0809.1434].

sources.summary: The paper uses HIPEQ0821 00 as an irregular-galaxy example, emphasizing its gas richness, low luminosity, very blue color, and moderately high surface brightness.

quotes.quote: Our third type is the irregular for which HIPEQ0821 00 (UGC04358) in Fig. 12c serves as an example though they are very heterogeneous. ES irregulars are naturally gas-rich, HIPEQ0821 00 having (log M _ H I = 9.3) almost as much as the Milky Way with an M _ H I /L _ B = 3.3. At M _ g = 16.4 it is L _ * /20, has a very blue colour [ (g ) = 0.17 ] and moderately high SB ( _ 50 (g) = 22.5) though SB's among the type vary by 5 magnitudes and global SB's for such irregular objects are rather meaningless.

gmw_00008194.name: HIPEQ1145+02
gmw_00008194.overview: HIPEQ1145+02 is an extremely faint, low surface brightness galaxy that is barely visible in SDSS imaging [arXiv:0809.1434]. It is characterized by a blue color and an extremely high hydrogen gas content relative to its luminosity [arXiv:0809.1434].

sources.summary: The paper presents HIPEQ1145+02 as an inchoate, extremely faint blind-survey object, barely visible in SDSS but detectable through high H I content and blue color.

quotes.quote: Our example HIPEQ1145+02 [ Fig. 12d ] can barely be seen on the SDSS, having a SB _ 50 (g) = 24.8 at the very limit of the survey. We detect it only because it has a lot of hydrogen for its luminosity (M _ H I /L _ B = 7) which at M _ g = 14.31 is 1/200 L _ * .
In addition to being extremely gas-rich (M _ H I /L _ B > 5) they are generally blue, thus HIPEQ1145+02, by no means extreme in colour, has B V = 0.48 and (g ) = 0.27.

gmw_00008195.name: AM 0053-353
gmw_00008195.overview: AM 0053-353 is classified as an axis-symmetric, O-type collisional ring galaxy [arXiv:1308.2024]. Its ring structure is believed to originate from a head-on collision with a dwarf galaxy moving along its symmetric axis [arXiv:1308.2024].

sources.summary: The paper treats AM 0053-353 as one of six axis-symmetric O-type collisional ring galaxies and argues N-body head-on dwarf collisions can reproduce their rings at early ring-generation stages.

quotes.quote: The Axis-Symmetric Ring Galaxies: AM 0053-353, AM 0147-350, AM 1133-245, AM 1413-243, AM 2302-322, ARP 318, and Head-On Penetrations Yu-Ting Wu and Ing-Guey Jiang Department of Physics and Institute of Astronomy, National Tsing-Hua University, Hsin-Chu, Taiwan Axis-symmetric ring systems can be identified from the new catalog of collisional ring galaxies in Madore et al. (2009). These are O-type-like collisional ring galaxies. Head-on collisions by dwarf galaxies moving along the symmetric axis were performed through N-body simulations to address their origins.

It was found that the simulations with smaller initial relative velocities between two galaxies, or the cases with heavier dwarf galaxies, could produce rings with higher density contrasts. There are more than one generation of rings in one collision and the lifetime of any generation of rings is about one dynamical time. It was concluded that head-on penetrations could explain these O-type-like ring galaxies identified from the new catalog in Madore et al. (2009), and the simulated rings resembling the observational O-type-like collisional rings are those at the early stage of one of the ring-generations.

gmw_00008200.name: IRAS 14026
gmw_00008200.overview: IRAS 14026 is classified as a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1408.3633]. Observations reveal that the object is strongly X-ray weak and was not detected in NuSTAR imaging [arXiv:1408.3633].

sources.summary: The paper includes IRAS 14026 in its NuSTAR BAL-quasar sample, finding the targets strongly X-ray weak and this object undetected in the imaging.

quotes.quote: The _ OX constraints for the targets are shown in Figure 2, which indicate that their rest-frame 2  keV luminosities are 14 to >330 times weaker than expected from the Steffen2006 _ OX - L_ 2500  relation. These _ OX values have not been corrected for any intrinsic optical/UV reddening, which would render the values even more negative. Note that the spread of the Steffen2006 data points constrains the expected amount of quasar variability e.g., Gibson2012 , and after accounting for the measured flux variability of our targets (see Section 3 below), they are still significantly X-ray weak.

The other three targets, PG 0043, IRAS 07598, and IRAS 14026, are not detected; we also verified the non-detections via visual inspection of the smoothed images. None of the targets is detected in the 24--79 keV band, and the constraints from the non-detections in this band are not as tight as those in the other bands.

gmw_00008201.name: PG 1001
gmw_00008201.overview: PG 1001 is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2212.07001] and a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1408.3633, arXiv:2212.07001]. The system exhibits fast outflowing warm absorbers [arXiv:2212.07001] and is characterized by extremely weak X-ray emission [arXiv:1408.3633, arXiv:2212.07001].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PG 1001+054 as an NLS1/BAL quasar and reports fast ultraviolet warm absorbers whose velocities, ionization, locations, and kinetic power may connect to AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: PG 1001+054 (z=0.16012, from NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu ) is classified as NLS1 galaxy, with a FWHM of H 1740 ( Wills+etal+2000 ). Meanwhile, the broad and Ly absorption lines in UV spectrum (a also make it qualified as a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar Brandt+etal+2000 ; Wills+etal+2000 ; Wang+etal+2000 ), a subclass with extremely weak X-ray emission.

We report on the discovery of fast outflowing warm absorbers (WAs) in the NLS1 PG 1001+054, with velocities in the range of 7000-9000 km s ^ 1 . They are identified with blueshifted Ly , \ and \ lines in the high resolution ultraviolet (UV) spectra taken with the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph (COS) onboard the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). We perform photoionization modeling using XSTAR with three WAs. The derived physical properties are typical of WAs in terms of ionization and column density, whereas the outflow velocities are significantly higher. The estimated location of these WAs ranges from 1 to 73 parsecs away from the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper includes PG 1001 in its NuSTAR BAL-quasar sample, finding the targets strongly X-ray weak and this object detected in at least one band.

quotes.quote: The _ OX constraints for the targets are shown in Figure 2, which indicate that their rest-frame 2  keV luminosities are 14 to >330 times weaker than expected from the Steffen2006 _ OX - L_ 2500  relation. These _ OX values have not been corrected for any intrinsic optical/UV reddening, which would render the values even more negative. Note that the spread of the Steffen2006 data points constrains the expected amount of quasar variability e.g., Gibson2012 , and after accounting for the measured flux variability of our targets (see Section 3 below), they are still significantly X-ray weak.

PG 0946, PG 1001, and PG 1254 are detected in at least one band in each FPM. The minimum positional offsets (Table  tbl-obs ) between the optical and 3--24 keV positions are within expectations for faint sources. The chance of getting any spurious detections by wavdetect at these known source positions is negligible.

gmw_00008202.name: PG 1254
gmw_00008202.overview: PG 1254 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1408.3633]. Observations show that the object is strongly X-ray weak, though it has been detected in at least one band [arXiv:1408.3633].

sources.summary: The paper includes PG 1254 in its NuSTAR BAL-quasar sample, finding the targets strongly X-ray weak and this object detected in at least one band.

quotes.quote: The _ OX constraints for the targets are shown in Figure 2, which indicate that their rest-frame 2  keV luminosities are 14 to >330 times weaker than expected from the Steffen2006 _ OX - L_ 2500  relation. Note that the spread of the Steffen2006 data points constrains the expected amount of quasar variability e.g., Gibson2012 , and after accounting for the measured flux variability of our targets (see Section 3 below), they are still significantly X-ray weak.

PG 0946, PG 1001, and PG 1254 are detected in at least one band in each FPM. The minimum positional offsets (Table  tbl-obs ) between the optical and 3--24 keV positions are within expectations for faint sources. The chance of getting any spurious detections by wavdetect at these known source positions is negligible.

gmw_00008203.name: SDSSJ125636.63 271503.6
gmw_00008203.overview: SDSSJ125636.63 271503.6 is a faint dwarf galaxy in the Coma Cluster with a magnitude of F814W = -15.9 [arXiv:1409.4766]. It is a non-nucleated galaxy that hosts several physically associated clusters and features a slightly redder core [arXiv:1409.4766].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ125636.63 271503.6 as a non-nucleated Coma dwarf example, noting nearby associated clusters, faint luminosity, older color, and a slightly redder core.

quotes.quote: As an example, we show in Fig fig:example_nonnuc the galaxy SDSSJ125636.63 271503.6, which, since it is in class 2, belongs to Coma with an 90 probability. The galaxy is not nucleated, but shows several clusters within one effective radius, though all of them several pixels away from the photometric centre. The spatial coincidence of these clusters and the galaxy implies that they are physically associated. The galaxy, found in one of the more remote tiles (visit 63), is rather faint (F814W = -15.9), and the colour, which compared to other galaxies in the sample is typical for a galaxy of this magnitude, implies an SSP age (assuming [ Z/H ] =- 0.3, half the solar metallicity) of more than 3 Gyr. The core of this galaxy appears to be slightly redder than the outer parts, with no indication of the bluer central colour often found for NSCs.

gmw_00008204.name: SDSS J113009.40+495247.9
gmw_00008204.overview: SDSS J113009.40+495247.9 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1907.13535]. The object exhibits absorption line variability driven by ionization changes in response to fluctuations in its ionizing continuum [arXiv:1907.13535].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes two-epoch spectra of SDSS J113009.40+495247.9, arguing its BAL systems transition from Type S to Type N as one clumped outflow changes ionization, column density, and structure.

quotes.quote: We study the relationship between the broad absorption lines (BALs) that can be decomposed into multiple narrow absorption lines and those that cannot (hereafter Type N and Type S BAL, respectively), based on the analysis of three BAL systems (systems A, B, and C) in two-epoch spectra of quasar SDSS J113009.40+495247.9 (hereafter J1130+4952). As the velocity decreases (from systems A to C), these three BAL systems show a gradual transition from Type S to Type N BAL, and their equivalent widths (EWs) and profile shapes vary in a regular way. We ascribe the absorption line variability in J1130+4952 to the ionization change as a response to the fluctuation of the ionizing continuum based on several factors: (1) coordinated EW strengthening over a wide range in systems A and B, (2) the system B shows an obvious change in Si IV but no significant change in C IV BAL, and (3) asynchronized variability between the continuum and absorption lines. Based on the analysis of the variation mechanism, location, ionization state, and structure of systems A, B, and C, we hold the view that the Type S and Type N BALs in J1130+4952 probe the same clumped outflow, with the Type S BALs originating from the inner part of the outflow with a relatively higher ionization state, smaller column density and more clumpy structures, while the Type N BALs originate from the outer part of the outflow with relatively lower ionization state, larger column density, and fewer clumpy structures.

gmw_00008205.name: BCG of WARPJ1415
gmw_00008205.overview: The BCG of WARPJ1415 is a massive, luminous central red galaxy [arXiv:1111.3642]. It hosts a central radio source that is spatially coincident with the peak of the cluster's X-ray emission and exhibits star formation [arXiv:1111.3642].

sources.summary: The paper describes the BCG of WARPJ1415 as an unusually massive central red galaxy with coincident radio/X-ray emission, [OII]-inferred star formation, and feedback coupling to the cool core.

quotes.quote: The BCG of WARPJ1415 is very large, luminous and unusually massive, with a reported stellar mass of 2 10 ^ 12 M (Fritz et al. ). The color image obtained with SUBARU- Suprime Cam BRZ (Fig. subaru ) shows that the cluster central galaxy stands out as a large, massive red galaxy, and we confirm that the BCG, the central radio source and the peak of the X-ray emission, are spatially coincident well within 1 . In this section, we analyze the radio and optical properties of the central brightest galaxy, in order to investigate the feedback mechanism between the BCG and the ICM.

Using VLA high-resolution data we detected a source coincident with the BCG with a radio luminosity L_ 1.4 GHz =2.0 10 ^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 . A faint, one-sided structure with an extent of 80 kpc is seen in the north-west direction, where a significant lack of X-ray emission was found. Furthermore, the analysis of optical spectroscopy of the central galaxy shows a broad [ OII ] emission line, with a moderate to strong equivalent width of -25 , corresponding to an associated star formation rate in the range [ 2.3-8.3 ] M_ /yr . We were also able to trace a connection between the radio and optical properties of the central galaxy with the X-ray cool-core.

gmw_00008206.name: NGC 5784
gmw_00008206.overview: NGC 5784 is classified as a lenticular (S0) galaxy [arXiv:2208.14950]. It contains a molecular gas mass of 10^9.4 solar masses and exhibits a vanished spiral arm structure [arXiv:2208.14950].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5784 as its only S0 galaxy, notes a molecular gas mass of 10^9.4 solar masses, and uses its vanished arms to illustrate weak spiral-arm influence on gas.

quotes.quote: We include one S0 galaxy, NGC 5784.
NGC 5784 has molecular gas mass of 10^ 9.4 M_ Bolatto2017 .
The vanished arm structure in NGC 5784 therefore represents weak effect of spiral arms on radial distribution of gas.

gmw_00008207.name: NGC 447
gmw_00008207.overview: NGC 447 is a barred galaxy that features a disk-dominated region containing both spirals and a bar [arXiv:2208.14950]. It exhibits a high concentration of molecular gas, with its average central molecular mass surface density being eight times higher than its disk-averaged value [arXiv:2208.14950].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 447 as a barred-galaxy example, measuring its disk-structure amplitude and bar, and identifying it as the barred sample member with the highest central CO and molecular-gas concentration.

quotes.quote: The top-right and bottom-right panels of Fig. exmp illustrate the A_ tot (R) for NGC 447 and UGC 9476, respectively. The disk-dominated regions of NGC 447 and UGC 9476 are marked by the region between the vertical dotted (the end of bars or bulges) and the vertical solid (the end disks) lines in Fig. exmp . The bar of NGC 447, quantified using isophotal analysis described in Appendix ISO , is illustrated by the dashed ellipse in the top-left panel and the bar radius is shown as the vertical dashed line in the top-right panel. The disk-dominated region for barred galaxies contains spirals and bars, but that for unbarred galaxies contains only spirals. The average relative amplitude of the disk structure, s_ disk , is defined as the mean A_ tot over the disk dominated region: s_ disk = avg [ A_ tot (R_d) ] , where R_d belongs to the disk-dominated region. s_ disk = 0.49 for NGC 447 and s_ disk = 0.29 for UGC 9476.

The barred galaxy with the highest concentration is NGC 447, which has C_ CO =1.01 and C_ mol =0.89 , meaning that the average central CO intensity and molecular mass surface density are a factor of 10 and 8 higher than the disk-averaged values, respectively.

gmw_00008208.name: PGC 28308
gmw_00008208.overview: PGC 28308 is a disk galaxy that forms a pair with the companion galaxy MCG-02-25-019 [arXiv:1901.10294]. While its inner kinematics appear undisturbed, the galaxy's outer regions are asymmetric with a warp on its eastern side and a massive central spheroid [arXiv:1901.10294].

sources.summary: The paper finds PGC 28308 has discrepant H I and MUSE circular velocities, asymmetric warped outskirts and a companion, while its high central velocity dispersion is attributed to a massive central spheroid.

quotes.quote: However, this might not be the case for the galaxy with the largest discrepancy, PGC 28308 ( v_ =130 km s^ 1 for us and v_ =180 km s^ 1 in the literature). The Extragalactic Distance Database EDD; TU09 H i line profile for this galaxy is not symmetric, which might indicate that the galaxy is disturbed in its outskirts or that the line comes from the superposition of two objects. However, within the radius that we have studied with MUSE, our maps indicate undisturbed kinematics (Fig. PGC28308 ). At larger radii, the S ^4 G images show that the galaxy is asymmetric and has a warp on its Eastern side. PGC 28308 has been identified to form a pair with MCG-02-25-019 in the HIPASS catalogue ME04 .

This only occurs in PGC 28308, where high values at large heights and small radii can be seen (Fig. PGC28308 ). This is probably due to the central spheroid and not to a retrograde thick disc. Indeed, PGC 28308 has a central mass concentration with a mass of 10 of the baryonic galaxy mass, which makes it the largest one in the sample CO18 .

gmw_00008209.name: IC 1553
gmw_00008209.overview: IC 1553 is a low-mass, edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:2201.00566] that lies on the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:2201.00566]. The galaxy exhibits a warp [arXiv:1901.10294] and a distorted extraplanar diffuse ionized gas morphology [arXiv:2201.00566], likely driven by diffuse accretion rather than interactions with nearby companions [arXiv:2201.00566, arXiv:1901.10294].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 1553 with MUSE and H-alpha imaging, finding main-sequence star formation, asymmetric lag/eDIG kinematics, possible diffuse accretion, and localized hard ionization.

quotes.quote: We investigate the origin of the extraplanar diffuse ionized gas (eDIG) and its predominant ionization mechanisms in five nearby (17--46 Mpc) low-mass ( 10^9 - 10^ 10 M_ ) edge-on disk galaxies: ESO 157-49, ESO 469-15, ESO 544-27, IC 217, and IC 1553. We acquired Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) integral field spectroscopy and deep narrowband H imaging of our sample galaxies. To investigate the connection between in-plane star formation and eDIG, we measure the star formation rates (SFRs) and perform a photometric analysis of our narrowband H imaging. Using our MUSE data, we investigate the origin of eDIG via kinematics, specifically the rotation velocity lags. We also construct standard diagnostic diagrams and emission-line maps (EW(H ), [ N   ii ] /H , [ S   ii ] //H , [ O   iii ] /H ) and search for regions consistent with ionization by hot low-mass evolved stars (HOLMES) and shocks.

Of our galaxies, only IC 1553 is firmly in the star-forming main sequence noeske2007ms . As IC 1553 has no known nearby companions, accretion from a diffuse source seems like a more plausible explanation for its distorted eDIG morphology and kinematics. The [ O   iii ] enhanced emission in IC 1553 originates from a small in-plane region at the northern edge of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper reports a large thick-disc lag in IC 1553 without a matching velocity-dispersion increase, plus a strong asymmetry and warp not attributable to a nearby comparable galaxy.

quotes.quote: A large lag of up to 60 km s^ 1 is observed in some regions of IC 1553 at an axial coordinate x 10 20 and at a height of z 8 (Fig. IC1553 ). This lag appears well above the limit where the thick disc starts to dominate the luminosity. It might correspond to a small fraction of retrograde material, but no significant increase in the velocity dispersion is found in the lagging bins. IC 1553 also shows a significant asymmetry and a prominent warp (Fig.

It is tempting to link this warp to a galaxy covered by the MUSE FOV and found to the southeast of IC 1553. However we found it to be a background galaxy, at a redshift z 0.05 (vs. z 0.01 for IC 1553). According to the NED, IC 1553 has no large nearby galaxy with a comparable recession velocity.

gmw_00008210.name: CXOU J134905.8+263752
gmw_00008210.overview: CXOU J134905.8+263752 is an X-ray quasar located behind the galaxy cluster Abell 1795 [arXiv:2108.04827]. Its low-count spectrum has been modeled with a power law and additional absorption from neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2108.04827].

sources.summary: The paper includes CXOU J134905.8+263752 as the A1795 background quasar, fits its spectrum with absorption and notes that ML methods constrain ALPs for its low-count data where chi-square does not.

quotes.quote: The quasar CXOU J134905.8+263752 behind A1795 (A1795Quasar).

A1795Sy1, A1367 and A1795Quasar have been fitted with a power law with additional absorption from neutral hydrogen, Coma1 only with a power law and Coma2 with a power law and a Fe K line ( E=6.4 keV ).

In particular, for sources where only a small number of counts is available (e.g. the quasar behind A1795 and the quasar SDSS J130001.48+275120.6 behind Coma) the advantage of the ML methods is significant. For those sources the ^2 statistic does not return bounds at a 95 C.L., whereas the ML methods are able to constrain ALPs very well at that confidence level.

gmw_00008211.name: SDSS J130001.48+275120.6
gmw_00008211.overview: SDSS J130001.48+275120.6 is an X-ray quasar located behind the Coma cluster [arXiv:2108.04827]. Its X-ray spectrum has been modeled using a power law combined with an iron K spectral line [arXiv:2108.04827].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS J130001.48+275120.6 as the Coma2 quasar, fitting a power-law plus Fe K line and emphasizing ML constraints for its low-count ALP spectrum.

quotes.quote: The quasar SDSS J130001.48+275120.6 behind Coma (Coma2).

A1795Sy1, A1367 and A1795Quasar have been fitted with a power law with additional absorption from neutral hydrogen, Coma1 only with a power law and Coma2 with a power law and a Fe K line ( E=6.4 keV ).

In particular, for sources where only a small number of counts is available (e.g. the quasar behind A1795 and the quasar SDSS J130001.48+275120.6 behind Coma) the advantage of the ML methods is significant. For those sources the ^2 statistic does not return bounds at a 95 C.L., whereas the ML methods are able to constrain ALPs very well at that confidence level.

gmw_00008212.name: arclet in A3266
gmw_00008212.overview: The arclet in A3266 is an elongated candidate gravitational lensing arclet located near a bright elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0005200]. It has a measured heliocentric radial velocity of 21900 km/s, suggesting it may reside either inside or behind the cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0005200].

sources.summary: The paper describes the A3266 arclet candidate as an elongated object near a bright elliptical, with a measured centroid and velocity that could place it inside or behind the cluster.

quotes.quote: The candidate arclet is indicated with an arrow. Its centroid is at = 4^h 31^m 15.53^s and = -61^ 30^ 3.7^ (J2000). It is at 16.6 arcsec (29 h _ 50 ^ 1 kpc at the cluster distance) from the center of a nearby, bright elliptical, and at 2.89 arcminutes (303 h _ 50 ^ 1 kpc) from the center of the cluster (at the position of the dumbbell pair).

Spectroscopic observations of the arclet by W. Kunkel (private communication) with the 2.5m Dupont telescope have shown that this object has an heliocentric radial velocity of 21900 km s ^ 1 . With this velocity, this object may be inside the cluster, since most of the cluster members have velocities between 15000 km s ^ 1 and 21000 km s ^ 1 QRW96 On the other hand it can also be behind the cluster, which strengths the probability that it has been lensed either by the cluster potential or by the nearby galaxy, or both.

gmw_00008213.name: arclet in A3408
gmw_00008213.overview: The arclet in A3408 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z=0.073 that is gravitationally lensed by the cluster A3408 [arXiv:astro-ph/0005200]. It exhibits non-uniform H-alpha emission that is strongest on its western side and has a probable companion galaxy at approximately the same redshift [arXiv:astro-ph/0005200].

sources.summary: The paper treats the A3408 arclet as a z=0.073 lensed spiral-galaxy source and reports nonuniform H emission plus a probable companion at the same redshift.

quotes.quote: This structure has been discussed by CKH98 , who successfully modeled it as a galaxy at z=0.073 lensed by the cluster A3408, at z=0.042 . The adopted cluster mass distribution is a scaled version of mass profiles derived from the study of high redshift clusters. Their preferred model has a component that follows the brightness profile of the central elliptical galaxy and is immersed in a massive dark halo. From the lens model and the equivalent widths of some prominent emission lines ( [ O II ] 3727, [ O III ] 5007 and H ) they have suggested that the source galaxy is probably a spiral with intrinsic diameter 14.6 kpc and magnitude M_B=-18.2 .

Figure ha also indicates that the H emission of the arc is not uniform and is strongest at the western side of this object. An interesting feature present in the residual image is a point-like object, between the side of the arc with the strongest H emission and the star below the arc. This object also appears in broad band images, after removing the image of this star, as shown in Figure point . This feature is probably a companion galaxy of the arc source, at approximately the same redshift. Unfortunately, the presence of the star precludes further analysis of this object and its use to constrain more sophisticated lens models. No other objects are seen in Figure ha at the same redshift of the arc.

gmw_00008214.name: GOODS J123646.84+621608.1
gmw_00008214.overview: GOODS J123646.84+621608.1 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy with a photometric redshift of 3.09 [arXiv:1305.5849]. It exhibits a distorted, tadpole-shaped morphology featuring a bow-shock-like head with multiple blue cores and an extended, turbulent wake [arXiv:1305.5849].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GOODS J123646.84+621608.1 as object 1/tadpole 1, matching the Ly redshift and showing blue head substructure, a bow-shock-like shape, and an extended wake.

quotes.quote: Inspection of archival GOODS-N v2.0 images (Giavalisco et al 2004; fig. specplusim , top right panel) shows the continuum source closest to the inferred slit position to be GOODS J123646.84+621608.1. A photometric redshift of 3.09 (Xue et al 2011) for that galaxy, referred to as object 1 in the table and the figures, agrees well with our spectroscopic redshift.

In an area extending to about 5 arcsecs on either side of the slit, two highly distorted, tadpole-shaped galaxies (1 and 2; with object 1 producing the continuum in the spectrum) can be seen, Both have substructure with multiple cores of blue light in their heads (figs. tads , bowshock ).

the main tadpole 1 shows a shape reminiscent of a bow shock, with a projected tangential angle of close to 45 degrees (indicating, at face value, motion with a moderate Mach number 1.5), and what looks like a turbulent wake consisting of a vortex street with a longitudinal extent of about 12 kpc and a maximum width of about 5 kpc (fig. bowshock ). A faint, linear structure vaguely resembling a "helix" extends almost perpendicular to the axis of symmetry (i.e., the head-tail direction) to both sides of the galaxy, with a maximum traceable extent of 20 kpc to the south and somewhat less to the north. The nature of this feature is not clear. Some of the extended emission seen in fig. juxtaspec appears to arise from that region, suggesting that it may be associated with both low and highly ionized gas.

gmw_00008215.name: tadpole 2
gmw_00008215.overview: Tadpole 2 is a highly distorted, tadpole-shaped galaxy that contains multiple cores of blue light in its head [arXiv:1305.5849]. The galaxy features a narrow, turbulent tail that is bluer than its head and lacks a discernible bow shock [arXiv:1305.5849].

sources.summary: The paper describes tadpole 2 as a highly distorted galaxy with blue tail colors, a narrow turbulent wake, and no discernible bow shock in the inferred stripping sequence.

quotes.quote: In an area extending to about 5 arcsecs on either side of the slit, two highly distorted, tadpole-shaped galaxies (1 and 2; with object 1 producing the continuum in the spectrum) can be seen, Both have substructure with multiple cores of blue light in their heads (figs. tads , bowshock ).

However, there is a clear trend in that all the cases where there is a distinct head-tail shape (objects 1, 2, 3, 5, and 8), with the exception of object 4, have bluer tails than heads. The tendency for the tails to be bluer than the heads is different from the prevailing pattern for tadpole galaxies (e.g., Elmegreen Elmegreen 2010).
Tadpole 2 is more turbulent, but the tail is still narrow, and it does not have a bow shock either.

gmw_00008216.name: object 4
gmw_00008216.overview: Object 4 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by a linear, non-turbulent tail that is visible only in the V band [arXiv:1305.5849]. This thin tail is associated with high equivalent width Lyman-alpha emission, which may indicate star formation occurring within a ram-pressure stripped wake [arXiv:1305.5849].

sources.summary: The paper presents object 4/tadpole 4 as a high-redshift galaxy with a V-band-only tail, possible high-equivalent-width Ly emission, and a linear non-turbulent wake.

quotes.quote: The object 4 is an interesting case in that the tail is only visible in the V band (fig. tad4 ), with a total flux of (5.8 1.2) 10^ 17 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , corresponding to a 4.8 detection. The situation here may be similar to the one presented in paper II, a narrow filament of emission visible in only one broad band, that may be glowing in Ly , except that in the current situation, the Ly line, due to its higher redshift, would be situated in the V band and not in B. Because of the low flux, other broad band images do not provide useful constraints (e.g., V-I = 1.3 1.0 ).

The object 4 that we associated above with high equivalent width Ly emission exhibits a linear, thin tail (fig. tads , tad4 ), not unlike the expected outcome of Bondi-Hoyle accretion (Sakelliou 2000). The proximity of the galaxies to each other, the presence of a bow shock in one but not in the others, and the various degrees of turbulence in their tails may imply that the flow of galaxies is encountering inhomogeneous conditions. Specifically, the spatial sequence from tadpole 4 to 2 to 1 (fig. tads ) could be interpreted as indicating passage through a zone with a significant temperature gradient (perhaps an accretion shock) from a colder to a hotter gaseous medium: the tadpole 4, which is most advanced in the direction of the flow, has a linear, apparently non-turbulent tail, suggesting that it may be experiencing the higher viscosity and thermal pressure of a hotter environment (e.g., Roediger Br uggen 2008). Tadpole 2 is more turbulent, but the tail is still narrow, and it does not have a bow shock either. Tadpole 1 has a bow shock, presumably because it is on the colder side of the interface between hot and cold gas, and its velocity relative to the intergalactic medium exceeds the sound speed for the colder gas. This condition would easily be satisfied when passing through the general filamentary IGM with even moderate velocity, as the typical temperature at z 3 is only a few times 10^4 K (Rauch et al 1996), corresponding to sound speeds of a few tens of kms ^ 1 . The tail of tadpole 1 is flaring up and shows a turbulent vortex pattern, consistent with the lower viscosity expected in a colder medium. A particular interesting consequence of ram-stripping is the formation of stars in the stripped gas, which has been the subject of recent observational (e.g., Yoshida et al 2012; Smith et al 2010: Hester et al 2010; Fumagalli et al 2011; Boissier et al 2012; Owers et al 2012) and theoretical study (e.g., Kapferer et al 2009, Tonnesen Bryan 2012; see also the Ly emitting filament in paper II, which may have a similar origin). Several strands of evidence suggest that the current situation is indeed an instance of star formation in galactic wakes: the tails of most of the objects have blue colors, indicative of very young stars. The finding of extended metal line emission far out from the galaxies suggests the presence of a stripped, or re-created, interstellar medium that is being excited by the newly forming stars. The high Ly equivalent width suggested by the tail of galaxy 4 may be another sign of hot, young stars, as discussed in paper II. The usual condition for ram-pressure stripping to take place is that the ram-pressure on the gas in a galaxy, experienced when passing through the intergalactic medium, needs to exceed the gravitational binding force per surface area, v^2 ( /2) GM(< R) _ gal (R)/R (e.g., McCarthy et al 2008). Here is the ambient gas density, v the relative velocity of galactic gas and ambient intergalactic medium, and G , R , M(<R) and _ gas are the gravitational constant, the distance of a given gas volume element from the center of the galaxy, the total gravitating mass internal to that radius, and the galactic gas density, respectively. It has often been assumed that ram pressure stripping is most relevant for low redshift, massive clusters. However, the hierarchical nature of structure formation, leading to more compact gravitational potential wells, higher gas densities, and higher interaction rates at z 3 (when compared to the local universe) suggests that one should expect miniature versions of the ram-pressure stripping seen in low redshift clusters to occur among satellites in individual high z galactic halos. With the higher density at high redshift favoring a higher pressure for a given velocity, the in-falling satellites themselves collapse from a denser background as well, so for the effect of ram pressure stripping one would have to look to lower mass, dwarf galaxies, perhaps aided by processes that may lower the binding energy of the gas further. The higher merger rate at high redshift may also work to increase the amount of ram-pressure stripped gas (e.g., Domainko et al 2006; Kapferer et al 2008), as may stellar or galactic outflows, as long as they can offset a significant part of the gravitational binding energy. In addition, new cosmological hydro-simulation techniques suggest more efficient stripping (e.g., Hess Springel 2012) and the presence of puffed up, high-angular momentum gas (e.g., Keres et al 2012), "ready to go". Recently it has been argued (Benitez-Llambay et al 2012) that, in particular, dwarf galaxies do not even require the encounter with fully formed massive halos but can lose gas to ram-pressure stripping in large-scale structure filaments at high redshift when entering terminal nodes like the (future) Local Group pancake. In this case, our spectrograph slit may have intersected a filament of the cosmic web, lit up by the star-formation in the ablated contrails of a swarm of coherently moving galaxies. To attain the high relative velocities required for stripping, the galaxies would have to move highly supersonically with respect to the gas they are plunging into. While we appear to be seeing one galaxy with a bow shock, it is not clear if the velocities of the other objects are high enough for this to work. However, as argued above, the presence of an accretion shock in the terminal node with hot gas on one side may make this scenario consistent with the observations. A variant of this picture may explain the stripping and the apparent gradient in the properties of the tadpoles as a group of galaxies being "hosed down" when obliquely passing an accreting stream of gas. Metal enrichment and escape of ionizing radiation from star formation in stripped gas The existence of such extended structures at high redshift, the relatively large number density of galaxies with tidal or ram-pressure related features (see also the disturbed halos described in paper I and II), and the presence of multiple sites of star-formation in a common gaseous halo or large scale filament suggest that the stripping of gas from galaxies in interactions could be an important contributor to the metal enrichment of the intergalactic medium, analogous to the lower redshift process leading to the enrichment of the gas in galaxy clusters. To explain the finding of metal enrichment in the IGM at large distances from the nearest bright galaxy, galactic winds from Lyman break galaxies have been invoked to drive metal-enriched gas far into intergalactic space (e.g., Pettini et al 2001; Steidel et a 2010). Among the persistent uncertainties with this scenario is that the actually observed ranges of galactic winds invariably fall short of accounting for the metals seen in QSO absorption systems at large distances from such galaxies. However, if, as we have argued above, ram pressure stripping of in-falling dwarf galaxies and star formation in the stripped wake operate at high redshift, there may be less need to invoke long-range winds from the central galaxy of a halo. In this alternative picture, the ram-pressure that led to the ablation of gas and subsequent star formation may also act to dispel newly formed metal enriched gas from the tails, aided by stellar winds and supernova explosions that would find it much easier to escape from the weakly bound (and dark-matter free) star forming regions of galactic wakes. In any case, differential motion between the lost gas and the parent star forming galaxies will distribute the gas spatially over time, and the assumption that this process mostly occurs in in-falling dwarf satellites implies that the gas is automatically reaching distances from any brightest halo galaxy as large as commonly observed (in metal absorption lines; e.g., Chen 2012). Tidal interactions between satellites may lead to a similar result, metals expelled into the gaseous halo of brighter galaxies, that came from the shredded interstellar medium of its satellites or from outflows in tidal dwarfs, and both processes may exist among the extended, asymmetric Ly emitters in our study. There may be differences between the metallicities of the gas ejected from tidal star-forming regions, and the gas lost by star forming regions in galactic contrails. Stars in the former arise from the relatively metal-rich ISM of the parent galaxy, as would stars in gas ablated by ram-pressure or viscous stripping. Stars forming in turbulent wakes behind the galaxies may feed on the lower metallicity gas in the halo or intergalactic medium as well, which may contribute to the signatures of hot, young stars described in paper II. As argued earlier in paper II, extragalactic star formation in the wakes of stripped galaxies and in tidal tails may facilitate the production and escape of ionizing photons and may have brought about the reionization of the universe at high redshift. Star formation outside of the dense galactic HI cocoons would lead to lines of sight with reduced optical depth for ionizing photons. The presence of young, massive stars in the wakes, with the weak gravitational binding force enabling easy removal of neutral gas by even moderate amounts of stellar winds or supernova outflows would all tend to enhance the escape of ionizing radiation. Recently, Bergvall et al (2013), examining selection effects in the search for local galaxies leaking ionizing radiation, have come to similar conclusions as to the likely conditions required, including the importance of stripping. We have detected a Ly emitting halo with several faint filaments stretching over tens of kpc. The filaments correlate with star-forming regions in the form of mostly blue, faint galaxies, several of which have a distinct tadpole shape and blue, partly turbulent tails, with one object showing what appears to be a bow shock. The GOODS-N ACS F435W image reveals many such features criss-crossing an area several times bigger than the visible extent of the Ly halo. The emission of the central halo and of the filaments is broadly consistent with being powered by stellar photoionization. We detect spatially extended emission lines from gas surrounding the main tadpole, including HeII 1640, NV 1240 and probably OIII ] , AlII, and FeII, suggesting an extended, extragalactic, interstellar medium with current star formation. The tadpole shapes, partial alignment, and the considerable numbers of unusual broad band objects make it unlikely that the features observed are predominantly tidal in origin (i.e., caused by individual two-body encounters). Instead, the galaxies may have experienced stripping of gas when moving relative to the intergalactic or intra-halo medium, with stars forming downstream in the galactic contrails. This process is observationally and theoretically well established in the local universe. Our observations have identified an occurrence of ram-pressure stripping at high redshift, possibly involving dwarf galaxies interacting with the gas in more massive, individual galactic halos. The filamentary structure trailing behind a galaxy in the z=2.63 halo described in paper II may be another example of this effect. In the present case, the properties of several tadpoles change along their general direction of motion, which may be consistent with these galaxies passing into a hotter gaseous environment, possibly the region behind an accretion shock. Such a stripping scenario may play out on a larger scale when differential motions of galaxies relative to the nodes in the gaseous cosmic web strip galaxies off their gas, as suggested by Benitez-Llambay et al 2012. As in the case of local clusters, the galactic contrails should be able to release metal-enriched gas, perhaps enhanced by local stellar feedback, more easily than normal galaxies. At the very least these objects should provide a contribution to the intergalactic metal budget of galactic halos. The loss of enriched gas from galactic contrails may suggest a solution to the long-standing puzzle of how the intergalactic medium at large distances from bright galaxies was polluted with metals. Star formation in galactic contrails would involve young stars, surrounded by lower HI gas columns than stars born in ordinary galaxies, and capable of clearing their environment of dense gas, suggesting a way in which galaxies can ionize the intergalactic medium.

gmw_00008217.name: MCG-01-26-013
gmw_00008217.overview: MCG-01-26-013 is an edge-on lenticular companion galaxy located 38 kpc from NGC 3110 [arXiv:1809.04788]. It has a mass ratio of 1:14 relative to its larger neighbor and exhibits centrally concentrated hydrogen-alpha emission in its nucleus [arXiv:1809.04788].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG-01-26-013 as NGC 3110’s nearby companion, an edge-on S0/S0-a galaxy with much lower blue luminosity, a 14:1 mass ratio, and central H emission.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy is MCG-01-26-013 (PGC029184), which is located at just 1 8 from NGC 3110, or 38 kpc (Fig. fig1 ), and with a (projected) velocity difference of 235 . Its optical diameter is 0 98, about half of that of the main galaxy. It is seen nearly edge-on and it is classified as S0 ^+ pec or S0-a Hyperleda 1991rc3..book.....D . Its (extinction corrected) blue-band luminosity is more than one order of magnitude smaller than that of NGC 3110, 7.3 10 ^ 9 L _ versus 10 ^ 11 L _ (Table tbl-1 ), so the mass difference is estimated to be 14:1.
There exists H emission towards the nucleus of the companion as well, and it is more centrally concentrated than that in NGC 3110 2002ApJS..143...47D .

gmw_00008218.name: NGC 232
gmw_00008218.overview: NGC 232 is a barred Sa spiral galaxy [arXiv:1809.04788] classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.15842] and a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1809.04788]. It hosts a circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:2310.15842], asymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:1809.04788], and a biconical gas outflow [arXiv:2310.15842] alongside a long optical emission-line jet [arXiv:1809.04788].

sources.summary: VLT-MUSE data show NGC 232 has disc gas plus a biconical outflow, old central stars, a circumnuclear star-forming ring, and feedback capable of suppressing star formation.

quotes.quote: We use VLT-MUSE integral field unit data to study the ionized gas physical properties and kinematics as well as the stellar populations of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 232 as an opportunity to understand the role of AGN feedback on star formation. The data cover a field of view of 60 60 arcsec ^ 2 at a spatial resolution of 850 pc. The emission-line profiles have been fitted with two Gaussian components, one associated to the emission of the gas in the disc and the other due to a bi-conical outflow. The spectral synthesis suggests a predominantly old stellar population with ages exceeding 2 Gyr, with the largest contributions seen at the nucleus and decreasing outwards.

We estimate a maximum outflow rate of 1.26 0.16 M _ yr ^ 1 observed at 560 pc from the nucleus. The maximum kinetic power of the outflow is 3.4 0.4 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , which corresponds to 0.003 L_ AGN . The energy released seems to be enough to quench star formation within the cone, as suggested by the lower star formation rates in this region. Old ( t >2 Gyr ) stellar populations dominates the mass and light fractions.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 232 as a barred Sa LIRG with asymmetric arms, high star formation, molecular gas, a nuclear starburst/LINER classification, and a long optical emission-line jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 232 At a distance of D = 90.5 Mpc (1 \ corresponds to 420 pc), NGC 232 is a barred Sa galaxy seen with an inclination of 47 (Table tbl-1 ), and with two arms which seem to form a ring-like structure in optical images. The size of the disk is 0 97, or 24 kpc. In the optical image (Fig. fig1 ) one can discern that the two spiral arms (to the N and S) are asymmetric and emerge from the barred central component, which is elongated along the SE to NW direction. A high SFR of about 30 M _ yr ^ 1 can be derived from its FIR flux e.g. 2006ApJ...643..173S .

NGC 232 was classified to host a nuclear starburst ( H 2 ) 1995ApJS...98..171V,2002ApJ...564..650C , although it has also been classified as a low ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) by 2000AJ....120...47C . A very extended ( 3 kpc) and highly collimated linear structure extending from the nucleus has been recently found using VLT/MUSE 2017ApJ...850L..17L , which indicates that this is in fact an AGN. This is the second longest optical emission-line jet reported so far.

gmw_00008219.name: 3C241
gmw_00008219.overview: The radio galaxy 3C241 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits a broad emission line in its near-infrared spectrum [arXiv:0710.5220]. It is characterized by an intermediate X-ray luminosity, a relatively large intrinsic absorption, and a high reprocessing fraction [arXiv:0710.5220].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C241 as an intermediate 3CR galaxy with a near-infrared broad line, intermediate X-ray luminosity, relatively large intrinsic absorption, and a high reprocessing fraction.

quotes.quote: In particular, the galaxy 3C241 has been found to exhibit a broad line in the near-infrared observer-frame spectrum ( ), and the broad line object 3C318 has a very red, underluminous optical continuum ( ).

Under this hypothesis the peculiar position of 3C241 is nicely accounted for. This source exhibits at the same time an intermediate X-ray luminosity and a relatively large N _ H , as if the line of sight were grazing at the same time the boundaries of the wide jet and the boundaries of a particularly large absorber: indeed, here the reprocessing fraction is as high as 8 , and the corresponding empty triangle falls right in the region of Compton thick sources.

gmw_00008220.overview: The object 100008.9+021440 is classified as an obscured, broad-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1809.01350]. It exhibited intranight optical variability in the B, V, and R bands during observations in February 2015 [arXiv:1809.01350].

sources.summary: The paper classifies 100008.9+021440 as a broad-line obscured Chandra-COSMOS AGN and reports February 2015 B, V, and R intranight variability, with no usable later data.

quotes.quote: 100008.9+021440 This object is classified as a broad-line and obscured AGN in the Chandra-COSMOS identification catalog. The 2015 February data shows variability strength of 0.130 mag, 0.172 mag and 0.115 mag in B , V , and R bands, respectively with 20.3 mag, 20.0 mag, and 19.4 mag in each band. Unfortunately, no usable data were obtained in 2015 March and 2016 April.

gmw_00008221.overview: The galaxy 100021.7+020000 is classified as a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1809.01350]. However, both its optical spectrum and its morphology exhibit the characteristics of an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1809.01350].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 100021.7+020000 as a radio-loud AGN variable in B and V, while noting its SDSS spectrum and image resemble an early-type galaxy, making the variability origin uncertain.

quotes.quote: 100021.7+020000 Radio-loud AGN with 20.0, and 18.6 magnitudes in B and V bands, respectively. This object is classified as variable in B and V bands with variability strength of 0.278 and 0.190 mag, respectively. However, the SDSS spectrum has the characteristics of early-type galaxy, not AGN. The SDSS image shows the morphology of an early-type galaxy too. The variability can arise from an increased nuclear activity or due to an unidentified systematic error in measuring the central variability of an extended source like this.

gmw_00008222.overview: 100434.3+025011 is a Sloan Digital Sky Survey quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1809.01350]. It is the brightest variable active galactic nucleus in its sample, exhibiting detected variability in both the B and R bands [arXiv:1809.01350].

sources.summary: The paper calls 100434.3+025011 the brightest variable AGN in its sample, identifies it as an SDSS quasar, and reports B and R variability in two observing runs.

quotes.quote: 100434.3+025011 The brightest variable AGN in our sample. This SDSS quasar has apparent magnitudes of 18.7 and 18.4 in B and R bands, respectively. Variability is detected in both the 2015 February and 2016 April data with the strength of 0.06 to 0.13 magnitudes.

gmw_00008223.name: J1223 + 5038
gmw_00008223.overview: J1223 + 5038 is a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2102.12609] that exhibits a steep overall radio spectrum [arXiv:1505.03394]. High-resolution observations reveal a double structure at 1.7 GHz [arXiv:1505.03394], while its X-ray spectrum is characterized by an emission line consistent with neutral iron [arXiv:2102.12609].

sources.summary: 1223+5037 has a double 1.7 GHz structure, a likely radio core with jet and possible lobe components, lost low-frequency flux, and a steep overall spectrum.

quotes.quote: 1223+5037 . The EVN 1.7 GHz image shows double structure with a weak eastern component E, which is not present in 5- and 8.4-GHz observations. Our VLBA 8.4 GHz map is consistent with that obtained in the VIPS project helm07 at 5 GHz and we present all three images in Fig. images1 . The inverted spectrum component C1.1 is probably a radio core, components C1.2 and C2 are parts of the radio jet and E might be a part of radio lobe. We lost 26 of the total flux density at 1.7 GHz probably connected with the extended emission that can be present between the eastern and central components. The overall spectrum of 1223+5037 is steep.

sources.summary: The paper finds J1223 + 5038 is best fit by an X-ray spectral model with an emission line consistent with neutral Fe at 6.4 keV.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we determined from our likelihood ratio tests that J1223 + 5038 was best fit with Model B due to the presence of an emission line in its spectrum. The measured emission line is consistent with neutral Fe emission at 6.4 keV, while the N_ H ^i and best-fit parameters agree with the Model A results. The observed spectrum and model fit for J1223 + 5038 are shown in Figure fig:spectrum , and the best-fit results are in Table table:xray .

gmw_00008224.name: B2 1447+27
gmw_00008224.overview: B2 1447+27 is a galaxy that hosts a compact radio source [arXiv:1003.2835]. It has a marginal, tentative detection of H I absorption, though it remains unclear whether this gas resides in the nuclear region or at larger scales [arXiv:1003.2835].

sources.summary: The appendix says B2 1447+27 has a marginal H I absorption detection that needs confirmation, with uncertain gas scale and a compact radio source.

quotes.quote: B2 1447+27: The detection of \ absorption in this system is very marginal, and only seen in a data cube with robust or natural weighting. For a uniform weighted cube the absorption feature disappears in the noise. Additional observations are necessary to verify this tentative detection. It is not clear whether this absorption represents gas in the very nuclear region or at larger scales. The radio source in B2 1447+27 is compact.

gmw_00008225.name: NGC3994
gmw_00008225.overview: NGC 3994 is an Sbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1804.04039, arXiv:astro-ph/9811342] that is interacting within a close galaxy system [arXiv:1804.04039, arXiv:astro-ph/9811342]. Its composite spectrum is dominated by starburst emission [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342], with enhanced star formation occurring in both its center and outer disk [arXiv:1804.04039].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC3994 as an Sbc spiral in an interacting pair, deriving a J-PLUS SFR of 2.9 and finding enhanced star formation in its center and outer disk.

quotes.quote: As an illustrative example, the close pair of interacting galaxies NGC3994 and NGC3995 is analyzed, finding an enhancement of the star formation rate not only in the center, but also in outer parts of the disk of NGC3994.

NGC3994 is a Sbc spiral CALIFA with smaller size, and is part of the sample analyzed by CALIFA. We calculated its total star formation rate to be SFR =2.9 0.6 . There are several estimates of the total SFR of this galaxy based on the same CALIFA data: catalan give SFR =5.6 1.4 , Sanchez2 measure SFR =3.7 0.4 , cano report SFR =4.5 1.5 , and catalan2 recently estimate SFR =3.16 0.12 . The estimate given by the MPA-JHU group is SFR =1.6 1.3 . J-PLUS SFR estimates for NGC3994 and NGC3995 are compatible with the ones in the literature within 1 , supporting both the developed methodologies and the quality of the J-PLUS dataset.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3994 as an interacting Sbc galaxy whose composite spectrum is dominated by starburst emission plus a likely Liner component.

quotes.quote: NGC 3994 is an Sbc galaxy (Sandage Bedke 1994) in a triple system, interacting both with NGC 3991 and NGC 3995. Based on the observed line ratios, Keel et al. (1985) classified it as a Liner; the \ and \ values rather suggest a \ region. We found that its spectrum is ``composite'', the main contribution to the Balmer lines coming from a starburst. The relative strength of the \ lines is large in the other component ( \ = 0.53), suggesting that it is a Liner.

gmw_00008226.name: NGC3995
gmw_00008226.overview: NGC3995 is an interacting galaxy that forms a close pair with NGC3994 [arXiv:1804.04039]. It has a stellar mass of approximately 10^9.52 solar masses and exhibits enhanced star formation in its central region and around one effective radius [arXiv:1804.04039].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC3995 in an interacting pair, measuring SFR 4.2 with J-PLUS and finding radial star-formation enhancement in the central region and near one effective radius.

quotes.quote: As an illustrative example, the close pair of interacting galaxies NGC3994 and NGC3995 is analyzed, finding an enhancement of the star formation rate not only in the center, but also in outer parts of the disk of NGC3994.

NGC3995 is too big to fit in the FoV of IFUs of surveys such as CALIFA or MaNGA, but not for J-PLUS. We measured a star formation rate of SFR =4.2 0.9 for this galaxy. he estimate by the MPA-JHU group MPA , is SFR =4.8 4.2 , made with a combination of spectroscopic and photometric data from SDSS that induces large uncertainties.

In the case of NGC3995 ( upper panel in Fig.  figure9 ), the _ SFR decreases as the galactocentric distance increases. In order to estimate the impact of the interaction in the star formation of NGC3995, we compared our _ SFR measurements with a control sample of non-interacting galaxies matched in morphology and stellar mass from Rosa . The stellar mass of NGC3995 was calculated using the empirical relationship given by masas based on the ( g i ) color of the galaxy, giving a value of log \ M_ \ [ M_ ] = 9.52 . We found that a _ SFR enhancement is present not only in the central part of the galaxy but also around 1 R/R_ eff , in addition, a decrement is observed between 1.4 and 2.4 R/R_ eff .

gmw_00008227.name: 2MASS J23410667 0914327
gmw_00008227.overview: 2MASS J23410667 0914327 is a type-1 quasar at a redshift of z=0.236 that exhibits an intrinsically red continuum [arXiv:0708.3109]. The quasar is accompanied by a star-forming emission-line galaxy located at a projected separation of 6.7 kpc [arXiv:0708.3109].

sources.summary: The paper studies J2341 as an unusually red type-1 quasar at z=0.236, finding host dilution, a companion galaxy, weak far-infrared emission, and an intrinsically red continuum.

quotes.quote: Here we report on a detailed case study of the reddest type -1 quasar of our sample, 2MASS J23410667 0914327 (for short J2341) at redshift z=0.236 with M _ K =-25.8 and J-K_s=1.95 . This source has a very red optical appearance but lacks the far-infrared emission typically seen in known dust enshrouded AGN.

To explain the red optical continuum of the quasar we examine nuclear dust reddening of an intrinsically blue quasar continuum in combination with dilution by stellar light of the host galaxy. The optical and NIR observations reveal a star forming emission--line galaxy at the same redshift as the quasar with a projected linear separation of 1 8 (6.7 kpc). The quasar and its companion are embedded in diffuse extended continuum emission.

Even the combination of absorption and host dilution is not able to match J2341 with standard quasar templates. While the BLR shows only a rather moderate absorption of E _ B-V =0.3 , the continuum shorter than 4500 requires strong obscuration with E _ B-V =0.7 , clearly exceeding the constraints from the low upper limit on the silicate 9.7 m absorption. This leads us to conclude that the continuum of the quasar J2341 is intrinsically redder than that of typical quasars.

gmw_00008228.name: B 1359+154
gmw_00008228.overview: B 1359+154 is a lensed quasar located at a redshift of z=3.24 [arXiv:0802.0370]. The system exhibits at least six images of a radio source and its star-forming host galaxy, which are lensed by a central group of three galaxies [arXiv:0802.0370].

sources.summary: The paper studies B 1359+154 as a lensed quasar with six radio/host images and lensing galaxies, but reports no recovered microlensing information or nearby line-of-sight galaxies.

quotes.quote: We present VIMOS integral field observations of 27 27 \ fields of view around four lensed quasars: HE 0230-2130, RX J0911.4+0551, H 1413+117 and B 1359+154. Using the low, medium and high resolution modes, we study the quasar images and the quasar environments.

The multiple quasar 1359 The lensed quasar 1359 \ was discovered as part of the CLASS survey and discussed by . The quasar is at redshift z=3.24 and has a maximum image separation of 1.7''. As identified by , this system shows at least six images of a radio source and its star forming host galaxy. The main share of the lensing potential is produced by three lensing galaxies inside the Einstein ring with currently no spectroscopic redshift available; however, the three galaxies have been identified as the core of a galaxy group possibly at redshift z 1 based on optical colors and mass estimates derived from models of the system .
We have not been able to recover any microlensing information or galaxies close to the line-of-sight in the neighborhood of 1359.

gmw_00008229.name: GL-z10
gmw_00008229.overview: GL-z10 is a luminous high-redshift galaxy candidate with a photometric redshift of approximately 10.4 [arXiv:2207.09434, arXiv:2208.01612]. It has built up about 10^9 solar masses in stars [arXiv:2207.09434] and displays an extended exponential light profile potentially consistent with a disk galaxy [arXiv:2207.09434, arXiv:2208.01612].

sources.summary: The paper presents GL-z10 as a luminous z>10 candidate with a sharp Lyman break, about 10^9 solar masses, negligible lensing, and an extended exponential profile suggesting a possible disk.

quotes.quote: Our most secure candidates are two M_ UV 21 systems: GLASS-z12 and GLASS-z10. These galaxies display abrupt 1.8 mag breaks in their spectral energy distributions, consistent with complete absorption of flux bluewards of Lyman- that is redshifted to z=12.4^ +0.1 _ 0.3 and z=10.4^ +0.4 _ 0.5 . Lower redshift interlopers such as quiescent galaxies with strong Balmer breaks would be comfortably detected at >5 in multiple bands where instead we find no flux. From SED modeling we infer that these galaxies have already built up 10^9 solar masses in stars over the 300-400 Myrs after the Big Bang.
Tantalizingly, GLASS-z10 shows a clearly extended exponential light profile, potentially consistent with a disk galaxy of r_ 50 0.7 kpc.
For both GL-z10 and GL-z12 we find negligible lensing ( <1.1 ) from all foreground sources within 10 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies GL-z9-1 as a bright GLASS high-redshift candidate with extended morphology and a photometric redshift consistent with earlier GLASS-z10 and GHZ1 measurements.

quotes.quote: We find that some bright candidates, i.e., GL-z9-1, GL-z12-1, CR2-z12-1, CR-z16-1, and S5-z16-1, are clearly more extended than the PSF, although GL-z12-1 is compact compared to other candidates, implying potential AGN activity.

Using the ERS CEERS and GLASS datasets, 2022arXiv220709434N found two bright galaxy candidates at z 10 and 12 , GLASS-z10 and GLASS-z12, which correspond to GL-z9-1 and GL-z12-1 in our sample, respectively. Their estimates of the photometric redshifts ( z=10.35^ +0.38 _ 0.51 and z=12.38^ +0.13 _ 0.27 for GL-z10-1 and GL-z12-1, respectively, with prospector , from the ApJL published version ) are consistent with our estimates ( z=10.49^ +0.53 _ 0.72 and z=12.28^ +0.08 _ 0.07 ).
GHZ1 (GHZ2) is GL-z9-1 (GL-z13-1) in our sample, and their photometric redshift, z=10.53-10.63 ( z=12.11-12.30 ) is comparable with our estimates.
